always_an_exit_desc-u: Once per session, when you would be trapped, surrounded, or cut off from movement, you may declare a plausible route of retreat, bypass, or concealment that fits the environment. The GM will confirm how risky it is, but the option exists unless the situation is truly sealed.
ammo_breacher_slugs_effect-u: Single-target slug. Deals double Structural Damage against objects and barriers. Normal damage against living targets.
ammo_hollow_point_effect-u: vs Soak 0-2: +1 damage die. vs Soak 3+: target's Physical Soak counts double.
ammo_shock_rounds_effect-u: +2d6 damage against mechanical targets only. Systems check on hit. No effect on biological targets.
ammo_subsonic_rounds_effect-u: Enables BF fire while suppressed. Damage die drops one size in all modes.
ammo_veil_charged_rounds_effect-u: Converts damage to [V] for the clip. Fumble = Veil Burnout.
animal_handling_desc-u: Governs the ability to calm, command, and train non-sapient creatures—including mundane animals, arcane beasts, genetically engineered pets, or bonded familiars with limited intelligence. Includes communication and behavioral control such as issuing commands to trained creatures, establishing trust with wild animals, or soothing an agitated mount. Does not cover riding or mounted combat (see Ride) and cannot control truly sentient beings.
anthropology_desc-u: The study of sentient cultures across all races—Veyra, Feran, Khadra, Dravi, Lyranni, Alteri, Kitsu, and Humans included. Allows a character to analyze social structures, customs, values, taboos, and behavioral patterns within and across societies. Used by diplomats, deep operatives, historians, and scholars studying post-Cataclysm shifts in societal evolution.
arcana_desc-u: Represents academic knowledge of magical theory, spellcraft principles, arcane history, and the underlying mechanics of ritual and enchantment. Used to identify magical phenomena, analyze runes, understand casting structures, and recognize different schools or traditions of magic—but not to cast spells. Essential for scholars, Codeweavers, spell engineers, and artifact appraisers.
arcanotech_desc-u: Covers the understanding, repair, and interfacing of arcane-technology hybrids—devices where magic and machinery are fused into one system. Includes rune-fused processors, spell-core batteries, living code matrices, arc-reactive engines, and enchanted circuits. Required for diagnosing, building, or modifying archanotech devices beyond standard user access.
archaeology_desc-u: The study and interpretation of ancient ruins, artifacts, and pre-Cataclysm civilizations. Allows characters to analyze excavation sites, identify cultural relics, determine authenticity, and reconstruct historical narratives from fragmented remains. Essential for researchers, relic hunters, and operatives working in lost zones, ruins, or derelict vaults.
archery_desc-u: Covers the use of bows, crossbows, and similar manually-powered ranged weapons. Favored by stealth operatives, wilderness trackers, and arcanists who channel spells through physical projectiles. Archery weapons are silent and not reliant on modern ammunition, making them ideal for covert operations or environments where magic and technology are unstable. As a Combat Skill, Archery cannot be Pushed.
architecture_desc-u: Covers the design, analysis, and understanding of constructed environments—buildings, bridges, fortifications, and urban layouts. Used to interpret blueprints, identify structural weak points, deduce historical construction patterns, or locate hidden rooms, vaults, or escape routes embedded within older designs. Invaluable when navigating ruins, planning sabotage, or deciphering pre-Cataclysm structures.
archivist_or_librarian_desc-u: Maintains access to restricted records, sealed collections, or pre-Cataclysm data stores.
athletics_desc-u: Represents overall physical conditioning and performance in physically demanding situations. Covers running, jumping, climbing, and feats of endurance—from sprinting through gunfire to scaling a building, chasing a suspect, or surviving long-distance travel through hostile terrain.
audience_liaison_desc-u: Someone who understands trends, outrage cycles, and how to make something go viral—or disappear.
back_alley_gear_desc-u: Lock picks, concealment aids, disposable comms, and equipment useful for quick access and fast exits.
biology_desc-u: Covers the study and understanding of living organisms, including anatomy, physiology, evolution, and ecosystems. Used to analyze tissue samples, identify species (natural or arcane), detect mutations or toxins, and understand biological processes relevant to healing, disease, or genetic manipulation. Vital for field researchers, medics, and xenobiologists.
bot_crawlerbot_labor_unit_effect-u: Multi-limbed maintenance bot. Navigates tight environments, carries up to 15 kg, follows task loop or direct commands.
bot_ghostkit_stealth_utility_bot_effect-: Veil-coated covert utility bot. Carries up to 8 kg. Moves quietly enough for occupied spaces. No combat capability.
bot_medbot_field_assistant_effect-u: Applies QuickPatch and StimPax without a skill roll. First Aid at base 40% autonomously. Cannot perform Medicine.
bot_packframe_cargo_bot_effect-u: Carries up to 80 kg. Follows operator at distance, accepts delivery routes or holding positions.
bot_sentryframe_security_bot_effect-u: Motion detection, audio monitoring, threat assessment. One non-lethal deterrent. Security Rating 3 for hacking/spoofing purposes.
brief_catch_desc-u: 5 minutes, no active threat. Recover 1d6 Strain.
bureaucracy_desc-u: Your understanding of administrative structures, regulations, and institutional systems—whether corporate, civic, or arcane. Used to navigate red tape, fill out forms correctly, manipulate official processes, or influence how quickly the machine grinds.
burner_package_desc-u: Disposable comms, false IDs, encrypted storage, and basic counter-surveillance tools.
career_arcane_gunslinger_anchor_shot_rul: Type: Action (Attack; Anti-Veil/Brand) • Cost: 1d4+1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make a firearm attack with your Bound Focus. On hit, until the end of your next turn the target cannot teleport, phase, or become invisible by any means. Veil-native targets must also pass POW with 1 penalty die or, for that duration, cannot move or change range bands.
career_arcane_gunslinger_arcane_cartridg: Type: Free Action (Infuse) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: This attack only — Your Focus shot deals arcane damage and +1 damage. Does not stack with other Infuse effects. Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_blackout_salvo_: Type: Maneuver (Infuse: Anti-Tech+) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — On hit, plus damage, emit a Short-radius shutdown pulse: unattended devices & drones off 1 round; attended-gear users must pass POW with 1 penalty die or lose Reactions and take –20% to all attacks until end of next turn (on success, still –10%). Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_bound_reload_ru: Type: Free Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — If you used any Infuse talent this round, reload your Focus as a Free Action instead of a Maneuver.
career_arcane_gunslinger_break_contact_r: Type: Reaction (Mobility/Guard) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you hit with your Bound Focus or when an enemy misses you with a melee attack, immediately Disengage and move one range band without triggering reactions. If you were not Engaged, instead gain Light Cover until the start of your next turn. This movement does not grant additional Actions or Reactions.
career_arcane_gunslinger_chain_shot_rule: Type: Free Action (Follow-Up) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you hit with an Infused attack using your Bound Focus, make one additional firearm attack with your Bound Focus at –10% against a different target within Short range of the original target. The follow-up attack deals arcane damage and may impale. The follow-up cannot receive any Infuse or Geometry effects, does not grant additional Reactions, and cannot trigger further follow-up attacks.
career_arcane_gunslinger_close_orbit_rul: Type: Maneuver (stance; ends end of your turn) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: At-will — When firing Handgun/SMG at Engaged range with your Focus, reduce close-quarters penalty by one step.
career_arcane_gunslinger_corner_cut_rule: Type: Free Action (Geometry) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: This attack only — Ignore Partial Cover for this Focus attack (no accuracy penalty). Cannot bypass Full Cover. Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_deadmark_beacon: Type: Action (Attack; Brand+) • Cost: 1d4+1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make a firearm attack with your Bound Focus. On hit, until the end of the current round: allies gain +1 bonus die on attacks against the target and ignore Partial Cover when doing so; the target suffers 1 penalty die on Dodge rolls.
career_arcane_gunslinger_disruptive_roun: Type: Maneuver (Infuse: Suppression) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: This attack only — On hit, the target loses Reactions and takes –10% to all attacks until the end of its next turn. If the target is a drone, turret, vehicle, or wearing a powered exosuit, it also suffers 1 penalty die on its next attack or Piloting/operation roll before the end of its next turn. Damage resolves normally. Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_flicker_ward_ru: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before the attacker’s roll to impose 1 penalty die on an incoming ranged or spell attack. If the attack misses, regain 1 Strain (once per Scene).
career_arcane_gunslinger_overcharged_car: Type: Maneuver (Infuse+) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: This attack only — This shot becomes arcane, gains +1 damage, and on a Hard+ hit treats the attack as AP 2 (stacks with Mark’s AP). Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_phase_pin_rules: Type: Maneuver (Brand/Anti-Veil) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: Mark ends when you attack or at Scene end — Mark a visible target within Medium range. Your next Infused hit against that target this Scene applies the following effect: Veil-native targets must pass POW with 1 penalty die or, on their next turn, cannot move, teleport, or phase by any means. Non-Veil targets instead suffer –10% to all movement-based actions until the start of their next turn.
career_arcane_gunslinger_rangefold_nudge: Type: Free Action (Geometry) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: This attack only — Treat the target as one range band closer for penalties (cannot enable out-of-range shots). Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_rangefold_step_: Type: Free Action (Geometry/Mobility) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Shift one range band; if not Engaged, this move does not trigger reactions. Cannot be used while Engaged.
career_arcane_gunslinger_severing_round_: Type: Action (Attack; Infuse: Anti-Ward+) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make a firearm attack with your Bound Focus. On hit, the attack deals arcane damage and for this attack only, the target’s physical and arcane DR are treated as 0. After damage resolves, the target must pass POW with 1 penalty die; on a failure, it is Staggered (loses Reactions and suffers –20% to attacks) until the end of its next turn. This effect does not bypass immunities or effects that prevent damage entirely.
career_arcane_gunslinger_short_fold_rule: Type: Maneuver (Geometry — turn-long) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene; lasts to end of your turn — Until turn end, Focus shots treat targets as one band closer and ignore 1 penalty die from light obstructions (smoke, shimmer, glare). Requires Bound Focus.
career_arcane_gunslinger_spell_conduit_r: Type: Action (Attack; Conduit) • Cost: 1d6 Strain + spell’s Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make a firearm attack with your Bound Focus and resolve its damage first. If the attack hits, immediately resolve one known single-target spell against the same target as part of the same Action. The spell is cast normally but the target suffers 1 penalty die on its resistance roll (2 penalty dice if the firearm attack achieved a Hard Success or better). This sequence counts as one attack action, does not grant additional Reactions, and cannot be combined with other capstone effects.
career_arcane_gunslinger_spellmark_rules: Type: Maneuver (Brand) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: Expires at the start of your next turn — Tag one visible target within Medium range. Your next attack against that target before the Mark expires gains +10%. If that attack also uses Arcane Cartridge, it gains AP 1 for that attack only. The Mark ends immediately after the attack resolves.
career_arcane_gunslinger_ward_piercing_r: Type: Maneuver (Infuse: Anti-Ward) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: This attack only — On hit, for this attack only, reduce the target’s arcane DR or ward DR by 2 (minimum 0). In addition, the target cannot gain new magical shielding until the start of your next turn. Requires Bound Focus.
career_bioforged_adamant_frame_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You harden skin, bone, and connective tissue into an impossibly dense living shell. Until the end of the Scene, you gain Damage Reduction 2 against all incoming damage (applied after armor). In addition, the first two times each Round you would be forcibly repositioned, knocked prone, or disarmed, the effect fails. Damage still resolves normally.
career_bioforged_adaptive_apex_rules-u: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would suffer a Major Wound or a physical effect that would Stun, Immobilize, Knock Prone, Disarm, or otherwise remove your control of your body. You negate the triggering effect entirely. Damage still resolves normally.
career_bioforged_adaptive_stance_rules-u: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You destabilize baseline physiology for rapid correction. Once per round, the first time you are hit by an attack, gain +1 bonus die on your next defense or counteraction against that creature.
career_bioforged_bone_weaponization_rule: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — While in Density Stance or Kinetic Stance, you extrude dense bone through your forearms or hands, forming controlled biological blades. Until you retract the blades or the Scene ends, you may use them for unarmed or melee attacks. Attacks made with these blades have the Impale quality and deal 1d8 damage. The blades are considered part of your body, not equipment. When the blades are retracted or at the end of the Scene (whichever comes first), you suffer 1d4 damage ignoring armor as your physiology returns to baseline.
career_bioforged_breath_control_rules-u: Type: Passive — You may hold your breath for five times the normal duration. Gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to resist gas, smoke, vacuum exposure, or oxygen deprivation.
career_bioforged_density_reinforcement_r: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Density Stance. Trigger: You make a CON check to resist crushing force, pressure, exhaustion, or suffocation. Gain +1 bonus die on the triggering roll.
career_bioforged_density_stance_rules-u: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You compress skeletal alignment and muscle density. Until the end of the current exchange, you count as one size category heavier for resisting forced movement, knockdown, and physical contests.
career_bioforged_explosive_entry_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Rider) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Kinetic Stance. Declare when you enter Engaged range and make a melee or unarmed attack. If the attack hits, it deals +1d6 damage.
career_bioforged_extreme_adaptation_rule: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You push your body beyond safe limits for a single task. Until the end of your turn, reduce difficulty for one physical feat by one step and gain +1 bonus die. This does not apply to attacks or defenses.
career_bioforged_fleshcraft_blades_rules: Type: Action • Cost: 2 Strain + 1d4 damage • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Density Stance or Kinetic Stance. You biofabricate up to two biological blades. Each blade deals 1d4+2 damage, has Impale and Thrown (Short), and AP 1. These blades are part of your body, not equipment. If at least one blade is retrieved before the end of the Scene, recover the 1d4 damage taken to create them as you reabsorb them.
career_bioforged_inertial_bracing_rules-: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Density Stance. Trigger: You would be forcibly repositioned, knocked prone, or pushed. Negate the forced movement or knockdown. Damage and other effects resolve normally.
career_bioforged_kinetic_singularity_rul: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You compress all kinetic output into a single, perfectly controlled surge. Until the end of your turn, you may make up to two melee or unarmed attacks, and you may move one range band between these attacks without triggering reactions. These attacks resolve normally and do not grant additional Reactions or trigger follow-up effects.
career_bioforged_kinetic_stance_rules-u: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You preload muscle fibers for explosive output. Until the end of the current exchange, the first time you enter Engaged range with a hostile creature on your turn, gain +1 bonus die on your next melee or unarmed attack.
career_bioforged_momentum_pressure_rules: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Any Stance. Trigger: A creature Engaged with you disengages or repositions. That creature suffers 1 penalty die on its next Action before the end of its next turn.
career_bioforged_no_safety_margin_rules-: Type: Passive — As long as you are Injured and using Bioforged biological weapons or another Bioforged talent that creates or extrudes biological weapons, attacks made with those weapons ignore the first point of armor or natural protection.
career_bioforged_perfected_fleshcraft_ru: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6+2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Required Stance: Any Stance. You assume a perfected living-weapon form. Until the end of the Scene, your melee and unarmed attacks deal 1d10 damage, have Armor Piercing 1, and Impale. Your body counts as a single integrated biological weapon. This does not stack with Bone Weaponization or Fleshcraft Blades.
career_bioforged_reactive_hardening_rule: Type: Reaction • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Adaptive Stance. Trigger: You are hit by an attack you can see. Reduce the damage taken by 1d4 after armor and soak.
career_bioforged_structural_interface_ru: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You adapt your body to directly interface with the environment. Until the end of the Scene, you may climb as normal movement, ignore penalties from unstable or hazardous footing, or treat your body as appropriate tools for bracing, anchoring, or forcing structures. This grants no combat bonuses.
career_bioforged_thorn_mantle_rules-u: Type: Free Action • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Required Stance: Any Stance. You extrude hardened, thorn-like growths across exposed skin. Until the start of your next turn, any creature that is grappling you, being grappled by you, restraining you, or being restrained by you must succeed on a DEX check or suffer 1d4 damage at the end of its turn. This damage has Armor Piercing 2 and cannot Impale.
career_bioforged_unyielding_physiology_r: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Until the end of the Scene, your body continues to function regardless of systemic failure. You do not lose Actions, Reactions, or mobility due to being Injured, Exhausted, suffocating, exposed to vacuum, crushing pressure, extreme temperatures, poison, or environmental hazards. You ignore effects that would otherwise Stun, Immobilize, Slow, Impair, or physically disable you. Damage and Strain still apply normally. This effect ends immediately if you are reduced below 0 HP or become Unconscious.
career_codeweaver_arc_sigil_link_rules-u: Type: Passive (Skill Substitution) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When interacting with Arcanotech systems, you may roll Magic (Technomancy) instead of Electronics or Computer Use. Does not apply to physical repairs or mechanical breakdowns.
career_codeweaver_arc_thread_override_ru: Type: Reaction (Spell) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A hostile device or automated system within Medium activates. Effect: Immediately cast a Technomancy spell you know with a casting time of 1 Action or less that targets technology. The spell resolves normally and cannot benefit from other cost-reduction talents. This talent may affect Hardened systems only if the chosen spell explicitly allows it.
career_codeweaver_backdoor_ritual_rules-: Type: Maneuver (Anchor) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you successfully establish access to a network, you embed a temporary backdoor during that intrusion. Once during that same intrusion, you may designate one system interaction you perform as priority execution. That interaction resolves immediately and does not require time to complete. The interaction must be a live authorization effect such as toggling or redirecting sensors, opening or closing a door or access point, suppressing or downgrading an alarm for 1 round, or issuing a simple command to a non-Hardened endpoint you already control. This does not apply to data extraction, Deep Scans, or latent intrusions, does not prevent escalation or System Overrun, and ends immediately when the intrusion ends. Only one Backdoor may be active at a time. Backdoors cannot be embedded into Hardened systems unless the initial access explicitly allowed Hardened interaction.
career_codeweaver_cascade_override_rules: Type: Special immediate (Chain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After you successfully disable or control a device, immediately target a second device within Short; the second target resists with 1 penalty die.
career_codeweaver_clean_loop_feedback_ru: Type: Free Action (Stabilize) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully cast a Technomancy spell that targets or interfaces with a device, drone, or Arcanotech system, reduce the next instance of Strain you suffer this scene from a tech-based backlash, resistance effect, or Arcanotech malfunction by 1 (minimum 0). This does not reduce Strain from System Overrun, does not reduce the Strain cost of the triggering spell, and does not restore spent Strain.
career_codeweaver_code_injection_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Payload) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully establish access to a network, or after you successfully affect an endpoint system with a contested slice or Technomancy spell, you may immediately compress one system interaction during that intrusion. Choose one live authorization action you perform during this intrusion (such as redirecting cameras, suppressing alarms, or manipulating access controls); that action requires one fewer round to complete (minimum 0). This does not reduce the time required for data extraction, Deep Scans, or latent intrusions, and does not delay escalation or System Overrun.
career_codeweaver_command_injection_rule: Type: Free Action (Multicommand) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you cast a Technomancy spell on, or issue a command to, a device or drone you control, you may issue two distinct commands instead of one. These commands resolve normally and do not reduce the time required for objectives, sustained effects, or latent intrusions.
career_codeweaver_crash_field_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Field) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Create a Medium-radius crash zone for 1 round. Affected drones and Arcanotech systems must pass a POW or INT test or suffer 2 penalty dice on all Actions until the start of your next turn; on a Fumble, the system is disabled for that round only. This effect does not apply to system failsafe checks, resistance rolls, or upkeep tests unless explicitly stated. This effect applies to Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_hardlight_override_rul: Type: Maneuver (Override) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project a hardlight sigil into a simple, unattended device within Short (door, turret, basic drone, unsecured node). Roll Magic (Technomancy) or Slicing (your choice). On a success, trigger one basic function (open, shut down, disengage). Does not bypass secure, encrypted, or complex systems, or systems under direct control of another slicer or AI; such systems resist using their normal opposed roll, without penalty, or require higher-tier effects. This effect cannot target Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_live_patch_redirect_ru: Type: Reaction (Guard/Redirect) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An ally within Short is targeted by a spell, ability, or tech effect. Effect: Redirect the effect to yourself or to a construct or drone you control, as long as the new target would be a valid target for the original effect. This does not bypass targeting restrictions, immunities, range limits, or effect requirements.
career_codeweaver_null_interference_fiel: Type: Maneuver (Field) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: At-will — Create a Short-radius disruption field centered on you for 1 round. Drones, archanotech, and automated systems inside suffer 1 penalty die on targeting, Reactions, and interfacing rolls. This does not affect spellcasting, manual actions, or passive effects.
career_codeweaver_overcode_detonation_ru: Type: Action (Network/Field) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target an Arcanotech network segment, drone cluster, or group of automated systems within Medium. Each affected system must pass a POW or INT test (by system type) or suffer one of the following effects, chosen per target: shut down until the start of your next turn, take 1d6 damage, or enter an uncontrolled berserk state for 1 round, during which it targets the nearest valid object or entity within its operational parameters. This effect represents catastrophic logic destabilization, not sustained control. Living creatures, cyberware, smart weapons attached to living users, and manually operated devices are unaffected. This talent may affect Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_protocol_ascendance_ru: Type: Action (Rule Override) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While you are actively connected, forcibly impose protocol instability on all drones, automated systems, and Arcanotech devices within Medium for the remainder of the scene. When you activate this talent, choose one imposed instability: (1) system Actions require one additional Maneuver to execute, (2) automated targeting suffers 1 penalty die, or (3) affected systems cannot make Reactions. This effect represents imposed instability, not control. While this talent is active, affected systems cannot benefit from or be affected by other rule-altering fields or persistent modifiers; Protocol Ascendance supersedes them. The effect ends immediately when the intrusion ends. Living creatures, cyberware, and manually operated devices are unaffected. This talent may affect Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_pulse_hijack_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Control) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt to seize momentary control of a simple automated device within Short (surveillance camera, turret, basic drone). Roll Magic (Technomancy) or Slicing opposed by the system’s Security Rating. On a success, you control the device for 1 round and may issue one simple command; on a Hard Success, issue two simple commands. Complex, attended, or AI-controlled systems resist with a bonus die. Hardened systems are immune to this talent.
career_codeweaver_reality_patch_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Auto-Cast) • Cost: Double the triggering spell’s Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You fail a defense against a tech-based or Arcanotech effect, or a system within Medium catastrophically malfunctions. Effect: Immediately cast a Technomancy spell you know with a casting time of 1 Action or less that could reasonably counter, stabilize, suppress, or override the triggering effect. The spell automatically succeeds but cannot deal damage, directly target enemies, or apply offensive conditions.
career_codeweaver_recursive_pulse_rules-: Type: Reaction (Retry) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You fail a Slicing or Magic (Technomancy) roll to access or affect a device within Short. Effect: Reroll the check with 1 penalty die. On success, resolve normally.
career_codeweaver_scramble_protocol_rule: Type: Maneuver (Debuff) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: At-will — Disrupt one active drone or Arcanotech system within Short for 1 round; it suffers 1 penalty die on all tech-based or automated actions. This disruption relies on exploiting unsecured control logic rather than brute-force interference. This effect does not affect Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_signal_possession_rule: Type: Action (Technomantic Overwrite) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While you are actively connected to a network or system, you forcibly overwrite the command identity of one drone, turret, or Arcanotech construct within Medium. The target resists using System Integrity (SI); on a failure, it becomes possessed by your technomantic signature for the remainder of the scene. While possessed, the system acts on your turn as an allied entity and follows your commands to the best of its capabilities. This effect persists even after you disconnect and is not ended by System Overrun or forced ejection. At scene end, the overwrite collapses and control immediately ends. This effect represents a temporary identity rewrite, not maintained authorization, and does not establish permanent ownership or safe persistence. Living creatures, cyberware, and manually operated devices are unaffected. This talent may affect Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_signal_sync_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — On your next Access Roll to establish entry into a network this scene, gain +1 bonus die. If instead you are making a contested slice against a bot/drone or other endpoint system this scene, gain +1 bonus die on that roll.
career_codeweaver_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_codeweaver_subroutine_leash_rules: Type: Passive (Control) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you have control of a drone or automated system through any effect (such as a talent, spell, or successful Slicing or Technomancy interaction), you may direct it as an allied NPC on your turn for the duration of that control. This does not grant control on its own and does not extend the duration of control effects.
career_codeweaver_synthetic_harmony_rule: Type: Free Action (Tempo) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully establish access to a network, or after you successfully affect a device or drone with a contested slice or Technomancy spell, gain +1 bonus die on your next Technomancy spell that targets or interfaces with that same system during this scene.
career_codeweaver_system_sovereignty_pro: Type: Action (Authority Imposition) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While you are actively connected to a network or system, you assert overriding technomantic priority within a technological space inside Medium. Choose up to three drones, automated systems, or Arcanotech devices you can perceive. Each chosen system resists using System Integrity (SI); on a failure, it becomes subordinate to your imposed authority for the remainder of the scene. While subordinate, you may issue one command per affected system per round as a Free Action on your turn; systems otherwise act normally. At the start of each subordinate system’s turn, it may attempt to resist continued subordination using System Integrity; on a success, it ignores your commands for that round. This authority persists even if you disconnect and is not ended by System Overrun or forced ejection. Control always collapses at scene end and does not represent permanent ownership or maintained access. Living creatures, cyberware, and manually operated devices are unaffected. This talent may affect Hardened systems.
career_codeweaver_veil_reactor_stabilize: Type: Reaction (Catastrophe Guard) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would suffer Veil Corruption, an Arcanotech malfunction, or take damage from a tech-based or Veil-powered source. Effect: Choose one: (1) Negate the triggering Veil Corruption or Arcanotech malfunction entirely, or (2) reduce the triggering damage by 1d6 (minimum 0). This talent represents an emergency stabilization event and may only be used once per session.
career_codeweaver_veil_thread_tap_rules-: Type: Maneuver (Scan) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — You attune briefly to ambient signal bleed and Arcanotech resonance. Until the end of the scene, you can detect nearby digital activity or Arcanotech signatures within Short range, even if encrypted, passive, or attempting to remain quiet. This reveals the presence and general nature of nearby systems or activity, not their contents or control points, and does not replace Network Scan once access is established.
career_combat_engineer_auto_repair_subro: Type: Action (Mod) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Install a repair routine on a drone or turret. On a Mechanics success, it regains 1d3 HP at the end of each of the next two rounds. Only one such routine may be active on a unit at a time.
career_combat_engineer_breachfinders_eye: Type: Maneuver (Scan) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Roll Mechanics or Demolitions to identify a weak point on a target, device, or structure within Short range. On a success, choose one benefit for your next attack or sabotage attempt against that same target before the end of your next turn: it gains +1 bonus die, and either deals +1d4 damage on a hit or imposes 1 penalty die on the target’s next Action roll before the end of its next turn.
career_combat_engineer_catastrophe_buffe: Type: Reaction (Mitigation) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A Mapped structure, vehicle, drone, turret, or piece of equipment within Medium would be destroyed, collapse, or suffer a major malfunction. Effect: You reroute power, brace load, or shed systems at the last possible moment. Reduce all damage, collapse effects, or malfunction severity caused by the triggering event by half (rounded down). Any remaining catastrophic outcome becomes a delayed hazard that triggers at the end of the next round instead of immediately.
career_combat_engineer_collapse_engineer: Type: Action (Demolitions) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Rig a prepared structure to collapse on a designated trigger. Requires Demolitions.
career_combat_engineer_deadzone_entry_ru: Type: Maneuver (Positioning) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you succeed on Breachfinder’s Eye, you may reposition one range band as part of the same action, ignoring difficult terrain created by debris or damage.
career_combat_engineer_failsafe_architec: Type: Reaction (Prevent) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A system within Short you interacted with this round would suffer a major malfunction. Effect: Attempt Mechanics to downgrade the malfunction to a minor malfunction instead.
career_combat_engineer_failsafe_codex_ru: Type: Reaction (Restore) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Instantly restore a damaged system you have interacted with to up to one-third of its maximum HP, and it gains advantage on checks to resist malfunctions for the remainder of the scene.
career_combat_engineer_field_constructor: Type: Action (Deployable) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Rapidly construct one: (1) Turret (2d6 damage, 3 rounds), (2) Barricade (+2 Armor & cover), or (3) Utility drone. Requires Mechanics. Breaks down at scene end.
career_combat_engineer_field_fix_rules-u: Type: Action (Repair) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Mechanics on a physically accessible device, drone, or structure to restore 1d6 + 2 HP (not above its maximum HP).
career_combat_engineer_hijack_protocol_r: Type: Action (Seize Control) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt to seize control of an automated drone or device within Medium. Roll Mechanics or Electronics opposed by System Integrity (SI). On a Hard Success, you control the system for 1 round; you may extend control by spending 1 Strain per additional round. This talent can target Hardened systems.
career_combat_engineer_improvised_expert: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When making a Mechanics or Electronics roll, choose one: ignore penalties for lacking proper tools, or if using proper tools, gain +1 bonus die. Choice is made when the roll is declared.
career_combat_engineer_master_of_collaps: Type: Action (Networked Triggers) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger all sabotage points you’ve placed in a structure. On success, all collapse zones deal 3d6 damage; targets must Dodge or become trapped.
career_combat_engineer_power_splice_rule: Type: Action (Power Surge) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Divert power between systems. On a Mechanics success, choose one: restore 2d6 HP to a device or drone, or reactivate a disabled system until the end of the round and allow it to take one simple action.
career_combat_engineer_precision_overrid: Type: Passive (Reroll) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When using Quick Bypass, you may reroll the Security Systems check, taking the new result.
career_combat_engineer_quick_bypass_rule: Type: Maneuver (Bypass) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt Security Systems to pick a non-magical lock or disable a non-magical alarm as a Maneuver instead of an Action. Cannot affect warded, arcane, or Hardened systems.
career_combat_engineer_rapid_jury_rig_ru: Type: Action (Temporary Fix) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Mechanics to create a temporary battlefield fix. On a success, it functions for the scene (≈10 minutes), then fails safely.
career_combat_engineer_reactive_primer_r: Type: Maneuver (Failsafe) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Attach a primer so that when a drone or turret is reduced to 0 HP, it remains operational at 1 HP long enough to take one final Action, then shuts down. A device cannot benefit from this effect more than once per scene.
career_combat_engineer_reactive_scaffold: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A nearby device or structure within Short takes damage. Effect: Attempt Mechanics to reduce the damage by 1d6.
career_combat_engineer_reactive_tune_up_: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An allied drone or device within Short takes damage. Effect: Attempt Mechanics to reduce the damage by 1d4.
career_combat_engineer_rigged_countermea: Type: Action (Trap) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After repairing or sabotaging an object, rig it with a countermeasure. The first time it is tampered with, choose one effect: it deals 1d6 damage, or it shuts down until the end of the next round.
career_combat_engineer_saboteurs_signatu: Type: Passive (Upgrade) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When placing a sabotage effect, add one: +1d6 damage, +10% avoid detection, or +1 round delay.
career_combat_engineer_structural_collap: Type: Action (Demolitions) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — On a Demolitions success, collapse a weakened structure or section. Targets in range take 2d6 damage unless they successfully Dodge.
career_combat_engineer_structural_famili: Type: Passive (Navigation) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully interact with a structure or system using Mechanics or Demolitions, you and allies gain +10% to Navigate and Stealth checks within that structure for the rest of the scene.
career_combat_engineer_system_subjugatio: Type: Action (Area Seize) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt to seize all devices in a small area: Opposed Electronics vs. System Integrity (SI). On Hard Success, control lasts the scene. This talent can target Hardened systems.
career_combat_engineer_tactical_recompil: Type: Action (Override) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt to override a hostile drone within Short. Roll Mechanics or Electronics opposed by System Integrity (SI). On a success, it becomes Neutral for 1 round; on a Hard Success, it follows one simple command that round. Cannot affect Hardened systems.
career_combat_medic_adrenal_shield_matri: Type: Free Action (Combat Stim) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you successfully restore HP with First Aid or Medicine, grant the target 1d6 temporary HP until the end of the scene. Temporary HP does not stack and does not count as healing for Healing Saturation.
career_combat_medic_combat_revive_rules-: Type: Action (Revive) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt a Medicine check on an unconscious or dying ally within Engaged range. On a success, restore HP equal to the normal result of the Medicine roll and the target may immediately attempt a CON check to regain consciousness with +1 bonus die. This healing follows all Healing Saturation and daily healing limits. On a failure, no HP is restored and the target remains unconscious or dying.
career_combat_medic_emergency_triage_net: Type: Action (Split Heal) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to two allies within Short. Make Medicine once and divide the HP healed between them (each must receive at least 1 HP).
career_combat_medic_endurance_conditioni: Type: Passive (Resilience) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Gain +2 maximum HP.
career_combat_medic_field_expeditor_rule: Type: Free Action (Accelerate) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — On your turn, perform First Aid or Medicine as a Free Action instead of an Action.
career_combat_medic_field_stabilizer_rul: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before the end of your turn, your next First Aid or Medicine check you make on an unconscious or dying ally gains +1 bonus die.
career_combat_medic_ghost_of_the_er_rule: Type: Free Action (Advance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you take a Move Maneuver toward an injured ally, that Move does not provoke Overwatch or reactions. Until your next turn, attacks targeting you suffer 1 penalty die.
career_combat_medic_golden_hour_doctrine: Type: Free Action (Protocol) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare at the start of your first turn in a hostile scene. Until the end of the scene, the first successful First Aid or Medicine check you make does not increase Healing Saturation and ignores the daily instant-healing cap for that one instance only. All other limits apply normally.
career_combat_medic_injector_harness_rul: Type: Maneuver (Injector) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Perform a First Aid, stim, or injector-based action as a Maneuver instead of an Action. The item is consumed normally, and all healing limits, daily uses, and Healing Saturation rules still apply.
career_combat_medic_last_measure_injecti: Type: Action (Extreme Intervention) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Medicine on an unconscious or dying ally within Engaged. On a success, restore HP equal to twice the normal Medicine result and the target immediately gains +2 Healing Saturation. On a failure, the attempt has no effect but still counts toward Healing Saturation limits.
career_combat_medic_medical_override_rul: Type: Free Action (Boost) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Before rolling First Aid or Medicine, declare a Medical Override. If the check succeeds, double the HP restored by that check before applying Healing Saturation, daily healing limits, or other reductions.
career_combat_medic_miracle_protocol_rul: Type: Action (Critical Care) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Medicine on an ally within Engaged. On a success, restore HP equal to the maximum possible result of the Medicine roll. You may also remove one minor condition, one toxin, or one step of Veil Corruption. Major Wounds are not removed. Healing limits apply.
career_combat_medic_no_one_dies_today_ru: Type: Reaction (Last Stand) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An ally within Medium would be reduced to 0 HP. Effect: Immediately attempt Medicine. On a success, the target is reduced to 0 HP but does not suffer death checks, Major Wound effects, or loss of consciousness until the end of the round and may act normally. All consequences resolve afterward unless the target is healed or stabilized.
career_combat_medic_overwatch_healer_rul: Type: Passive (Covering Fire) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Designate one injured ally (≤ half HP) within Short at the start of your turn. If an enemy attacks that ally, you gain +1 bonus die on your next attack against that enemy this scene.
career_combat_medic_pain_doesnt_matter_r: Type: Action (Revive Consciousness) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt a Medicine check on an unconscious ally or an ally who failed a CON check due to a major wound. On a success, the target regains consciousness but does not recover HP, remove wounds, or clear conditions.
career_combat_medic_painkiller_protocol_: Type: Passive (Buff) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — After you successfully restore HP to an ally with First Aid or Medicine, that ally gains +1 bonus die on their next action this scene. This bonus may only be applied once per healing instance.
career_combat_medic_pressure_seal_rules-: Type: Action (Stabilize) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt a Medicine check on a bleeding-out ally below 0 HP within Engaged range. On a success, the target is stabilized and no longer bleeding out. This does not restore HP or remove wounds or conditions.
career_combat_medic_quickdraw_injector_r: Type: Reaction (Inject) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You or an ally within Engaged would benefit from an injector or stim. Effect: Immediately apply one injector or stim to the target as a Reaction. The item is consumed normally, does not interrupt enemy actions already in progress, and follows all usage limits and Healing Saturation rules.
career_combat_medic_stay_with_me_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Stabilize) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Engaged or Short is reduced to 0 HP. Effect: Immediately attempt a First Aid check as a Reaction on that ally before death checks, major wound effects, or other consequences are resolved. On a success, the ally is stabilized.
career_combat_medic_surgical_clarity_rul: Type: Reaction (Reroll) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After you fail a First Aid or Medicine check, you may immediately reroll the check with +1 bonus die. If the reroll succeeds, resolve the effect normally; the action still counts as having taken its usual time.
career_combat_medic_tactical_redeploymen: Type: Reaction (Move) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Medium takes damage. Effect: Move one range band closer toward that ally (normal movement rules apply).
career_combat_medic_trauma_team_reflexes: Type: Free Action (Tactics) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Swap Initiative with a willing ally; both must not have acted yet this round.
career_combat_medic_triage_commander_rul: Type: Free Action (Coordination) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you successfully restore HP to an ally on your turn, choose one other ally within Short. That ally may immediately take one Maneuver that does not provoke reactions. This movement must be resolved immediately or is lost.
career_combat_medic_veil_immunologist_ru: Type: Action (Cleanse) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt a Medicine check on an ally within Engaged who suffered Veil Corruption this scene. On a success, reduce the Corruption by one step as defined by the system. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on Medicine checks made to treat Veil-based or arcane afflictions.
career_combat_medic_vital_scan_overlay_r: Type: Free Action (Scan) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — If the target is below half HP, gain +1 bonus die on a First Aid or Medicine check against them. On a success, you may also identify medical conditions only, such as internal injuries, bleeding, toxins, infections, or trauma-related complications.
career_dreamshaper_constructed_nightmare: Type: Action (Weaponized Illusion) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Illusion) opposed by the target’s Perception. On a success, the target is trapped in a personal hallucination so complete that its body responds to perceived trauma. The target takes 2d6 Veil damage and is Stunned until the end of its next turn as its nervous system reacts to threats the mind insists are real. On a failure, the target takes 1d6 Veil damage and is Shaken until the end of its next turn. This effect represents psychosomatic harm — the body cannot distinguish what the senses report from what is actually happening.
career_dreamshaper_exploit_misjudgment_r: Type: Reaction (Exploit) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A creature fails a roll to detect or see through one of your illusions or Dreamshaper talent effects. Effect: That creature suffers 1 penalty die on its next Action taken before the end of its next turn. This represents the disorientation of committing to false information.
career_dreamshaper_false_cue_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before casting an Illusion spell that creates a sensory distortion, false image, or concealment effect, gain +1 bonus die on the spellcasting roll. This bonus applies to the next qualifying Illusion spell cast this Scene and expires if unused by Scene end.
career_dreamshaper_false_terrain_rules-u: Type: Action (Area Denial) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project an illusory environmental hazard — fire, collapsing flooring, toxic gas, electrical discharge — covering a Short-radius area at a point within Medium range. The illusion persists until the start of your next turn. Each creature entering or starting its turn in the area must roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature treats the hazard as real: it must spend one additional Maneuver to cross the area or choose to avoid it entirely. On a success, the creature recognizes the illusion and moves normally. The illusion deals no actual damage and does not create real obstacles.
career_dreamshaper_flickering_presence_r: Type: Maneuver (Concealment) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the start of your next turn, you distort your visual and auditory profile through minor illusion. The next ranged attack targeting you before the effect ends suffers 1 penalty die. This effect ends immediately after one attack targets you or at the start of your next turn, whichever comes first.
career_dreamshaper_ghost_protocol_rules-: Type: Action (Team Erasure) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to four willing creatures within Short range, which may include yourself. Until the end of the Scene, each target is woven into a persistent, layered illusion that masks their presence from external observation. While affected, each target gains +1 bonus die on all Stealth rolls, and any creature attempting to detect or target them must succeed on a Perception roll opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the observer cannot target that creature with attacks, spells, or targeted abilities this round and does not register their presence as a threat. This check must be made each time the observer attempts to target an affected creature. This effect ends for any individual who makes an attack or casts an offensive spell, and cannot be reapplied to that individual for the remainder of the Scene. While Ghost Protocol is active, you cannot cast Illusion spells or activate Dreamshaper talents that create constructs or impose perception-altering effects on enemies.
career_dreamshaper_layered_deception_rul: Type: Free Action (Augment) • Cost: +1 Strain added to the spell's cost • Usage: At-will — When casting an Illusion spell, you may weave additional sensory layers into the effect — secondary sounds, ambient scent shifts, peripheral motion cues. Creatures attempting to see through the resulting illusion suffer 1 penalty die on their Perception roll, or Insight if the illusion is part of a disguise or social deception. This does not affect resistance rolls against spells that deal damage or impose conditions directly.
career_dreamshaper_mirage_displacement_r: Type: Maneuver (Positional Decoy) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Create a brief illusory copy of yourself at your current position, then immediately move one range band without provoking Reactions. Until the start of your next turn, the first attack that would target you must first roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion) to identify the real target. On a failure, the attack targets the decoy and misses automatically. On a success, the attack resolves normally against you. The decoy dissipates after one attack resolves against it or at the start of your next turn.
career_dreamshaper_mirror_shroud_rules-u: Type: Action (Group Concealment) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose up to three willing creatures within Short range, which may include yourself. Until the start of your next turn, each target gains +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls. Creatures attempting to detect or target the shrouded group from beyond Short range must succeed on Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion) or treat the group as undetected — they cannot target any member of the group with attacks, spells, or targeted abilities while this result stands. This effect ends for any individual who makes an attack or casts an offensive spell. Only one Mirror Shroud may be active at a time.
career_dreamshaper_perceptual_isolation_: Type: Action (Sensory Cage) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Illusion) opposed by the target’s Perception. On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target treats all creatures and objects beyond Engaged range as unseen: attacks against targets beyond Engaged range suffer 2 penalty dice, and the target cannot use Reactions triggered by events beyond Engaged range. The target is aware something is wrong but cannot identify the source. On a failure, the target is instead Disoriented until the end of its next turn.
career_dreamshaper_perfect_mask_rules-u: Type: Action (Arcane Disguise) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Weave a complete illusory disguise over yourself or one willing creature within Engaged range. The disguise covers visual appearance, voice, and ambient scent. It persists for the remainder of the Scene or until you dismiss it as a Free Action. Creatures interacting directly with the disguised target may roll Insight opposed by your Magic (Illusion) to detect the deception. Enhanced or augmented senses grant +1 bonus die on this detection roll. The disguise does not replicate biometric data, arcane signatures, or cyberware interfaces, and does not hold up against physical contact that contradicts the illusion.
career_dreamshaper_persistent_construct_: Type: Passive (Construct Enhancement) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you create an illusory construct using a Dreamshaper talent (such as Phantom Threat or False Terrain), the construct persists for one additional round beyond its stated duration. Additionally, creatures that succeed on their Perception roll to see through the illusion cannot clearly communicate that certainty to others until the start of their next turn. They may shout a warning, but it grants no mechanical benefit before then.
career_dreamshaper_phantom_formation_rul: Type: Action (Mass Construct) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Create up to three illusory hostile presences at points within Medium range. Each construct appears real to all senses. Each creature within Short of any construct must roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature treats all constructs as real threats until the end of the round and cannot willingly move closer to any construct. Additionally, any creature that fails this roll and takes an offensive Action must target one of the constructs instead of a real target (creature’s choice of which construct). On a success, the creature recognizes all constructs and is unaffected. Constructs dissipate at the end of the round.
career_dreamshaper_phantom_sound_rules-u: Type: Action (Distraction) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Create a brief, convincing sound at a point within Medium range — a voice, a footstep, a weapon cycling, a door opening. Any creature within Short of that point that can hear it must roll Listen opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature must spend its next available Maneuver investigating or repositioning toward the sound source. If the creature has already used its Maneuver this turn, it instead suffers 1 penalty die on its next Action as its attention is divided between the false stimulus and its current objective. The GM determines the specific behavioral response consistent with the creature's disposition and alertness, but the Maneuver cost or penalty die always applies. This effect is momentary and does not sustain.
career_dreamshaper_phantom_threat_rules-: Type: Action (Construct) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Create an illusory hostile presence — a figure, a drone, a creature — at a point within Medium range. The construct appears real to sight and sound. Each creature within Short of the construct that can perceive it must roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature treats the threat as real until the start of your next turn and cannot willingly move closer to the construct. On a success, the creature recognizes the illusion and is unaffected. The construct does not act and cannot deal damage. It dissipates at the start of your next turn.
career_dreamshaper_redirect_perception_r: Type: Reaction (Misdirect) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You or an ally within Short range is targeted by a ranged attack or targeted spell. Effect: Roll Magic (Illusion) opposed by the attacker’s Perception. On a success, the attack suffers 1 penalty die as the attacker’s targeting is momentarily disrupted by a flash of false spatial data. This does not redirect the attack to another target and cannot be used against area-of-effect abilities.
career_dreamshaper_sensory_overload_rule: Type: Action (Disrupt) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Illusion) opposed by the target’s Perception. On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all Actions and cannot benefit from enhanced senses, targeting augmentations, or sensory cyberware. On a Hard Success or better, the target also cannot take Reactions until the start of your next turn. This effect represents a cascade of false stimuli overwhelming the target’s ability to process genuine information.
career_dreamshaper_sensory_read_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Assess) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Observe a target within Short range. Roll Perception opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the GM reveals one of the following: the target's primary sensory reliance (sight, hearing, technological augmentation), whether the target has enhanced or augmented perception (cyberoptics, Veilsight, sensor suites), or whether the target is currently under a perception-altering effect. Your next Magic (Illusion) roll targeting that creature this Scene gains +10%.
career_dreamshaper_shattered_confidence_: Type: Free Action (Exploit) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When a creature fails a Perception or Insight roll to see through one of your illusions or Dreamshaper talent effects by a margin of 20% or more, that creature becomes Shaken until the end of its next turn. The Shaken condition applies immediately and does not require a separate roll.
career_dreamshaper_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_dreamshaper_the_scene_is_mine_rul: Type: Action (Environmental Overwrite) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Overwrite the perceived environment within a Medium-radius area centered on you for the remainder of the Scene. You determine what all creatures within the area perceive: terrain, lighting, obstacles, ambient sound, and environmental conditions. Each hostile creature entering or starting its turn in the area must roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature treats the illusory environment as real for all movement and targeting decisions until the start of its next turn, when it must roll again. On a success, the creature is unaffected for that round. The overwrite deals no actual damage and does not create real barriers or hazards. You must maintain concentration — if you are incapacitated or Stunned, the overwrite collapses immediately. While this effect is active, you cannot create other illusory constructs using Dreamshaper talents.
career_dreamshaper_total_perceptual_coll: Type: Action (Mass Hallucination) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to four hostile creatures within Medium range that can perceive you. Roll Magic (Illusion) opposed by each target’s Perception. On a failure, for the next 2 rounds, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all Actions and Reactions, cannot benefit from enhanced senses or targeting augmentations, and the first time each round it attempts to take an Action, it must succeed on a Perception roll or lose that Action as it reacts to stimuli that do not exist. On a success, the target is instead Disoriented until the end of its next turn. When you activate this talent, you immediately suffer 1 penalty die on all Magic (Illusion) rolls for the next 2 rounds.
career_dreamshaper_unreality_cloak_rules: Type: Free Action (Personal Erasure) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Until the end of the Scene, you become extraordinarily difficult to perceive, target, or track. Any creature attempting to target you with an attack, spell, or ability must first succeed on a Perception roll opposed by your Magic (Illusion). On a failure, the creature cannot target you with that action — the action is wasted but any resources spent are still consumed. On a success, the action resolves normally. This effect ends immediately if you deal damage to another creature or apply an offensive condition. While this effect is active, allies cannot benefit from Dreamshaper talents that require you to designate or protect them (such as Veil Cloak, Mirror Shroud, or Redirect Perception).
career_dreamshaper_veil_cloak_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Concealment) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose yourself or one willing ally within Short range. Until the start of your next turn, the target gains +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls and attacks targeting them from beyond Engaged range suffer 1 penalty die. This effect ends immediately if the target makes an attack or casts an offensive spell.
career_dreamshaper_veil_static_field_rul: Type: Maneuver (Field) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Create a Short-radius field of persistent sensory distortion centered on a point within Medium range. The field persists until the start of your next turn. While inside the field, creatures other than you and up to three designated allies suffer 1 penalty die on Perception rolls and on ranged attacks targeting creatures outside the field. Creatures entering the field for the first time must roll Perception opposed by your Magic (Illusion) or become Disoriented until they leave the field or until the field ends.
career_faceman_adopt_the_angle_rules-u: Type: Reaction (declare before scrutiny or targeting) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When your presence or intent is questioned, you immediately lean into a believable angle: helper, bystander, messenger, contractor, distraction, or problem-solver. Make a contested roll using Deception or Persuade vs. POW. On a success, the next social check made against you that relies on assumptions about your intent or motive gains a penalty die for the remainder of this exchange. This does not affect hard evidence, physical inspection, or direct attacks.
career_faceman_all_eyes_no_cover_rules-u: Type: Passive • Usage: 1/Scene — Once per scene, when you draw hostile attention or suffer damage from an enemy, choose one ally who can see you. Until the end of the round, that ally ignores one penalty die from partial cover or situational modifiers when making an Action against the enemy that targeted you.
career_faceman_break_their_rhythm_rules-: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You disrupt tempo through movement, noise, or interference. Choose one visible enemy. Until the end of the round, that enemy cannot take Reactions. This does not affect their Action or Maneuver.
career_faceman_burn_the_opening_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You exploit a fleeting mistake with ruthless timing. Choose one visible enemy. The next allied Action taken against that enemy this round gains a bonus die. If no ally attacks that enemy before the round ends, the opening collapses and that enemy gains a bonus die on their next Action.
career_faceman_control_the_narrative_rul: Type: Free Action (Reframe) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After an enemy completes an Action that visibly escalates the situation (gunfire, threats, collateral damage, panic, or overt authority pressure), choose one ally who can see or hear you. Until the end of the round, that ally gains +1 bonus die on their next Action that directly exploits the confusion, fear, or emotional shift you highlighted. This does not interrupt the triggering Action and does not impose penalties, restrictions, or forced behavior on enemies.
career_faceman_cover_the_team_rules-u: Type: Reaction (declare before an ally’s roll) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When an ally makes a social roll in an interaction you initiated or reframed, you step into the spotlight. That roll gains a bonus die. If scrutiny or hostility follows immediately, it is directed at you instead.
career_faceman_dead_man_walking_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Defiant Stand) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You suffer damage from an enemy during a hostile scene. You refuse to go down cleanly. Until the end of your next turn, you ignore penalties from wounds and conditions. During this time, all allies gain +1 bonus die on their first Action taken against the enemy that damaged you. In addition, until the end of the scene, that enemy suffers 1 penalty die on all Actions that do not target you, as your refusal to fall makes you the center of the confrontation.
career_faceman_draw_the_eye_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Attention Pull) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A visible enemy declares an Action targeting an ally. You deliberately pull attention toward yourself through movement, provocation, or assertive positioning. If the enemy continues targeting the ally, the triggering Action suffers 1 penalty die. If the enemy switches their target to you, resolve the Action normally. If the enemy switches their target to you and proceeds with the Action, the first allied Action taken against that enemy before the end of the round gains +1 bonus die, as your intervention draws their focus and exposes them.
career_faceman_everyone_watches_you_blee: Type: Passive (Leverage) • Usage: 1/Scene — Once per scene, when you suffer damage or a negative consequence during a tense or hostile situation, choose one ally who can see you. That ally gains +1 bonus die on their next Action this scene if it directly exploits the opening created by that moment.
career_faceman_false_opening_rules-u: Type: Action (Bait) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — On your turn, deliberately present vulnerability, confusion, or retreat. Choose one visible enemy. Until the start of your next turn, that enemy suffers 1 penalty die on their first Action that targets anyone other than you. If they target you instead, resolve the Action normally.
career_faceman_familiar_signals_rules-u: Type: Passive • Usage: 1/Scene — Once per scene, when a symbol, phrase, behavior, or visual cue associated with belonging or credibility is present, you may lean into it. The next social roll you make that references or aligns with that signal gains a bonus die. If the roll fails, attention shifts toward you.
career_faceman_follow_my_lead_rules-u: Type: Reaction (after your success; before an ally’s roll) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you succeed on a social roll or misdirection effect during a tense or hostile situation, choose one ally who can see or hear you. Their next Action this scene gains a bonus die if it builds on your setup, distraction, or framing.
career_faceman_force_the_tell_rules-u: Type: Reaction (declare before Initiative is rolled) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Before Initiative is rolled, choose one visible enemy. That enemy must declare whether they intend to engage at range, close distance, or reposition before Initiative is set. Allies gain a bonus die on their first Action this scene that exploits that declared intent. This declaration does not restrict the enemy’s actions; it represents their stated intent at the start of the fight.
career_faceman_keep_it_messy_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When an interaction is on the brink of turning violent or fully hostile, you introduce a complication: a question, a movement, a contradiction, or a sudden change of focus. Make a contested roll using Persuade vs. POW. On a success, the next hostile Action taken in this scene suffers a penalty die. On a failure, the opposition commits hard and the next hostile Action against you gains a bonus die.
career_faceman_lock_the_mistake_rules-u: Type: Reaction (declare after an enemy resolves an Action) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a visible enemy misses an attack, fails a roll, or overextends, you capitalize instantly. That enemy may not change targets until the end of their next turn.
career_faceman_no_second_chances_rules-u: Type: Reaction (declare after an enemy resolves an Action) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a visible enemy misses an attack, fails a roll, or otherwise overcommits during combat, you immediately exploit the mistake before it can be corrected. Until the end of the round, the next two allied Actions taken against that enemy gain a bonus die, and that enemy may not benefit from Reactions during this window. If no ally acts against that enemy before the round ends, the opportunity is lost.
career_faceman_plausible_presence_rules-: Type: Passive • Usage: 1/Scene — The first time you initiate a social interaction with a new person or group this scene, choose Deception or Persuade. That roll gains a bonus die, provided you act as though you belong in the moment rather than forcing the interaction.
career_faceman_read_the_gate_rules-u: Type: Maneuver • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you engage a group or individual who controls access, movement, or information, make an Insight roll. On a success, you identify who is paying attention, who is making decisions in practice, and what kind of behavior they expect to see next (confidence, deference, speed, routine, hesitation). Your next social roll against that person or group this scene gains a bonus die.
career_faceman_set_the_tone_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a conversation, standoff, or encounter is about to turn hostile, make a contested roll using Persuade vs. POW against the most attentive opposing figure. On a success, the situation holds together long enough for one more exchange before escalation can occur. On a failure, tension spikes and the next hostile action against you gains a bonus die.
career_faceman_slip_the_moment_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Escalation Control) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before the first hostile Action of the scene resolves. Choose one ally who can see or hear you. That ally gains +1 bonus die on their first Action this scene. If Initiative has already been rolled, you may instead impose 1 penalty die on the first hostile Action taken against you this scene.
career_faceman_speak_their_language_rule: Type: Maneuver • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you engage a person or group whose norms, slang, or expectations differ from your own, choose Deception, Persuade, or Etiquette. Until the end of the current exchange, the first roll you make using that skill ignores one penalty die caused by unfamiliarity, cultural mismatch, or improper behavior.
career_faceman_split_attention_rules-u: Type: Action (Disruption) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You create conflicting cues through movement, noise, or positioning. Choose two visible enemies within Medium. Until the end of the round, the first Action taken by each of those enemies suffers 1 penalty die if it targets different allies.
career_faceman_the_exit_was_always_there: Type: Reaction (Extraction) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a scene turns decisively against the crew, you exploit a narrow escape vector. Choose up to three allies who can see or hear you. Each chosen ally may immediately take one Move Maneuver as a Free Action. This movement does not trigger attacks of opportunity, Overwatch, or other Reactions. After resolving these movements, each chosen ally may reposition into Cover, break line of sight, or disengage from enemies if terrain and positioning plausibly allow. This movement must be resolved immediately or the opportunity is lost.
career_faceman_the_long_con_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare that you prepared for this situation earlier. Choose one plausible setup detail you could have influenced (timing, positioning, misinformation, mistaken identity, false expectation). For the remainder of this scene, you gain a bonus die on rolls that directly exploit that setup.
career_faceman_the_perfect_setup_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You reveal that the last few moments were exactly where you wanted them. Choose up to three allies and one visible enemy. Until the end of the round, the first Action taken by each chosen ally against that enemy gains a bonus die.
career_gravesinger_army_of_dusk_rules-u: Type: Action (Mass Raising) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Raise up to three corpses within Medium range simultaneously. Up to two may be non-sentient corpses raised as per Raise the Dead. One may be a sentient corpse raised as per Pale Servant — applying all of that talent's rules, including the condition system and permanent collapse at Scene end. Roll Magic (Necromancy) once — the result applies to all three. All raised corpses act on your Initiative and persist for the remainder of the Scene. While Army of Dusk is active, the normal Pale Sovereignty limit of one active thrall is suspended — all three may remain active simultaneously. If a raised corpse is destroyed, it cannot be re-raised this Session.
career_gravesinger_corpse_anchor_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Control) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Briefly animate a corpse within Short range just enough to grab and impede an enemy adjacent to it. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's STR. On a success, the target is Restrained until the start of your next turn as dead limbs lock around them. The corpse is not raised — it does not act, does not count against Pale Sovereignty, and collapses back to inert once the effect ends.
career_gravesinger_dead_pull_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Intercept) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Short range takes damage. Effect: Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the damage source's relevant attack or casting skill. On a success, channel a surge of necrotic energy to absorb part of the incoming harm — the ally reduces the incoming damage by 1d4 before Soak is applied. This cannot reduce damage below 1.
career_gravesinger_death_curse_rules-u: Type: Action (Curse) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, until the end of the Scene, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all CON rolls and cannot recover HP through rest, passive regeneration, or non-magical means. On a failure, the effect does not resolve and the Strain is still spent.
career_gravesinger_death_knell_rules-u: Type: Action (Execute) • Cost: 2d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Target one living creature within Short range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities cannot be targeted. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, deal 2d10 Veil damage. On a failure of the initial opposed roll, deal 1d10 Veil damage only. When you activate this talent, you immediately take 1d6 Veil damage as the lethal resonance passes through you on its way out.
career_gravesinger_death_sense_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Assess) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Attune briefly to the death resonance of a target within Short range. If the target is living, roll Perception opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the GM reveals one of the following: whether the target is Dying or within 25% of their maximum HP, whether the target is currently under a curse or entropy effect, or whether the target has been previously affected by Necromancy this Scene. Your next Magic (Necromancy) roll targeting that creature this Scene gains +10%. If the target is a corpse, its condition — Fresh or Decayed — is revealed automatically without a roll.
career_gravesinger_deathmark_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Mark) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target one creature within Medium range. Roll Magic (Necromancy) opposed by the target's POW. On a success, the target is marked for death. Until the marked target is reduced to 0 HP or the Scene ends, the first attack made against the marked target each round gains +1 bonus die, regardless of who makes it. On a failure of the opposed roll, the effect does not apply and the Strain is still spent. Only one Deathmark may be active at a time.
career_gravesinger_drain_momentum_rules-: Type: Free Action (Exploit) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You deal Veil damage with a Gravesinger drain talent. Effect: Your next Magic (Necromancy) roll this Scene gains +10%. This bonus expires if unused by Scene end.
career_gravesinger_entropy_aura_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Field) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project a field of necrotic entropy centered on you. Until the start of your next turn, all hostile creatures within Short range suffer 1 penalty die on CON rolls and on rolls to resist conditions. This effect applies to all qualifying creatures simultaneously and does not require individual targeting rolls.
career_gravesinger_entropy_mark_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Mark) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Designate one creature within Short range. The next roll that creature makes directly opposed by you this Scene suffers 1 penalty die. This effect applies once and then ends. Only one Entropy Mark may be active at a time.
career_gravesinger_grave_pact_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Survival) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would be reduced to 0 HP. Effect: Immediately drain life from all hostile living creatures within Engaged range. Spirits, drones, constructs, and non-living entities are not affected.
