career_pilot_overdrive_calibration_rules: Type: Free Action (Speed) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Increase the Speed Rating of a vehicle you are piloting or linked to by +1 for one Pursuit or Evasion round. This is applied after the Pursuit roll is made.
career_pilot_priority_channel_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Assist) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: Another character within Medium fails a Perception, Piloting, or Drone Operation check. Effect: That character rerolls the check with +1 bonus die.
career_pilot_remote_override_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Command) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Issue one immediate command to a drone or linked vehicle to move, interact, or activate a system.
career_pilot_rigsight_linkage_rules-u: Type: Passive (Stabilization) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While piloting or drone-rigged, you ignore penalty dice caused by movement, unstable footing, or active rigging when firing your own handheld ranged weapons. In addition, 1/Session, gain +1 bonus die on one such attack made while actively rigged.
career_pilot_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_pilot_suppressive_vectoring_rules: Type: Reaction (Drone) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A drone you control misses an attack. Effect: The target of that attack suffers 1 penalty die on its next action this round due to erratic movement and suppressive fire.
career_pilot_swarm_logic_override_rules-: Type: Action (Coordinated Command) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Issue one command simultaneously to all drones you control within Medium. Each drone may immediately take one simple action (move, basic attack, or utility) in any order. This command does not count as multiple commands and cannot be repeated this scene.
career_pilot_tactical_exfiltration_proto: Type: Reaction (Forced Extraction) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: The current scene escalates into overwhelming danger or imminent mission failure. Effect: Immediately extract all willing allies within Medium to your vehicle or to a secured aerial position you control. This movement ignores enemy reactions, terrain, suppression, and restraints short of full immobilization. The scene immediately ends or transitions at the GM’s discretion.
career_pilot_tactical_uplink_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assist) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you succeed on a Perception or Drone Operation roll to identify a threat, choose one ally; that ally’s next attack against that target this round gains +1 bonus die. This counts as an Assist and does not stack with other Assist effects.
career_pilot_tethered_assault_ai_rules-u: Type: Action (Activate AI) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Activate a tethered combat subroutine in one drone you control. For the rest of the scene, that drone may take one simple action (move or basic attack) at the start of your turn without requiring a command. This action cannot trigger or benefit from command-based talents.
career_pilot_throttle_reflex_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you are required to immediately make a Piloting or Drone Operation roll to evade danger, escape pursuit, or navigate a hazard (including retreating under fire), gain +1 bonus die on that roll. Must be declared before the roll is made.
career_pilot_thruster_kickout_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Mitigate/Displace) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: Your vehicle is hit by an attack. Effect: Declare before damage is rolled; halve the damage (round up) and immediately move your vehicle 1 range band.
career_pilot_trace_disruptor_grid_rules-: Type: Reaction (Countermeasures) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are targeted by a Slicing attempt or vehicle lock-on. Effect: Impose 1 penalty die on the attacker’s roll and mask your vehicle or drone’s signature until the start of your next turn.
career_shadowblade_blurred_step_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Advance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are targeted by a ranged or melee attack and have not acted yet this round. Effect: After the attack resolves, move 1 range band as a Free Action.
career_shadowblade_cloak_in_motion_rules: Type: Passive (Movement Stealth) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls while moving, provided you take no Action that round other than movement or repositioning.
career_shadowblade_cloaked_predator_rule: Type: Passive (Ambush) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While Cloak in Motion is active or while you are in Stealth, gain +10% on melee or thrown attacks against unaware or distracted targets.
career_shadowblade_crimson_cascade_rules: Type: Passive (Bleed Amplification) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Against Bleeding targets, your melee attacks deal +1 damage. If a Bleeding target drops below 50% HP, they lose their next Reaction.
career_shadowblade_crimson_rhythm_rules-: Type: Passive (Bleed Debuff) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Targets Bleeding from your attacks suffer –10% to all physical actions. If a Bleeding target takes 2 or more damage in a round, their Bleed damage increases to 2 per round on their next turn.
career_shadowblade_cut_and_vanish_rules-: Type: Special immediate (Disengage) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you hit with a melee attack, immediately Disengage as a Free Action. This movement must end behind Cover, concealment, or outside Engaged range.
career_shadowblade_draw_and_cut_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Follow-up) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you use Throw, Move, Strike, your next melee attack this round gains +10% and ignores penalty dice from Partial Cover or concealment.
career_shadowblade_fade_from_the_record_: Type: Reaction (Total Disengage) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would be detected, restrained, or surrounded. Effect: Immediately enter Stealth and reposition yourself anywhere within Short where concealment is plausible. Enemy units lose awareness of your exact location, and the scene ends or transitions at the GM’s discretion.
career_shadowblade_final_knife_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Impossible Throw) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make one thrown weapon attack as a Free Action against a target within Medium, even if the target is behind Full Cover or out of line of sight. This attack cannot benefit from bonus dice.
career_shadowblade_ghost_opener_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before rolling a melee or thrown attack made from Stealth or against a target that is unaware or surprised; gain +1 bonus die on that attack.
career_shadowblade_knifes_memory_rules-u: Type: Passive (Draw/Initiative) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Once per round, you may draw a melee or thrown weapon as a Free Action. In the first round of combat, if you are armed with a light melee or thrown weapon, gain +1 bonus die on your Initiative roll.
career_shadowblade_momentum_slice_rules-: Type: Passive (Tempo) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/round — If you move at least 1 range band before making a melee attack, gain +10% on that attack. On a hit, you may take a second Maneuver this round by spending 2 Strain.
career_shadowblade_one_among_many_rules-: Type: Passive (Camouflage) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls when operating in crowded, cluttered, or high-traffic environments where blending in is plausible.
career_shadowblade_phantom_execution_rul: Type: Special immediate (Stealth Attack) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — After you use Vanish Step, make one melee or thrown attack from Stealth without breaking Stealth. That attack gains +1 bonus die and deals +1 damage.
career_shadowblade_phantom_reversal_rule: Type: Reaction (Riposte) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You successfully Dodge a melee attack. Effect: Immediately make one melee attack as a Free Action against the attacker; this attack cannot be Dodged or responded to with Reactions. On a hit, deal +1 damage.
career_shadowblade_precision_hemorrhage_: Type: Special immediate (Bleed) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you land a melee hit with a Hard Success or better, the target begins Bleeding (1 damage per round for 3 rounds or until treated with First Aid).
career_shadowblade_pressure_point_rules-: Type: Special immediate (Debilitate) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When your melee or thrown attack achieves a Hard Success or better, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all physical skill rolls and attacks until the start of their next turn.
career_shadowblade_quiet_terror_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Fear) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you kill a target from Stealth, all enemies within Short must pass POW or suffer –10% to all actions against you until the start of their next turn.
career_shadowblade_shatterpoint_strike_r: Type: Special immediate (Execution) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare before rolling a melee or thrown attack against a target that is unaware or below 50% HP. On a hit, the attack ignores Armor and deals +2 damage. This attack cannot trigger follow-up attacks or chain effects.
career_shadowblade_silent_collapse_rules: Type: Action (Fear & Disruption) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you kill a target from Stealth, all enemies within Short immediately lose their Reaction and suffer –10% to all actions until the end of their next turn. This effect does not stack with other fear effects.
career_shadowblade_spinal_cut_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Debilitate) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you land a melee hit from Stealth with a Hard Success or better, the target loses their Reaction for the rest of the round.
career_shadowblade_strong_arm_form_rules: Type: Passive (Thrown) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Ignore 1 penalty die on thrown weapon attacks made at Medium range.
career_shadowblade_throw_move_strike_rul: Type: Special immediate (Chain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Make a thrown weapon attack, then immediately move 1 range band toward the target as a Free Action. If you end this movement Engaged, you may draw a weapon freely.
career_shadowblade_unerring_step_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Stance) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — For the next 2 rounds, you ignore penalty dice to movement, Athletics, and Stealth, and you may Disengage once per round as a Free Action. This does not allow additional attacks or reactions.
career_shadowblade_vanish_step_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Hide) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you Disengage following a melee attack, immediately attempt Stealth. On a success, you enter Stealth despite being in combat, provided you end your movement behind Cover, in darkness, or in heavy concealment.
career_sigilbound_aegis_absolute_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Total Intercept) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An ally within Short is targeted by a Targeted Arcane effect. Effect: Negate the effect entirely and anchor the energy harmlessly; no reflection occurs. This cannot be used against area effects or ongoing zones.
career_sigilbound_arcane_lock_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Ward) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Designate a point within Short (doorway, corridor, or chokepoint) as warded. Until the ward ends, enemies making ranged attacks through it suffer 1 penalty die until the start of your next turn.
career_sigilbound_arcane_reversal_rules-: Type: Free Action (Restore) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you successfully resolve a Warding spell that blocks, suppresses, or shields an effect, you may immediately restore up to 1d4 Strain to yourself or one ally within Short, to a maximum equal to the triggering spell’s Strain cost.
career_sigilbound_barrier_projection_rul: Type: Action (Barrier) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Conjure a barrier within Short that provides Partial Cover. Enemies attacking a target using this barrier for Cover suffer 1 penalty die. The barrier lasts for the scene unless destroyed by sufficient damage or dispelled.
career_sigilbound_counterglyph_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Disrupt) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A nearby enemy within Short casts a spell. Effect: That caster suffers 1 penalty die on the spellcasting roll. If the spell fails, the caster also suffers 1d4 Strain.
career_sigilbound_glyph_warder_rules-u: Type: Passive (Maintain) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You may reposition or reassign one ward you control once per round as a Free Action, provided the ward’s normal placement rules are obeyed. This does not extend duration, reduce Strain cost, or allow more than one warded effect to be sustained at a time. Only one Null Field may be active at a time.
career_sigilbound_glyphstep_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Shift) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are targeted by a spell or attack. Effect: Move 1 range band as a Free Action, ending in a space you could reasonably reach by movement (such as open ground, behind cover, or through an unobstructed path).
career_sigilbound_line_unbroken_rules-u: Type: Action (Anchor Zone) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Create a fortified warded zone within Short for 2 rounds. Allies inside gain +1 bonus die on CON and POW to resist Arcane effects and cannot be forcibly moved. Only one Line Unbroken may be active at a time.
career_sigilbound_mirror_sigil_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Redirect) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are targeted by a Targeted harmful spell or Targeted ranged attack. Effect: The attacker suffers 1 penalty die. If the attack misses, you may redirect it to any enemy within Short range — including the original attacker. Make a Warding roll — on a success, the effect is redirected to that target using its original damage and properties. On a failure, the attack dissipates harmlessly. The redirected effect cannot be redirected again.
career_sigilbound_null_field_rules-u: Type: Action (Suppress) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Create a suppression zone within Short. For one round, spells cast inside or through the zone suffer 1 penalty die. This does not suppress existing magical effects or passive enchantments.
career_sigilbound_reflective_ward_rules-: Type: Reaction (Reflect) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are targeted by a Targeted spell. Effect: The caster suffers 1 penalty die on the spellcasting roll. If the spell fails, it is negated and reflects onto the caster as if they were the target, using the spell’s normal effects and resistances.
career_sigilbound_runesight_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Gain +1 bonus die on a Magic (Warding) check. The first Warding spell you cast this scene to block, suppress, or counter ignores 1 Strain of its cost. This benefit applies only once per scene and only to that first qualifying spell.
career_sigilbound_runesovereign_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Double Cast) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Cast two Warding spells in the same round, provided each has a casting time of 1 Action or 1 Maneuver. Both spells resolve normally and cannot benefit from cost reductions, reflections, or additional reactions this round. After resolving both spells, you cannot cast Warding spells until the start of your next turn.
career_sigilbound_sanctum_sigil_rules-u: Type: Action (Zone) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Create a warded zone centered on you with a Short-radius. Allies inside gain +1 bonus die on CON or POW rolls to resist Arcane effects. Only one Sanctum Sigil may be active at a time.
career_sigilbound_sigil_hardened_rules-u: Type: Passive (Fortitude) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die on CON rolls to resist knockdown, suppression, or forced movement.
career_sigilbound_sigil_of_anchoring_rul: Type: Maneuver (Ward) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Place a glyph on you or an ally within Short; until the start of your next turn, the target cannot be forcibly moved or teleported.
career_sigilbound_sigil_pulse_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Detonate) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When one of your wards ends naturally or is dispelled by an enemy, enemies within Short of that ward take 1d4 Arcane damage and suffer 1 penalty die on their next action. This effect may trigger only once per scene.
career_sigilbound_sigil_reservoir_rules-: Type: Free Action (Prepared Ward) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you would place an Arcane Lock or Null Field, you may place it as a Free Action instead of its normal Action or Maneuver. This does not allow you to maintain more than one warded effect at a time and does not alter the ward’s duration or usage limits.
career_sigilbound_sigilburst_protocol_ru: Type: Special immediate (Detonate Ward) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Detonate one active ward you control. Enemies in that ward’s area take 2d6 Arcane damage and suffer 1 penalty die on their next action. The ward ends immediately and cannot trigger additional detonations.
career_sigilbound_stabilization_glyph_ru: Type: Maneuver (Ward) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Ward an ally within Short. The next time they would drop below 1 HP this scene, they instead remain at 1 HP. Only one Stabilization Glyph may be active at a time, and a character cannot benefit from more than one per scene.
career_sigilbound_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_sigilbound_veil_seal_rules-u: Type: Action (Cleanse/Suppress) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Suppress a Veil anomaly or magical corruption within Short. You may also reduce an ally’s Veil Corruption by 1 level.
career_sigilbound_warded_advance_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Shield) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you and an ally both move in the same round and end closer to danger, you conjure a temporary glyph shield. The next time either of you is hit before the start of your next turn, reduce that damage by 2 after Armor.
career_sigilbound_warding_matrix_rules-u: Type: Action (Shield) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Shield up to two allies within Short. They gain +2 Armor against Arcane damage for the rest of the scene. This bonus does not stack with other magical Armor bonuses.
career_sigilbound_wardmasters_command_ru: Type: Action (Empower Allies) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to three allies within Medium. Until the end of the scene, they gain +2 Armor vs. Arcane damage and ignore Arcane terrain effects. This Armor bonus does not stack with other magical Armor bonuses. Ward Shatter (Reaction): Once per Scene, when a chosen ally would take Arcane damage, you may negate that instance of damage for that ally. If the effect is an area attack, only that ally is protected; other targets are affected normally. This reaction may be used only once per activation of Wardmaster’s Command.
career_slicer_aggressive_handshake_rules: Type: Free Action (Overreach) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you successfully establish access to a network, you may declare an aggressive handshake. If you do, the system immediately advances escalation by one additional step. Until the end of the current round, you reduce the time required for your first system interaction by 1 round (minimum 0). This does not prevent escalation or System Overrun.
career_slicer_black_trace_nullifier_rule: Type: Reaction (Clean Exit) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You voluntarily abort an intrusion. Effect: You sever the system’s active trace, audit linkage, and pursuit routines tied to this intrusion. The disconnection is treated as clean for narrative fallout purposes: the system may know it was accessed, but cannot reliably identify you, track your exit vector, or initiate immediate follow-up tied specifically to your intrusion. This does not prevent consequences already incurred during the intrusion, does not undo alerts generated, and has no effect if you are forcibly disconnected or reach Catastrophic Resolution.
career_slicer_code_pressure_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Boost) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Gain +1 bonus die on your Access Roll to establish entry into a network.
career_slicer_command_fork_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Fork) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you complete a Latent Intrusion interaction that affects a system element (such as opening a door, redirecting sensors, or suppressing an alarm), you may immediately apply the same interaction to one additional connected, non-Hardened subsystem as part of the same action. This does not reduce the time required for objectives, does not grant additional actions, and ends immediately if the intrusion ends.
career_slicer_dead_mans_switch_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Contingency) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are forcibly disconnected or suffer Catastrophic Resolution during an intrusion. Effect: Immediately trigger one prepared system-level effect affecting the system you were connected to (such as purging selected logs, hard-locking access points, wiping a specific dataset, or disabling a defined subsystem). This effect resolves even though the intrusion ends and represents pre-seeded logic rather than continued access. This does not prevent consequences to you.
career_slicer_deck_hardening_suite_rules: Type: Passive (Armor) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Your OmniDeck gains +1 Armor Rating (AR). Also 1/Session: when taking digital damage, reduce it by 2 before applying AR.
career_slicer_digital_ghost_state_rules-: Type: Free Action (Ghost Mode) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Activate while connected to a network. For the next two rounds, your interactions do not generate active alerts and cannot be upgraded beyond silent alerts. You may not initiate latent intrusions or issue disruptive commands during this state. Escalation and System Overrun proceed normally. When this effect ends, you must immediately abort the intrusion or suffer one automatic Overrun consequence.
career_slicer_emergency_partition_rules-: Type: Reaction (Fail-Safe) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would suffer OmniDeck Damage as a result of System Overrun. Effect: Negate that instance of OmniDeck Damage by one step. This does not prevent other System Overrun consequences, does not affect escalation, and has no effect on subsequent damage during the same intrusion.
career_slicer_expanded_node_logic_rules-: Type: Special (Parallel Execution) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you perform a live authorization interaction that affects a system element (such as opening access, redirecting sensors, suppressing alarms, or grounding defenses), you may apply that same interaction to up to two additional connected, non-Hardened system elements as part of the same action. Each affected element must be capable of receiving the same command. This does not reduce the time required for the interaction, does not grant additional actions, and all effects end normally when the intrusion ends.
career_slicer_failsafe_override_core_rul: Type: Reaction (Last Action) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would be forcibly disconnected or suffer Catastrophic Resolution. Effect: You remain connected long enough to take one final Action or Maneuver before the intrusion ends. This action resolves normally but cannot generate new Reactions or prevent the disconnection. Any consequences already triggered still apply.
career_slicer_false_readout_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Misdirection) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately after you establish access to a system, you cause one node or subsystem you interact with to misreport its current state to observers until the start of your next turn. This does not prevent escalation, does not conceal Exposure, and ends automatically if the intrusion ends.
career_slicer_fastjack_ready_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Utility) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully establish access to a system, you may immediately swap out one currently loaded Program for another Program you have available. This swap does not require additional time and may only be performed once per intrusion. This does not reduce escalation, does not affect System Overrun, and cannot be used after the intrusion ends. A slicer may not swap Programs more than once per round, regardless of source.
career_slicer_icebreaker_thread_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Boost) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Gain +1 bonus die when attacking or bypassing ICE. If that ICE is disabled or bypassed completely, regain 1 Strain. This bonus represents momentary exploitation and does not stack with other effects that specifically target ICE behavior.
career_slicer_iceburn_protocol_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Burn) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — While you are in System Overrun, you may activate ICEburn Protocol. Until the end of your next turn, reduce the time required for all system interactions you perform by 1 round (minimum 0). This does not prevent escalation, does not reduce Overrun severity, and does not stop consequences from occurring.
career_slicer_kill_switch_routine_rules-: Type: Free Action (Execution) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you begin a latent intrusion during an active intrusion, reduce the time required to complete that latent intrusion by 1 round (minimum 1). This does not prevent mandatory System Overrun, does not reduce escalation, and does not affect other latent intrusions during the same scene.
career_slicer_null_pulse_routine_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Disruption) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Activate when you successfully establish access to a network or at the start of your turn during an intrusion. Until the end of the current round, escalation does not advance due to time. This does not alter the current escalation level, does not reduce escalation rate, and has no effect once System Overrun begins.
career_slicer_omnideck_recompiler_rules-: Type: Free Action (Swap) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — During an active intrusion, you may immediately replace one loaded Program with another Program you have available. This does not reduce escalation, does not grant additional actions, and cannot be used after the intrusion ends. A slicer may not swap Programs more than once per round, regardless of source.
career_slicer_predictive_override_rules-: Type: Passive (Scan Insight) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you perform a Deep Scan on an asset, you gain a brief, partial glimpse of its nature or significance in addition to its classification. This may include hints about its contents, purpose, narrative importance, or the kind of consequences tied to extracting it. This insight never reveals full details, exact outcomes, or hidden traps, and does not prevent escalation or System Overrun.
career_slicer_pulse_cloak_shell_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Cloak) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An interaction you perform would generate an active alert. Effect: Downgrade that alert to a silent alert instead. The alert still exists and may resolve later, but it does not trigger immediate alarms, guards, or external response. This does not prevent escalation, does not suppress System Overrun consequences, and cannot be used once Catastrophic Resolution occurs.
career_slicer_root_authority_rewrite_rul: Type: Action (Control Rewrite) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While actively connected to a network, you seize temporary root authority over one system domain. For the remainder of the scene or until you disconnect, you may issue one system-level command per round without increasing its time cost (such as sealing access, grounding drones, suppressing alarms, or rewriting logs). This authority does not prevent escalation or System Overrun and ends immediately if Catastrophic Resolution occurs. Hardened systems may resist individual commands at the GM’s discretion.
career_slicer_signal_ghost_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Masking) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You would gain a Trace Lock. Effect: Immediately roll Masking; on success, negate the Trace Lock.
career_slicer_silent_loop_rules-u: Type: Action (Masking Loop) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Designate one asset or subsystem you interact with during an intrusion. Until the end of the scene, interactions with that asset do not generate additional alerts, flags, or external responses beyond those caused by escalation and System Overrun. This does not prevent escalation, does not reduce extraction time, and does not suppress System Overrun consequences.
career_slicer_system_sniffer_rules-u: Type: Passive (Scan) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you establish access to a system, your baseline Network Scan reveals one additional detail about each identified asset category (such as relative sensitivity, compartmentalization, or whether extraction is likely to accelerate escalation). This does not reveal contents or hidden consequences.
career_slicer_total_signal_collapse_rule: Type: Action (Network Shutdown) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While actively connected to a network, you forcibly collapse its active response state. Until the end of the current round, escalation does not advance, all automated defenses and counter-intrusion logic are suppressed, and no new alerts are generated. Existing alerts and consequences are not undone. At the end of the round, the intrusion ends immediately and the system enters full alert status. This does not prevent narrative fallout.
career_slicer_trace_evasion_protocol_rul: Type: Reaction (Cleanse) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A Trace Lock is applied to you. Effect: Immediately attempt to remove it with a Masking roll as a Free Action (doesn’t consume your turn’s Action).
career_soul_forger_battlefield_reclamati: Type: Action • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target a wrecked, powered-down, or abandoned small chassis within Short range (drone, bot, or turret shell). Bind a Bound Spirit into the shell, reanimating it under your command for the Scene. Statline: HP 6; DR 1 (physical); Attack 45% for 1d6 (melee or light mounted weapon if present). The shell counts as spirit-infused. When the Scene ends or the shell reaches 0 HP, it becomes inert scrap. The reclaimed shell acts on your Initiative and may take either one Action or one Maneuver each Round, not both.
career_soul_forger_bound_warchassis_rule: Type: Downtime Creation; Passive in Play • Build Cost: 10,000 credits (base frame) — In Downtime, permanently bind a Bound Spirit into a combat chassis built from a suitable salvaged shell. It acts on your Initiative with one Maneuver and one Action per Round. Baseline Statline: HP 10; DR 2 (physical); Evasion 40%; Attack 55%. Choose one role at binding: Skirmisher (melee 1d6+1), Gunner (weapon supplied separately), or Bulwark (allies Engaged gain +1 cover step; melee 1d6). The chassis is always spirit-infused and ignores EMP/Blackout (it instead becomes Impaired until the start of its next turn). You may apply one Tier 2 or Tier 3 Soul Forger buff to it at a time. If reduced to 0 HP, it becomes inert. Emergency Wake (1/Session): At Engaged range, take an Action, pay 1d6 Strain, and roll Arcanotech or Mechanics; on success it rises to 1 HP (Hard+ restores 1d6+1 HP). Full repair requires Downtime. Only one War-Chassis may be active at a time. Each Round, the War-Chassis may take either one Action or one Maneuver. If you forgo your Maneuver on your turn, the War-Chassis may take both one Action and one Maneuver that Round.
career_soul_forger_cathedral_of_scrap_ru: Type: Action • Cost: 1d8+2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — If sufficient debris exists within Short range, fuse multiple Bound Spirits into a single Large colossus at Engaged range for the Scene. Statline: HP 16; DR 3 (physical); Dodge 35%; Smash 65% (1d8+2); Hurl Debris 55% (1d6, Medium range). Traits: Bulwark Mass (allies Engaged gain Partial Cover; ranged attacks against them suffer 1 penalty die), Impact (on a Hard+ Smash, either knock the target back one range band or impose Impaired until the start of its next turn). The colossus acts with one Maneuver and one Action per Round and collapses into inert scrap when the Scene ends or at 0 HP.
career_soul_forger_chassis_ride_rules-u: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: At-will — Bind a Bound Spirit into a drone or turret within Medium range. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by Defense Rating or operator skill. While riding, choose one benefit for the Scene: +10% to attacks or +10% to evasion/control. When the ride ends, the unit becomes Impaired until the start of its next turn. Cannot target systems housing Greater Spirits. In combat scenes, this effect lasts until the end of your next turn unless extended by spending 1 Strain at the start of your turn (maximum 3 total turns).
career_soul_forger_circuit_apotheosis_ru: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d6+2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You project your spirit into the ArcNet for the Scene; your body falls prone but remains conscious. Choose an access point within Medium range as your entry node. While apotheosed, treat that node as Engaged for ArcNet actions. With an OmniDeck: gain +10% to all Slicing rolls; when targeted in the ArcNet, you may defend using Slicing instead of the deck’s Defense Rating. Without an OmniDeck: you may perform ArcNet support actions only (scan, assist, stabilize, open/close nodes); you cannot make ArcNet attack actions. Any ArcNet feedback damage you suffer is taken as Strain instead of HP. If this drops you to 0 Strain, the effect ends immediately and you are Impaired on your next turn. If your body takes damage, the effect ends immediately. You may end this effect as a Free Action at the start of your turn. Bound Spirits within the ArcNet may be leveraged; Greater Spirits and Avatars remain beyond your control. This talent does not reduce interaction time, escalation, or System Overrun effects.
career_soul_forger_city_weave_circuit_ru: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d6+1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You must be Engaged with an access point (console, junction box, relay) tied to a single site-scale security domain within Medium range. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the site’s Defense Rating. On success, you seize control of the domain for the Scene: site sensors ignore your crew; hostile remote actions suffer 1 penalty die; doors, gates, and elevators respond to your commands; site turrets and drones treat your crew as friendly. Protocol Pulse (Free, 1/Round): Choose one — Lock Cycle (force open or closed one door/gate), Blind Eye (one sensor cluster ignores all targets or only sees hostiles for 1 Round), or Sentry Jinx (one site turret/drone takes –20% until the start of your next turn). You must remain within the site; starting a new Concentration ends this effect. Bound Spirits within the site obey the weave; Greater Spirits are unaffected. Hardened subsystems ignore individual Protocol Pulse effects. Each Protocol Pulse costs 1 Strain.
career_soul_forger_counteremp_veil_rules: Type: Reaction (Interrupt) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before a disabling effect roll resolves. When a machine or spirit-frame within Medium range would be disabled by EMP or Blackout, it ignores the shutdown and instead becomes Impaired until the end of its next turn.
career_soul_forger_hand_of_the_forge_rul: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a spirit-infused ally within Short range takes damage, reduce that damage by 1d6. This reduction applies only to the triggering hit.
career_soul_forger_helmgeist_protocol_ru: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You must be in physical contact with or inside a vehicle or facility subsystem. Bind a Bound Spirit into the host system for the Scene. Choose one duty on activation: Assist — the living operator’s Piloting, Driving, or Gunnery rolls gain +10% each Round; or Autopilot/Gunnery — if the operator is absent, incapacitated, or cedes control, the spirit takes one vehicle action per Round at 40% (Piloting/Driving 40% or mounted Gunnery 40%). Changing duty costs a Maneuver. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the system’s Defense Rating or an opposing operator’s skill if contested. This talent cannot target systems already housing a Greater Spirit or Avatar.
career_soul_forger_infused_shielding_rul: Type: Reaction (Guard) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare when a spirit-infused ally within Short range is hit by an attack. Reduce the triggering damage by 1d6 + 1 (before DR). This reduction applies only to that single hit and does not persist beyond the triggering attack.
career_soul_forger_lockout_sigil_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: At-will — Ward a machine or automated system within Short range. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the system’s Defense Rating or an opposing operator’s skill if contested. On a success, until the end of the target’s next turn, the device loses Reactions. This talent does not reduce attack values, impose penalties, or affect manually operated actions.
career_soul_forger_nullwave_bloom_rules-: Type: Action • Cost: 1d8+2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Release a Veil-charged disruption pulse out to Medium range. Unattended devices, drones, and vehicles animated by Bound Spirits are Stunned until the start of your next turn (they lose their Action and Maneuver, but may still take Reactions if applicable), then become Impaired for 1 Round. Attended machines oppose your Technomancy or Summoning with the higher of the operator’s relevant skill or the device’s Defense Rating; on your success, the host machine is Stunned until the start of your next turn, then Impaired for 1 Round; on a success by the defender, it becomes Impaired for 1 Round only. Individuals in range test POW; on a failure, their comms, Smartlink, optics, and HUDs are disabled until the start of their next turn (no Condition applied). Cyberware, life-support systems, and non-smart firearms are unaffected. Greater Spirits and Avatars are unaffected.
career_soul_forger_overclock_lattice_rul: Type: Maneuver • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target a spirit-infused machine within Short range. Until the end of the Scene, grant either +10% to its attacks or +10% to its dodge/defense (choose once when activated). If the machine fumbles a roll while overclocked, it becomes Impaired until the start of your next turn.
career_soul_forger_seat_the_spirit_rules: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: At-will — Invite a Lesser Spirit to ride an unattended simple system within Short range (door, camera, unattended turret, civilian drone, parked vehicle subsystem). Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the system’s Defense Rating or an opposing operator’s skill if contested. On a success, you direct the system for the Scene using basic functions only. Cannot target systems already housing a Bound or Greater Spirit. Starting a new Concentration effect immediately ends this ride.
career_soul_forger_soulstitch_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene per target — At Engaged or Short range, repair 2d6 HP to a machine or spirit-frame. If the target is at 0 HP, it rises to 1 HP and clears Incapacitated. A given target may benefit from Soulstitch only once per Scene.
career_soul_forger_spirit_lattice_field_: Type: Action; Concentration • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project a Short-radius aura centered on you for the Scene. Once per Round, when a spirit-infused ally within the aura is attacked, you may impose 1 penalty die on that attack. This effect applies once per Round per affected unit, regardless of spirit tier.
career_soul_forger_spirit_patch_rules-u: Type: Action • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: At-will — At Engaged or Short range, repair 1d6 HP to a machine, drone, turret, or spirit-frame and clear one of the following conditions from the target: Jammed or Overheated. If the target is currently spirit-infused by you, restore +1 additional HP. This talent cannot target living tissue.
career_soul_forger_spirit_static_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Interrupt) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare when a machine, drone, or automated system within Short range targets you or an ally with an attack. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the system’s Defense Rating or an opposing operator’s skill if contested. On a success, impose 1 penalty die on the triggering attack. On a Hard Success, the target is Impaired until the start of your next turn.
career_soul_forger_spitework_rewrite_rul: Type: Action • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target a hostile drone or turret within Short range. Roll Technomancy or Summoning opposed by the unit’s Defense Rating or an opposing operator’s skill if contested. On a success, the effect lasts until the end of the target’s next turn. On a Hard Success, the target also loses its Reaction on its next turn.
career_soul_forger_workshop_wisp_rules-u: Type: Active (Downtime Creation; Utility Companion) • Cost: 500 credits (ground) or 1,000 credits (flying) + 5 XP • Usage: Persistent (one active at a time) — Create a Tiny non-combat Lesser Spirit bound into a simple machine shell (1 HP). Acts on your Initiative. Once per Scene, the Wisp may Assist one Arcanotech, Mechanics, or Electronics test for +1 bonus die. EMP or Blackout do not disable it; instead, it becomes Impaired until the start of your next turn.
career_soulmender_corruption_sense_rules: Type: Passive (Detection) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You are automatically aware of the presence of Veil Corruption in any target you touch or successfully treat with a Restoration spell, even if the Corruption has not yet manifested visibly. The GM confirms presence and general severity; specific details require further assessment.
career_soulmender_corruption_wall_rules-: Type: Action (Field Suppression) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Project a Short-radius suppression field centered on you for the remainder of the Scene. While active, Veil Corruption cannot be inflicted on allies within the field by any source — hostile spells, passive exposure, or ambient escalation. Corruption already present is not removed. This field does not affect the Soulmender. The field ends if you are incapacitated or move beyond Short range of an affected ally.
career_soulmender_draw_back_from_the_edg: Type: Reaction (Last Intervention) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An ally within Medium range would die — either by failing a final CON check while Dying, or by taking damage that would put them beyond recovery. Effect: Immediately attempt Magic (Restoration). On a success, the ally is stabilized at 1 HP and all death checks cease. They remain unconscious but stable. On a failure, the intervention has no effect but still costs Strain. This talent may be used even if the ally is beyond normal stabilization range, provided line of sight is maintained.
career_soulmender_draw_out_the_taint_rul: Type: Action (Purge) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt Magic (Restoration) opposed by the Corruption's severity rating (set by the GM). On a success, reduce the target's Veil Corruption by one step. On a Hard Success or better, also prevent the Corruption from worsening until the end of the Scene. This does not restore HP and has no effect on targets with no active Corruption.
career_soulmender_emergency_rite_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Crisis Cast) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Short range fails a CON roll to remain conscious or resist a Major Wound effect. Effect: Immediately cast one Restoration spell you know with a casting time of 1 Action or less targeting that ally as a Reaction. The spell resolves normally and cannot benefit from other cost-reduction talents this round.
career_soulmender_full_purge_rules-u: Type: Action (Complete Cleanse) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Magic (Restoration) on an ally within Engaged range. On a success, remove all active Veil Corruption from the target regardless of severity, provided the Corruption was not caused by a permanent or fundamental source. On a failure, reduce Corruption by one step instead. The GM determines whether a given Corruption qualifies as purgeable by this effect.
career_soulmender_mass_restoration_rules: Type: Action (Split Heal) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Cast one Restoration spell that restores HP and divide the total HP restored among up to two allies within Short range. Each recipient must receive at least 1 HP. Healing Saturation and daily healing limits apply to each recipient individually.
career_soulmender_mending_attunement_rul: Type: Free Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before casting a Restoration spell that restores HP, gain +1 bonus die on the spellcasting roll. This bonus applies to the next qualifying spell cast this Scene and expires if unused by Scene end.
career_soulmender_mending_overflow_rules: Type: Free Action (Augment) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare after a Restoration spell you cast successfully restores HP to a target. That target also clears the Bleeding condition if currently active. This does not affect Healing Saturation or daily healing limits.
career_soulmender_purification_absolute_: Type: Action (Total Cleanse) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose up to three allies within Short range. Each has all active Veil Corruption removed, regardless of severity, provided the source is not permanent or fundamentally irreversible. Additionally, until the end of the Scene, those allies cannot have Corruption inflicted on them by passive escalation or ambient Veil saturation. Direct hostile effects still apply normally. The GM determines whether any individual Corruption qualifies as permanent.
career_soulmender_purifying_presence_rul: Type: Passive (Aura) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you remain within Short range of an ally carrying active Veil Corruption, that Corruption cannot worsen due to passive escalation or ambient Veil saturation. Corruption caused by direct hostile effects — spells, attacks, or abilities — still applies normally. This effect requires no action and ends immediately if you leave Short range or are incapacitated.
career_soulmender_read_the_wound_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assess) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Observe a target within Short range and make a Perception roll. On a success, identify any hidden or subtle afflictions the target is carrying that have not yet manifested visibly — such as early-stage Veil Corruption, latent toxins, internal trauma, or psychological fracture beginning to surface. Also confirm whether the target is currently carrying a Major Wound and their approximate HP relative to maximum. This talent reveals what observation and arcane sensitivity can detect; it does not diagnose causes or predict outcomes.
career_soulmender_resonant_channel_rules: Type: Free Action (Economy) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you cast a Restoration spell that restores HP, reduce the Strain cost of that spell by 1 (minimum 1). This reduction applies to one spell per Scene and does not stack with other cost-reduction effects.
career_soulmender_restoration_surge_rule: Type: Free Action (Augment) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare before rolling a Restoration spell that restores HP. If the spell succeeds, double the HP restored before applying Healing Saturation or daily healing limits.
career_soulmender_restoration_without_en: Type: Free Action (Doctrine) • Cost: 1d6 + 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare at the start of your first turn in a hostile scene. Until the end of the Scene, the first successful Restoration spell you cast each round does not increase Healing Saturation for that instance and ignores that round's instant-healing cap. All other healing limits apply normally. In addition, Resonant Channel and Mending Overflow do not consume their Scene-usage limits while this doctrine is active.
career_soulmender_soul_catch_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Intercept) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: An ally within Short range would be reduced to 0 HP. Effect: Immediately attempt First Aid or Magic (Restoration) against the triggering damage total as a contested threshold. On a success, the ally is reduced to 1 HP instead of 0 and does not trigger death checks or Major Wound consequences from this hit. On a failure, the hit resolves normally.
career_soulmender_soul_sovereign_rules-u: Type: Action (Life Anchor) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one ally within Short range. The next time that ally would be reduced to 0 HP, they are instead reduced to 1 HP. This effect applies once and then ends, whether triggered or not. Only one Soul Sovereign effect may be active at a time.
career_soulmender_soul_tether_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Anchor) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Establish an arcane tether between yourself and one ally within Short range. The next Restoration spell you cast targeting that ally this Scene automatically succeeds without a roll, though Healing Saturation and daily healing limits apply normally. The tether ends once that spell is cast, when you are incapacitated, or at Scene end. Only one Soul Tether may be active at a time.
career_soulmender_stabilizing_touch_rule: Type: Action (Stabilize) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Attempt First Aid or Magic (Restoration) on a Dying ally within Engaged range. On a success, the target is stabilized and death checks cease. This does not restore HP or remove conditions.
career_soulmender_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_soulmender_the_body_remembers_rul: Type: Reaction (Restoration Echo) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A Restoration spell you cast this round successfully restores HP to an ally. Effect: That ally also gains +1 bonus die on their next CON roll made before the end of their next turn, as the restoration reinforces their immediate physical resilience.
career_soulmender_the_mending_holds_rule: Type: Action (Critical Restoration) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Attempt Magic (Restoration) on an ally within Engaged range. On a success, restore HP equal to the maximum possible result of your Restoration roll. You may also remove one Major Wound condition, one active toxin, or all current Veil Corruption. Healing Saturation applies. The target cannot benefit from further HP restoration from any source until the start of your next turn.
career_soulmender_triage_read_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Designate) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — At the start of your turn, designate one ally within Medium range as your priority patient. The next Restoration spell or First Aid check you make targeting that ally this Scene gains +1 bonus die. If that ally is reduced to 0 HP before you treat them, you may move one range band toward them as a Free Action without provoking Reactions. Only one ally may be designated at a time.
career_soulmender_veil_scar_mapping_rule: Type: Action (Deep Assess) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After one full round of contact with or direct observation of a target, roll Spirit Lore or Magic (Restoration). On a success, determine the source category of active Veil Corruption (ambient, hostile spellwork, possession residue, or chronic exposure), its current severity, and whether standard purging will be sufficient or whether additional measures are required. The GM answers honestly within the limits of observable evidence.
career_soulmender_wardens_grace_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Triage Advance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you move toward an injured ally as part of a Maneuver, that movement does not provoke Overwatch or Reactions. Until the start of your next turn, Restoration spells you cast targeting that ally ignore one penalty die imposed by cover, range, or environmental interference.
career_spirit_warden_avatars_mandate_rul: Type: Action (Petition) • Cost: 1d4 + 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Petition the local Avatar Spirit. If it answers, it enacts one significant intervention this Scene (environmental suppression, spiritual protection, enforced boundaries, or authoritative command). The nature and tone of the response reflect your standing and prior conduct in the domain.
career_spirit_warden_basic_ritual_circle: Type: Maneuver (Ritual) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After one full Round of uninterrupted work, create a simple ritual circle. If you are forced to move, incapacitated, or take damage during this time, the ritual is disrupted. While you remain inside the circle, gain +1 bonus die on banish or command actions against Lesser or Bound Spirits. The circle lasts for the Scene or until disrupted by movement or interference.
career_spirit_warden_bound_spirit_partne: Type: Action (Summon) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Summon your personal Bound Spirit within Short. This summon does not require Concentration and is a unique exception to normal sustain rules. The spirit acts on your initiative and remains for the Scene. If dispersed, it cannot be resummoned this Scene. You may not have more than one Bound Spirit Partner active at a time, and this talent does not allow additional sustained effects or establish a precedent for other summons.
career_spirit_warden_circle_flow_discipl: Type: Free Action (Escort) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you and one ally both move in the same round and end closer to a spiritual threat, reduce the damage or effect of the next spirit-origin attack against either of you this round by 1d6.
career_spirit_warden_circle_marked_recal: Type: Reaction (Recall) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A spirit you summoned is disrupted or forced to withdraw. Effect: If it is within Medium and you have line of effect, recall it to Engaged range. This movement does not provoke reactions and cannot place the spirit into an occupied or hazardous space.
career_spirit_warden_confront_the_echo_r: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — When you successfully banish a spirit, it suffers 1d4 Resonant backlash. This does not apply to Avatar Spirits.
career_spirit_warden_custodians_stand_ru: Type: Reaction (Anchor) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are targeted by forced movement, possession, or a spirit-origin control effect. Effect: Ignore the forced movement or negate the control effect, and you cannot be displaced until the start of your next turn.
career_spirit_warden_domain_resonance_ci: Type: Maneuver (Ritual) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After two uninterrupted Rounds of work, create a domain-attuned circle (such as street, building, transit hub, industrial zone). While you remain inside it, gain +1 bonus die on command or banish actions against spirits tied to that domain and +1 bonus die on cleanse attempts affecting corruption within the domain.
career_spirit_warden_eidolon_apotheosis_: Type: Action (Ascension) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Elevate your Bound Spirit Partner into its ascended form for the Scene. It gains +4 Integrity, +1 damage die step, DR 1, acts independently, and automatically reforms once if dispersed. It also gains one unique thematic ability chosen when you learn this talent.
career_spirit_warden_grand_exorcism_rite: Type: Action (Final Banishment) • Cost: 2d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Perform a full exorcism against a spirit within Short. Lesser Spirits are destroyed, Bound Spirits cannot return for 24 hours, and Greater Spirits are expelled from the area for 24 hours. If no Greater Spirit is present, choose one additional effect affecting the immediate scene space (GM discretion): suppress all possession attempts for the Scene, reduce a corruption pocket by one tier, or impose 1 penalty die on all hostile spirit-origin actions until scene end. This action does not provoke Reactions. Failure still costs Strain but causes no backlash.
career_spirit_warden_lesser_companion_bo: Type: Action • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Summon a personalized Lesser Companion Spirit. Choose one skill (Arcana, Spirit Lore, or Insight); gain +1 bonus die on one roll using that skill this Scene. The companion disperses if harmed or when the Scene ends.
career_spirit_warden_lesser_spirit_conju: Type: Action (Summon) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: At-will — Summon a Lesser Spirit you know. It acts on your initiative and lasts for the Scene unless destroyed. If you summon another Lesser Spirit while one is active, the previous spirit disperses immediately.
career_spirit_warden_line_held_against_t: Type: Reaction (Absolute Stand) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A spirit-origin effect would cause any of the following: more than one possession in a round, the creation of a new Greater Spirit, escalation of a corruption pocket by two or more steps, or the loss of control of a spiritual site. Effect: Until the start of your next turn, no new possession attempts can occur, no spirits may enter or leave the area, corruption cannot increase, and all active spirit-origin effects suffer 1 penalty die. This does not end effects, only halts escalation.
career_spirit_warden_purifications_edge_: Type: Action (Focus) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Target a corrupted spirit or corruption pocket. Your next cleanse, banish, or purify attempt this Scene gains +1 bonus die. On a success, reduce the corruption level by one step.
career_spirit_warden_rite_efficiency_rul: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Reduce the Strain cost of one summoning or banishment you perform this Scene by 1.
career_spirit_warden_rite_of_echo_severa: Type: Action (Sever) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Sever a spirit’s tether to a host, object, or location. Gain +1 bonus die on the banish roll. On a success, the spirit cannot reattach for the Scene. Against corrupted targets, reduce corruption by one tier.
career_spirit_warden_sanctify_the_ground: Type: Action (Site Consecration) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Cleanse and stabilize a location within Short for the remainder of the Scene. All corruption pockets are suppressed, possession attempts automatically fail, and spirits hostile to the site’s nature suffer 1 penalty die on all actions. This effect lingers narratively after the scene ends.
career_spirit_warden_silencing_hand_rule: Type: Action (Disrupt) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Your next banish attempt against a Greater Spirit this Scene causes its resistance to lose one bonus die or suffer one penalty die (GM chooses). This does not apply to Avatar Spirits.
career_spirit_warden_sovereign_pattern_r: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Reduce the Strain cost of banish attempts against Greater Spirits by one die size. Once per Session, reduce the Strain cost of a Greater Spirit petition by 1.
career_spirit_warden_spirit_economy_rule: Type: Free Action (Conversion) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Convert only the Strain rolled from this ability into a reduction on the next Summoning Strain cost you would pay this Scene, to a maximum reduction equal to the Strain rolled. If used while inside a circle you created, reduce the resulting Summoning Strain by an additional 1.
career_spirit_warden_spirit_sense_attune: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die to detect, identify, or interpret spiritual residue, echoes, or minor disturbances. When you Push a Perception or Arcana roll against spiritual phenomena, gain +1 bonus die on the pushed roll.
career_spirit_warden_wardbreaker_gesture: Type: Action (Setup) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Before making a banish attempt against a Lesser or Bound Spirit, perform the gesture. Your next banish this Scene gains +1 bonus die.
career_spirit_warden_wardens_barrier_sig: Type: Reaction (Interdict) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Short is targeted by a possession or spirit-origin attack. Effect: Negate one possession attempt, or reduce the damage or effect by 1d6. Lesser Spirits are repelled automatically. This reduction does not stack with other Spirit Warden reductions.
career_spirit_warden_wardens_binding_cir: Type: Maneuver (Ritual) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After one full Round of uninterrupted work, create a reinforced ritual circle. While you remain inside it, gain +1 bonus die on banish actions against Bound Spirits, and Greater Spirits inside suffer 1 penalty die on actions directly opposing you. Once this Scene, while inside this circle, reduce the Strain cost of one Summoning action you perform by 1.
career_spirit_warden_wardens_wards_rules: Type: Reaction (Interdict) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You or an ally within Short is targeted by a possession or spirit-origin effect. Effect: Reduce the effect by 1d6, or negate a single possession attempt from a Lesser Spirit. This reduction does not stack with other reductions from Spirit Warden talents.
career_stormweaver_arc_feedback_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Disrupt) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are hit by a melee attack. Effect: Roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the attacker’s CON. On a success, the attacker suffers 1 penalty die on their next action and cannot make Reactions until the end of the round.
career_stormweaver_arc_flare_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Dazzle) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: After you successfully resolve an Elemental spell against a target in Engaged. Effect: Roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the target’s CON. On a success, the target suffers 1 penalty die on their next action taken before the end of their following turn.
career_stormweaver_burn_current_rules-u: Type: Action (Elemental Shock) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You release a violent surge of elemental force that you can no longer fully contain. Each enemy currently in Engaged range must oppose your Magic (Elemental) roll with a CON roll. Any target you defeat takes 1d6 Arcane damage and suffers 1 penalty die on their next action before the end of their following turn. Targets that succeed suffer no damage and no penalty. This effect represents uncontrolled elemental discharge rather than a spell and cannot benefit from spell modifiers, bonus dice, cost reductions, or talents that modify spellcasting.
career_stormweaver_burnline_pressure_rul: Type: Reaction (Zone Pressure) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An enemy enters or leaves Engaged with you. Effect: Roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the target’s CON. On a success, the target suffers 1 penalty die on its next action.
career_stormweaver_dig_in_the_storm_rule: Type: Reaction (Stance) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You would be subjected to forced movement, a shove, or a knockdown effect. Effect: You immediately enter a braced stance. Negate that instance of forced movement, and gain +1 bonus die on CON rolls to resist knockdown or shove effects until the end of your next turn. While this stance is active, you cannot benefit from talents that grant additional movement or teleportation.
career_stormweaver_eye_of_the_storm_rule: Type: Free Action (Veil Trance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Activate at the start of your turn. Until the end of your next turn, you ignore one penalty die imposed on your Elemental spellcasting rolls each round. Once during this duration, you may reroll one failed Elemental spellcasting roll. While this effect is active, you cannot benefit from talents that grant additional movement or teleportation. This talent is not a spell and is not affected by effects that modify or penalize spells unless explicitly stated.
career_stormweaver_flashfire_assault_rul: Type: Special Immediate (Follow-Up) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Immediately after you successfully cast an Elemental spell, make one Melee or Thrown attack against a target in Engaged or Short as an incidental. This attack does not consume your Action, does not grant additional Reactions, and cannot benefit from bonus dice from talents. If this attack misses, you suffer 1 Strain.
career_stormweaver_flashstep_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Mobility) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately take one Move Maneuver. This movement ignores difficult terrain and does not provoke Reactions. You may not gain more than one additional movement effect from Stormweaver talents in the same round.
career_stormweaver_hold_the_current_rule: Type: Free Action (Cast Mod) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Gain +1 bonus die on a Magic (Elemental) roll as you forcibly stabilize volatile energy. If the spell succeeds, resolve its Strain cost normally. If the spell fails, increase the spell's Strain die by one step before rolling Strain.
career_stormweaver_killzone_instinct_rul: Type: Passive (Threat Sense) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you are at Engaged or Short range, you gain +10% to Dodge rolls against any enemy that has been successfully affected by one of your Elemental spells earlier this scene. An enemy is considered affected if they failed a resistance roll or otherwise suffered the spell’s primary effect. This benefit does not apply against enemies unaffected by the spell’s resolution.
career_stormweaver_scorchmark_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Brand) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Brand a living enemy within Short with arcane fire. Your next Elemental spell targeting that enemy gains +1 bonus die. The brand ends when that spell resolves, the target dies, or the scene ends. This bonus die does not stack with other talent-granted bonus dice.
career_stormweaver_shockstep_conduit_rul: Type: Action (Teleport) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Teleport to a visible point within Medium. Enemies in Engaged range at your starting and ending positions must each oppose your Magic (Elemental) roll with a CON roll. Any target you defeat takes 1d4 Arcane damage. Targets that succeed suffer no damage. You may not gain more than one additional movement effect from Stormweaver talents in the same round.
career_stormweaver_singularity_discharge: Type: Action (Terminal Overload) • Cost: 1d10 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one hostile creature within Short and roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the target’s CON. On a success, the target takes 4d6 Arcane damage. On a failure, the target takes 2d6 Arcane damage. Regardless of the outcome, you cannot cast Elemental spells again this scene.
career_stormweaver_stormbreak_rules-u: Type: Action (Structural Rupture) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose a visible terrain feature or structural element within Medium (such as a wall, floor, ceiling, pillar, barricade, or natural formation). You violently rupture it, creating a gaping breach or explosive collapse point. Each hostile creature within Short of that feature must oppose your Magic (Elemental) roll with CON. On a failure, each affected creature takes 2d6 Physical damage and is either knocked Prone or forcibly displaced one range band (your choice) as the structure collapses. This effect does not destroy armored vehicles or hardened military assets and represents catastrophic environmental destruction rather than a spell.
career_stormweaver_stormcallers_edge_rul: Type: Free Action (Overchannel) • Cost: 4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Before you cast an Elemental spell, declare overchanneling. That spell gains +1 bonus die. If the spell succeeds, you may affect one additional valid target within Short of the original target. This talent may not be used on spells that already affect multiple targets. The bonus die from this talent does not stack with other talent-granted bonus dice. This talent cannot be combined with Surge Engine, Veilcore Meltdown, or any effect that sets a spell’s Strain cost to 0.
career_stormweaver_stormpulse_drive_rule: Type: Free Action (Post-Cast) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately after casting an Elemental spell, take one Move Maneuver. This movement follows normal rules, may provoke Reactions, and does not allow you to exceed the normal limit of two Maneuvers per turn. You may not gain more than one additional movement effect from Stormweaver talents in the same round.
career_stormweaver_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase maximum Strain by +1. (This talent appears in multiple Career Trees.)
career_stormweaver_surge_engine_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Cost Waiver) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Cast your next Elemental spell this turn for 0 Strain. Eligibility: the spell’s base Strain die must be 1d6 or smaller before any modifiers. Anti-stacking: this spell cannot benefit from any other cost-reduction or bonus-die talents.
career_stormweaver_tinder_spark_rules-u: Type: Action (Utility/Fire) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Ignite a flammable object or surface within Short as residual elemental bleed-off escapes containment. If used against a creature in Engaged, roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the target's CON. On a success, the target suffers 1 penalty die on their next physical action due to heat and distraction.
career_stormweaver_total_elemental_colla: Type: Action (Overload Release) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You release all remaining elemental pressure in a violent, uncontrolled surge. Each hostile creature within Medium must oppose your Magic (Elemental) roll with CON. Any target you defeat takes 3d6 Arcane damage. Targets that succeed take no damage. After resolving this action, you cannot cast Elemental spells or activate talents that modify spells for the remainder of the scene. This effect represents catastrophic elemental failure rather than a spell and cannot benefit from spell modifiers, bonus dice, or cost reductions.
career_stormweaver_veil_backlash_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Disrupt) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: After you take damage from a melee attack. Effect: Roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the attacker's CON. On a success, the attacker suffers 1 penalty die on their next action as arcane feedback disrupts their movement and focus.
career_stormweaver_veilcore_meltdown_rul: Type: Action (Overchannel Catastrophe) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You deliberately overload your elemental channeling. Choose one Elemental spell you cast as part of this action. If that spell deals damage, double the number of damage dice rolled. This effect applies after all other modifiers are determined and cannot be combined with other effects that increase damage dice. After resolving the spell, you suffer 1 penalty die on all Elemental spellcasting rolls until the end of the scene. If the spell fails, it still consumes its normal Strain cost in addition to Veilcore Meltdown’s cost.
career_stormweaver_veilstorm_mantle_rule: Type: Free Action (Storm Cloak) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the end of your next turn, the first time a creature resolves a melee attack against you, roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the attacker’s CON. On a success, the attacker suffers 2 penalty dice on their next action as violent arcane feedback disrupts their momentum and focus. While this effect is active, you cannot benefit from talents that reduce Strain cost or Strain dice.
career_stormweaver_volcanic_path_rules-u: Type: Action (Hazard Terrain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Tear open a line of unstable, burning terrain up to Short in length, placed anywhere within Short of you. The hazard remains until the start of your next turn. The line is wide enough that crossing it requires deliberate movement. When a creature crosses the line, roll Magic (Elemental) opposed by the creature’s CON. On a success, the creature takes 1d6 Physical damage. A creature that chooses to avoid crossing the line must spend one additional Maneuver that turn to cover the same distance. This effect represents unstable elemental terrain rather than a spell and cannot benefit from spell modifiers or bonus-die talents.
career_stormweaver_voltaic_reflex_rules-: Type: Free Action • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are targeted by a ranged attack and declare a Dodge reaction. Effect: Gain +1 bonus die on your Dodge roll. This does not count as a reaction and does not trigger the cost of a second reaction. This bonus die does not stack with other talent-granted bonus dice.
career_street_ronin_blade_discipline_rul: Type: Passive (Substitute) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When taking the Dodge action while wielding a melee weapon, you may roll Melee Weapons instead of Dodge.
career_street_ronin_breath_before_the_cu: Type: Free Action (Stance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — If you win Initiative at the start of a combat scene, designate one target; until the end of Round 1, gain +1 bonus die on melee attacks against that target and reduce the Strain cost of a second Reaction to 2 strain. This does not allow more than two total Reactions in a round.
career_street_ronin_clean_entry_rules-u: Type: Action (Draw & Strike) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Draw and strike with a melee weapon as a single Action. If you begin the scene undetected or neutral, your first melee attack of that scene gains +1 bonus die.
career_street_ronin_combat_rhythm_rules-: Type: Reaction (Flow) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully Dodge an attack, gain +1 bonus die on your next Melee Weapons or Coordination roll before the end of your next turn. This bonus represents momentary control gained through evasion and applies even if no follow-up attack is made.
career_street_ronin_counterguard_precisi: Type: Reaction (Counter) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully Dodge a melee attack, make one free melee counterattack with +1 bonus die. This attack cannot itself trigger additional Street Ronin talents unless explicitly stated.
career_street_ronin_disciplined_focus_ru: Type: Free Action (Ignore Penalty) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before making an attack, defense, or Initiative roll; ignore 1 penalty die on that roll.
career_street_ronin_draw_cut_rules-u: Type: Attack modifier • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When striking a target that has not yet acted this scene, deal +1d6 bonus damage.
career_street_ronin_duelmasters_claim_ru: Type: Free Action (Mark Rival) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare one opponent as your focused rival. Until the end of the scene, gain +1 bonus die on melee attacks, Dodge, and Reaction-based talents against that target. If you reduce the rival to 0 HP, immediately make one melee attack against another target in Engaged range or gain +1 bonus die on all melee attacks for the remainder of the scene.
career_street_ronin_final_cut_doctrine_r: Type: Reaction (Counter — Finisher) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When making a melee counterattack, you may declare Final Cut before rolling. If your counterattack succeeds, it deals maximum weapon damage instead of rolling damage and you may apply one Tier 1–3 Street Ronin talent effect that normally triggers on a successful hit. If the counterattack fails, it resolves normally. This attack cannot itself trigger additional Street Ronin talents unless explicitly stated.
career_street_ronin_flow_reversal_rules-: Type: Reaction (Clash) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When your melee counterattack fails to exceed the attacker’s success, resolve a mutual exchange: both sides take damage once, using the original attack’s damage values; the attacker then suffers 1 penalty die on their next action.
career_street_ronin_ghost_step_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Reposition) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately move 1 range band as a Free Maneuver, even if you have already moved this round. If no enemy makes an attack roll targeting you before your next turn, your next melee attack gains +1 bonus die.
career_street_ronin_ghost_walk_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (Burst) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Move up to Medium range as a Free Action and make one melee attack. If the target is unaware or has not yet acted this scene, deal +1d6 bonus damage and force a POW test; on failure, the target is Stunned until the end of the target’s next turn.
career_street_ronin_hold_the_line_rules-: Type: Maneuver (Threat Line) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Designate a small area within Engaged range (such as a doorway or alley). Until the end of the current round, the first time each enemy crosses this line, they suffer 1 penalty die on their next movement or attack roll that round.
career_street_ronin_interception_cut_rul: Type: Reaction (Interrupt — Strike) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An enemy moves into Engaged with you. Effect: Make one free melee attack; on a hit, their movement is interrupted. This attack cannot itself trigger additional Street Ronin talents unless explicitly stated.
career_street_ronin_last_breath_rules-u: Type: Special immediate (End the Exchange) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare after an enemy you are Engaged with completes an attack against you. Immediately resolve one melee attack against that enemy; if this attack hits, both you and the target cannot take Actions for the remainder of the current round, and the target suffers 1 penalty die on all rolls until the end of their next turn. This attack cannot itself trigger additional Street Ronin talents unless explicitly stated.
career_street_ronin_measured_finality_ru: Type: Free Action (Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — At the start of your turn, designate one enemy you can see within Short range. Until the end of the scene, you cannot make attacks against other targets, but gain +1 bonus die on all melee attacks, Dodge, and Reaction-based talents against the designated enemy; if either of you is reduced to 0 HP, the effect immediately ends.
career_street_ronin_moment_severed_rules: Type: Special immediate (Stagger) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — After you land a melee hit, choose the target; until the end of their next turn, they cannot take Actions but may still take Reactions. This effect cannot be combined with other immediate follow-up attacks triggered by the same hit.
career_street_ronin_no_second_strike_rul: Type: Special immediate (Follow-up) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you reduce a target to 0 HP with a melee attack, immediately make one melee attack against another target in Engaged range; this additional attack cannot trigger other immediate attacks or talents. This attack cannot itself trigger additional Street Ronin talents unless explicitly stated.
career_street_ronin_posture_breaker_rule: Type: Special immediate (Debuff) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After you successfully hit a target with a melee attack, apply 1 penalty die to the target’s next attack or defense roll made this scene.
career_street_ronin_slip_the_gap_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Reposition) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are missed by a melee attack. Effect: Immediately move 1 range band as a Free Maneuver; this movement follows all normal engagement rules and may still trigger Reactions.
career_street_ronin_steel_calm_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Resolve) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Until the end of the current round, ignore the effects of the Frightened, Panicked, or Impaired conditions and gain +1 bonus die on Dodge rolls during this time.
career_street_ronin_steel_reflex_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Mitigate) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When targeted by a ranged attack made from Engaged range or while you are in Cover, declare after the attack roll but before damage; reduce the damage taken by 1d4.
