career_street_ronin_stillness_in_motion_: Type: Free Action (Mobility Stance) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Ignore movement penalties for the rest of the round; during this time, gain +1 bonus die on Athletics or Melee Weapons rolls that involve movement. (Does not grant free Disengage; standard reactions still apply.)
career_street_ronin_strain_buffer_rules-: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_street_ronin_urban_footwork_rules: Type: Free Action (Terrain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the end of the current round, gain +1 bonus die on Dodge or Athletics rolls made as part of your own movement through tight spaces, vertical terrain, or cluttered urban environments.
career_vanguard_battle_hardened_rules-u: Type: Passive (Condition Resistance) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You gain +1 bonus die on POW rolls made to resist the Suppressed condition.
career_vanguard_beaten_zone_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Establish) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Designate one visible enemy within Medium range. Until you move, are Suppressed, or that enemy is reduced to 0 HP, any other enemy who moves into Engaged range of that target or attempts to move through the space between you and them must roll Dodge opposed by your Heavy Weapons skill; on a failure, they take heavy weapon damage as a Reaction from you. This Reaction costs no Strain and does not count against your normal Reaction limit.
career_vanguard_controlled_withdrawal_ru: Type: Reaction (Tactical) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You or an ally within Short is forced to move or voluntarily disengages from an enemy. Effect: You immediately make one Heavy Weapons or Rifles attack against the triggering enemy as a Reaction before the movement resolves. This attack gains +1 bonus die. The movement then continues normally and may still trigger Attacks of Opportunity from other enemies.
career_vanguard_covering_fire_rules-u: Type: Action (Suppress) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Make a Full Auto heavy weapon or rifle attack targeting a zone rather than a specific target. Every enemy within Short range of the target point must test POW for Suppression, regardless of whether the attack hits any individual. Standard Suppression rules apply.
career_vanguard_dead_ground_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Establish) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Designate a zone within Medium range. For the rest of the scene, any enemy who enters or begins their turn in that zone is automatically Suppressed — no POW roll permitted. The zone persists until the end of the scene or until you are incapacitated. Enemies aware of the zone may choose not to enter it.
career_vanguard_dig_in_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Brace) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Enter a braced firing position. While braced, you gain +10% on POW rolls to resist Suppression and ignore movement penalties imposed by heavy weapons. Moving immediately ends this stance.
career_vanguard_fieldcraft_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Versatility) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare at the start of your turn. Until the end of the round, when firing a weapon associated with a Secondary Skill at Engaged range, reduce the penalty die for that weapon type by one — rifles and long arms fire with one penalty die rather than two, and handguns fire with no penalty die. Changing firing modes this round counts as a Free Action rather than a Maneuver. If you reload a Secondary Skill weapon this round, that reload counts as a Free Action rather than a Maneuver.
career_vanguard_fire_and_adapt_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Transition) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Immediately after resolving a heavy weapon attack, draw and fire a handgun as a Free Action. That first shot gains +1 bonus die. If you continue firing the handgun this turn using Semi-Auto, the first shot of that sequence retains the +1 bonus die. This attack cannot trigger additional Vanguard talents unless explicitly stated.
career_vanguard_grind_them_down_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Attrition) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Designate one visible enemy. Until the end of the scene, each round in which you hit that target with a heavy weapon or rifle they suffer a cumulative 1 penalty die on all attack rolls, to a maximum of 2 penalty dice. The penalty resets if you miss that target.
career_vanguard_grunts_eye_rules-u: Type: Passive (Assess) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — At the start of a combat scene or when entering a new area under hostile conditions, you may immediately identify the most defensible position, the best avenue of approach, and the most dangerous threat in the environment without a roll. The GM confirms these assessments or indicates if conditions are too obscured to read clearly.
career_vanguard_iron_gut_rules-u: Type: Passive (Endurance) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you reach 0 HP, you remain active until the end of your next turn before falling. During this window, you do not make CON rolls to remain conscious unless you take additional damage. At the end of your next turn, if you have not received First Aid, you enter the Dying state.
career_vanguard_keep_moving_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Rally) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: An ally within Short becomes Suppressed. Effect: That ally may immediately make a POW roll to shake the Suppressed condition; if they succeed, they may use their Reaction to take a Move Maneuver before their next turn. This movement follows all normal rules and may trigger Attacks of Opportunity.
career_vanguard_last_round_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Endure) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: You are below 25% of your maximum HP and would take damage that would reduce you further. Resolve the damage. Until the end of your next turn, the Bloodied condition, the Grievous condition, the Suppressed condition, and forced movement effects do not apply to you. Your first attack during this window deals +1d6 damage.
career_vanguard_old_dog_rules-u: Type: Passive (Mastery) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — You always roll Initiative with +1 bonus die. This bonus cannot be negated by conditions, wound penalties, or environmental effects unless you are Surprised, in which case standard Surprise rules apply.
career_vanguard_open_the_lane_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Coordinate) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You generate Suppression against one or more enemies this round. Effect: Choose one ally within Medium who has not yet acted. That ally may use their Reaction to take a Move Maneuver and gains +1 bonus die on their next attack or action this round. This movement follows all normal rules and may trigger Attacks of Opportunity.
career_vanguard_read_the_field_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Tactical) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare before any ally acts this round. Identify one enemy position, movement pattern, or tactical vulnerability; the first ally who acts on that information this round gains +1 bonus die on their action.
career_vanguard_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_vanguard_sustained_fire_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Heavy Weapons) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare when making a Full Auto heavy weapon attack from a braced position. That attack gains +1 bonus die and generates Suppression on both hits and misses within effective range, using standard Suppression rules. Suppression on a miss functions identically to Suppression on a hit for the purpose of the Suppressed condition.
career_vanguard_tactical_anchor_rules-u: Type: Action (Command) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Designate up to three allies within Medium. Until the end of the round, each designated ally may reroll one failed POW or CON roll and gains +1 bonus die on their next attack or action. Enemies who target a designated ally while you have LOS to the attacker must test POW; on a failure, they redirect their attack to you instead.
career_vanguard_unbroken_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Stand) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare at the start of your turn. Until the start of your next turn, you cannot be Suppressed, Staggered, or forced from your position by any effect, and the Bloodied and Grievous conditions impose no penalties. During this window, Last Round may be activated without spending Strain. If you reduce an enemy to 0 HP during this window, one ally within Medium may use their Reaction to take a Move Maneuver.
career_vanguard_unit_anchor_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Cohesion) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare at the start of your turn. Until the end of the round, allies within Short range gain +10% on POW rolls to resist Suppression and +1 bonus die on First Aid rolls made during combat. You must not be Suppressed or behind Full Cover when you activate this talent.
career_vanguard_unstoppable_barrage_rule: Type: Action (Sustained Heavy Fire) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While braced or in Weapon Platform stance, unleash a full-capacity Full Auto heavy weapon barrage against a target zone within your weapon's maximum range. Each enemy in that zone is targeted; roll Heavy Weapons against each target separately. Enemies who have not yet used their Reaction this round may Dodge using standard Dodge resolution; a successful Dodge avoids the attack entirely. Enemies who have already used their Reaction cannot make a new Dodge roll — the attack resolves against their existing Dodge result if one was made, or against their base defense if none was. Enemies who are hit take weapon damage as normal. For each target hit, generate Suppression. Enemies already Suppressed at the start of this action take +1d6 additional damage.
career_vanguard_weapon_platform_rules-u: Type: Action (Establish) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — While stationary and braced with a heavy weapon, establish a sustained fire position. Until the end of the scene or until you move, heavy weapon attacks deal +2 damage on each hit and generate Suppression on all misses regardless of fire mode. While this stance is active, you cannot take Move Maneuvers or benefit from movement-based talents.
career_vanguard_weapon_transition_rules-: Type: Free Action (Swap) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Switch between a heavy weapon and any other equipped weapon as a Free Action rather than a Maneuver. Your first attack with the new weapon this round suffers no transition penalty.
career_vanguard_weight_of_experience_rul: Type: Free Action (Wound Resistance) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Declare at the start of your turn. Until the end of the scene, the Bloodied condition imposes no penalty on you. If you also have the Grievous condition, the combined penalty is reduced to –10% rather than –20% for the same duration.
career_veil_dancer_borrowed_horizon_rule: Type: Free Action (Spatial Rewrite) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — At any point during your turn, choose one visible creature within Medium range. You immediately reposition to any space at Engaged or Short range relative to that creature that you could physically occupy. This movement may cross vertical distance or open space but may not pass through sealed barriers or total cover.
career_veil_dancer_borrowed_step_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Vector Shift) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one ally within Short range. You reposition to any space within Short range of that ally that you could physically reach by normal movement if barriers were not present. If this movement begins while Engaged with a hostile, it counts as a Disengage.
career_veil_dancer_break_the_rhythm_rule: Type: Reaction (Rhythm Break) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A creature within Engaged range completes an Action. Effect: Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the target’s DEX. On a success, the target becomes Impaired until the end of its next turn as its timing and momentum are disrupted. This effect represents broken flow, not pain or fear.
career_veil_dancer_claim_the_space_rules: Type: Action (Engagement Authority) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one creature within Engaged range. Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the target’s STR or DEX (target’s choice). On a success, until the start of your next turn, the target cannot voluntarily move beyond Engaged range with you. Disengage is still allowed but costs two Maneuvers instead of one. This effect ends immediately if you leave Engaged range or are incapacitated. This effect counts as a movement-restricting effect for the purposes of Veil Dancer spatial limitations.
career_veil_dancer_falling_star_impact_r: Type: Action (Impact Descent) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Move up to two range bands in a straight line toward a point you can see, ignoring difficult terrain and vertical distance. When you land, all enemies within Engaged range must oppose your Magic (Alteration) with STR or DEX. Each creature you defeat takes 2d6 Arcane damage, is knocked Prone, and becomes Impaired until the end of its next turn. You remain standing at the center of the impact.
career_veil_dancer_gravity_hold_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Localized Constraint) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose one creature within Short range. Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the target’s STR. On a success, until the start of your next turn the target’s voluntary movement is limited to one range band total, and it cannot benefit from effects that grant additional voluntary movement. Disengage is still allowed and costs a Maneuver as normal.
career_veil_dancer_grounded_form_rules-u: Type: Maneuver (Stance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the start of your next turn, the first effect that would knock you Prone or forcibly reposition you instead fails. Any further forced movement effects during this duration resolve normally. This represents deliberate stabilization rather than immunity.
career_veil_dancer_heaven_shattering_seq: Type: Action (Perfect Assault) • Cost: 2d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare a sequence of flawless motion against a single target within Short range. Make up to three melee or unarmed attacks against that target as part of this Action. Each attack is rolled separately and resolves normally. The target may Dodge or Fight Back only once during this sequence; all subsequent attacks bypass Reactions. After the sequence resolves, you may immediately reposition to any space within Short range of the target without provoking Reactions.
career_veil_dancer_impossible_footing_ru: Type: Action (Mythic Movement) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the end of your turn, you may move across surfaces that could not normally support you, including vertical walls, unstable debris, narrow ledges, or liquid surfaces. This movement ignores difficult terrain and unstable ground penalties. You must begin and end this movement in a space that can physically contain your body; you cannot end your turn suspended in open air or unsupported space.
career_veil_dancer_latch_the_moment_rule: Type: Action (Commitment) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Make one melee or unarmed attack against a target at Engaged range. If the attack hits, choose one: the target cannot Disengage from you before the start of your next turn, or the target suffers 1 penalty die on its next physical action taken before the end of its next turn.
career_veil_dancer_reading_the_weight_ru: Type: Passive (Awareness) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When a hostile creature enters or leaves Engaged range with you, gain +10% on your next Dodge or Coordination roll made against that creature before the end of your next turn. This bonus does not stack with other talent-granted percentage bonuses. This bonus applies only once per triggering movement.
career_veil_dancer_shear_and_slip_rules-: Type: Reaction (Force Redirection) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You are hit by a melee or unarmed attack made at Engaged range. Effect: Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the attacker’s STR or DEX. On a success, reduce the damage from that hit by half (rounded down) after Armor, then you may immediately Disengage from that attacker. This Disengage follows all normal rules and does not grant additional movement beyond the Disengage.
career_veil_dancer_slip_inside_the_strik: Type: Reaction (Engagement Shift) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A hostile creature moves into Engaged range with you. Effect: You may immediately reposition to any other valid space within Engaged range of that creature, or choose to impose 1 penalty die on the triggering attack if it occurs before the end of the creature’s current action. This does not deal damage and does not prevent further Reactions.
career_veil_dancer_stillness_between_hea: Type: Reaction (Temporal Interdiction) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Trigger: A visible creature within Short range begins an Action. Effect: Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the creature’s DEX (or relevant casting skill, if a spell). On a success, the triggering action is suspended. You immediately take a full turn (Action, Maneuver, Free Actions) outside Initiative. When your turn ends, the suspended action resolves with 1 penalty die. Whether you succeed or fail, you cannot take Reactions until the start of your next turn.
career_veil_dancer_stonefall_stance_rule: Type: Maneuver (Physics Imposition) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the start of your next turn, you dramatically increase your effective mass and inertia. During this time, you cannot be forcibly moved or knocked Prone by effects that do not explicitly ignore stabilization. In addition, the next melee or unarmed attack you hit with before this effect ends deals +1d4 damage after Armor. This bonus damage applies only once and does not stack with other bonus damage effects.
career_veil_dancer_strain_buffer_rules-u: Type: Passive • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Increase your maximum Strain by +1. (This talent may appear in multiple Career Trees.)
career_veil_dancer_the_veil_will_not_let: The Veil Will Not Let You Fall

Type: Action (Existential Assertion) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Until the end of the Scene, the first time you would be reduced to 0 HP or removed from play by forced movement, that effect is negated. You instead reposition to any space within Short range and remain at 1 HP. This effect then ends. The Strain cost is applied immediately when this talent is activated.
career_veil_dancer_the_world_says_no_rul: Type: Reaction (Absolute Rebuttal) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: You would be forcibly moved, knocked Prone, restrained, grappled, or repositioned. Effect: Negate the triggering effect entirely. If the source was a creature within Engaged or Short range, that creature suffers 1 penalty die on its next action taken before the end of its next turn as the imposed force rebounds against it.
career_veil_dancer_turn_the_rush_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Momentum Theft) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Trigger: A creature within Short range moves toward or past you as part of its action or ability (including Charges or forced repositioning). Effect: Roll Magic (Alteration) opposed by the mover’s STR or DEX. On a success, the creature’s movement is redirected: it stops at Engaged range with you and cannot continue moving this round. This does not affect teleportation.
career_veil_dancer_unbound_dance_rules-u: Type: Action (Ascendant Stance) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the start of your next turn, you may take one additional Maneuver without paying Strain, may Disengage as a Free Action, and gain +10% on all Dodge and Coordination rolls. While this stance is active, you cannot take the Focused Aim action or benefit from effects that require remaining stationary. While Unbound Dance is active, you cannot apply Veil Dancer talents that restrict enemy movement.
career_veil_dancer_veil_economy_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Economy Adjustment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you would spend 3 Strain to take a second Maneuver during your turn, you may instead pay 2 Strain. This affects only the Strain cost; the additional Maneuver follows all normal rules and limitations.
career_veil_dancer_veilbreaker_palm_rule: Type: Action (Altered Strike) • Cost: 3 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Until the end of your turn, the next melee or unarmed attack you successfully hit with deals Arcane damage instead of Physical damage and reduces the target’s Armor by 2 (minimum 0) against that hit only. This effect applies to one attack and does not stack with other effects that reduce or bypass Armor.
career_veil_dancer_veilstep_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Reposition) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Immediately move 1 range band. This movement ignores difficult terrain and unstable footing. If this movement begins while you are Engaged with a hostile creature, it counts as a Disengage. You must end this movement in a space you could physically occupy by normal movement; this effect does not allow phasing through solid barriers or sealed spaces.
career_veil_dancer_you_dont_leave_me_rul: Type: Passive (Positional Influence) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While you are Engaged with at least one hostile creature, hostile creatures within Short range treat voluntary movement away from you as difficult terrain (each range-band change away from you costs one additional Maneuver). This does not apply to teleportation or forced movement caused by allies.
career_veil_dancer_zero_point_mastery_ru: Type: Action (Mythic State) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Until the end of the Scene, you partially sever your relationship with mass and inertia. Each round, you may take one additional Maneuver without paying Strain, and your movement between range bands never provokes Reactions. While this state is active, you cannot be Slowed, Hobbled, knocked Prone, or forcibly repositioned by effects that do not explicitly ignore stabilization. When the Scene ends, you become Exhausted until you recover at least 1 Strain.
charm_desc-u: The art of winning others over through likability, charisma, or subtle seduction. Whether flashing a smile, offering a sincere compliment, or projecting a calming presence, Charm nudges social dynamics in your favor. Ideal for friendly persuasion, romantic influence, casual negotiation, or easing tension without confrontation.
chemistry_desc-u: Covers the understanding of chemical reactions, compounds, toxins, pharmaceuticals, and materials synthesis. Used to identify substances, craft drugs or explosives, detect contaminants, and understand the physical behavior of volatile materials—both natural and synthetic, arcane or mundane. Crucial for medics, saboteurs, alchemists, and black market chemists.
clothing_basic_streetwear_effect-u: No mechanical effect. Blends in across most non-corp environments without drawing attention.
clothing_corporate_casual_ensemble_effec: Passive anti-scanner materials reduce profile drift at standard corp checkpoints. Smart-fabric ID sync populates nearby systems with a plausible employee signature. Bonus die on Deception checks to pass as a corp employee or contractor in standard corp zones. Ineffective against House-tier security or active identity verification.
clothing_district_transition_outfit_effe: +10% bonus to Stealth and Deception checks when moving between districts or entering environments where your background would draw attention.
clothing_dreamline_formal_attire_effect-: Embedded mood-reactive lighting, sonic-thread resonance, and scent-cue release. +10% bonus to Persuade and Charm checks in formal social contexts where appearance contributes to authority. Conspicuous and impractical outside those contexts.
clothing_electrochromatic_threads_effect: Change color, pattern, and surface design as a Free Action via Omni interface. Breaks surveillance continuity in footage; ineffective against biometric scans or careful observers.
clothing_house_courtier_attire_effect-u: House-specific cut, sigil-embroidery, and color language signals legitimate affiliation. Bonus die on Deception and Persuade checks in House-controlled social environments. Wrong House's attire in the wrong room carries its own consequences.
clothing_syndicate_street_kit_effect-u: Bonus die on Streetwise and Deception checks when moving through syndicate-controlled territory with the appropriate faction's aesthetic. Wrong kit in the wrong district is more dangerous than no kit at all.
clothing_synthleather_jacket_effect-u: Weather-resistant. Projects street credibility in mid-tier social and street-level environments without standing out.
clothing_veil_treated_duster_effect-u: +10% bonus to CON rolls made to resist passive or environmental Veil exposure only.
clothing_wildsproof_cloak_effect-u: Dustproof, moisture-resistant, and Veil-shielded against ambient fringe exposure. Designed for Wildlands and off-grid traversal. Reversible — urban camo / high-vis emergency orange.
colony_logistics_contact_desc-u: Familiar with supply chains, shortages, and who still trades honestly beyond the core worlds.
command_presence_desc-u: Once per session, when you assert authority or issue a direct command in a tense situation, gain +1 bonus die on one Intimidate, Persuade, or Leadership roll. If the roll succeeds, the compliance or de-escalation achieved is immediate but short-lived, lasting only long enough to resolve the current moment unless reinforced by further leverage. The action draws attention and may create resentment, scrutiny, or escalation appropriate to the situation.
computer_use_desc-u: Represents proficiency with digital systems, user interfaces, data entry, software utilities, and network navigation outside of hostile or secured environments. The baseline skill for interacting with terminals, managing communications, decrypting basic files, running diagnostics, and interfacing with magi-enhanced operating systems. While Slicing is used to break into systems, Computer Use is used to operate them as intended.
concealment_rig_desc-u: Hidden compartments, body concealment wear, and tools for moving restricted items unnoticed.
condition_blind_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Whether caused by injury, arcane interference, sensory overload, or a blinding flash, blindness imposes serious difficulty in combat. A blind character suffers two penalty dice on all sight-based or precision-dependent physical actions. They cannot make targeted attacks or called shots, and may not use Reactions unless the Reaction is specifically non-visual. Enhanced optics or proximity sensors may mitigate this condition at the gamemaster's discretion.
condition_bloodied_rules-u: Type: Condition (HP) • Trigger: HP drops to or below 50% of maximum — The character is visibly wounded and operating under duress. Bloodied status is applied automatically when HP reaches half of maximum and removed when HP recovers above that threshold.
condition_burning_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • A character who is on fire suffers 1d6 damage at the start of each of their turns until the flames are extinguished. To put out the fire, the character must spend a full Action and a Maneuver on the same turn and must be in an appropriate setting — rolling on the ground, activating flame retardant gear, or jumping into water. Some armor or cybernetic mods may reduce fire damage or prevent ignition entirely.
condition_chilled_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1 Round — The target suffers –10% to their next action or reaction before the end of their next turn. This condition may stack with other impairments, and reflects slowed nerves, sluggish reflexes, or freezing limbs.
condition_deafened_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Concussive blasts, sonic discharges, or Veil resonance can rupture eardrums or overload auditory sensors. A deafened character automatically fails any Listen-based Perception rolls and suffers –10% to all actions, reflecting the disorientation and loss of spatial awareness caused by the condition. They may still perceive loud vibrations or visual cues at the gamemaster's discretion. Deafness typically lasts for a duration determined by the source — 1d3 rounds for temporary effects, or until treated if caused by lasting injury or persistent interference.
condition_disoriented_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Loud explosions, sonic discharges, mental shocks, or sudden Veil surges can leave a character Disoriented. A Disoriented character suffers –10% to all skill rolls and one penalty die on Initiative rolls for the duration of the condition. Perception checks, spellcasting, ranged attacks, and complex interactions are particularly affected. Disorientation lasts for a number of rounds determined by the source, or until the character receives focused medical attention or clears their mind through environmental relief.
condition_entangled_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Whether caught in a spell-woven snare, held in a grapple, or pinned beneath rubble, a restrained character cannot Disengage, move between range bands, or reposition by any conventional means. To break free, the character must spend their Action on their turn to attempt a contested Strength roll against the force restraining them. If the source is one full size category larger, the restrained character rolls with one penalty die. An ally may spend their Action to grant a bonus die to the next escape attempt. Magical movement abilities or teleportation effects may bypass the Strength roll entirely.
condition_exhausted_rules-u: Type: Condition (Strain) • Trigger: Strain reduced to 0 — An Exhausted character may only take one action total on their turn: either an Action, a Maneuver, or a Reaction — not all three. Free actions are still allowed, but anything requiring expenditure of effort must be chosen carefully. The Exhausted condition remains until the character recovers at least 1 point of Strain. Certain cyberware, talents, or drugs may temporarily override this limit.
condition_hobbled_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1 Round — The target's voluntary movement is limited to one range band total on their next turn. They cannot Dash, Charge, Sprint, or use effects that grant extra bands. Disengage still costs a Maneuver as normal. Athletics checks to vault, climb, or jump are –10% while Hobbled. This condition does not affect actions — they can still attack or cast. Ends at the start of the attacker's next turn, or sooner at GM's discretion if treated or assisted.
condition_impaired_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1 Round — The target is briefly overwhelmed, off-balance, or thrown out of rhythm. On their next turn, they may only take an Action or a Maneuver — not both. Reactions are allowed. Impaired can be applied via spells, environmental effects, or certain attacks.
condition_jammed_rules-u: Type: Condition (Weapon) • A jammed weapon is temporarily inoperable due to mechanical fault, debris, overheating, or magical interference. Clearing a jam requires a Maneuver and a successful Hard Mechanics roll (or the weapon's associated combat skill roll if the jam is simple). On a success, the weapon is immediately restored to working order. If the attempt fails, the weapon remains jammed and the user may try again on a later turn. A character may only attempt to clear a jam once per round. If a clearing roll results in a fumble, the weapon becomes completely inoperable for the remainder of the scene.
condition_panicked_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1d3 Rounds — Whether induced by a psychic assault, supernatural aura, or mental manipulation, a Panicked character is overwhelmed with the primal urge to flee. On the round a character becomes Panicked, they must move as far as possible away from the source of the fear using all available Maneuvers to retreat. They may not take any offensive actions, but may take Reactions if doing so would protect them. Characters may attempt to end the condition early by succeeding on a POW roll at the start of their turn.
condition_poisoned_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • While Poisoned, a character suffers –10% to all physical skill checks, including melee, ranged, athletics, and coordination-based actions. The specific poison's description determines its duration, onset, and any additional effects. In the absence of a stated duration, Poisoned lasts until treated by medical, cybernetic, or magical means.
condition_shaken_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: Until end of next turn — A Shaken character is rattled by a close call, sudden shock, or calculated intimidation. While Shaken, they suffer –10% to all offensive rolls until the end of their next turn. This condition does not force movement or prevent attacks, but represents hesitation and fractured focus in the heat of battle.
condition_slowed_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • A Slowed character moves as though wading through molasses — whether due to frost magic, heavy injury, or temporal distortion. While Slowed, the character may only move one range band per turn, and any Maneuvers involving movement cost an Action instead. Slowed lasts until the cause is removed, the character is healed, or the effect is dispelled.
condition_stunned_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1 Round — A stunned character is momentarily overwhelmed by a concussive strike, sudden Veil pulse, or disruptive spell effect. They lose both their Action and Maneuver on their next turn. They may still take Reactions if the source of the stun does not explicitly prevent it. Some cyberware or magical talents may reduce the duration or severity of being stunned.
condition_suppressed_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • Duration: 1 Round — A character who is the target of a Full Auto attack may become Suppressed if they fail a POW roll at the moment of impact. On a failed POW roll, the character is unable to make an attack on their next turn, regardless of whether the FA attack actually hit. They may still move, cast defensive spells, or take Reactions, but may not take any offensive attack action. If the suppressed character moves out of cover or across open space, the attacker may use their Reaction to make a Suppressive Follow-up Attack: a standard attack roll dealing base weapon damage only. This Reaction may only be taken once per suppressed target per round, though a second Reaction may be taken by spending 3 Strain.
condition_tired_rules-u: Type: Condition (Strain) • Trigger: Strain drops to or below 50% of maximum — Skill and Stat rolls suffer a -10 penalty. This condition is automatically applied when Strain falls to or below half its maximum value and is removed when Strain recovers above that threshold.
condition_weakened_rules-u: Type: Condition (Combat) • The character is recovering from serious injury, trauma, or medical treatment that limits their combat and cognitive efficiency. While Weakened, the character suffers –10% to all physical skill checks (including melee, ranged, athletics, and coordination-based actions) and takes one penalty die on all Initiative rolls. Additionally, they cannot take more than one Reaction per round, regardless of Strain spent or abilities used. Weakened typically lasts for the entire duration of Major Wound recovery, or until removed through successful treatment, magic, or specific talents.
contextual_analysis_desc-u: Once per session, after examining a document, structure, artifact, or recorded data, you may ask the GM one clarifying question about its origin, purpose, or historical significance. The answer will be accurate, but limited to what could reasonably be inferred from available evidence.
control_the_narrative_desc-u: Once per session, when information about an event involving you or your allies begins to spread, you may influence how it is framed. Choose one: the story emphasizes chaos over intent, competence over morality, or confusion over clarity. This does not erase evidence, but it shapes public perception in the short term.
coordination_desc-u: Represents fine motor control, balance, reflex timing, and bodily precision. Governs activities requiring dexterous movement—vaulting over obstacles, rolling to avoid injury, maintaining footing on unstable surfaces, or executing acrobatic maneuvers. Focuses on finesse more than brute physicality, complementing Athletics.
corporate_credentials_desc-u: Active, expired, or falsified ID granting surface-level access to corporate facilities, jargon, and internal processes.
corporate_fixer_desc-u: Specializes in smoothing problems before they reach legal or public exposure.
corporate_framing_desc-u: Once per session, when interacting with a corporate employee, contractor, or institutional authority, you may frame your request or explanation in corporate language. If you do, you may ignore one penalty die imposed due to hierarchy, red tape, or institutional resistance on a Persuade, Bureaucracy, or Law roll. This does not grant obedience—only access and credibility.
corporate_or_political_insider_desc-u: Someone who understands how power actually moves behind closed doors.
ct_vitality_total_title-u: Total Vitality used by installed cybertech
cybernetics_desc-u: The specialized knowledge required to install, maintain, and troubleshoot cyberware—implants, neural interfaces, cyberlimbs, sensory nodes, and internal rigging. Combines medical understanding with mechanical precision. Required for surgical-grade cyberware installation or tuning, used by medtechs, back-alley ripperdocs, and corporate cyber-engineers alike.
debt_marker_desc-u: Proof of a settled—or outstanding—obligation within the Underworld. It has no mechanical effect, but opens doors or causes problems.
deception_desc-u: Your ability to mislead, lie, or obscure the truth—verbally or otherwise. Used to fabricate convincing falsehoods, cover up illicit activity, impersonate someone, or distract with confident misdirection. Unlike Persuade or Charm, this skill is rooted in falsehood, and its effects are often temporary or fragile once scrutiny returns.
defector_or_heretic_desc-u: Someone who left before you—or after—and knows what the doctrine hides.
demolitions_desc-u: The knowledge and practical application of explosive devices—how to arm, disarm, shape, conceal, or detonate them safely and effectively. Covers both military-grade and improvised explosives, as well as arcane-infused charges. Used for structural sabotage, controlled breaching, and trap deployment.
department_insider_desc-u: A clerk, dispatcher, or analyst who still has access to records and knows how to quietly move information.
detonator_omnilink_detonator_chip_effect: Syncs to Omni device. Up to 4 linked charges, triggered simultaneously or in sequence as a Free Action.
detonator_omnilink_traceable_note-u: Traceable — advanced surveillance and corporate monitoring can detect OmniLink activation signatures in monitored zones.
detonator_pressure_pad_trigger_effect-u: Triggers on 25 kg+ contact. Arm manually. Spot: Perception roll. Disarm: Regular Demolitions Maneuver; Fumble triggers charge.
detonator_remote_trigger_jamming_note-u: Vulnerable to signal jamming. GM calls opposed Electronics roll if jamming is active.
detonator_remote_trigger_unit_effect-u: Wireless. Trigger from up to Long range as Free Action once synced. One charge at a time unless multiple receivers are linked.
detonator_standard_wired_detonator_effec: Hardwire. Trigger from up to Short range. Immune to signal jamming, EMP, and ArcNet disruption.
detonator_timer_module_effect-u: Countdown 10 seconds to 10 minutes. Single use. Cannot be paused or disarmed remotely. Disarm: Regular Demolitions (or Hard Electronics); Fumble triggers charge.
disgruntled_analyst_desc-u: Knows where the numbers don’t add up and who benefits from that fact.
disguise_desc-u: Covers the art of concealing one's identity, altering appearance, voice, posture, or behavior to pass as someone else. May involve physical makeup, clothing, digital forgery, vocal modulation, or simply adopting new mannerisms. Whether impersonating a corp official, slipping past a security checkpoint, or disappearing into a crowd, Disguise enables infiltration and subterfuge.
doctrine_before_fear_desc-u: Once per session, when acting in accordance with a belief, doctrine, or oath that meaningfully constrains your choices in the current situation, you may either gain +1 bonus die on a social, resolve-based, or fear-related roll, or ignore one penalty die imposed by fear, intimidation, or emotional pressure. This represents conditioning and conviction—not divine favor. If the roll succeeds, the GM may introduce consequences tied to visibility, dogma, or ideological fallout.
dodge_desc-u: Your instinctive ability to evade danger in close quarters—be it a melee strike, incoming projectile, or grappling attempt. Used as a reactive defense in combat. A character may attempt to Dodge multiple attacks in a round, but only once per incoming attack. Note that bullets and beam weapons cannot be dodged unless a specific rule, spell, or ability allows it. As a Combat Skill, Dodge cannot be Pushed.
drive_auto_desc-u: Covers the operation and control of ground-based vehicles, including wheeled, treaded, hover, and arcane-assisted transports. Governs navigation at high speeds, sudden maneuvers, and vehicle-related stunts or evasions—whether through city streets, off-road terrain, or active chase scenes.
drone_burstbound_breach_unit_effect-u: One-use rolling drone delivering a payload (Flashburst, EMP, fragmentation, or concussive). Detonates on contact or remote signal.
drone_ghost_spoofer_drone_effect-u: Impersonates security drone ID signatures. Drone Operation vs. Security Rating to loop or redirect a target drone for up to 3 minutes.
drone_interceptor_drone_effect-u: Mid-sized response drone with shock baton or arc-disruptor spike. Engages one target per round. Failed defense roll = Impaired or Disoriented.
drone_operation_desc-u: Covers the control and tactical use of unmanned vehicles—including aerial drones, ground recon bots, aquatic probes, and autonomous combat platforms. Governs precision, coordination, and situational awareness when piloting drones via line-of-sight controller, neural rig, or remote relay. Essential for reconnaissance specialists, slicers, and field agents deploying multi-drone strategies.
drone_packmule_utility_drone_effect-u: Carries up to 20 kg. Stationary relay node extends Omni and comms range by 300 meters. Unarmed.
drone_sentry_drop_unit_effect-u: Autonomous patrol drone. 15-meter detection radius. Alerts via Omni ping. Optional tranq or shock charge before going dormant. 4-hour battery.
drone_shadowmote_microdrone_effect-u: Veil-coated stealth microdrone for interior ops. Reactive camo defeats visual and IR. Thermal imaging, audio relay, low-light visual. Fragile.
drone_skyeye_recon_drone_effect-u: Compact quadcopter. Stabilized high-angle visuals, zoom, audio relay. 10-minute battery. Must be piloted during operation.
duty_gear_desc-u: Protective clothing, restraints, personal comms, and standard non-lethal enforcement tools.
edge_sense_desc-u: Once per session, when entering an unfamiliar or unstable environment, you may ask the GM one of the following questions: What here is most dangerous? or What here is most likely to fail first? The answer will be truthful but not exhaustive.
editor_or_producer_desc-u: Decides what runs, what gets buried, and what needs “one more angle.”
electronics_desc-u: Covers the construction, modification, and repair of electronic systems—from basic circuits and signal relays to advanced magi-tech hybrids. Used to maintain communications arrays, patch damaged equipment, craft signal boosters, or wire portable devices with arcane triggers or bypasses. Essential for field engineers, drone technicians, and saboteurs.
encrypted_commlink_desc-u: Secure device preloaded with corporate-grade encryption, scheduling software, and internal-format documentation templates.
encrypted_personal_device_desc-u: A private comms unit, datapad, or credential hub with legacy access frameworks and subtle security features.
enforcer_or_crew_mate_desc-u: Someone who has bled with you and hasn’t forgotten it.
engineering_desc-u: Covers the principles of structural design, mechanical construction, and load-bearing systems—from basic architecture to advanced arcane-infused engineering. Used to assess buildings, design machinery, reinforce defenses, or detect structural weaknesses in both modern and post-Cataclysm infrastructure. Essential for architects, demolitionists, siege tacticians, and archanotech builders.
ent_black_echo_chip_effect-u: Recording from edge of death. Without Veil shielding: POW roll or 1d6 Strain + Disoriented 1 scene. Critical Failure: lasts one full session.
ent_blacksoft_bundle_effect-u: Mix of cracked feeds, corrupted SkillChips, degraded Lang-Chips, and unknown PulseSim. Contents and quality unknown until used.
ent_combat_sim_chip_effect-u: +20% to one combat skill for one scene. 3 uses. Requires Skill Rig. Cannot exceed 60% skill cap.
ent_dateslate_effect-u: Encrypted memory tablet for recording, editing, and transmitting personal, professional, or illicit data.
ent_deepdrift_sleeve_effect-u: Full-body DreamFeed immersion. Deeper than HALO Rig; approaches direct hardwired intensity. Extended unsupervised use carries GM-determined consequences.
ent_dreamfeed_chip_effect-u: Mass-market recorded sensory experience. Requires NeuroCast Player or HALO Rig. No mechanical benefit from standard entertainment feeds.
ent_driftlink_module_effect-u: Synchronized DreamFeed experience for 2–4 users simultaneously. Improper sync or PulseSim use can cause cross-sensory contamination.
ent_feeltrack_effect-u: Short-form emotional imprint. Requires DreamFeed Interface Port. Cannot be processed by external hardware.
ent_halo_rig_effect-u: External DreamFeed interface via AR overlay and calibrated neural stimulation. No cyberware required. Cannot process SkillChips or Combat Sims.
ent_lang_chip_comprehension_effect-u: ~25% functional comprehension in a target language. Requires Skill Rig Level 1+.
ent_lang_chip_fluency_effect-u: ~50% practical language capability. Requires Skill Rig Level 1+. Does not stack with Comprehension in the same language.
ent_luxury_dreamfeed_effect-u: Bespoke Dream Architect production. Tactical/skill reinforcement feed may grant +10% to one relevant skill for next downtime scene.
ent_memory_stim_effect-u: Records up to 10 minutes of continuous subjective experience as a transferable neural imprint.
ent_neurocast_player_effect-u: Portable DreamFeed and live channel playback device. Cannot process SkillChips or Combat Sims.
ent_pulsesim_chip_effect-u: Illegal high-intensity sensory flood without safety architecture. 15–30 min experience. Unpredictable — consequences determined by GM.
ent_skillchip_tier1_effect-u: +10% to one Technical, Science, or Knowledge skill for one scene. 3 uses. Requires Skill Rig Level 1+. Cannot exceed 60%.
ent_skillchip_tier2_effect-u: +20% to one Technical, Science, or Knowledge skill for one scene. 3 uses. Requires Skill Rig Level 2. Cannot exceed 60%.
ent_softmuse_ai_companion_effect-u: Personal companion AI. Entertainment recommendations, dream therapy guidance, information retrieval. Syncs with NeuroCast and DeepDrift.
ent_warfeed_effect-u: Classified combat recording without safety architecture. POW roll or 1d6 Strain + Disoriented. Critical Success: +10% to one combat/survival/tactical skill for next session.
environmental_discipline_desc-u: Once per session, when operating in a hazardous or unfamiliar environment, you may ignore one situational penalty die caused by environmental conditions (low gravity, radiation exposure, extreme cold, pressure instability, or similar). This does not negate damage or long-term effects—only momentary operational difficulty.
etiquette_alteri_desc-u: Reflects familiarity with the formal customs, identity protocols, and social expectations of Alteri culture—including their fluid identities, memory-based hierarchy, and rites of recognition. Required when navigating Alteri circles, negotiating with their social strata, or avoiding unintentional identity offenses.
etiquette_corporate_desc-u: Covers the formal protocols and behavioral expectations of corporate environments—board meetings, executive functions, contract signings, and internal hierarchies across major megacorps. Errors in corporate etiquette can close doors, trigger suspicion, or invite legal risk.
etiquette_draevi_desc-u: Covers the traditions, honor codes, and behavioral expectations of Draevi culture—including their clan obligations, combat courtesies, and spiritual protocols. Required when navigating Draevi gatherings, negotiating with clan leadership, or avoiding dishonor.
etiquette_high_society_desc-u: Governs behavior in elite social settings—noble galas, House salons, high-end corporate events, and arcane society functions. Includes knowledge of formal address, table manners, fashion expectations, and the unspoken rules of power and deference.
etiquette_lyranni_desc-u: Covers the ceremonial protocols, veilmark customs, and hierarchical norms of Lyranni society. Required when attending Lyranni rites, negotiating with their archivists or elders, or presenting oneself within their deeply tradition-bound social structure.
etiquette_magi_desc-u: Covers the expected behavior within Magi House functions—including arcane formality, school-specific protocols, and the subtle politics of spell-caster circles. Required when attending House rituals, presenting magical credentials, or avoiding interpersonal arcane insults.
etiquette_military_desc-u: Covers rank acknowledgment, chain-of-command behavior, proper forms of address, and operational decorum within military and paramilitary organizations. Required when interfacing with Peace Commission units, corporate security forces, or other structured armed groups.
etiquette_other_desc-u: Represents familiarity with the customs and behavioral norms of a group or culture not covered by other Etiquette specializations. Must be specified at time of purchase. The GM sets difficulty based on how obscure or formal the group's norms are.
etiquette_underworld_desc-u: Covers the unwritten rules of criminal organizations, black markets, and underground enclaves—including how to show respect to fixers, avoid gang politics, and not accidentally offend a syndicate boss. Getting this wrong can be fatal.
everyday_carry_desc-u: Concealed storage clothing, a few practical tools, emergency cash, and items chosen to blend in rather than stand out.
evidence_of_before_desc-u: Documents, recordings, or objects recovered from what was lost. Identification, proof of ownership, a personnel file, or records that may still carry legal or personal weight.
explosive_arcshock_mine_save_note-u: Cyberware users: CON roll or Stunned 1 round.
explosive_arcshock_pulse_mine_effect-u: 2d8 Physical (Engaged) on trigger. Cyberware users: CON or Stunned 1 round. Arm: Demolitions Maneuver. Spot: Perception roll.
explosive_breaching_charge_effect-u: 3d10 Structural Damage to one surface. Placement: 1 round + Regular Demolitions. Safe radius: Short. Not for use against living targets.
explosive_coreline_m32_launcher_effect-u: Fires any grenade at Medium range as a standard attack. Uses Rifles skill. Reload: Maneuver. MR 00.
explosive_emp_grenade_effect-u: Short range burst. 2d10 Physical to machines/drones/cyberware only. Cyberware users: CON or Impaired 1 round, cyberware ceases. No effect on biological targets.
explosive_emp_grenade_save_note-u: Cyberware users: CON roll or Impaired 1 round, cyberware ceases.
explosive_flashbang_grenade_effect-u: Short range burst. CON roll. Fail = Stunned. Success = Impaired 1 round. Extreme/Critical = no effect. Bonus die with solid cover or flash-shielded optics.
explosive_flashbang_save_note-u: CON roll. Fail = Stunned. Success = Impaired 1 round. Extreme/Critical = no effect.
explosive_fragmentation_grenade_effect-u: 4d8 Physical (Engaged), 2d8 Physical (Short). DEX to halve. Cover provides no additional protection against fragmentation.
explosive_incendiary_grenade_effect-u: 3d10 Physical (Engaged), 2d10 Physical (Short). DEX to halve. Fail = Burning condition (1d6 per turn until extinguished).
explosive_incendiary_grenade_save_note-u: DEX roll to halve. Fail = Burning condition.
explosive_knockout_gas_grenade_effect-u: Short range burst. CON roll. Fail = Unconscious 1d4 rounds. Success = Impaired 1 round. Respirators/breathing cyberware grant full immunity.
explosive_knockout_gas_save_note-u: CON roll. Fail = Unconscious 1d4 rounds. Success = Impaired 1 round.
explosive_nullburst_disruption_grenade_e: Short range burst. All active spells, enchantments, and magical sensors suppressed 1 round. Does not dispel. Silent, no flash.
explosive_plastic_explosives_effect-u: 5d10 Structural Damage (Engaged). Requires detonator. Each additional block adds 2d10 Structural Damage to same area.
explosive_riot_gas_grenade_effect-u: Short range burst. POW roll or Disoriented 2 rounds. Sealed gear/respirators: bonus die. Full sealed gear: immunity at GM discretion.
explosive_scatterfoam_deployment_puck_ef: Covers Engaged area in adhesive foam. Hard STR to escape; ally assist grants bonus die. Dissolves after 3 minutes.
explosive_scatterfoam_save_note-u: Hard STR roll to escape foam.
explosive_smoke_grenade_effect-u: Short range burst. Dense obscurant cloud 3 rounds. Total obscurement. Attack rolls into/through smoke: 1 penalty die. Bypassed by thermal vision, arcane sight, or equivalent.
family_connection_desc-u: A relative or sponsor still embedded in wealth or influence, cautious but reachable.
family_relic_desc-u: An object tied to your background: signet, archive key, inherited data shard, or symbolic marker of standing.
fellow_believer_desc-u: Still committed, quietly doubtful, or secretly afraid, but tied to the same belief system you came from.
fellow_nomad_desc-u: A person who understands the rhythm of leaving and knows when not to ask why.
fellow_survivor_desc-u: Someone who escaped the same collapse and understands what you don’t say out loud.
fence_desc-u: Specializes in moving illegal goods quickly and quietly.
field_analysis_tools_desc-u: Measuring instruments, sample containers, documentation gear, and compact diagnostic equipment suited for on-site investigation.
firearms_handgun_desc-u: Covers the use of one-handed ballistic sidearms such as revolvers, semi-automatics, and mag-pulse handguns. Quick to draw and ideal for close-quarters engagements, concealed carry, and backup roles. Excels in confined environments and allows for off-hand use or dual-wielding under certain conditions. As a Combat Skill, handguns cannot be Pushed.
firearms_rifle_desc-u: Covers the use of long guns—bolt-action and semi-automatic rifles, assault carbines, and standard or tactical shotguns. Designed for mid-to-long range engagements with superior stopping power and accuracy. Shotguns deal devastating damage at short range while rifles provide precision and reach. As a Combat Skill, Rifles/Shotguns cannot be Pushed.
firearms_shotgun_desc-u: Covers the use of standard and tactical shotguns—devastating at short range and effective for breaching and suppression. Shotguns lack long-range precision but excel in close quarters or when penetrating armor or cover matters. As a Combat Skill, Shotguns cannot be Pushed.
firearms_smg_desc-u: Covers the use of compact automatic weapons firing handgun-caliber rounds—SMGs, PDWs, and burst-fire carbines. Favored for their high rate of fire, portability, and effectiveness in urban and close-quarters combat. Can switch between single shot, burst, and full-auto modes. As a Combat Skill, Submachine Guns cannot be Pushed.
first_aid_desc-u: Covers immediate emergency care to stabilize injuries and save lives in the field—including stopping bleeding, closing wounds, resetting fractures, reviving unconscious allies, and applying trauma patches or stimulants. Does not treat disease, arcane affliction, or long-term conditions. Must be applied within minutes of injury.
fixer_desc-u: Knows who is hiring, who is hunting, and what things are worth today.
flaw_addiction_major_description-u: Your vice is a functional dependency. You must indulge in your addiction at least once per session. If you do not, make a Hard POW check at the end of the first major scene of the session. On a failure, you suffer one penalty die on all tasks requiring concentration, restraint, or sustained attention until the addiction is indulged. This penalty stacks with other effects and cannot be ignored.
flaw_addiction_major_flavor-u: You rely on a drug, stim, or neuro-chemical regulator to stay functional.
flaw_addiction_minor_description-u: You struggle to deny a specific vice. When you are directly presented with a clear opportunity to indulge, the GM may call for a POW check. On a failure, you must indulge if it is at all possible. On a success, you resist, but suffer 1d6 Strain as the urge claws at your focus. This Flaw only triggers when the vice is meaningfully available.
flaw_addiction_minor_flavor-u: You rely on a drug, stim, or neuro-chemical regulator to stay functional.
flaw_blood_debt_description-u: You owe something dangerous to a House, syndicate, corporation, or other power. Once per session, the GM may invoke your debt to demand compliance, resources, silence, or action. Refusal results in immediate narrative consequences such as pursuit, retaliation, or loss of Contacts.
flaw_blood_debt_flavor-u: You owe something dangerous—House, syndicate, or worse.
flaw_burned_bridges_description-u: You have a history of broken relationships, betrayals, or severed ties. Once per session, when you attempt to rely on a Contact, Ally, or informal support network, the GM may introduce a complication: delayed assistance, refusal, increased cost, partial misinformation, or unwanted strings attached. This Flaw does not remove Contacts or Allies, but it makes reliance on others unreliable and risky.
flaw_burned_bridges_flavor-u: You have a history of broken relationships and severed ties.
flaw_compulsive_gambler_description-u: You are drawn to risk and high-stakes reward. When presented with a dangerous shortcut, wager, criminal gamble, or high-risk opportunity with clear upside, the GM may call for a POW check. On a failure, you must pursue the gamble if it is at all possible. On a success, you resist, but suffer 1d6 Strain as the urge gnaws at you. This Flaw only triggers when a meaningful gamble is available.
flaw_compulsive_gambler_flavor-u: You can’t walk away from a high-risk reward.
flaw_cyber_rejection_description-u: Your body resists invasive technology. Whenever you install Restricted or Illegal cyberware, increase its Vitality cost and installation difficulty. On a failed install, you immediately suffer backlash such as system shock, rejection symptoms, or partial malfunction until repaired.
flaw_distinctive_look_description-u: You are difficult to forget. Once per session, when anonymity or blending in would matter, the GM may impose one penalty die on rolls to avoid recognition, surveillance matching, or eyewitness recall. You cannot benefit from effects that erase or obscure identity.
flaw_hex_stained_description-u: Arcane systems react poorly to your presence. Once per session, when you use arcanotech, sustain a spell, or interact with a Veil-stabilized device, the GM may trigger interference: glitches, backlash, power drain, or unstable behavior.
flaw_hex_stained_flavor-u: Arcane systems and sustained magic react poorly to your body.
flaw_hunted_description-u: An individual or organization is actively searching for you. Once per session, the GM may introduce pursuit, surveillance, confrontation, or indirect pressure related to your hunter. Ignoring this threat allows it to escalate in scale and severity.
flaw_hunted_flavor-u: Someone is actively searching for you—and they have power.
flaw_marked_by_the_black_description-u: You bear a Veil corruption scar or metaphysical mark. Veil-sensitive entities automatically register your presence within sensing range. Once per session, the GM may introduce unwanted attention, hostility, or supernatural interference linked to the mark.
flaw_marked_by_the_black_flavor-u: You bear a Veil corruption sigil or metaphysical scar.
flaw_mental_scar_description-u: A past trauma still claws at you. When exposed to a defined trigger (such as fire, confinement, bloodshed, or abandonment), the GM may force a Willpower check. On a failure, you suffer panic, dissociation, or hallucination for the scene.
flaw_mental_scar_flavor-u: A trauma still bleeds into your present.
flaw_obligation_description-u: You are responsible for a person, group, or cause that matters to you. Once per session, when events threaten or conflict with this obligation, the GM may force a choice: divert time, resources, or attention to address it, or accept escalating narrative consequences if it is ignored. Obligations create pressure through responsibility rather than force, and they do not disappear unless resolved through significant story developments.
flaw_obligation_flavor-u: You are bound to a person, group, or cause that matters to you.
flaw_soul_flare_description-u: Once per scene, when you cast a spell or are targeted by one, the GM may call for a 1d10 roll. On a 1, the spell surges, fizzles, or causes backlash, at GM discretion.
flaw_soul_flare_flavor-u: Your magical signature is unstable—chaotic and unpredictable.
flaw_veil_touched_description-u: The Veil has altered you just enough to be noticed. Spirits and arcane sensors detect you easily, wards may behave unpredictably near you, and Veil-sensitive systems flag your presence whenever secrecy would matter.
flaw_veil_touched_flavor-u: You’ve been altered by the Veil—just enough to be noticed.
flaw_wanted_low_priority_description-u: Your identity is flagged in a registry, watchlist, or bounty system. Once per session, the GM may introduce checkpoints, scans, delays, or complications caused by your status. Repeated exposure may escalate your priority.
flaw_wanted_low_priority_flavor-u: You’re flagged in some system—corp registry, street watchlist, or local bounty net.
forbidden_lore_desc-u: Represents knowledge of hidden truths, cosmic anomalies, forgotten gods, lost planes, and entities that exist beyond the boundaries of sane understanding. Includes Veil-shattered realities, pre-Cataclysm truths erased from history, and names of beings not meant to be spoken. Characters cannot begin play with ranks unless granted by a background, traumatic event, or direct contact with the unknowable.
forgery_desc-u: Covers the creation and alteration of documents, credentials, contracts, software licenses, identity tags, and physical seals. Includes both digital and analog forgeries, requiring careful observation of formatting, materials, and authentication methods. In Arcadia's shadows, faking a travel visa or backdating a spell license can be more valuable than any blade.
former_colleague_desc-u: A fellow researcher who stayed in the field, shifted careers, or now works somewhere with resources you no longer have.
former_crew_member_desc-u: Someone who survived the same Offworld posting and understands the kind of trouble you don’t talk about.
former_partner_desc-u: Still active, retired, or burned like you, but they know how enforcement really works and who can be trusted.
fringe_fixer_desc-u: Knows how to get things quietly, move people discreetly, and avoid attention where authority is thin.
fringe_mobility_gear_desc-u: Durable clothing, climbing aids, weather protection, and lightweight travel equipment suited for rough terrain and long movement.
gear_adrenapatch_effect-u: Restores 1d6+3 Strain. Usable on unconscious characters. Counts as 1 daily stim dose.
gear_arc_latch_decoder_effect-u: +20% to Security Systems vs. electronic lock systems. 1 round to decode. Failed roll may trigger alert.
gear_arcano_calibration_lens_effect-u: +10% to Arcanotech checks when working on hybrid or magi-cybernetic systems.
gear_audiomesh_wall_mic_effect-u: Acoustic pressure plate for thin walls. Transduces speech from adjacent rooms. Ineffective on reinforced surfaces.
gear_biometric_bypass_clamp_effect-u: +30% to Security Systems vs. biometric locks when a valid sample is available. No bonus without a sample.
gear_blackgrid_pocket_jammer_effect-u: Scrambles VoxGrid, Omni, and civilian comms within 15 meters for up to 10 minutes. Cannot block high-tier corp or House comms.
gear_climbing_harness_kit_effect-u: Bonus die on Athletics checks for climbing or rappelling when properly rigged.
gear_combat_medkit_effect-u: +20% Medicine. Heals per Medicine table on success. 3 uses. Requires Field Surgery Kit.
gear_corruption_assay_strip_effect-u: Reveals presence and severity of Veil-taint by contact or proximity. Color-pattern readout within 30 seconds. 3 strips.
gear_cosmetic_disguise_kit_effect-u: +10% to Disguise checks for surface-level cover. Does not defeat biometric scanners.
gear_counter_surveillance_sweep_unit_eff: +20% to Perception checks when actively sweeping for planted surveillance. Detects active signal emitters within 20 meters.
gear_cryochill_iv_effect-u: Fully restores all Strain after 30 uninterrupted minutes in a safe environment. Counts as 2 daily doses. Once per day.
gear_cybernetic_install_kit_effect-u: +20% to Cybernetics checks when installing or removing implants. Requires controlled environment. Cannot be used in combat.
gear_deep_cover_document_set_effect-u: Fabricated identity package. Bonus die on Deception and Disguise while operating under the cover identity.
gear_echobug_audio_tap_effect-u: Coin-sized listening device. Transmits audio to linked Omni within 150 meters. Self-wipes on removal.
gear_echolock_comms_encoder_effect-u: Encrypted rolling hash over all Omni transmissions. Both sender and receiver must be equipped for full encryption.
gear_emergency_breather_mask_effect-u: 10-minute filter cartridge. Protects against smoke, biohazards, chemical fog, and low-atmosphere Veil bleed. Single use.
gear_facial_sculptor_gel_effect-u: +20% to Disguise checks against automated scanning. Lasts 48 hours. Solvent included.
gear_field_expedition_pack_effect-u: +20% to Survival in wilderness, ruins, or off-grid environments. Includes 5-day rations, shelter, purifier, climbing line, navigation slate.
gear_field_mechanic_rig_effect-u: +20% to Mechanics checks when repairing vehicles, support systems, or infrastructure in the field.
gear_field_surgery_kit_effect-u: Required for Medicine skill use in the field. Durable — not consumed on use.
gear_ghostline_effect-u: Restores 2d6+2 Strain. Bonus die on Initiative or +10% to a chosen skill for 10 minutes. Once per scene. Daily limit: 3 doses.
gear_ghostmark_tracker_dart_effect-u: Soft-impact ArcPulse transceiver dart. Broadcasts real-time location for up to 24 hours. Polymer sheath masks thermal signature.
gear_grappling_cartridge_effect-u: Single-use reload for the Grappling Launcher. Fresh microspike head and pre-coiled line.
gear_grappling_launcher_effect-u: Fires high-tensile line up to 20 meters. Auto-anchors with mag-clamp. Internal spool for manual or powered retraction.
gear_heavy_pcell_effect-u: Powers arcane SMGs, rifles, heavier arcanotech weapons. ~20–30 shots before depletion.
gear_identity_chip_spoofer_effect-u: Broadcasts false Omni credential for up to 30 minutes. Slicing roll vs. target security rating. Ineffective against House-tier biometric systems.
gear_industrial_pcell_effect-u: Powers powered armor, combat bots, vehicles. Sustains one full mission of active use. VantaCore maintains purchase records.
gear_irghost_scope_effect-u: Thermal imager. Detects body heat through one wall at up to 20 meters. Unaffected by smoke, fog, or darkness.
gear_micro_welder_torch_effect-u: +10% to Mechanics or Electronics in emergency repair scenarios. Can cut panels, fuse patches, or repair cybernetic sheaths.
gear_nanoclot_injector_effect-u: Stops all ongoing HP loss effects immediately. Does not restore HP. Does not tick Healing Saturation. Requires First Aid check.
gear_neuronedge_x_drive_effect-u: Stability 6 / Program Slots 5 / Mod Slots 2. On Type III networks, a clean entry opens 4 safe rounds.
gear_null_seal_flask_effect-u: Arrests Veil-decay in recently deceased, preserving arcane resonance for up to one Scene after death. 2 flasks, single use each.
gear_nullread_enclosure_bag_effect-u: Signal-isolated pouch. Blocks all outgoing wireless transmission from items inside.
gear_omni_ascend_effect-u: Veil signature tracker, discrete spell registration sync, and arcane overlay compatibility. Must be registered with a House or city authority.
gear_omni_core_effect-u: AR overlay, transit sync, identity verification including spell signatures, full VoxGrid integration. Accepted everywhere.
gear_omni_core_pro_effect-u: Stability 3 / Program Slots 2 / Mod Slots 1. Baseline professional deck.
gear_omni_lite_c1_effect-u: Stability 2 / Program Slots 1 / Mod Slots 0. Entry-level slicer platform.
gear_omni_lite_effect-u: Baseline civic Omni. Secure transactions, basic VoxGrid, identity verification. No AR overlay or spell registration.
gear_omni_phantom_effect-u: Cloaked AR signatures, silent VoxGrid access, active digital trail scrubbing. Requires documentation to carry legally.
gear_omni_stratus_effect-u: Supports 2 simultaneous credential profiles. Enhanced display, secure comms partitioning.
gear_omniphantom_mkiii_effect-u: Stability 4 / Program Slots 3 / Mod Slots 2. Can push deep into Type II networks cleanly.
gear_psytranz_effect-u: Restores 4d6 Strain instantly. Comedown: mild dissociation and heightened sound sensitivity. Once per scene. Daily limit: 3 doses.
gear_quickpatch_effect-u: +20% First Aid. Heals per First Aid table on success. Can stabilize Dying. 3 uses.
gear_ritual_supply_pack_effect-u: Field bundle of arcane components covering material requirements for most common and uncommon ritual workings. Consumed in use.
gear_shockbright_effect-u: Suppresses zero-Strain penalties for 1d4 rounds without restoring Strain. Penalties apply immediately when effect ends. Once per scene. Separate daily limit: 1 dose.
gear_sightline_tactical_optics_effect-u: Up to 15× zoom, light filtering, still-capture. Syncs to OmniDecks for remote relay.
gear_signaltac_whispercom_effect-u: Sub-vocal throat-mount comm. Silent team communication. Undetectable by audio surveillance; detectable by physical inspection.
gear_smartlink_hud_visor_effect-u: Grants Smartlink bonus on compatible weapons while worn and synced. Maneuver to sync to a new weapon. Does not stack with cyberware Smartlink.
gear_standard_lockpick_set_effect-u: +10% to Security Systems vs. mechanical locks. No effect on electronic systems.
gear_standard_pcell_effect-u: Powers arcane sidearms, arc-blades, and light arcanotech devices. ~10–15 shots before depletion.
gear_standard_restraint_kit_effect-u: Polymer zip ties, mag-cuffs (Extreme STR to escape), binding cord. No effect on Users who can access the Veil while restrained.
gear_sterile_field_emitter_effect-u: +1 HP to daily passive recovery in non-clinical settings. Stacks with Medicine bonus. 5 charges, one per rest period.
gear_stimpax_advanced_effect-u: Instantly restore 1d8 HP. Remove Stunned or Exhausted. Once per scene. Ticks Healing Saturation.
gear_stimpax_basic_effect-u: Instantly restore 1d6 HP. Once per scene. Ticks Healing Saturation.
gear_stratos_v_trace_effect-u: Stability 3 / Program Slots 2 / Mod Slots 2. Identical performance to OmniCore Pro with double hardware upgrade potential.
gear_streetplug_mini_effect-u: Stability 1 / Program Slots 1 / Mod Slots 0. Desperation or training use only. Not a professional tool.
gear_tactical_optics_visor_effect-u: +10% to Perception in low-light, smoke, or thermal-obscured conditions. Low-light amplification and thermal overlay.
gear_tech_tool_basic_kit_effect-u: +10% to Mechanics or Electronics for basic field repairs. Some repairs impossible without proper tools.
gear_threadglass_laser_mic_effect-u: Laser mic reading surface vibrations on glass or concrete at range. Requires line of sight and stable platform.
gear_trauma_patch_effect-u: Instantly stabilizes Dying — no roll. Hard CON roll to remove Major Wound. One attempt per 24 hours. Single use.
gear_universal_bypass_rig_effect-u: +20% to Security Systems vs. any lock type it can physically interface with. Cannot defeat House-tier biometric vaults or Veil-sealed barriers.
gear_urban_emergency_kit_effect-u: +10% to Survival in urban or structural emergency contexts. Includes breather mask, arc-lamp, flares, thermal blanket, first aid, 3-day rations.
gear_vantix_void_mirror_effect-u: Stability 7 / Program Slots 6 / Mod Slots 3. Ceiling of commercially available hardware. Illegal.
gear_veil_binders_effect-u: Physical restraints (Extreme STR to escape) that sever the wearer's Veil connection entirely. No spells, no Veil abilities while worn.
gear_veil_containment_vessel_effect-u: Sealed arcane-grade container with stasis sigils. Prevents contained volatile arcane material from interacting with the outside world until unsealed.
gear_veil_sight_goggles_effect-u: Perceive active spells, Veil residue, arcane signatures, and Veil-active entities within Short range. +10% to Arcana and Veil Lore checks when relevant info is in range.
gear_veil_zone_operations_pack_effect-u: +20% to Survival in Veil-active or arcane hazard environments. Includes Veil-resistant cloak, Assay Strips, Null-Seal Flasks, sealed breather, emergency ritual supplies.
gear_voxstim_effect-u: Restores 1d6+3 Strain instantly. Once per scene. Daily limit: 3 doses.
gear_wraith_x_ghostdeck_effect-u: Stability 5 / Program Slots 4 / Mod Slots 2. Extended operational window for complex jobs.
go_bag_desc-u: Lightweight pack containing preserved food, water filtration, emergency shelter materials, and basic medical supplies.
gunnery_desc-u: Covers the operation of vehicle-mounted or stationary heavy weapons such as autocannons, plasma turrets, remote-linked coilguns, or mag-accelerator arrays. Includes both manual and sensor-assisted targeting, whether seated in a cockpit, behind a turret, or interfacing remotely via control rig. As a Combat Skill, Gunnery cannot be Pushed.
head: Head
heavy_weapons_desc-u: Covers the use of high-powered infantry-carried heavy weapons such as rocket launchers, grenade launchers, flamethrowers, mag-cannons, and other shoulder-mounted ordnance. These weapons deal devastating damage but often have limited ammo, setup time, or collateral risk. Typically used in breaching operations, anti-vehicle roles, or against heavily armored threats. As a Combat Skill, Heavy Weapons cannot be Pushed.
hidden_cache_marker_desc-u: Coordinates, tags, or encoded directions leading to a small stashed supply or safe spot somewhere in the Fringe.
hidden_doctrine_cache_desc-u: Encrypted writings, coded prayers, memory shards, or forbidden teachings preserved from your past.
history_desc-u: Covers the study of recorded events, civilizations, conflicts, breakthroughs, and collapses across known time—including pre-Cataclysm nations, the rise of the Houses, corporate expansions, major magical discoveries, and forgotten eras buried by time or deliberate manipulation. Used to place artifacts and cultures in context, identify altered records, or connect modern patterns to ancient precedents.
influencer_rig_desc-u: Personal presentation gear, branded or anonymized accounts, and tools for rapid dissemination.
info_broker_desc-u: Trades in rumors, leaks, and half-confirmed stories before they become public.
infrastructure_tech_desc-u: Specializes in power, water, transit, or environmental systems and knows where the weak points are.
insight_desc-u: Allows a character to interpret body language, micro expressions, tone, and behavioral cues to discern a person's emotional state, honesty, and true intentions. The art of reading the room—sensing when someone is bluffing, or noticing what is left unsaid. Whether interrogating a suspect, detecting a lie, or understanding a companion's hidden doubts, Insight is the key to social perception.
institutional_liaison_desc-u: A bureaucrat, professor, or administrator who understands how to navigate academic or research systems—legally or otherwise.
interrogation_desc-u: The skill of extracting truthful information from unwilling or guarded subjects through controlled questioning. Encompasses formal and informal techniques—from psychological pressure and strategic deception to subtle verbal traps and veiled threats. As likely to be used in corporate boardrooms as in Blackshield basements.
intimidate_desc-u: Represents your ability to apply pressure—physically, psychologically, or emotionally—to bend others to your will. Can involve veiled threats, raw aggression, implied power, or a cold unblinking stare. Successful intimidation might force someone to talk, back down, or comply with demands—at least in the short term.
investigation_desc-u: Represents analytical reasoning, evidence reconstruction, and clue interpretation. Used to connect physical clues, identify patterns, and form accurate conclusions from crime scenes, ruins, hacked data, or magical residue. Combines logic, inference, and deductive thinking rather than instinct or perception alone.
keep_moving_desc-u: Once per session, when you fail a roll while escaping danger, enduring harsh conditions, or pushing through exhaustion, you may immediately reroll the check and must keep the new result. If the reroll succeeds, the action still carries a cost chosen by the GM, such as lost time, expended resources, increased visibility, strain, or leaving evidence behind.
keepsake_relic_desc-u: A personal object from somewhere you left behind. It has no mechanical value, but may carry emotional weight, proof, or leverage.
known_quantity_desc-u: Once per session, when dealing with criminals, fixers, or black-market actors, you may treat yourself as a known, vetted operator rather than an unknown variable. The GM must frame the initial reaction as cautious but open rather than hostile or dismissive. A roll may still be required, but you are not starting from zero.
labor_fixer_desc-u: Arranges work, cover jobs, or access through unofficial industrial channels.
language_caltheran_desc-u: Represents fluency in Caltheran—a structured trade tongue and diplomatic language used across many post-Cataclysm polities and House-aligned institutions. Proficiency scales from basic phrase recognition to full native-equivalent fluency in formal and technical contexts.
language_other_desc-u: Represents fluency in a language of your choice, selected during character creation or play. Humans begin without a fixed racial tongue and may instead develop proficiency in any language available in the setting. Proficiency scales from basic phrase recognition to full native-equivalent fluency in formal and technical contexts.
language_own_desc-u: Represents your primary spoken and written language, typically learned in childhood or formal education. Includes vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension in both conversational and technical contexts. Begins at a percentage equal to your EDU stat and rarely requires rolling unless reading highly technical or archaic material.
