Effect: One target in Short range rolls DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d10 Arcane Cold damage and becomes Slowed until the end of its next turn. On a success, no effect. If the target is Wet or in freezing conditions, the GM may apply disadvantage to the resistance roll.
spell_ele_ice_shards_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Short; 1d10 Cold (Arcane); Slowed on fail
spell_ele_kindle_spark_desc-u:
A bright speck jumps from your nail like a mischievous firefly, eager to catch on anything that wants to burn.

Effect: You ignite a prepared or readily flammable object at Touch range — tinder bundle, stove, fuse line, solvent smear. The effect is instantaneous and non-damaging to creatures, though creative applications involving fuses, alarms, or diversions are encouraged.
spell_ele_kindle_spark_notes-u: Uncontested; Utility; Touch; Instant; ignite object or tinder
spell_ele_lava_coil_desc-u:
A rope of molten stone whips from a hairline crack and flash-cools into glossy black cuffs. Steam spits from the seams; the air smells like hot metal and rain on asphalt.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the target becomes Restrained. At the start of its next turn, it takes 1d6 Arcane Fire damage from the still-smoldering cuffs. During that turn, it may use an Action to attempt a Hard STR roll to break free early. The cuffs can also be destroyed: Physical Armor 2, 10 HP. Destroying them ends the effect. Regardless of escape attempts, the Restrained condition ends at the end of the target's next turn — this spell has a hard one-round cap. On a success against your initial roll, no effect.
spell_ele_lava_coil_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Short; Restrained in cooling magma; 1d6 Fire (Arcane) per turn; 1-round cap
spell_ele_lightning_lash_desc-u:
You crack a vicious whip of raw current across a target's nervous system — not a grab, not a pull, just a direct and personal introduction to more electricity than the body was designed to meet. The result is immediate and undignified.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d10 Arcane Lightning damage and becomes Stunned until the end of its next turn. On a success, no effect.
spell_ele_lightning_lash_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d10 Lightning (Arcane); Stunned on fail; Reflexive
spell_ele_molten_grasp_desc-u:
Your palm lights from within as if a furnace door just cracked open. Anything you seize scorches, and the heat forces instinct over training — drop it or get burned.

Effect: Make a contested attack against a target in Engaged range. On a failure, the target takes 1d8 Arcane Fire damage and must drop one held item of your choice. In addition, all creatures in Engaged range of you take 1d4 Arcane Fire damage from the flash — this splash ignores Physical Armor. On a success, no effect. Objects not meant to withstand high heat may blister, melt, or ignite at the GM's discretion.
spell_ele_molten_grasp_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Engaged; 1d8 Fire (Arcane); force drop item; 1d4 splash; Reflexive
spell_ele_obsidian_bloom_desc-u:
The ground flowers into brittle, glossy knives — petals of volcanic glass that drink light and give it back as razor gleams. It turns open floor into a glittering hazard zone that nobody wants to be standing in.

Effect: Creatures in the bloom roll DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature takes 2d6 Physical (Piercing) damage. On a success, no effect. Regardless of individual resistance, the area becomes hazardous terrain for the rest of the scene: a creature that enters or ends its turn in the bloom takes 1d4 Physical damage (once per turn), and moving through it counts as difficult terrain. The bloom can be cleared from a small area with one round of focused effort and appropriate tools; larger patches take longer at the GM's discretion.
spell_ele_obsidian_bloom_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Medium AoE; 2d6 Piercing (Physical); hazardous terrain for scene
spell_ele_pyroclastic_surge_desc-u:
A pressure-front of incandescent ash blasts in a straight corridor, rolling over bodies with oven-door heat and flensing grit. The roar rattles glass; the heat chases breath out of lungs.

Effect: Choose a straight line out to Medium range, no wider than Engaged. Each creature in the line rolls DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 2d8 Arcane Fire damage and gains the Burning condition. On a success, no effect. The surge ignores minor obstacles but is stopped by solid barriers.
spell_ele_pyroclastic_surge_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Medium line; 2d8 Fire (Arcane) + Burning on fail; no effect on success
spell_ele_quake_step_desc-u:
You stamp, and the world flinches. Tiles jump. Window frames buzz. It's not a big quake — it's a targeted insult to balance and bravado from anyone foolish enough to stand close.

Effect: All enemies in Engaged range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, each takes 1d4 Physical damage and is Knocked Prone. On a success, no effect. The impact is loud and may alert nearby guards or sensors.
spell_ele_quake_step_notes-u: Contested (CON); Engaged AoE; 1d4 Physical + Prone on fail; noise hazard; Reflexive
spell_ele_sandglass_veil_desc-u:
Fine amber grit turns the air around you into a heat-shimmer mirage. Edges blur and aim slips; every shot feels a hair off.

Effect: You gain concealment. Ranged attacks against you suffer 1 penalty die. Against targeted spells, you count as concealed — attackers must be able to perceive you clearly to target you. Wind or heavy rain can degrade the veil at the GM's discretion, but the concealment persists while you concentrate.
spell_ele_sandglass_veil_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); ranged attacks vs you suffer 1 penalty die; Reflexive
spell_ele_scoria_wave_desc-u:
A low, hungry surge of half-cooled slag rolls off your outstretched hand, bursting across armor with spattering, hissing impact. Those it catches do not walk away clean.

Effect: Creatures in the cone roll DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature takes 2d8 Physical (Fire) damage. On a success, no effect. Structures may be scorched; flammable objects can ignite regardless of individual resistance.
spell_ele_scoria_wave_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Short cone; 2d8 Physical Fire on fail; no effect on success
spell_ele_seismic_rift_desc-u:
The ground splits with a socket-rattling crack. Floor panels tilt; rebar sighs; a jagged trench spider-webs into a no-go zone.

Effect: Creatures in the initial rupture roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature takes 2d6 Physical (Sonic) damage and is Knocked Prone. On a success, no effect. Regardless of individual resistance, the ruptured area becomes difficult terrain for the rest of the scene. Crossing the deepest sections may require a Hard DEX roll or an alternate approach at the GM's discretion.
spell_ele_seismic_rift_notes-u: Contested (CON); Medium AoE; 2d6 Sonic (Physical); Prone; difficult terrain for scene
spell_ele_shattered_current_desc-u:
You crack a messy surge through a target's nervous system — a disruptive, off-rhythm jolt that drops signals and leaves legs rubbery. This is not the clean sting of Arc Spark; it's the kind of current that makes a body forget what it was doing.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d8 Arcane Lightning damage and is Stunned until the end of its next turn. On a success, no effect. Metal armor or heavy metal gear imposes disadvantage on the resistance roll.
spell_ele_shattered_current_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d8 Lightning (Arcane); Stunned on fail; metal gear disadvantage; Reflexive
spell_ele_shatterstone_desc-u:
A tight pop of pressure detonates inside the target's armor plates and bone-adjacent spaces. It's not elegant; it's effective.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d10 Physical (Bludgeoning) damage and 1 point of Physical Armor is ignored against this damage. On a success, no effect. Cracked cover, glass, or stone fixtures may also fail or fracture at the GM's discretion.
spell_ele_shatterstone_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d10 Bludgeoning (Physical); ignores 1 Physical Armor
spell_ele_shock_pulse_desc-u:
A cough of blue light inflates from your palm and pops — muscles seize, nerves buzz, and metal bites fingers like an angry eel.

Effect: Creatures in the burst roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature takes 1d6 Arcane Lightning damage and must drop any metal objects it is holding — weapons, tools, shields. Non-metal objects are unaffected unless the GM rules otherwise. On a success, no effect.
spell_ele_shock_pulse_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short burst; 1d6 Lightning (Arcane); drop metal objects on fail; Reflexive
spell_ele_steam_veil_desc-u:
Moisture erupts into a dense, roiling cloud. Edges blur. Voices damp. Visibility drops to arm's length and the air tastes like wet metal.

Effect: You create a Short-burst concealment cloud that persists for the scene. Any creature that starts its turn inside the steam is Disoriented for that turn — no resistance roll required, the disorientation is environmental. Strong wind disperses the veil early; enclosed spaces hold it longer.
spell_ele_steam_veil_notes-u: Uncontested; Short burst; Scene; concealment cloud; Disoriented (no roll) on start of turn inside; Reflexive
spell_ele_stoneguard_desc-u:
Your skin takes on a matte, mineral density. Footing settles. Recoil feels distant, like someone else's problem.

Effect: You gain +1 Physical Armor, immunity to being Knocked Prone, and +20% to checks that resist shove or knockback effects. This protection ends if your concentration breaks.
spell_ele_stoneguard_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); +1 Physical Armor; Prone immunity; Reflexive
spell_ele_stormcall_ward_desc-u:
A faint auroral corona clings to you — frost in the breath, hair lifting with latent charge. Strikes against you spit harmless sparks back at whoever threw them.

Effect: You gain +2 Arcane Armor against Lightning and Cold damage. When a creature in Engaged range hits you with a melee attack, it takes 1d4 Arcane Lightning damage — once per attacker per round. The ward collapses if your concentration is broken.
spell_ele_stormcall_ward_notes-u: Uncontested; Self-aura; Scene (Conc); +2 Arcane Armor vs Lightning/Cold; Engaged attackers take 1d4 Lightning (Arcane); Reflexive
spell_ele_tectonic_cage_desc-u:
Pillars of stone punch up and interlock like a ribcage around your target. The gaps are cruelly tight — breathing room, nothing more.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls STR opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it is Restrained inside a stone cage. The cage has Physical Armor 3 and 15 HP per pillar; destroying any two pillars opens an exit and ends the restraint. Allies can attack the pillars from outside. The effect persists while you concentrate. On a success, the pillars misalign and collapse harmlessly.
spell_ele_tectonic_cage_notes-u: Contested (STR); Short; Restrained in stone pillars; breakable; Scene (Conc)
spell_ele_thermal_sink_desc-u:
You yank heat out of a body and vent it as a breath-fog burst. Metal creaks. Teeth chatter. Muscles slow like syrup — and the cold bleeds outward.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d8 Arcane Cold damage and becomes Slowed until the end of its next turn. In addition, all enemies in Engaged range of the target take 1 Arcane Cold damage from the vented chill — this splash cannot be resisted. On a success, no effect on the primary target; the splash does not trigger.
spell_ele_thermal_sink_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d8 Cold (Arcane); Slowed on fail; 1 Cold splash to Engaged
spell_ele_thunderstep_desc-u:
You pop out of reality with a gunshot thundercrack and rematerialize in a bright after-image, sound chasing light by a heartbeat.

Effect: Teleport to a point you can see within Short range. Creatures in Engaged range of your departure point and your arrival point each roll CON opposed by your casting roll; on a failure they take 1d6 Arcane (Sonic) damage. On a success, no damage. The path between points is irrelevant — only line of sight to the destination matters.
spell_ele_thunderstep_notes-u: Uncontested; Self (AoE); Short teleport; 1d6 Sonic (Arcane) at origin and destination; Reflexive
spell_ele_tremor_blast_desc-u:
A narrow, floor-hugging shockwave sprints away from you, buckling knees and rattling fillings along a single file.

Effect: Choose a straight line out to Medium range, no wider than Engaged. Each creature in the line rolls STR opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d8 Physical damage and is Knocked Prone. On a success, no effect.
spell_ele_tremor_blast_notes-u: Contested (STR); Medium line; 1d8 Physical; Prone on fail
spell_ele_water_whip_desc-u:
A cord of pressurized water lashes out, hits with the weight of a sandbag, then yanks like a rip current — or shoves like a wall, depending on what the moment needs.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls STR opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, it takes 1d6 Arcane Water damage and you choose to either pull it to Engaged range or push it one range band directly away. If the movement would pass through a hazard, apply those effects normally. On a success, no effect.
spell_ele_water_whip_notes-u: Contested (STR); Short; 1d6 Arcane Water; pull to Engaged or push 1 band on fail
spell_enc_anchor_thought_desc-u:
You lodge a single phrase or image in the target's mind so firmly that every other thought must circle around it. The mental snag is subtle yet inescapable, catching their focus whenever they try to act or react.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target loses their Reaction for the current round and suffers -10% to their next action roll before the end of their next turn, caught in the loop you have planted. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_anchor_thought_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; 1 round; target loses Reaction and -10% to next action
spell_enc_borrowed_presence_desc-u:
Your stance, the cadence of your speech, and the subtle rhythms of your body language shift until they echo those of someone the target respects. You are not wearing their face, but you wear the impression of them — the unshakable qualities that earned them loyalty or deference.

Effect: You gain +10% to all social rolls against anyone who personally knows and respects the individual you are mimicking in mannerisms. You need not name the person aloud — the impression is carried in behavior, not declaration. This effect does not work if the target knows the individual to be dead, imprisoned, or otherwise impossible to encounter.
spell_enc_borrowed_presence_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; mimic respected figure's mannerisms; +10% social vs those who know them
spell_enc_cognitive_loop_desc-u:
You spin a spiral of self-contradicting thoughts in the target's mind, trapping them in a micro-maze of mental corrections. The longer they try to think clearly, the deeper the loop digs in — like a finger tracing the same groove over and over until it wears a trench.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target suffers a penalty die on all rolls until the end of their next turn — attacks, defenses, skill checks, and spellcasting alike. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_cognitive_loop_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; 1 round; penalty die on all rolls
spell_enc_cognitive_spike_desc-u:
A thought honed to a killing edge lances into the target — pure psychic overpressure that doesn't try to manipulate anything, it simply detonates. This isn't subtle influence; it's concentrated will fired like a round. It works on creatures and constructs alike because it isn't speaking to the mind. It's hitting through it.

Effect: On a successful Spellcasting roll, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage and becomes Impaired until the end of their next turn. This spell deals Veil damage and works against constructs, drones, and any target a Veil attack would normally affect.
spell_enc_cognitive_spike_notes-u: Targeted; Medium; instant; 1d10 Veil damage; target Impaired
spell_enc_command_tone_desc-u:
Your voice takes on a resonance that is not entirely your own — a pressure in the air that settles into the listener's chest and locks their muscles. It is more than just sound; it's the weight of inevitability behind a single, decisive word. Those who hear it feel as if the world itself pauses, waiting for them to comply.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target is frozen in hesitation and loses their Action on their next turn. They may still take Reactions and Maneuvers but cannot initiate deliberate actions. Must be cast before the target acts to take effect. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_command_tone_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; 1 round; target loses Action next turn
spell_enc_dominion_pulse_desc-u:
You release a wave of authority that fills the space with an unspoken command to yield. For an instant, you are the highest recognized authority in the room — and all instinct bends toward acknowledging it.

Effect: All hostile creatures within Short range must succeed on a POW roll opposed by your casting roll or be unable to take offensive actions against you for 1 round. They may still move, act defensively, and target your allies — only direct offensive action against you is denied. On a success, no effect. If you take any offensive action against a creature affected by this spell, that creature is immediately freed from the effect.
spell_enc_dominion_pulse_notes-u: Contested (POW); Self (Short radius); 1 round; hostiles cannot take offensive actions against you
spell_enc_dread_echo_desc-u:
You whisper a fear into the target's subconscious — not in your voice, but in the one they dread most. The sound bypasses the ears, resonating in the bones, carrying with it the weight of a nightmare made real.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target is Frightened for 1 round — unable to willingly move toward you and suffering a penalty die on all actions. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_dread_echo_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; 1 round; Frightened on fail
spell_enc_emotion_spike_desc-u:
You pry open the emotional core of your target and flood it with raw, untempered feeling. Whether it's rage that shakes their hands, terror that turns their knees to water, sorrow so heavy it pins them in place, or joy so dizzying it blinds them — the effect is total and immediate.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, choose one emotion to inflict for 1 round: Rage — the target must attack the nearest creature on their turn, friend or enemy; they cannot retreat, disengage, or take non-offensive actions. Terror — the target gains the Frightened condition and must spend their movement moving away from you; they cannot willingly close distance. Sorrow — the target becomes Impaired; they cannot initiate actions and may only react to direct threats. Ecstasy — the target cannot take hostile actions against any creature; they are overwhelmed by the sensation and treat all creatures as non-threatening for the round. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_emotion_spike_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; 1 round; overwhelms target with chosen emotion; explicit behavioral effects
spell_enc_erase_moment_desc-u:
You pluck the last few seconds from the target's short-term memory, leaving a clean gap where an event once was. It's as if they blinked and skipped ahead in time — confused but unaware anything is missing.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, erase up to the last 10 seconds of the target's memory. In combat, this can make them forget they were about to attack, that they saw you, or that a threat is present. In social scenes, it can remove a slip of the tongue, an incriminating admission, or a recognition that just occurred. The target is not harmed, merely confused by the gap. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_erase_moment_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; instant; erase last 10 seconds from target's memory
spell_enc_eye_of_compliance_desc-u:
A single glance becomes a leash. You focus your will until it condenses into your eyes — pulling the target's gaze toward you as if gravity itself has shifted. In that moment, you are the only thing they can process clearly, and every other thought or action feels sluggish and uncertain.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target suffers a penalty die on the next roll they make before the end of their next turn — attack, defense, or skill check. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_eye_of_compliance_notes-u: Contested (POW); Engaged; 1 round; penalty die on next roll
spell_enc_false_familiarity_desc-u:
You weave the faintest outline of a memory into the target's mind — a half-remembered meeting, a warm smile in passing, a laugh shared in some distant place. They can't place the details, but the feeling is there: you are someone they've met before, someone safe. Their posture loosens and they behave as though they already know you.

Effect: The target believes they know you from a prior encounter, treating you as a familiar and safe presence for the scene. This does not create trust beyond normal acquaintanceship unless reinforced by other actions or abilities, and it does not hold if directly contradicted by strong evidence. Because you are planting a fragment rather than actively reshaping their will, this spell requires no resistance roll — the memory is simply there, quiet and convincing.
spell_enc_false_familiarity_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene; target believes they know you
spell_enc_final_word_desc-u:
You fix the end of a conversation in the target's mind like a stone in wet cement — whatever you say last on a matter becomes the truth they believe, as long as it isn't utterly impossible.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target believes your final statement in any dispute or negotiation for the rest of the scene, unless confronted with overwhelming contradictory evidence or direct physical proof that contradicts the claim. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_final_word_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene; last statement believed unless strongly contradicted
spell_enc_focus_thread_desc-u:
You weave a silken thread through the noise in your mind, binding scattered thoughts into a single unbroken line. The chatter of doubt, the background hum of distraction — both fade as the thread draws taut, leaving you in razor-edged clarity for the task you've chosen.

Effect: Choose one non-combat mental skill — Lore, Slicing, Research, Arcana, or similar. Gain +20% to that skill for the scene. Only one Focus Thread may be active at a time.
spell_enc_focus_thread_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; +20% to one non-combat mental skill
spell_enc_fractured_will_desc-u:
You embed a persistent splinter of doubt and contradiction into the target's decision-making. It doesn't overwhelm them — it just slows them, a half-step behind every thought, second-guessing every choice before it lands. The longer it runs, the more exhausting the simplest decisions become.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target must spend a Maneuver at the start of each of their turns wrestling with the fracture before they can act. They still get their full turn but the Maneuver is consumed and cannot be used for anything else. In addition, at the start of each of the target's turns, they roll POW opposed by your original casting roll; on a failure that round the fracture flares and they also lose their Reaction. On a success against the initial casting roll, no effect. The fracture persists while you maintain concentration; if your concentration breaks, the effect ends immediately.
spell_enc_fractured_will_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene (Conc); target loses Maneuver at start of each turn; POW fail also loses Reaction
spell_enc_inspire_confidence_desc-u:
You reach out with a spark of assurance, kindling belief in someone's ability to meet the moment. It's more than encouragement — it's a deep, gut-level certainty that settles into the chest and holds steady under pressure.

Effect: Choose one ally within Short range. For the rest of the scene, they gain +10% to all non-combat social and mental skill rolls — Persuade, Charm, Deceive, Insight, Lore, Research, and any comparable skill at the GM's discretion. This effect does not stack with similar bonuses.
spell_enc_inspire_confidence_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene; ally gains +10% to all non-combat social and mental skill rolls
spell_enc_lingering_word_desc-u:
You whisper a single word into the target's mind, laced with emotional weight. Whether it's 'trust,' 'fear,' or 'doubt,' the word echoes in the background of their thoughts — ready to trip them when it surfaces.

Effect: Choose a single word. Whenever that word is spoken aloud during the scene — by anyone present — the target suffers a -10% penalty on their next roll. The effect ends if the scene concludes or the target becomes aware of the enchantment through Insight or magical detection.
spell_enc_lingering_word_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene; chosen word triggers -10% penalty on next roll when spoken
spell_enc_link_thread_desc-u:
You cast an invisible filament from your mind to another, then another, until the weave forms a quiet circle of shared thought. No words pass, no lips move — only fragments of emotion, flickers of images, and impressions sharp enough to feel real.

Effect: You and up to four willing creatures within Medium range gain a private mental link for the remainder of the scene. Each participant may send one brief thought, image, or emotional impression to the group as a free action each round. The link does not allow mind reading, compel behavior, or transmit complex information — only short impressions and intent. Willing targets only; an unwilling target cannot be included.
spell_enc_link_thread_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium; Scene; telepathic link for up to 4 willing targets
spell_enc_loyalty_loop_desc-u:
You weave yourself into the folds of the target's emotional memory, sewing in threads of familiarity and shared trust. The bond feels old and weathered, as if you've been through storms together — despite never having met.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target treats you as a trusted friend for the scene. Mechanically this means they will not attack you or your allies unprovoked, will answer your direct questions honestly to the best of their knowledge, and will provide reasonable assistance within the limits of their personality, beliefs, and self-interest. They will not take actions that feel suicidal or completely contrary to their values, but within those limits they are genuinely cooperative. The effect ends early if you attack them directly or take an action that obviously contradicts the manufactured trust. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_loyalty_loop_notes-u: Contested (POW); Touch; Scene; target will not attack you/allies, answers honestly, provides reasonable aid
spell_enc_memory_ghost_desc-u:
You craft a moment in time that never happened and drop it into the target's past like a stone into water. The ripples spread instantly — the false memory fits snugly among the real ones, weighted with convincing emotion and sensory detail.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, implant a memory no longer than roughly one minute of subjective experience. The memory must be plausible — it cannot contradict things the target knows with certainty, and it cannot involve people or places the target has never encountered. The effect ends early if the target is confronted with irrefutable contradictory evidence. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_memory_ghost_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene; implant plausible false memory
spell_enc_mind_lash_desc-u:
You craft a single, barbed thought and hurl it into the target's consciousness — pure, alien interference that scrambles their neural pathways for a heartbeat. The pain is real, searing through the skull and leaving the victim blinking away phantom light. It doesn't bruise the body. It exhausts the mind.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target takes 1d8 Strain damage. This damage bypasses all Soak — no armor or physical resistance applies because it is not a physical attack. This spell has no effect on creatures without a POW stat, including drones, turrets, and constructs. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_mind_lash_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; instant; 1d8 Strain damage; creatures only
spell_enc_mindfire_burst_desc-u:
You do not try to manipulate anything. You simply detonate — a short-range pulse of raw psychic force that hits whatever it touches without asking whether it has a mind to deceive. Constructs feel it as a surge of hostile interference. Creatures feel it as a sudden, brutal pressure behind the eyes. Either way, it hurts.

Effect: One target in Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage. This spell deals Veil damage and works against constructs, drones, turrets, and any target a Veil attack would normally affect. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_mindfire_burst_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; instant; 1d10 Veil damage; works on constructs
spell_enc_mirror_mood_desc-u:
A ripple of resonance aligns the target's emotional tone with yours. Smile, and they brighten. Frown, and their mood turns dark. It's a quiet puppeteering, made natural by the way people instinctively mirror those they feel connected to.

Effect: The target mirrors your outward emotional state for the scene. In social scenes, this grants +20% to Persuade rolls against them as their emotional alignment makes you easier to believe. In combat, you may feign fearlessness or composure to affect their judgment at the GM's discretion.
spell_enc_mirror_mood_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene; target mirrors caster's emotions; +20% Persuade vs them
spell_enc_misdirect_desc-u:
With a flicker of will, you bend the target's sense of priority — pulling their hostility a step to the side. A blow meant for you shifts toward another, or a spell veers toward an unintended foe. Subtle enough that it might look like an honest mistake.

Effect: When a creature within Short range declares an attack or harmful spell against you, you may cast this as a Reaction. The creature must succeed on a POW roll opposed by your casting roll or redirect their action to a different valid target they can see. The new target must be a valid target for the original action. On a success, the original action proceeds as intended.
spell_enc_misdirect_notes-u: Reaction; Contested (POW); Short; redirect hostile action to different valid target
spell_enc_neural_dissonance_desc-u:
A subtle twist in micro-expressions, body language, and emotional cadence shifts the target's presence ever so slightly off-key. Their words land wrong, their tone feels mismatched, and their timing is just — off. To others, it's nothing they can point to. Just an unshakable feeling that something about this person is wrong.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, anyone interacting with the target for the rest of the scene feels an instinctive unease or mistrust, imposing a penalty die on the target's social skill rolls for the duration. NPCs will be harder to persuade or charm. Allies may grow uncertain of the target's reliability at the GM's discretion. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_neural_dissonance_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; Scene; target appears untrustworthy; social setbacks
spell_enc_obsession_thread_desc-u:
With a deft flick of will, you slip a hook into the target's mind and anchor it to an idea, person, or object. The hook glows with alluring certainty, pulling their attention back no matter how they try to look away.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target becomes fixated on a subject of your choosing — a person, object, or concept. They cannot fully ignore or disengage from that subject and may act irrationally to approach, defend, or pursue it. The fixation does not compel self-destructive behavior, but it consistently colors their decisions until the scene ends. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_obsession_thread_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; Scene; fixation on person/object/concept
spell_enc_passive_field_desc-u:
You exhale a pulse of quiet certainty that you are unimportant — so convincingly that even hostility feels misplaced. The target's attention slides off you like water over glass, their mind scrambling to justify why you aren't worth the trouble.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target cannot voluntarily take hostile actions against you for 1 round. They may still act, move, and target your allies — only direct hostility toward you specifically is denied. The effect ends immediately if you take any offensive action directly against that creature. Targeting other creatures does not break the effect. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_passive_field_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; 1 round; target cannot take hostile actions against you; ends if you target them
spell_enc_persona_fracture_desc-u:
You split the target's awareness into conflicting impulses, making them feel as though they are two different people arguing over what to do. The discord is paralyzing — both selves reach for control, and neither wins cleanly.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, choose whether the target loses their Action or their Maneuver for the round. They retain the other and may still take Reactions. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_persona_fracture_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; 1 round; target loses Action or Maneuver
spell_enc_psychic_undertow_desc-u:
You release a pulse of raw mental pressure that radiates outward, dragging at the Strain reserves of every mind it touches simultaneously. Against a room full of Users, it is devastating. Against a single target, it is a calculated trade. Against anything without a mind, it has no effect whatsoever.

Effect: All creatures with a POW stat within Short range roll POW opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, each affected creature takes 1d6 Strain damage. This damage bypasses all Soak. This spell has no effect on constructs, drones, turrets, or anything without a POW stat. The power of this spell scales with the number of targets caught in the burst.
spell_enc_psychic_undertow_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short burst; instant; 1d6 Strain AoE; creatures only
spell_enc_slip_of_the_tongue_desc-u:
You plant a seed in the target's mind — a sudden need to fill the silence with truth. The words spill out before they can think to stop them, raw and unpolished, like a thought spoken by accident.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target involuntarily blurts out an honest answer to one direct question you ask. The answer is quick and unconsidered, rarely more than a sentence or two. The target is aware something just happened but cannot immediately identify what. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_slip_of_the_tongue_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; instant; forces brief truthful answer
spell_enc_social_mask_desc-u:
A shimmer runs across your mannerisms — your posture shifts, your eyes carry a different weight, and your voice finds the perfect tone for the moment. It's not a physical disguise, but in the emotional spectrum you become the person others most want to trust, admire, or obey.

Effect: When you cast Social Mask, choose one of the following modes — Directed Persona: you gain +20% to Persuade, Charm, and Deceive rolls against a single chosen target for the remainder of the scene. If they realize they are being actively deceived, if you threaten them directly, or if you contradict their deeply held values, the effect ends immediately. Public Facade: you gain +10% to Persuade, Charm, and Deceive rolls against anyone who can hear or observe you for the remainder of the scene. You may swap modes once during the scene without recasting. This bonus does not stack with other effects granting bonuses to the same skills.
spell_enc_social_mask_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; +20% social vs one target or +10% broadly; mode swap once
spell_enc_soothe_mind_desc-u:
Your thoughts sink into another's mind like a warm current into cold water. The tension in their shoulders loosens, breathing evens, and the chaos in their gaze fades. Anger dissolves into reason; fear fades to stillness. For a moment, their inner storm clears enough for them to choose a different course.

Effect: You immediately remove the Panicked, Enraged, Shaken, or Distracted condition from a willing creature at Touch range. If cast on an unwilling target, they may resist with a POW roll opposed by your casting roll.
spell_enc_soothe_mind_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove Panicked, Enraged, Shaken, or Distracted
spell_enc_subtle_echo_desc-u:
You plant a faint repetition of the target's own doubts just behind their thoughts — a ghost of their own voice, barely audible but impossible to ignore. The hesitation it causes is brief, but enough to cost them precision at a critical moment.

Effect: The target takes a -20% penalty to their next attack or defense roll before the end of the round. This penalty applies to the first qualifying roll made and then ends. Because you are nudging their own doubt rather than actively contesting their will, no resistance roll is required.
spell_enc_subtle_echo_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium; 1 round; -20% to target's next attack or defense roll
spell_enc_suggestion_virus_desc-u:
You seed the target's thoughts with an idea so subtle they'll never notice it's not their own. It doesn't bark orders; it whispers possibilities until they seem like common sense.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target adopts a plausible belief for the scene — something like 'he's probably telling the truth,' 'we should leave now,' or 'that person seems trustworthy.' The belief must be internally consistent and cannot force self-harm, blatantly irrational acts, or behavior that directly contradicts the target's deepest held convictions. It functions as a quietly held assumption that colors their decisions throughout the scene. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_suggestion_virus_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene; implants plausible belief
spell_enc_synaptic_lash_desc-u:
A filament of psychic force snakes invisibly toward the target, slipping between neurons and shorting the connections that allow decisive action. It's less pain and more a sudden, dizzying collapse of certainty — the feeling of reaching for a thought and finding nothing there.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target takes 1d10 Strain damage and loses their Reaction this round. This damage bypasses all Soak and is not reduced by any armor or physical resistance. This spell has no effect on creatures without a POW stat. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_synaptic_lash_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; instant; 1d10 Strain damage; lose Reaction; creatures only
spell_enc_thought_leak_desc-u:
You set up a siphon in the upper layers of the target's mind, drawing away whatever bubbles closest to the surface. It's not memory theft — it's listening to the hum of their consciousness while they speak to you.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, you can read the target's surface thoughts whenever they speak directly to you for the rest of the scene. You receive their immediate emotional state, current intent, and any thought pressing hard enough against consciousness to be close to speech. Silence, unconsciousness, or magical shielding blocks the effect. You may not ask for specific memories or reach deeper than the present moment. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_thought_leak_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene; read surface thoughts during interaction
spell_enc_turn_the_crowd_desc-u:
A few words, a subtle gesture, the perfect pitch of tone — your influence ripples outward like a pebble dropped in still water. What begins as a nudge toward agreement grows into a quiet swell of approval, binding the small group's mood in your favor.

Effect: You affect up to five creatures within Medium range. Each must succeed on a POW roll opposed by your casting roll or become predisposed toward you for the remainder of the scene, granting +10% to all social rolls you make against them. Creatures that succeed are unaffected and do not know the attempt was made.
spell_enc_turn_the_crowd_notes-u: Contested (POW); Medium; Scene; up to 5 targets predisposed toward you
spell_enc_veil_of_rapport_desc-u:
Your words carry the exact inflection the target wants to hear, your body language echoing the gestures of someone who understands them completely. It feels to them as if you've shared a hundred private conversations before this moment — as though you know the shape of their soul.

Effect: On a failed Insight roll opposed by your casting roll, the target perceives you as someone who deeply understands them. You gain +20% to Persuade, Charm, and Deceive rolls against them for the rest of the scene. The effect ends early if you contradict their core values, threaten them directly, or are caught in a clear lie. On a success, no effect.
spell_enc_veil_of_rapport_notes-u: Contested (Insight); Short; Scene; +20% Persuade, Charm, Deceive vs target
spell_ill_blur_desc-u:
You distort your visual profile for a single moment — just long enough to make the shot difficult. Not invisibility, not concealment. A shimmer of uncertainty at the exact moment someone has you in their sights.

Effect: The next attack targeting you before the start of your next turn suffers 1 penalty die. Declare after an attacker announces their attack but before they roll. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty. The effect ends after one attack resolves against you or at the start of your next turn, whichever comes first.
spell_ill_blur_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Reflexive; next attack targeting you this round suffers 1 penalty die
spell_ill_echo_chamber_desc-u:
You fill a space with layered false sounds — overlapping voices, mechanical noise, ambient distortion — until the real audio in the room is indistinguishable from fabricated signal. Communication becomes unreliable. Tactical coordination based on sound breaks down. The ears stop being useful.

Effect: Create a Short-radius zone of false audio within Short range. While inside the zone, all hearing-based INT rolls — including listening for enemies, identifying sounds, and following verbal orders — suffer 2 penalty dice. Communication within the zone is not prevented but becomes unreliable. Requires concentration to maintain.
spell_ill_echo_chamber_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium zone; Scene (Conc); all hearing-based INT rolls inside suffer 2 penalty dice
spell_ill_false_face_desc-u:
Your appearance shifts — not physically, but perceptually. A different face, a different voice, a different bearing. It does not fool biometric scanners or arcane signature readers, but it fools eyes and ears with enough conviction to hold up under casual scrutiny. More than casual scrutiny requires more than casual deception.

Effect: You take on a chosen illusory appearance, including visual features, voice, and approximate size. The disguise holds against observation from a distance and casual interaction. Any creature that interacts with you directly and suspiciously — close conversation, scrutiny, physical contradiction — may roll Insight opposed by your casting roll. On a success, they identify the disguise. On a failure, they accept the appearance as genuine. The disguise does not replicate biometric data, arcane signatures, or cyberware interfaces, and does not survive physical contact that reveals the contradiction.
spell_ill_false_face_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; illusory disguise; contested Insight to see through on direct interaction
spell_ill_false_trail_desc-u:
You leave behind evidence of a path you did not take — footprints heading the wrong direction, disturbed surfaces suggesting movement through a different corridor, the faint residue of presence somewhere you weren't. It is not a perfect deception. It is a delay, and sometimes a delay is enough.

Effect: As you move, you leave a convincing illusory trail in a direction of your choice rather than the direction you actually traveled. Any creature attempting to track you rolls Tracking at -20% to identify the correct path. The false trail persists for the scene or until a tracker succeeds on their Tracking roll, at which point it is recognized as fabricated.
spell_ill_false_trail_notes-u: Uncontested; utility; trackers following you roll Tracking at -20%; scene duration
spell_ill_flicker_step_desc-u:
As you move, a brief afterimage of yourself lingers at your origin point — the same posture, the same silhouette, present for exactly long enough to catch someone's swing or shot in the wrong place.

Effect: When you take a movement action or Maneuver that would trigger an attack of opportunity, the AoO targets your afterimage instead of you and misses automatically. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty. The afterimage disperses immediately after the AoO resolves or if no AoO is triggered.
spell_ill_flicker_step_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Reflexive; AoO triggered by your movement targets afterimage; misses automatically
spell_ill_ghost_sound_desc-u:
A sound appears at a point in the distance — a voice, a weapon cycling, a door handle turning, boots on stairs. It is entirely convincing and entirely wrong. Trained people respond to sounds before they think about them. That response costs them something.

Effect: Create a brief, convincing sound at any point within Long range. Any creature within Short of that point rolls Listen opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the creature must spend its next Maneuver moving toward or responding to the false stimulus. If the creature has already used its Maneuver this turn, it instead suffers 1 penalty die on its next action as its attention splits between the false stimulus and its current objective. On a success, the creature recognizes the sound as false and is unaffected. The event is instantaneous and does not sustain.
spell_ill_ghost_sound_notes-u: Contested (Listen); Long; distraction sound; fail = Maneuver spent investigating
spell_ill_glamour_field_desc-u:
You project a perceptual overlay across a Short-radius area — making everyone inside appear to be something they are not. A crew of Ghosts moves through a secure floor looking like maintenance workers, corp security, or empty air. The effect is broad and sustained. What it requires is that no one looks too closely.

Effect: Create a Short-radius glamour field within Short range. Choose one designation — a specific group identity such as maintenance crew, security personnel, or simply empty space. While inside the field, all creatures within it appear to match that designation to outside observers. Creatures outside the field who observe it roll INT opposed by your casting roll — on a failure they accept the glamour. On a success they see through it. Creatures inside the field are aware of the effect. Requires concentration to maintain. Physical interaction that contradicts the glamour breaks the illusion for the interacting creature.
spell_ill_glamour_field_notes-u: Contested (INT); Short radius; Scene (Conc); all creatures inside appear as designated
spell_ill_identity_anchor_desc-u:
You lock a false identity into place across multiple simultaneous observers — not just appearing as someone else, but being perceived as that person by an entire room at once. The illusion is more than visual. It carries presence, history, and the specific weight of familiarity. People do not merely see someone they do not recognize. They see someone they do.

Effect: Choose a false identity — a specific person, a type, or a role. Up to six creatures within Short range roll Insight opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature perceives you as the designated identity for the rest of the scene, treating you with whatever social regard that identity would receive. On a success, the creature sees through the overlay and perceives you normally. Creatures that fail cannot be re-tested unless something physically contradicts the illusion. Does not replicate biometric data or arcane signatures.
spell_ill_identity_anchor_notes-u: Contested (Insight); Scene; up to 6 creatures perceive you as false identity
spell_ill_invisibility_desc-u:
You bend light around yourself completely. Not a shimmer, not a distortion — absence. You are simply not there to see. Trained observers may catch displaced air, the sound of breathing, or the indent of your footstep, but their eyes report nothing. Shimmer Veil is a partial version of this. This is the real thing.

Effect: You become invisible to all natural and cybernetic visual senses for the scene while you maintain concentration. Creatures that cannot see you may still detect your presence through hearing, arcane sensing, or Veil Sight. While invisible, you gain +20% to Stealth and +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls. Attacks targeting you suffer 2 penalty dice — though attackers who have accurately located you by non-visual means only suffer 1 penalty die. You may not take more than one move Maneuver per turn — taking a second move Maneuver in the same turn breaks the light-bending pattern and the spell ends immediately. Ends immediately if you make an attack, deal damage, or cast an offensive spell.
spell_ill_invisibility_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); cannot be seen by natural or cybernetic senses; ends on offensive action
spell_ill_light_bend_desc-u:
You wrap a small object in bent light, making it visually indistinguishable from its surroundings. The object is still there. It still has mass, still makes sound if disturbed, still occupies space. It simply cannot be seen.

Effect: One small object at Touch range becomes visually invisible for the scene. The object must be no larger than something that could be held in two hands. It can still be found by touch, sound, or arcane detection. This spell does not prevent the object from being interacted with, only from being seen. Useful for hiding placed items, concealing tools mid-operation, or masking evidence. This spell cannot be cast on a creature or on an object being actively held by another creature.
spell_ill_light_bend_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene; make small object visually invisible
spell_ill_mass_invisibility_desc-u:
The same working as Invisibility, extended outward to cover the team. Each person becomes their own absence. The cost of holding four separate light-bending fields simultaneously is substantial — this is one of the most draining spells in the school.

Effect: Up to four willing creatures within Short range become invisible, each following the same rules as Invisibility. Each ally's invisibility ends individually if they make an attack, deal damage, cast an offensive spell, or take more than one move Maneuver in the same turn. Other allies remain invisible when one individual's invisibility ends. The caster's concentration sustains all active fields simultaneously — if concentration breaks, all fields collapse at once. Each invisible ally gains +20% to Stealth and +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls. Attacks targeting them suffer 2 penalty dice, reduced to 1 penalty die for attackers who have located them by non-visual means.
spell_ill_mass_invisibility_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene (Conc); up to 4 allies invisible; each ends individually on offensive action
spell_ill_mimicry_desc-u:
Your voice becomes whoever you need it to be. A supervisor's clipped authority. The specific timbre of a contact. An alarm tone that matches the facility's system exactly. You have heard it before. That is the only requirement.

Effect: For the scene, your voice can perfectly reproduce any voice, sound, or audio pattern you have previously heard. This includes specific individuals, system sounds, alarms, announcements, or ambient audio. The reproduction is exact enough to fool listeners who know the original source. Creatures with specific reason to be suspicious may roll Listen opposed by your casting roll to identify the mimicry. This spell does not alter your physical appearance, only your voice and any sounds you intentionally produce.
spell_ill_mimicry_notes-u: Uncontested; utility; perfectly reproduce any previously heard voice or sound; Listen only if suspicious; scene
spell_ill_mirror_walk_desc-u:
Mirrors are not surfaces — they are thresholds wearing the disguise of surfaces. You have always known this. You step into the reflection and emerge from another, somewhere else, and for a moment the city's geometry yields to a shortcut that has no physical explanation.

Effect: Step into any reflective surface at Engaged range — a mirror, a window, a still pool, polished metal — and emerge from any other reflective surface within Medium range that you can perceive or that you know the location of. This movement is instantaneous, does not provoke attacks of opportunity, and bypasses physical barriers between the two surfaces. The destination surface must be large enough to accommodate your body. If no valid reflective surface exists within Medium range, the spell has no effect and the Strain is still spent.
spell_ill_mirror_walk_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; instant; step into reflection and emerge from any reflective surface within Medium; no AoO
spell_ill_obscure_desc-u:
You layer visual distortion across a zone — subtle bending of light, conflicting shadows, peripheral movement that dissolves when looked at directly. Nothing appears obviously wrong. Everything is slightly harder to parse. Details become uncertain. Distances mislead. It is less a wall and more a persistent grain of doubt in every pair of eyes inside it.

Effect: Create a Short-radius zone of visual distortion within Short range. All INT rolls made to perceive, identify, or target something inside the zone suffer 1 penalty die. Ranged attacks targeting a creature inside the zone from outside also suffer 1 penalty die. Requires concentration to maintain. You and designated allies are unaffected.
spell_ill_obscure_notes-u: Uncontested; Short zone; Scene (Conc); all INT perception rolls inside suffer 1 penalty die
spell_ill_phantom_decoy_desc-u:
You leave an image of yourself standing where you were — same posture, same silhouette, same clothing. Then you move. Anyone targeting you has to figure out which one is real before they commit to the shot. And at the end of the round, you can make the question harder to answer by becoming the one they already decided wasn't you.

Effect: Create a stationary illusory copy of yourself at your current position. You may immediately move up to one range band without provoking attacks of opportunity. Until the start of your next turn, any attack or targeted ability directed at you must first succeed on an INT roll opposed by your casting roll to identify you as the real target. On a failure, the action targets the decoy and misses or has no effect automatically. On a success, the action resolves normally against you. At the end of your next turn, before the decoy disperses, you may choose to instantly swap positions with it. This movement is instantaneous and is not a move Maneuver. The decoy disperses at the start of your next turn whether or not the swap is taken, or after one attack resolves against it, whichever comes first.
spell_ill_phantom_decoy_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; 1 round; illusory copy at current position; attacks must beat INT; optional swap at end of turn
spell_ill_phantom_flank_desc-u:
A figure appears at the target's flank — solid-seeming, threatening, appearing from nowhere in their sightline. It does not need to fool them for long. It needs to cost them half a second. The Veil does not care what face it uses. It uses whatever is loudest in the moment.

Effect: One creature within Short range rolls INT opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the target loses their Reaction this round and suffers 1 penalty die on their next action before the end of their next turn — their attention has split in the wrong direction at the wrong moment. On a success, no effect. The figure is instantaneous and requires no maintenance. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_ill_phantom_flank_notes-u: Contested (INT); Short; figure appears at target's flank; fail = lose Reaction + 1 penalty die next action
spell_ill_phantom_space_desc-u:
You change what a room appears to be. Not entirely — not to someone who knows it well and looks carefully — but enough to redirect, delay, and misdirect. The room that looks empty discourages entry. The corridor that appears to dead-end reroutes movement. The space that looks different from what it is buys time.

Effect: Choose one area within Short range up to the size of a large room. Choose one of the following overlays: the space appears empty of occupants, the space appears occupied and actively patrolled, a passage appears impassable or sealed, or the space appears to be an entirely different room or environment. Creatures entering or observing the space roll INT opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, they accept the overlay and make decisions accordingly. On a success, they see through it. Creatures inside the illusion when it is cast are not affected — only those observing or entering from outside. Requires concentration to maintain.
spell_ill_phantom_space_notes-u: Contested (INT); Short; Scene (Conc); room appears occupied, empty, impassable, or different
spell_ill_psychosomatic_strike_desc-u:
You project a hallucination so complete and so precisely targeted that the body responds to it as though it were physical reality. The mind reports damage. The nervous system fires accordingly. The body believes what the eyes insist is happening, and belief at this level of intensity has physical consequences. This is not subtle. This is weaponized conviction.

Effect: One creature within Short range rolls POW opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 2d6 Veil damage as their body physically responds to perceived trauma. This damage bypasses Physical Soak — the harm is psychosomatic, originating inside the target's own nervous system rather than from an external force. On a successful resistance, no effect. This spell resists with POW rather than INT because it crosses from perceptual deception into direct psychological assault — the mind is not being fooled, it is being overwhelmed.
spell_ill_psychosomatic_strike_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; 2d6 Veil; body responds to perceived trauma; bypasses Physical Soak
spell_ill_sensory_ghost_desc-u:
A full-sensory false event appears at a point within Medium range — a figure crossing a doorway, a sound and its source together, movement and the visual confirmation of movement. It is more complete than Ghost Sound and more immediate than a sustained construct. It is gone almost before it registers, leaving behind only the decision to investigate.

Effect: Create a brief full-sensory false event — combining visual, auditory, and if appropriate tactile stimuli — at any point within Medium range. Any creature within Short of that point rolls Perception opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the creature treats the event as real and must spend its next Maneuver moving toward or responding to the false stimulus. On a success, the creature identifies it as fabricated and is unaffected. The event is instantaneous and does not sustain.
spell_ill_sensory_ghost_notes-u: Contested (Perception); Medium; full-sensory false event; fail = drawn to investigate
spell_ill_shatter_mirage_desc-u:
You detonate one of your own illusions deliberately — collapsing the light-structure in a sudden burst rather than letting it fade. The result is a flash of disorienting visual noise that carries the full weight of the Veil energy that was sustaining the construct releasing all at once.

Effect: Trigger this as a Reaction when one of your active Illusion spells or illusory constructs is about to end, is dispelled, or when you choose to collapse it. All creatures within Short range of the illusion's location become Disoriented until the end of their next turn as the sudden perceptual collapse overwhelms their visual processing. This does not require a contested roll — the disorientation is environmental, caused by the burst itself rather than a targeted effect. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_ill_shatter_mirage_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Reflexive; detonate your own illusion; Disoriented to all in Short of it
spell_ill_shimmer_veil_desc-u:
Not invisibility — something more precise. You bend light around yourself in a way that makes your outline shimmer and slip rather than vanish completely. Move slowly, stay in shadow, control your breathing — and you become extremely difficult to track visually. Move fast, cross open ground in bright light, and the cloak loses its conviction. It rewards discipline, the way all good concealment does.

Effect: For the scene, you gain +20% to Stealth rolls. Ranged attacks targeting you suffer 1 penalty die as your outline is difficult to fix precisely. This effect requires concentration to maintain. It ends immediately if you make an attack or cast an offensive spell — the sudden motion breaks the light-bending pattern. Unlike Invisibility, you remain partially visible — a determined observer who knows where to look can see you.
spell_ill_shimmer_veil_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); +20% Stealth; ranged attacks suffer 1 penalty die; ends on offensive action
spell_ill_terror_image_desc-u:
You reach into the target's personal frequency of dread and give it a face. Not a random monster — the specific thing, the correct face, the right context. The Veil reads fear the way it reads everything else: as information. You just decide what to do with the information. The effect is not simply visual. It is the full weight of what the target already carries, suddenly present and aimed at them.

Effect: One creature within Short range rolls POW opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the target gains the Frightened condition for 2 rounds — unable to willingly move toward you and suffering a penalty die on all actions while affected. On a success, no effect. This spell resists with POW because it bypasses the senses entirely and strikes at what the target already fears — it is psychological pressure, not perceptual deception.
spell_ill_terror_image_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; personalized fear image; fail = Frightened 2 rounds + penalty die while affected
spell_ill_unreal_terrain_desc-u:
You overlay false terrain across a Medium area — a wall that does not exist, a floor that appears to drop away, cover that looks solid but yields to a hand, obstacles that are not there. The illusion is tactile as well as visual, right up until the moment someone walks through where the wall should be and discovers it isn't. That moment of discovery costs them something too.

Effect: Choose a Medium area within Medium range. Fill it with illusory terrain of your choice — false walls, illusory drops, phantom cover, or any other environmental feature. Creatures who have not physically tested the terrain treat it as real for all movement and tactical decisions. A creature that physically interacts with illusory terrain — attempts to touch it, walk through it, or fire through supposed cover — immediately discovers the deception and is unaffected for the rest of the scene. Creatures that succeeded on their INT roll against this spell already know the terrain is false. Requires concentration to maintain.
spell_ill_unreal_terrain_notes-u: Contested (INT); Medium area; Scene (Conc); illusory terrain treated as real until physical interaction
spell_ill_veil_cloak_desc-u:
You extend the same light-bending discipline of Shimmer Veil to an ally — sharing the working outward from your focus to cover another body moving through the same space. Holding it on someone else costs more than holding it on yourself.

Effect: Choose one willing ally within Touch range. For the scene, they gain +20% to Stealth rolls and attacks targeting them from beyond Short range suffer 1 penalty die. This effect requires your concentration to maintain. It ends for that ally immediately if they make an attack or cast an offensive spell. You may only maintain one Veil Cloak at a time — casting it again replaces the previous instance.
spell_ill_veil_cloak_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene (Conc); one ally +20% Stealth; attacks from beyond Short suffer 1 penalty die
spell_ill_veil_silence_desc-u:
You mask the arcane signature of your spellcasting — the ozone, the light distortion, the Veil resonance that trained observers and detection systems use to identify an active User. Your spells still work. They simply leave no trace that they were cast by anything at all.

Effect: For the scene, your spellcasting produces no visible arcane signature. The light distortions, Veil resonance, and atmospheric effects that normally accompany casting are suppressed. Your spells cannot be detected by Detect Magic, Resonant Echo, or similar magical detection during casting — though the effects of the spells themselves remain visible if they produce visible effects. Spellprints are still left if the spell is powerful enough — Veil Silence masks the act of casting, not the consequence of it. This spell does not require concentration, leaving your focus free for other sustained effects.
spell_ill_veil_silence_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; no concentration; spellcasting leaves no visible arcane signature; immune to Detect Magic
spell_ill_ward_bleed_desc-u:
You make your presence read as authorized — as belonging — to whatever detection system is watching the threshold. The ward does not trigger. The sensor does not flag. The pattern of your arrival matches the pattern of something that is supposed to be there.

Effect: Choose one ward, security sensor, arcane detection threshold, or similar monitoring system you are about to cross. Make a contested roll against the system's Security Rating or the Arcana skill of any living observer monitoring it. On a success, your crossing registers as authorized — no alert, no flag, no memory of the event in the system's log. On a failure, you are detected normally. This applies to a single threshold crossing — each new barrier requires a new casting. Does not work against physical barriers, only detection and alerting systems.
spell_ill_ward_bleed_notes-u: Contested (Security Rating or Arcana); single threshold; presence reads as authorized to ward or detection system
spell_nec_bone_grasp_desc-u:
You push just enough animating force into the dead tissue nearby to make it grab. Not raised — just briefly compelled. The corpse doesn't think. It holds. That's enough.

Effect: Choose one enemy within Short range that is adjacent to a corpse or significant amount of dead organic matter — a body, a pile of bones, severed limbs. Make a contested roll vs the target's STR. On a failure, the target is Restrained until the start of your next turn as dead tissue locks around them. The corpse is not raised and does not count against Pale Sovereignty — it does not act, cannot be given commands, and returns to inert once the effect ends. On a successful resistance, no effect.
spell_nec_bone_grasp_notes-u: Contested (STR); Short; animate dead limbs to Restrain enemy for 1 round
spell_nec_corpse_read_desc-u:
You press your hand to a corpse and read what it remembers. Not thought — impression. The body keeps records the mind never knew it was making. Time of death. Cause. The last thing it felt, heard, or feared. A Gravesinger learns to read these quickly and without sentiment.

Effect: Touch a corpse and immediately learn its condition — Fresh or Decayed as defined by the Corpse Condition system. You also learn the approximate time since death, the primary cause of death, and up to two strong sensory or emotional impressions from the final moments — what the creature last saw, heard, or felt with significant intensity. This is not full communication and does not access memories from before the final moments. For deeper communication with a death imprint, see Grave Voice or Pale Communion.
spell_nec_corpse_read_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; forensic death reading; cause, time, condition, last impressions
spell_nec_death_knell_strike_desc-u:
You reach into the target's life-pattern and apply everything you know about what ends things. Not a drain — a strike. Focused, deliberate, and expensive. The amount of Veil energy required to do this properly leaves your hands numb afterward.

Effect: One living creature within Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 3d6 Veil damage. On a successful resistance, no effect. This spell has no effect on constructs, drones, spirits, or the undead.
spell_nec_death_knell_strike_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 3d6 Veil damage; living creatures only
spell_nec_death_sense_desc-u:
You read the death-resonance of a living target — how close they are to the threshold, what is already pulling at them from the other side. A Gravesinger who has cultivated this sense does not need to count HP. They can feel when a target is close to breaking.

Effect: Choose one living creature within Short range. You immediately learn whether the target is currently Dying or below 25% of their maximum HP, whether they are currently under an active curse or entropy effect, and whether they have been targeted by a Necromancy spell this scene. This spell reveals state, not exact values — you know they are critically low, not the precise number.
spell_nec_death_sense_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; read target's proximity to death, HP state, and curse status
spell_nec_death_ward_desc-u:
You wrap yourself in a layer of death-attuned Veil energy — the resonance of entropy turned inward as a shield. Incoming force meets the frequency of its own opposite and loses coherence. It is not comfortable to maintain. The sensation of holding death-energy close for a sustained period leaves marks.

Effect: For the scene, you gain +2 Veil Soak. Requires concentration to maintain. This is the pure defensive version of the school's self-protection options — where Necrotic Shroud punishes attackers, Death Ward simply reduces what they can do to you.
spell_nec_deathmark_sight_desc-u:
Death leaves receptors behind. You push your perception through the nearest one — a corpse's eyes that still hold their last image, a skull's hollow sockets still oriented toward the thing that killed it. You see what they saw last. You stay as long as you need. And when you reach out to shape the Veil, you reach from where they are — not where you are.

Effect: Choose any corpse within Medium range that you can perceive. For the scene while you maintain concentration, you may switch your perception to see and hear through that corpse's position. Your own body continues to stand where it is — blind and deaf to its immediate surroundings while attuned. You may switch back to your own senses as a free action. While attuned to a corpse, you may use the corpse's position rather than your own body's position for the range and line of sight requirements of any spell you cast — allowing you to see around corners, cast over barriers, or target creatures that would otherwise be out of your personal line of sight. You still roll your own skill for any spell cast this way. Spells requiring Touch still require your physical body to be present at the target — the corpse cannot substitute for physical contact. Line of sight from the corpse's position must be clear and unobstructed for this benefit to apply. You may only attune to one corpse at a time.
spell_nec_deathmark_sight_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium; Scene (Conc); see and cast through any perceived corpse's position; Touch spells still require physical presence
spell_nec_deaths_embrace_desc-u:
You stop holding death at arm's length and let it in. Not far enough to do harm — just far enough that fear stops making sense. Death has already touched you. What is there left to be afraid of? The answer reshapes how you move through a fight.

Effect: For the scene, you are immune to the Frightened condition and to any fear-based effect regardless of its source — spells, talents, environmental, or psychological. You gain +2 Veil Soak. All Veil damage dealt by your drain spells — Life Drain, Vitality Leech, Necrotic Touch, and Necrotic Pulse — gains +1d4 bonus damage per hit. Requires concentration to maintain. If your concentration breaks, all three benefits end immediately.
spell_nec_deaths_embrace_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); fear immunity; +2 Veil Soak; +1d4 to drain spell damage
spell_nec_deaths_intercession_desc-u:
The hit is coming. You reach sideways — into the life-force of something nearby — and pull just enough to soften the blow. The cost comes from someone else. It always does. Whether that someone else cooperates is a different matter.

Effect: When you would take damage, you may cast this as a Reaction. Choose one living creature within Short range — enemy or ally. That creature rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, it takes 1d6 Veil damage and your incoming damage is reduced by the same amount, applied before Soak. On a successful resistance, the creature takes no damage and your incoming damage is unaffected — though the Strain is still spent. An ally who is aware of the spell being cast may choose to voluntarily fail their resistance roll, willingly offering their life force to absorb the blow. If no valid living target is within Short range, the spell has no effect but the Strain is still spent. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty. The damage reduction is exempt from Healing Saturation.
spell_nec_deaths_intercession_notes-u: Reaction; Contested (CON); Short; drain 1d6 from nearby living creature on fail to reduce your incoming damage
spell_nec_entropic_curse_desc-u:
You lay a quiet curse into the target's pattern — one that closes off the body's natural recovery. The wound stays open. The fatigue accumulates. The body works harder and gets less for it. It does not hurt more. It just stops getting better.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target cannot recover HP through rest, passive regeneration, first aid, or any non-magical means for the rest of the scene. Magical healing from spells still functions normally. The curse does not deal damage — it prevents recovery. Combined with sustained pressure it becomes increasingly punishing as the scene progresses. On a successful resistance, no effect.
spell_nec_entropic_curse_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; Scene; target cannot recover HP through non-magical means
spell_nec_entropic_field_desc-u:
You project a field of necrotic entropy outward from your position — a low, persistent pressure that makes the body's defenses work against it. Hostile creatures inside feel their CON rolls slip, resistance checks soften, and the body's ability to push back against harm gradually undermines itself.

Effect: Create a Short-radius zone of entropic pressure centered on you that moves with you for the scene. All hostile living creatures within the zone suffer 1 penalty die on all CON rolls — including rolls to resist conditions, maintain consciousness, resist knockdown, and endure poisons or toxins. Requires concentration to maintain. You and designated allies are unaffected. Spirits, constructs, and the undead are unaffected by this field.
spell_nec_entropic_field_notes-u: Uncontested; Short zone; Scene (Conc); hostiles suffer 1 penalty die on CON rolls
spell_nec_entropy_cascade_desc-u:
Entropy is contagious when you push it hard enough. You drive it into the first body and let the resonance look for the next one — a chain of biological disruption that jumps from target to target until something resists long enough to stop it.

Effect: Choose a primary target within Short range. On a failed CON roll, the target takes 1d8 Veil damage. The entropy then cascades to up to two additional living creatures, each within Short range of the last struck creature. Each chained target rolls CON opposed by your casting roll — on a failure they take 1d6 Veil damage. The chain stops immediately at the first target that succeeds on their resistance roll. A given creature can only be struck once per casting. On a successful resistance by the primary target, no effect and the cascade does not trigger.
spell_nec_entropy_cascade_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d8 Veil primary; chains 1d6 to 2 additional targets; stops at first success
spell_nec_grave_voice_desc-u:
The dead have not forgotten everything. The last few minutes before death imprint more strongly than most of a lifetime — terror, recognition, betrayal, resolution. Grave Voice does not speak to a mind. It speaks to what the pattern retained when the mind stopped. The responses are fragmented, emotional, and often more honest than anything the person would have said alive.

Effect: Touch a corpse and receive fragmented communication from the death imprint — the residual pattern left in the physical form after death. You may ask up to three questions. The responses come as sensory impressions, emotional flashes, brief visual fragments, or single words rather than full sentences. The imprint can only answer what was present in the final moments — it has no access to earlier memories and cannot reason or elaborate. For full cognitive communication with a death imprint, see Pale Communion. This spell requires the corpse to be no more than 48 hours dead — surface emotional residue persists longer than deep cognitive memory, making Grave Voice viable in situations where Pale Communion no longer is.
spell_nec_grave_voice_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; fragmented death imprint communication; last moments and impressions
spell_nec_life_drain_desc-u:
You reach across the short distance between you and pull. Not hard — a sip rather than a theft. Enough to matter. The target feels something leave them. You feel something arrive.

Effect: One living creature within Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 1d8 Veil damage and you restore HP equal to half the damage dealt, rounded up. This healing is exempt from Healing Saturation — it is parasitic conversion, not restoration. On a successful resistance, no effect. Constructs, drones, spirits, and the undead cannot be targeted.
spell_nec_life_drain_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1d8 Veil drain; restore half to self; living only; exempt from Healing Saturation
spell_nec_mass_wither_desc-u:
You push entropy into every living body in reach simultaneously — a broad application of the same principle that Wither Flesh applies to one target. The effect is brief but the timing can be devastating, turning a coordinated enemy response into a stumble.

Effect: All living creatures within Short range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, a creature suffers 1 penalty die on all physical actions until the end of its next turn. On a successful resistance, no effect. Constructs, drones, spirits, and the undead are unaffected.
spell_nec_mass_wither_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short AoE; 1 penalty die on all physical actions 1 round; living only
spell_nec_necrotic_pulse_desc-u:
