A short, sharp release of necrotic force radiates outward from you — not directed at a single target but at everything living within reach. It is loud in the Veil even when it is silent in the room. Every living creature in range feels it as a sudden wrongness, like a cold hand pressed briefly against the back of the neck.

Effect: All living creatures within Short range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, a creature takes 1d6 Veil damage. On a successful resistance, no effect. Constructs, drones, spirits, and the undead are unaffected. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_nec_necrotic_pulse_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short burst AoE; 1d6 Veil; living creatures only
spell_nec_necrotic_shroud_desc-u:
You coat yourself in a thin layer of active necrotic force — not enough to see, just enough to bite. Anyone who strikes you in melee contacts it and pays for the proximity. Where Death Ward absorbs incoming force, Necrotic Shroud punishes those who generated it.

Effect: For the scene, whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack, unarmed strike, or physical combat maneuver, it takes 1d4 Veil damage. This triggers once per attacker per round and requires no roll — the damage is automatic on contact. Requires concentration to maintain.
spell_nec_necrotic_shroud_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene (Conc); melee attackers, unarmed strikers, and combat maneuver users take 1d4 Veil on hit
spell_nec_necrotic_touch_desc-u:
The baseline. You reach out and apply Necromancy directly — a focused discharge of entropic force at contact range. No drain, no secondary effect. Just damage, applied where you are already standing.

Effect: One creature within Engaged range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage. On a successful resistance, no effect. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_nec_pale_communion_desc-u:
Where Grave Voice catches fragments, Pale Communion opens a channel. The death imprint within the corpse can speak — not the person as they were in life, but the pattern they left behind, which retains cognition, memory, and the capacity to reason. It answers because it must. What it chooses to reveal is another matter.

Effect: Maintain sustained contact with a corpse for up to 10 minutes. For the duration, you can engage in full two-way communication with the death imprint retained in the physical remains. The imprint possesses all memories and knowledge the person had at the time of death and can reason, elaborate, and answer complex questions. It cannot lie about factual memories — the pattern does not permit distortion — but it can choose silence, deflect, or refuse to engage on topics where the person had strong emotional resistance. The imprint is not the person and should not be mistaken for one. It is a sophisticated echo. This spell requires the corpse to have been dead no more than 24 hours. The deep cognitive pattern degrades faster than surface emotional residue — once the window closes, the coherent self is no longer present to communicate with. For older remains where the cognitive pattern has faded, Grave Voice can still access surface impressions for up to 48 hours.
spell_nec_pale_communion_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; 10 minutes; full death-imprint communication with corpse
spell_nec_pale_fortitude_desc-u:
You push additional death-energy into the raised corpse's structure, reinforcing the animating force that holds it together. The corpse does not feel it. You do — a brief, uncomfortable surge of necrotic resonance as the energy passes through your hands into the physical form.

Effect: Touch a raised corpse under your command. It immediately gains +2 Physical Soak and +1d6 HP added to its current maximum for the scene. This effect persists for the scene and does not require concentration — your concentration remains on the raise itself. If the corpse is destroyed, the effect ends with it. A corpse may only benefit from one Pale Fortitude at a time.
spell_nec_pale_fortitude_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch (raised corpse); Scene; no concentration; +2 Physical Soak; +1d6 HP
spell_nec_pale_vigor_desc-u:
You pour sustained animating force into the raised corpse — not a brief surge but a steady reinforcement that holds for the duration. A Decayed corpse that shuffled and stumbled suddenly moves with the fluency it had in life. The body remembers what it was. You are simply reminding it loudly enough for it to listen.

Effect: Choose a raised corpse under your command within Short range. For the rest of the scene, the corpse acts as though Fresh regardless of its actual condition — it may use all skills at their original values and is no longer subject to Decayed capability restrictions. In addition, if the corpse is Decayed it immediately gains HP equal to half its original maximum HP rounded down — the same threshold a Fresh corpse begins with — replacing its current Decayed HP total if that total is lower. This effect does not require concentration — your concentration remains on the raise itself. A corpse may only benefit from one Pale Vigor at a time. This spell has no effect on a corpse that is already Fresh.
spell_nec_pale_vigor_notes-u: Uncontested; Short (raised corpse); Scene; no concentration; Decayed acts as Fresh; gains Fresh HP threshold
spell_nec_plague_touch_desc-u:
You introduce a strand of accelerated biological entropy into the target — not a wound from outside but a process from within. The body begins fighting itself on a cellular level. There is no armor for that. There is no Soak for what is already inside.

Effect: One living creature within Engaged range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target gains a plague effect that lasts 3 rounds. At the start of each of the target's turns while the plague is active, they take 1d4 Veil damage. This damage bypasses all Soak — Physical and Veil Soak both provide no reduction against it, because the damage originates from within the target's own biological pattern rather than from an external force. The plague does not require concentration — once applied, it runs its course regardless of what happens to you. On a successful resistance, no effect.
spell_nec_plague_touch_notes-u: Contested (CON); Engaged; 1d4 Veil DoT 3 rounds; bypasses all Soak; no concentration
spell_nec_soul_echo_desc-u:
The dead leave impressions everywhere they gather or pass. A battlefield carries the weight of its violence long after the bodies are cleared. A place where people have died peacefully feels different from one where they died in terror. A Gravesinger who opens this sense does not just see what is present — they feel what was. The ground remembers. So do the walls. So does the air above old graves.

Effect: For the scene while you maintain concentration, you gain two distinct perceptions simultaneously. First, you perceive spirits and unmanifest Veil presences within Short range as visible forms — not clearly, but as distinct shapes in your peripheral awareness, similar to Veil Sight but limited specifically to spirits, death echoes, and entities with a strong connection to death or the Veil. You cannot be surprised by spirits within this range and can perceive their approximate tier — Lesser, Bound, or Greater — and whether they are passive, agitated, or hostile. Second, you feel the emotional resonance imprinted on the space by significant death, trauma, or spiritual activity. A room where someone was murdered feels like the moment before it happened. A battlefield carries exhaustion, fear, and the particular weight of mass dying. The GM provides the emotional character of the space honestly — this sense does not lie, though it may be difficult to interpret. The intensity of the impression reflects the intensity and recency of what occurred there.
spell_nec_soul_echo_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene (Conc); perceive spirits as visible forms; feel emotional resonance of death and trauma
spell_nec_soul_shackle_desc-u:
Death is a threshold, not an instant. You reach into the passage and hold the door open — forcing the dying creature to remain on this side of it for one more round. A medic can reach them. A Soulmender can stabilize them. The window is narrow but it is real, and sometimes a single round is the difference between a funeral and a field dressing.

Effect: Choose one creature within Short range that is currently making death CON saves. That creature is held on the threshold for 1 round — all death checks cease and they cannot die from their current injuries during this round. They remain incapacitated but may receive HP restoration and stabilization normally. At the end of the round, if no further intervention has stabilized them, death checks resume. This spell is primarily a tool for buying time — a window for a medic or Restoration User to close distance and act before the body gives out.
spell_nec_soul_shackle_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; hold dying creature on threshold for 1 round; cannot recover HP
spell_nec_unhallowed_ground_desc-u:
You consecrate a patch of ground to death — infusing it with necrotic resonance that devours the life-force of anything living that lingers there. What is already dead finds it welcoming. What still lives finds the opposite.

Effect: Create a Short-radius zone of necrotic terrain within Short range. Any living creature that starts its turn inside the zone takes 1d4 Veil damage — no resistance roll, the terrain simply costs them. Any undead creature or animated corpse that starts its turn inside the zone — regardless of who controls it or whether it is allied or hostile — gains +1d4 bonus damage on its next melee or unarmed attack that turn. The zone is fixed at the point of casting and does not move. Requires concentration to maintain. You are not immune to the terrain damage and neither are your allies — position accordingly, and be aware that hostile undead benefit from the zone as readily as your own.
spell_nec_unhallowed_ground_notes-u: Uncontested; Short zone; Scene (Conc); 1d4 Veil/turn to living; +1d4 melee/unarmed to any undead inside
spell_nec_vitality_leech_desc-u:
Not a sip — a proper drain. You pull a substantial amount of life force from the target and redirect it where it is needed. The target feels the loss as a sudden, profound exhaustion. What you take goes somewhere more useful.

Effect: One living creature within Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 2d6 Veil damage and you restore HP equal to half the damage dealt, rounded up, to either yourself or one ally within Short range of your choice. This healing is exempt from Healing Saturation. On a successful resistance, no effect. Constructs, drones, spirits, and the undead cannot be targeted.
spell_nec_vitality_leech_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 2d6 Veil drain; restore half to self or ally; living only; exempt from Healing Saturation
spell_nec_wither_flesh_desc-u:
You push entropy into the target's musculature — a brief but complete degradation of physical function. The body resists its own commands. Movements become imprecise. Actions that should be automatic require effort. For one round the target operates at a meaningful disadvantage.

Effect: On a failed CON roll, the target suffers 1 penalty die on all physical actions until the end of its next turn. This includes attacks, movement-based maneuvers, and physical skill checks. On a successful resistance, no effect.
spell_nec_wither_flesh_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; 1 penalty die on all physical actions for 1 round
spell_nec_wither_spirit_desc-u:
Entropy does not care about the boundary between physical and spiritual. A spirit is a pattern — coherent, structured, and vulnerable to the same forces that unravel living things. You apply those forces directly to the spiritual form and watch it struggle to hold itself together.

Effect: Choose a spirit or Veil entity within Short range. On a failed POW roll, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage and suffers -20% to its next action involving focus, cohesion, movement, attack, or manifestation. On a successful resistance, no effect. This spell only affects spirits and Veil entities — it has no effect on living creatures or constructs.
spell_nec_wither_spirit_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; 1d10 Veil; -20% to target's next action; spirits and Veil entities only
spell_res_accelerate_mending_desc-u:
You weave a sustained thread of restorative force through the target's pattern, priming their biology to recover more aggressively during rest. The healing itself happens naturally — you simply give it direction and momentum. This is not combat medicine. This is what you do when the shooting has stopped and the team needs to be ready for what comes next.

Effect: This spell may only be cast outside of active combat during a rest period. The target's daily HP recovery for that rest is increased by one category — field rest becomes equivalent to shelter rest, shelter rest becomes equivalent to clinic rest, and clinic rest produces the maximum possible daily recovery. Healing Saturation applies normally. One target may benefit from one Accelerate Mending per rest period.
spell_res_accelerate_mending_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; out of combat only; increase target's daily HP recovery by one category
spell_res_body_reweave_desc-u:
The school's ultimate healing act. Not a fix — a complete restoration of the target's pattern to functional coherence, clearing every physical condition, every Major Wound, every injury that has been degrading their capacity to exist. You pay heavily for it. Your hands shake afterward. The target is whole, which is the only result that matters.

Effect: Remove all physical conditions from the target, including Major Wounds. If they are currently dying, they are stabilized and restored to 1d6 HP in addition to the condition removal. This spell does not restore full HP beyond the stabilization roll — it clears the damage status, not the damage total. Healing Saturation applies to the HP component if triggered. This spell cannot be cast on yourself — the process requires external focus to complete. Veil Corruption is not addressed by this spell — for corruption, see Deep Purge or Body Reweave combined with Veil Cleanse in sequence.
spell_res_body_reweave_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove all physical conditions including Major Wounds; stabilize if dying; Strain 1d12
spell_res_cleanse_influence_desc-u:
You restore the target's pattern against what a hostile spell has done to it — not by contesting the original working, but by undoing its grip on the person it affected.

Effect: Remove one of the following conditions from a willing target, provided it was caused by a spell or magical ability: Compelled, Dominated, Controlled, Frightened, Stunned, Immobilized (magical source), Restrained (magical source), Bound (magical source), active possession, or possession residue. If the effect is being actively sustained by a concentrating caster, make a contested Magic (Restoration) roll against their POW to remove it.
spell_res_cleanse_influence_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove one spell-caused intrusion: compulsions, Frightened, Stunned, magical restraints, or possession
spell_res_corruption_read_desc-u:
You open your arcane senses to the target's pattern and read what the Veil has done to it. This is diagnostic work — precise, clinical, and fast enough to do in the middle of an engagement when you need to know whether what you're looking at is corruption you can handle or something that will kill them regardless.

Effect: You scan one target within Short range. You immediately learn whether they are carrying any active Veil Corruption, its current stage — Minor, Moderate, Major, or Lethal — and its source category: ambient exposure, hostile spellwork, possession residue, or chronic contact. You also learn whether standard purging will be sufficient or whether more intensive intervention is required. The GM answers honestly within the limits of observable evidence. This spell does not remove or reduce corruption — it only reads it.
spell_res_corruption_read_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; instant; detect Veil Corruption type, severity, and source
spell_res_deep_purge_desc-u:
Where Veil Cleanse offers support for the body to expel surface contamination, Deep Purge reaches into the target's pattern and works at a deeper level — reinforcing the biological architecture against deeply embedded taint. The target still has to fight it out. You just give them considerably better odds.

Effect: The target immediately makes a CON roll with +2 bonus dice. On a success, their Veil Corruption is reduced by one step. On a Hard success, it is reduced by two steps instead. On a failure, the corruption does not reduce but the Strain is still spent. A target may only benefit from one corruption-reduction attempt per 24 hours regardless of outcome. This spell cannot remove corruption that the GM has determined to be permanent, mythic in origin, or rooted in a fundamental unmaking.
spell_res_deep_purge_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; target rolls CON +2 bonus dice; success = -1 corruption step; Hard success = -2 steps
spell_res_entropic_pulse_desc-u:
The same understanding that lets you reweave a damaged pattern lets you disrupt an intact one. You push biological coherence the wrong direction — a focused pulse of entropic force that tears at the living structure of a target rather than restoring it. It is not pleasant to cast. The understanding of what you are doing is uncomfortably clear.

Effect: One living creature within Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage as its biological pattern briefly destabilizes. On a success, no effect. This spell has no effect on constructs, drones, turrets, or the undead — it disrupts living biological coherence, which non-living targets do not possess.
spell_res_entropic_pulse_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; instant; 1d10 Veil damage; living creatures only
spell_res_entropic_wave_desc-u:
Entropic Pulse focused on one target. Entropic Wave released outward — a pulse of biological disruption that radiates in all directions from your position, hitting every living body in range simultaneously. The Soulmender who uses this spell has crossed a line in their own head. Most will cross it anyway when the situation demands it.

Effect: All living creatures within Short range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, a creature takes 2d6 Veil damage. On a success, no effect. This spell has no effect on constructs, drones, turrets, or the undead. Your allies are not exempt — use with positional awareness.
spell_res_entropic_wave_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short burst; instant; 2d6 Veil damage AoE; living creatures only
spell_res_field_stabilize_desc-u:
You reach into the pattern of a dying creature and force it to hold. Not healed — held. The thread between them and death is still thin, but it is no longer fraying. This buys time. Sometimes time is enough.

Effect: A dying or unconscious ally within Engaged range is immediately stabilized — all death checks cease. This does not restore HP, remove conditions, or bring them back to consciousness. They remain incapacitated but stable. Does not count toward Healing Saturation. If they take further damage, death checks may resume.
spell_res_field_stabilize_notes-u: Uncontested; Engaged; instant; stop death checks on dying ally; no HP restored
spell_res_fortify_body_desc-u:
You reinforce the target's biological resilience — not armor, not magical shielding, but a genuine hardening of the body's ability to resist hostile conditions. It makes them harder to poison, harder to stagger, harder to knock unconscious with a single catastrophic hit.

Effect: For the scene, the target gains +20% to all CON resistance rolls — including rolls to resist poisons, toxins, conditions, knockdown, unconsciousness, and Major Wound consequences. This bonus does not apply to damage rolls, only to resistance checks. One target may benefit from one Fortify Body at a time.
spell_res_fortify_body_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene; +20% to CON resistance rolls
spell_res_knit_bone_desc-u:
A fractured joint, a torn muscle, a dislocated shoulder — damage that doesn't register as HP loss but stops a fighter from functioning. You locate the specific physical disruption and force it back into coherence. The target will feel it. It is not comfortable. It works.

Effect: Remove the Impaired condition from a target when that condition was caused by a physical injury — broken limb, joint damage, muscle tear, dislocated bone, or similar trauma. This restores full limb function. It does not remove Impaired caused by spells, toxins, or non-physical sources. This spell does not restore HP — it specifically addresses the functional loss from physical structural damage.
spell_res_knit_bone_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove Impaired from a physical injury source; restore limb function
spell_res_life_leech_desc-u:
You reach into a living creature's pattern and take some of what it has. Not graceful. Not gentle. The target feels the pull like something important is being extracted — because it is. What you take becomes what someone else needs. The school does not encourage this. It does not prohibit it either.

Effect: One living creature within Short range rolls CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failed resistance, the target takes 2d6 Veil damage as life force is stripped from its pattern. Half the damage dealt, rounded up, is immediately restored as HP to either yourself or one ally within Short range of your choice. This healing is exempt from Healing Saturation — it is parasitic conversion, not restoration, and the body's saturation limit does not apply to energy drained from another living source. On a successful resistance, no effect. This spell has no effect on constructs, drones, or the undead.
spell_res_life_leech_notes-u: Contested (CON); Short; instant; drain 2d6 HP from living target; restore half to self or ally; exempt from Healing Saturation
spell_res_major_restoration_desc-u:
You commit fully to the work — not a quick patch, but a substantial reweaving of the target's damaged pattern. This is what you bring out when an ally is genuinely close to broken and the minor interventions are no longer adequate.

Effect: Restore 4d6 HP to one target you are touching. This healing is subject to Healing Saturation. Cannot be cast on yourself. This spell does not remove conditions, stabilize the dying, or address corruption — it restores HP and nothing else, but it does so at a scale no Common spell can match.
spell_res_major_restoration_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; restore 4d6 HP; subject to Healing Saturation
spell_res_mass_mend_desc-u:
You spread yourself thin — reaching outward to every ally in range simultaneously rather than concentrating on one. The individual restoration is lighter than a focused heal. The scope is what justifies the cost.

Effect: All allies within Short range immediately recover 2d4 HP each. Each individual's healing is subject to their own Healing Saturation threshold — a fully saturated ally gains nothing. This spell does not affect you. It does not remove conditions or address corruption. It is a field restoration — broad application, moderate per-target return.
spell_res_mass_mend_notes-u: Uncontested; Short (all allies); instant; restore 2d4 HP to each ally in range; subject to Healing Saturation
spell_res_mend_wounds_desc-u:
The baseline. The spell every Restoration User knows first and casts most. Tissue knits, bleeding slows, and the body's pattern reasserts itself toward coherence. It is not dramatic. It is the reason people bring a Soulmender.

Effect: Restore 2d6 HP to one target you are touching. This healing is subject to Healing Saturation. May be cast on yourself.
spell_res_mend_wounds_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; restore 2d6 HP; subject to Healing Saturation
spell_res_mending_aura_desc-u:
You extend a field of restorative force outward, touching every ally close enough to benefit from it. The effect is ambient rather than targeted — you are not directing the healing so much as creating the conditions for it to happen faster. This is post-engagement work, the spell you cast when the immediate threat is gone and the team needs to recover before the next one arrives.

Effect: This spell may only be cast outside of active combat during a rest period. All allies within Short range at the time of casting have their daily HP recovery for that rest increased by one category — field rest becomes equivalent to shelter rest, shelter rest becomes equivalent to clinic rest, and clinic rest produces the maximum possible daily recovery. Healing Saturation applies normally to each ally individually. Each ally may only benefit from one recovery-boosting Restoration spell per rest period — Accelerate Mending and Mending Aura do not stack on the same target.
spell_res_mending_aura_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; out of combat only; all allies in range increase daily HP recovery by one category
spell_res_purge_toxin_desc-u:
You read the foreign chemistry in the target's system and expel it — forcing it out of the pattern before it can settle into permanent damage. Fast, precise, and one of the most practically valuable things a Soulmender can do in the middle of a job that's gone wrong.

Effect: Remove one active toxin, chemical condition, or environmental contamination from the target. This includes poisons, sedatives, combat drugs with negative riders, inhaled compounds, and similar chemical intrusions. It does not remove conditions caused by magic or physical trauma — for magical conditions see Cleanse Influence, for bleeding see Stanch. If a toxin has multiple stages and has already advanced to a severe stage, this spell removes the active effect but cannot undo damage already dealt.
spell_res_purge_toxin_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove one active toxin, chemical condition, or environmental contamination
spell_res_purification_wave_desc-u:
A wash of restorative force radiates outward from you, stripping away the accumulated weight of active conditions and giving every ally in range their best chance to shed corruption simultaneously. The effect is broad and immediate — not as targeted as working on one person, but when the whole team is degraded, this is the fastest way to restore their collective function.

Effect: All allies within Short range immediately lose all active minor conditions — Disoriented, Shaken, Slowed, Chilled, Bleeding, Impaired, and similar status effects of equivalent severity. Major Wounds and severe conditions are not removed. In addition, each affected ally who carries Veil Corruption immediately makes a CON roll with +1 bonus die — on a success, their corruption reduces by one step. The once-per-24-hours limit applies to each ally individually. This spell does not restore HP and does not affect you.
spell_res_purification_wave_notes-u: Uncontested; Short (all allies); instant; remove all minor conditions; each corrupted ally rolls CON +1 bonus die to reduce by one step
spell_res_reactive_mend_desc-u:
The blow lands — but not entirely. You catch it in the pattern before the full damage settles into tissue, partially absorbing it with a burst of restorative force that meets the injury as it forms. The timing is everything. A fraction of a second either way and the moment has passed.

Effect: When an ally within Short range takes damage, you may cast this as a Reaction. The damage they take is reduced by 1d6 before applying Soak. This reduction applies to any damage type — Physical or Veil. Declare before damage is calculated. This spell reduces damage rather than restoring HP and does not count toward Healing Saturation. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty. You may not use this on yourself.
spell_res_reactive_mend_notes-u: Reaction; Uncontested; Short; reduce incoming damage to ally by 1d6; Reflexive
spell_res_regenerate_desc-u:
You pour an intense surge of restorative energy into the target's pattern — not a slow ambient mend but an accelerated, almost violent push toward recovery. The target's body responds rapidly, healing visible in real time. It costs you significantly. The effect does not last. For three rounds it is dramatic enough to matter.

Effect: For 3 rounds, at the start of each of the target's turns they recover 1d8 HP and 1 Strain. The entire spell counts as one instance toward Healing Saturation — not one per round. The Strain recovery component is not subject to Healing Saturation. This spell does not require concentration once cast — you spend the Strain and step away. May be cast on yourself.
spell_res_regenerate_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; 3 rounds; target recovers 1d8 HP and 1 Strain at start of their turn; no concentration
spell_res_remote_stabilize_desc-u:
When you cannot reach them — when the distance or the fire or the collapsed floor makes closing to Engaged range impossible — you reach across the gap with will alone and do what you can. It costs more to stretch that far. It is worth every point.

Effect: A dying or unconscious ally within Medium range is immediately stabilized — all death checks cease. This does not restore HP, remove conditions, or bring them back to consciousness. They remain incapacitated but stable. Does not count toward Healing Saturation. If they take further damage, death checks may resume.
spell_res_remote_stabilize_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium; instant; stop death checks on dying ally at range; no HP restored
spell_res_restore_mind_desc-u:
Mental conditions are physical problems — the nervous system under stress, neurochemistry disrupted by trauma or fear or arcane interference. You restore the biological substrate and the condition resolves. This is medical intervention, not comfort. The distinction matters to the people you treat with it.

Effect: Remove one of the following conditions from a target: Panicked, Shaken, Disoriented, or Distracted. The removal is immediate and requires no roll. This spell targets the biological cause of the condition — it is distinct from Soothe Mind (Enchantment), which operates through emotional influence. Restore Mind works even on targets who are resistant to social or magical persuasion, as it addresses the underlying physical state rather than the psychological one.
spell_res_restore_mind_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove Panicked, Shaken, Disoriented, or Distracted
spell_res_revive_desc-u:
Not stabilization — revival. You bring them back across the threshold: conscious, functional, and back in the fight. The cost is real and the process is not gentle. But they are standing, and standing changes everything.

Effect: A dying or unconscious ally within Engaged range who is currently making death CON saves is restored to 2d6 HP and regains consciousness. All active death check consequences are removed and death checks cease. The ally may act on their next turn normally. This spell counts as 1 toward Healing Saturation. Major Wounds remain in place — revival does not fix structural damage, only ensures the target is no longer dying. This spell has no effect on a character who has already died — once death saves have been failed and death is confirmed, Revive cannot help them. For characters who are merely unconscious but stable, use Mend Wounds to restore HP instead.
spell_res_revive_notes-u: Uncontested; Engaged; instant; restore dying/unconscious ally to 2d6 HP; remove immediate death consequences
spell_res_sever_the_wound_desc-u:
A Major Wound is not just injury — it is a persistent disruption in the target's pattern that continues to impair them long after the initial trauma. You locate the disruption, identify its shape, and sever its hold on the body's coherence. The tissue beneath still remembers the damage. The pattern no longer reflects it.

Effect: Remove one Major Wound condition from the target. The associated physical consequences — mechanical penalties, functional limitations, and ongoing effects — end immediately. This spell counts as 1 toward Healing Saturation even though it does not restore HP — Major Wound removal is a significant biological intervention and the body's recovery capacity is engaged regardless. This spell addresses one Major Wound per casting. If the target has multiple Major Wounds, each requires a separate casting and each counts toward Healing Saturation individually.
spell_res_sever_the_wound_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove one Major Wound condition
spell_res_stanch_desc-u:
Blood loss kills slowly and then all at once. Stanch addresses the mechanism directly — cauterizing the pattern that keeps bleeding active, closing the wound at the level of biological coherence rather than just applying pressure. The target stops bleeding. They stay stopped.

Effect: Remove the Bleeding condition from the target immediately. For the rest of the scene, the target cannot gain the Bleeding condition again from any source. This does not restore HP and does not interact with Healing Saturation. It is a condition removal specialist — the cheapest and most targeted tool in the school for a specific problem.
spell_res_stanch_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; remove Bleeding; target cannot gain Bleeding again this scene
spell_res_tissue_armor_desc-u:
You compress and reinforce the target's biological structure — skin, muscle, connective tissue, bone surface — tightening the density of living matter against physical impact. It is not a ward. It is not armor. It is the body hardened from within, temporarily more than it was.

Effect: For the scene, the target gains +2 Physical Soak. This stacks with worn armor. It does not protect against Veil damage. Requires concentration to maintain — if your concentration breaks, the reinforcement fades. Does not count toward Healing Saturation. One target may benefit from one Tissue Armor at a time.
spell_res_tissue_armor_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene (Conc); +2 Physical Soak; stacks with armor
spell_res_veil_cleanse_desc-u:
You reinforce the target's biological and spiritual pattern, giving their body the support it needs to expel minor Veil taint on its own. The magic doesn't remove the corruption — it empowers the body to do so. Whether it succeeds depends on how much fight the body still has.

Effect: The target immediately makes a CON roll with +1 bonus die. On a success, their Veil Corruption is reduced by one step — Lethal becomes Major, Major becomes Moderate, Moderate becomes Minor, Minor is fully cleared. On a failure, the corruption does not reduce but the Strain is still spent. A target may only benefit from one corruption-reduction attempt per 24 hours regardless of outcome. This spell has no effect on targets with no active corruption.
spell_res_veil_cleanse_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; instant; target rolls CON +1 bonus die; success = -1 corruption step
spell_res_veil_scar_sealing_desc-u:
Corruption removed is ideal. Corruption sealed is sometimes the best you can manage in the field. You close the wound in the pattern that allows Veil-taint to enter — not cleaning what's already there, but preventing it from getting worse. The target carries what they carry. They stop accumulating more.

Effect: The target's current Veil Corruption level cannot worsen for the remainder of the scene, regardless of cause. Hostile spells, ambient exposure, passive escalation, possession residue — none of it can increase their corruption while the seal holds. This does not remove existing corruption — for that, use Veil Cleanse or Deep Purge. It only prevents further accumulation. The seal ends at scene's end or if the target is subjected to a source of corruption powerful enough that the GM rules it overrides the seal.
spell_res_veil_scar_sealing_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene; seal Corruption wound; target's Corruption cannot worsen for the scene
spell_sum_anchor_shunt_desc-u:
A taut pull ripples along the tether you share with your Resonant Spirit. Space folds, shadows bend, and the spirit blinks from one point to another with a sharp crack of displaced ether. Its form steadies in a lattice of flickering motes as the Veil snaps closed behind it.

Effect: Teleport your Resonant Spirit from any location within Medium range to any other point within Medium range you can see. This movement is instantaneous and does not provoke reactions. The Resonant Spirit gains +20% to its next attack, defense, or movement check before the end of the scene — you choose which benefit when the shunt completes.
spell_sum_anchor_shunt_notes-u: Uncontested; teleport Resonant Spirit anywhere in Medium; +20% to its next attack/defense/move
spell_sum_arc_lamp_guardian_desc-u:
A thin filament of veillight threads into the device, sparking an obedient flicker as a tiny machine spirit stirs awake. It hums beneath the casing like a heartbeat made of copper and glass, alert to every hostile presence.

Effect: Bind a Lesser Machine Spirit into a device you are touching. For the duration, the spirit performs one protective intervention per round — either negating a single tampering or sabotage attempt directed at the device, or imposing 1 penalty die on a hostile attempt to disrupt, overload, or hack it. If the attempt comes from an attended or actively controlled device, the operator may contest normally. If you provide a small mechanical offering — a fresh filament, a charged microcell, a polished housing — the binding persists for a full Day instead of a Scene.
spell_sum_arc_lamp_guardian_notes-u: Uncontested; bind Lesser Machine Spirit to device; negate tamper or impose penalty die once/round
spell_sum_banishers_rebuke_desc-u:
A sharp command lashes from your focused will like a cracking whip made of hissing veilline. Spirits recoil as if struck by something weightless and electric, their forms fraying at the edges under the sudden pressure.

Effect: Choose a visible spirit within Short range and attempt a banish contested by the target's POW. On a success, Lesser Spirits are immediately banished, Resonant Spirits lose cohesion and are forced to withdraw for the rest of the scene, and Greater Spirits are driven back 1 range band and cannot advance toward you until their next turn. The Strain Cost die is based on the target spirit's tier: 1d4 for Lesser, 1d6 for Bound, and 1d10 for Greater. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty when used at Engaged range.
spell_sum_banishers_rebuke_notes-u: Reflexive; Contested vs POW; Short; banish by tier; Strain varies by tier
spell_sum_binding_accord_desc-u:
You speak a tiny promise, etched with intent so fine it glints like hairline gold. The spirit leans toward the offered term, its form brightening as it accepts the single-threaded agreement binding it to your will — for now.

Effect: Form a one-clause contract with a Lesser Spirit within Engaged or Short range. The contract may demand one simple behavior: carrying a small object, watching a location, fetching or delivering something, alerting you to danger, holding a spot, or glowing in response to a specific trigger. If the spirit violates the clause, the effect immediately ends and you gain +1 bonus die to banish that spirit for the rest of the scene. The spirit disappears when the scene ends or the agreed task is completed, whichever comes first.
spell_sum_binding_accord_notes-u: Uncontested; form one-clause contract with Lesser Spirit; violation gives +1 bonus die to banish
spell_sum_clear_the_echoes_desc-u:
You sweep your hand outward, washing the nearby Veil clean in a quick, decisive pass. Lingering whispers, minor fragments, and drifting residue dissolve like dust in sudden sunlight — quickly suppressed rather than permanently resolved, but good enough for right now.

Effect: You create a Short-range cleansing pulse that suppresses lingering echoes, spirit residue, and minor fragments in the affected area. For the rest of the scene, Summoning checks made by you in this area gain +10% due to reduced spiritual interference. Small pockets of corruption are weakened, granting you +20% to identify, analyze, or otherwise interact with that corruption for the scene. This spell does not affect active Bound or Greater Spirits and does not permanently lay echoes to rest — it clears the immediate noise around them. Use Rite of Returning when you have time to do the work properly.
spell_sum_clear_the_echoes_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; dissolve lingering residue; +10% Summoning in area for scene
spell_sum_co_signal_desc-u:
You send a brief pulse through the Veil, tightening the tether between you and a nearby spirit. Its outline sharpens as your intent cuts more cleanly through the ambient noise, creating a direct line between you and the chosen entity.

Effect: Choose one spirit within Short range. For the rest of the scene, you gain +10% on all checks that directly involve that spirit — commanding or directing your Resonant Spirit, stabilizing or dismissing the entity, attempting a banish specifically targeting it, or identifying, reading, or calming it. This spell does not enhance the spirit's own actions — it only clarifies your interactions with it. If the chosen spirit moves beyond Medium range, the spell immediately ends.
spell_sum_co_signal_notes-u: +10% to all checks involving one chosen spirit for scene
spell_sum_conscript_of_ash_desc-u:
A faint humanoid silhouette gathers from drifting flakes of ash, its form held together by will more than by structure. It moves with quiet purpose and little weight, exuding a fragile determination that lasts only as long as the task it has been given.

Effect: You summon a Lesser Mortality Spirit within Short range to accomplish one complex mundane task for the duration of the scene. This task might include retrieving or hiding a small object, scouting through cracks or vents, delivering a whispered message, or following a target through tight spaces. The spirit cannot attack, manipulate intricate mechanisms, or cross wards and arcane barriers. Whenever a roll is required for its actions, use your Summoning skill at -20% to reflect its fragile and inconsistent form. The spirit disperses immediately if harmed, blocked by a mystical ward, or once the assigned task is completed.
spell_sum_conscript_of_ash_notes-u: Uncontested; summon Lesser Mortality Spirit for one complex mundane task; scene
spell_sum_contract_seal_desc-u:
A clean, white sigil flashes into place around the spirit, closing like a clamp made of luminous script. The symbol hums with the unmistakable weight of an imposed rule, declaring a single inviolate clause that the entity must obey for as long as your focus holds.

Effect: Choose any spirit within Short range and impose one prohibited action for the scene — forbidding it from crossing a line, targeting a specific person, manifesting visibly, or interacting with a chosen object. Make a contested Summoning vs POW roll. On a success, the constraint holds for the scene as long as you maintain concentration. On a failure, the clause does not take hold though the Strain is still expended.
spell_sum_contract_seal_notes-u: Contested vs POW; Short; forbid one action by a spirit; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_counter_possession_stance_desc:
A stabilizing lattice of spiritual tension forms around the host, knitting a defensive barrier between the invading presence and the victim's will. The host's posture steadies, their breath deepens, and a subtle glow outlines their features as your intent braces their essence from within.

Effect: Choose a creature currently under possession or at immediate risk of it. For the remainder of the scene, the host gains +1 bonus die to all attempts to resist or expel the possessing spirit, while the possessor suffers -1 penalty die on any attempt to control, suppress, or force actions through the host body. The effect ends immediately if the possessing spirit departs voluntarily or if the host is rendered unconscious.
spell_sum_counter_possession_stance_note: Defensive; Short; host gains +1 bonus die vs possession; possessor -1 penalty die to control
spell_sum_fetchling_courier_desc-u:
A darting spark of veillight condenses before you, quick and eager, its form constantly shifting between a streak and a flicker. It vibrates with the urgency of a message half-spoken, ready to slip through cracks and narrow gaps with effortless speed.

Effect: You summon a Lesser Courier Spirit in Short range, tasking it with carrying a brief message or tiny token along a path you define. The spirit can slip through narrow spaces and travel through Short, Medium, and even Long-range distances within the scene as long as the route is physically continuous. It cannot cross hostile wards or arcane barriers — if such an obstruction blocks its path, it retreats to the nearest safe point before dispersing. Once it completes its delivery or the scene ends, the spirit fades.
spell_sum_fetchling_courier_notes-u: Uncontested; summon courier spirit; Short to Long delivery; scene
spell_sum_gentle_reversal_desc-u:
You steady your breathing and pull a thin strand of the Veil taut between your fingers, drawing it until it feels like a perfectly balanced wire. Your senses sharpen along that line, preparing to invert a spirit's momentum with immaculate timing.

Effect: Until the end of the scene, your next banish attempt gains +20% and ignores the first penalty die that would normally apply — whether from environmental disruption, corruption, spirit interference, or casting in melee. This spell affects only your next banish attempt. Once that attempt is made — successful or not — the spell ends immediately.
spell_sum_gentle_reversal_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; next banish this scene gains +20% and ignores first penalty die
spell_sum_glyphwrights_delegate_desc-u:
A crisp glyph unfolds over the device's surface like luminous origami, and a Bound Machine Spirit settles into its circuitry with the precision of a master artisan. Its presence is quiet yet unmistakably vigilant, attentive to cues and intrusions you designate.

Effect: Bind a Bound Machine Spirit to a device you are touching. For the duration of the scene, assign the spirit one simple automated behavior — maintaining a particular setting against interference, cycling a mechanism on cue, monitoring for intrusion and delivering an alert, or repeating a basic mechanical action. The spirit cannot parse complex logic, override security, or perform multi-step tasks. Once per round, it may oppose one hostile tamper, overload, or false input attempt with a +1 bonus die. If your concentration breaks, the binding collapses and the spirit departs.
spell_sum_glyphwrights_delegate_notes-u: Uncontested; bind Bound Machine Spirit to device; automate one behavior; +1 bonus die vs tamper
spell_sum_guardians_intercession_desc-u:
A geometric veil-sigil settles over the chosen target like a second shadow, pulsing with a slow, vigilant rhythm. A faint silhouette hovers at its edges — a watchful guardian spirit that seldom reveals itself fully but radiates quiet resolve.

Effect: Choose one ally or device within Short range. A minor guardian spirit surrounds the target for the scene. The protected target gains +1 bonus die to resist possession, tracking, or spiritual influence. Hostile spirits attempting to harm, suppress, or interact with the target suffer -20% to their checks. Once per round, the guardian may intercept a single hostile spiritual action directed at the warded ally or device, negating the effect outright or imposing a penalty die on the attempt. The spell ends immediately if your concentration breaks.
spell_sum_guardians_intercession_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; guard ally/device; +1 bonus die vs possession; hostiles -20%; once/round block spirit action
spell_sum_hostile_invitation_desc-u:
You slice a narrow seam through the Veil — no wider than a whisper, but deep enough to offer passage. The breach hums with unstable resonance like a cracked bell struck underwater. Your Resonant Spirit senses the opening instantly, slipping toward the forbidden space with predatory confidence.

Effect: Choose your Resonant Spirit within Short range. You open a temporary conduit that allows the spirit to bypass forbidden boundaries — hostile wards, Veil turbulence, domain seams, or arcane pressure zones. The conduit lasts for the scene or until the Resonant Spirit passes through it once, whichever comes first. If the barrier is actively maintained by another caster or anchored through a Greater Spirit, make a contested Summoning vs POW roll. Greater Spirits maintaining the ward gain +10% if defending within their native domain. On failure, the conduit collapses and you suffer one penalty die on your next Summoning attempt this scene.
spell_sum_hostile_invitation_notes-u: Contested vs maintained wards or barriers; Short; let Resonant Spirit bypass one forbidden barrier
spell_sum_lattice_of_hands_desc-u:
Faint translucent hands reach out from the Veil — woven together like a net made of memory and intent. They do not grasp flesh but resonance itself, holding spirit-forms in place with soft, unyielding pressure.

Effect: Choose a spirit or possessed host within Short range. On a failed POW roll, the target becomes restrained by a net of spectral limbs and cannot change its position unless it wins a contested roll of its POW against your Summoning skill. The restrained entity suffers -20% on any action involving movement, refocusing, shifting forms, or repositioning in the Veil. When cast on a possessed host, the effect restrains the invading spirit rather than the body. If the target wins the contested roll, the restraint collapses and the spell ends immediately. On a success against your initial roll, no effect.
spell_sum_lattice_of_hands_notes-u: Contested vs POW; Short; restrain spirit or possessor; -20% to movement; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_name_chain_desc-u:
You speak or trace the spirit's signature — the Name it wears within the Veil — and luminous script coils around it like a tightening chain. The chain vibrates with the unmistakable tone of recognition, the sound of a spirit being seen too deeply to ignore.

Effect: Choose any spirit within Engaged range whose true name, pattern, or Veil-signature you have identified. Make a contested Summoning vs POW roll. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on all actions involving that spirit for the scene — commanding, banishing, restraining, or analyzing it. The spirit suffers -1 penalty die on any contested roll made against you. If you attempt to banish that spirit during the scene, you gain an additional +10% on the banish attempt. The effect ends if you lose concentration or if the spirit leaves your sensory reach entirely.
spell_sum_name_chain_notes-u: Contested vs POW; Engaged; true-name bind; +1 bonus die vs that spirit; -1 penalty die vs you
spell_sum_purifying_lattice_desc-u:
A crystalline lattice of silver-blue veillight forms momentarily around the spirit, flickering like a cage woven from starlight and razor-thin glass. Corruption burns away beneath the pattern, peeling off in drifting motes that dissolve with a faint hiss.

Effect: Touch a Lesser, Bound, or Greater (willing) Spirit suffering corruption. The lattice removes all minor corruption and may reduce a moderate or major corruption state by one step. It seals the cracks that once allowed Veil-warp to spread, preventing further corruption for the rest of the scene. This spell does not heal Integrity damage, nor does it cure corruption rooted in mythic wounds, Avatars, Anchors, or region-level spiritual scars.
spell_sum_purifying_lattice_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; remove minor corruption; reduce major by one step; seal leakage for scene
spell_sum_quiet_the_line_desc-u:
You draw the ambient Veil-noise inward and suppress it, muffling the low static hum of lingering resonance. The air becomes dense and muted, as though someone wrapped the world in heavy cloth. Even hostile spirits seem to hesitate as the background energy collapses into silence.

Effect: Create a Short-range zone of dampened Veil activity. Within this zone, all Summoning or possession attempts suffer a -1 penalty die unless the caster is performing the ritual inside a properly established circle or ward. Efforts to track or detect spirit activity through the area suffer -20%. Lesser Spirits become noticeably calmer, and agitated spirits drop one tension step — from hostile to wary, or from wary to neutral. Your own Summoning actions remain unaffected. The dampening field collapses instantly if you lose concentration.
spell_sum_quiet_the_line_notes-u: Uncontested; Short zone; Summoning/possession suffer -1 penalty die; tracking -20%; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_resonant_lash_desc-u:
A thin whip of condensed veillight snaps outward like lightning made of thought. It strikes the spiritual form directly — not tearing it but jarring its inner structure with a sharp, resonant crack. The air trembles with the aftershock, and the spirit reels as its cohesion flickers.

Effect: Choose a spirit within Short range and make a contested Summoning vs POW roll. On a success, the spirit takes 1d8 Resonant damage — Veil damage with an additional 1d4 against spirits and Veil entities — and suffers -10% to its next action involving focus, cohesion, movement, attack, or manifestation. On a failure, no effect. Because the spell is Reflexive, you do not suffer the normal penalty for casting while engaged in melee.
spell_sum_resonant_lash_notes-u: Reflexive; Contested vs POW; Short; 1d8 Resonant; -10% to next cohesion action
spell_sum_rite_of_returning_desc-u:
With care and patience you smooth the Veil's surface around a lingering echo, giving it what it needs to finally let go. This is not suppression — it is completion. The fragmented presence relaxes beneath your guidance and disperses cleanly, leaving the environment quieter in a way that doesn't fade when the scene ends.

Effect: This spell requires 1 full uninterrupted round of focused work and cannot be cast in combat. Touch or target a lingering echo, minor spirit remnant, corpse-trace, or patch of Veil residue within Short range. The addressed echo is permanently laid to rest — it does not return. For the rest of the scene, your Summoning attempts made in this area gain +1 bonus die due to the cleaner spiritual environment. Any corrupted residue in the vicinity weakens, granting +10% to identifying or interacting with such corruption. This spell does not affect active Bound or Greater Spirits, and if interrupted before the round completes, the Strain is still paid but the echo is not resolved.
spell_sum_rite_of_returning_notes-u: Ritual; 1 full round; Short; permanently lay echo to rest; +1 bonus die Summoning in area for scene
spell_sum_sever_the_anchor_desc-u:
You focus on the tether that binds the spirit to this plane and slice through it with a razor-fine edge of intent. The Veil flashes like a snapped wire, giving off a brief metallic sting in the air as the connection recoils into nothingness.

Effect: Choose a spirit within Short range and make a contested Summoning vs POW roll. On a success, Lesser Spirits are banished instantly, Resonant Spirits lose their foothold and are unable to return for the rest of the scene, and Greater Spirits are forced out of the material entirely and cannot reappear during the scene unless narrative conditions explicitly allow it. If you fail the contested roll, the spell has no effect though you still suffer the Strain cost.
spell_sum_sever_the_anchor_notes-u: Contested vs POW; Short; banish by tier; instant
spell_sum_shadow_scout_desc-u:
A near-invisible presence peels away from your shadow and moves ahead with quiet intent — through walls, beneath doors, around corners that line of sight can't reach. It does not interact with what it finds. It only watches, and what it sees, you see.

Effect: You summon a small, nearly invisible scouting spirit that can pass through non-warded physical barriers — doors, walls, vents, and similar obstacles — and travel up to Long range from you while you maintain Concentration. While the scout is active, you receive sensory impressions from its location: a rough visual impression of the space, awareness of creatures present, and a sense of emotional or arcane atmosphere. The scout cannot attack, carry objects, or interact with the physical world in any way. If it contacts an active ward, Veil barrier, or hostile spiritual presence, it disperses immediately and the spell ends. If your concentration breaks, it disperses harmlessly.
spell_sum_shadow_scout_notes-u: Uncontested; summon scouting spirit; Long range perception relay; passes non-warded barriers; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_shard_bound_volley_desc-u:
You manifest a Resonant Spirit shaped for ranged warfare. Its form fractures into angular shards of crystalline veillight that orbit its center like razor-edged satellites. Each movement sends a shimmering pulse through the air as the spirit aligns itself, ready to fire volleys of resonant force.

Effect: You summon a Resonant Spirit in a ranged-attacker configuration within Short range. It acts immediately after you in initiative. Once per round, the spirit may launch a volley of spectral shards, attacking with your Summoning skill against the target's Defense. A successful hit deals 1d6 Resonant damage — Veil damage with an additional 1d4 against spirits and Veil entities. The spirit cannot engage in melee combat and cannot move farther than Short range from you. If your concentration breaks, the manifestation disperses instantly.
spell_sum_shard_bound_volley_notes-u: Uncontested; summon ranged Resonant Spirit; 1d6 Resonant (+1d4 vs spirits); Short; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_spirit_mend_desc-u:
You gather loose resonance drifting around the spirit and draw it inward, weaving its pattern back into alignment. Fractured edges smooth, scattered motes rejoin, and the entity's form stabilizes with a quiet shimmer — like returning a misplaced heartbeat to its rhythm.

Effect: Choose a Lesser or Resonant Spirit within range. You may restore Light Integrity damage, remove the Shaken or Disturbed condition, or restore a partially collapsed form back to full presence. For Moderate or Severe Integrity damage, the spell stabilizes the spirit and prevents further deterioration but cannot fully repair the injury. This spell does not affect corruption — it only mends damage analogous to physical harm for spirits.
spell_sum_spirit_mend_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; heal Light Integrity; remove Shaken/Disturbed; stabilize Moderate/Severe
spell_sum_spirit_touch_desc-u:
A thin veil of resonance settles along your hand and forearm, allowing the boundary between flesh and spirit to soften. The air tingles faintly as you reach out — and for the duration you may make contact with forms normally intangible.

Effect: For the scene, you may physically interact with spirits within Engaged range. This includes touching, steadying, guiding, briefly restraining small motions, or receiving objects that a spirit chooses to hand you. You can interact with Lesser Spirits, Resonant Spirits, and lingering echoes or imprints. This spell does not grant offensive capacity or allow grappling — it merely enables physical contact that would otherwise be impossible.
spell_sum_spirit_touch_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; physically interact with spirits at Engaged range; scene
spell_sum_suture_the_echo_desc-u:
Slender threads of pale veillight cross the surface of the spirit's form, knitting fractures and sealing shaky resonance lines. Each stitch tightens the spirit's presence and quiets the tremor of instability that once rippled through it.

Effect: Choose a Lesser or Resonant Spirit within range. For the remainder of the scene, remove one cohesion penalty the spirit is currently suffering due to corruption, disruption, or strain. The spirit gains +10% on its next action involving movement, stability, or manifestation. Stabilized spirits resist forced banishment more effectively, imposing a -10% penalty on attempts to forcibly disperse or disrupt them. This spell does not repair Integrity damage — it solely addresses cohesion instability.
spell_sum_suture_the_echo_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; remove 1 cohesion penalty; +10% next stability action; forced banish -10% vs it
spell_sum_tether_sigil_desc-u:
A glowing sigil sinks into the surface beneath your hand, smoldering like molten ink. The mark anchors itself in the Veil and creates a subtle gravitational pull that tugs at nearby spirits — a quiet hand urging them to remain.

Effect: Place a Tether Sigil on a surface or object you are touching. For the scene, spirits within Short range suffer -10% on any attempt to move away from the sigil. Resonant or Lesser Spirits attempting to leave the sigil's zone must win a contested POW vs your Summoning skill roll to escape its influence. While inside the sigil's reach, you gain +1 bonus die on efforts to dismiss, stabilize, or interact with spirits. Greater Spirits are not restrained by the sigil, though they may feel its pull as a minor impediment.
spell_sum_tether_sigil_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; spirits suffer -10% to move away; Lesser/Resonant must contest to leave; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_token_trace_desc-u:
By pressing your hand to an object steeped in a spirit's resonance, you pull a faint directional thread from the Veil. The thread tugs at your senses like a compass made of intuition and memory — pointing not with force but with quiet, persistent certainty.

Effect: Touch a token, mark, or object that carries a recognizable spiritual echo. For the rest of the scene, you always know the direction of the associated spirit as long as it remains within Extreme range. Tracking or locating that spirit gains +20%. The spirit cannot fully conceal itself from you using spiritual masking or distortion — its attempts to hide suffer -10% when directed against your perception. The spell reveals direction but not distance. Where Veil Footprint requires you to have seen the spirit directly first, Token Trace works from any object carrying its resonance — making it the forensic and prepared-tracking option.
spell_sum_token_trace_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; track spirit from resonance object; always know direction within Extreme; scene
spell_sum_veil_evacuation_desc-u:
A compressed shock of banishing force detonates outward from your gesture. The Veil fractures for a moment into rippling rings of pressure. Lesser Spirits scatter like dust in a windstorm, while stronger entities stagger against the force that tries to sweep them away.

Effect: You release a Short-range burst of banishing resonance. Lesser Spirits are dispersed instantly with no contest. Resonant Spirits must win a contested POW vs your Summoning roll or be forced back one range band. Greater Spirits contest normally but gain +10% if this resistance occurs within their native domain, and on failure are pushed back a single band but not beyond Medium. Non-hardened electronic devices flicker as the Veil shock rolls through the area, though no lasting damage is caused. The effect resolves immediately once the shockwave dissipates.
spell_sum_veil_evacuation_notes-u: Uncontested; Short burst; Lesser dispersed; Resonant/Greater pushed 1 band on failed contest
spell_sum_veil_footprint_desc-u:
You sweep your hand through the space a spirit just occupied and its resonance sharpens to your senses like a heat trail in cold air. A faint, luminous filament stretches away from the spirit's departing form, visible only to you, tugging like a subtle afterglow.

Effect: Choose a spirit you can see within Short range. For the rest of the scene, you always know the direction of that spirit, even when it leaves sight or crosses into another range band. Tracking or identifying its Veil trail gains +20%. The spirit's efforts to hide or obscure its resonance suffer -10% when directed against your senses. This effect functions only for you and does not make the spirit visible to others. If the spirit is banished or moves beyond Extreme range, the spell ends.
spell_sum_veil_footprint_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; track spirit you can see; direction always known; +20% to track; scene
spell_sum_veil_handshake_desc-u:
You release a soft, probing pulse through the Veil — like tapping a tuning fork beneath still water. The pulse brushes against nearby spirits and returns carrying impressions of presence, temperament, and resonance. It feels like shaking unseen hands one by one in the dark.

Effect: You sweep Medium range to sense spirits. For the scene, you become aware of any active spirits within Short range, learn their broad tier — Lesser, Bound, or Greater — and gain +10% on your next Summoning interaction with one detected spirit this scene, whether that interaction involves banishing, analyzing, commanding, or stabilizing. The spell reveals only presence and general shape — not allegiance, intent, or identity.
spell_sum_veil_handshake_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium sweep; detect spirits; learn tier; +10% to one interaction this scene
spell_sum_veil_shroud_desc-u:
Your spiritual outline blurs beneath a drifting haze of distortion. To nearby entities, your resonance becomes indistinct — a smudged silhouette fading into ambient noise. Spirits strain to pin you down, as though trying to focus on a face lost behind fogged glass.

Effect: For the scene, you are more difficult for spirits to detect, target, or influence. Spirit detection or tracking rolls against you suffer -20%. Possession attempts suffer a -1 penalty die. Hostile spirits cannot pinpoint your exact location unless they are in Engaged range — at Medium or farther, they sense only your general direction. Your Resonant Spirit remains unaffected and can still home to you normally. This spell obscures your spiritual signature but has no effect on your physical stealth.
spell_sum_veil_shroud_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; spiritual signature concealed; detection -20%; possession -1 penalty die; scene (Conc)
spell_sum_voice_of_the_domain_desc-u:
You cast your intent through the Veil not toward a single spirit, but toward the vast presence that shapes the land itself. The domain listens as though a mountain held its breath or a city paused its heartbeat. Whatever answers always carries the unmistakable weight of something ancient, patient, and aware.

Effect: You attempt to petition the Avatar or Greater Spirit of the surrounding domain — a forest's guardian, a river's memory, a mountain's will, or a district's latent spirit. If the entity chooses to respond, it may provide symbolic insight into danger or disruption, grant you +20% on Summoning checks tied to the domain for the rest of the scene, shield you once from hostile spirit or environmental Veil effects, or manifest a subtle intervention such as shifting wind, moving debris, shimmering lights, or a whispered omen. Greater Spirits cannot be commanded, only approached — they may ignore or distort the request depending on mood, corruption, or narrative weight.
spell_sum_voice_of_the_domain_notes-u: Petition Greater Spirit/Avatar; omen, guidance, protection, or minor intervention; scene
spell_sum_whisper_recall_desc-u:
You send a silent pulse through the tether that binds you to your Resonant Spirit and it slips through the Veil like a shadow returning home, emerging with a faint shimmer of displaced resonance.

Effect: Choose your Resonant Spirit within Medium range. It immediately transitions to Engaged range with you, phasing through walls or obstacles so long as they are not explicitly warded. This movement does not provoke reactions as it is a Veil traversal rather than a physical relocation. If the spirit is unstable, one cohesion penalty is removed as it reforms beside you.
spell_sum_whisper_recall_notes-u: Uncontested; Medium; pull Resonant Spirit to Engaged; proactive repositioning; no reactions
spell_sum_wisp_blade_attendant_desc-u:
A faint silhouette coalesces from drifting veillight, its limbs tapering into delicate blade-like arcs. The spirit hums with sharp, simple purpose — its presence more like a flicker of intent than a creature of thought.

Effect: You summon a Lesser Combat Spirit in Short range. It acts immediately after you in initiative. It can strike, guard a designated target, or intercept a threat. Attacks use your Summoning skill against the target's Defense and deal 1d4 Resonant damage — Veil damage with an additional 1d4 against spirits and Veil entities. The attendant disperses instantly if struck by any direct attack, reflecting its fragile nature. It can move Short range on its turn and vanishes immediately if concentration breaks.
spell_sum_wisp_blade_attendant_notes-u: Uncontested; summon Lesser Combat Spirit; melee 1d4 Resonant; disperses on any hit; scene (Conc)
spell_tec_arcane_pingjam_desc-u:
You saturate the local bands with carrier ghosts and needle-noise. To the ear it is a high, metallic hush; to machines it is a migraine. Communications don't fail cleanly — they get confused, retransmit into static, and decide the signal wasn't worth chasing.

Effect: When a device first enters the aura, it attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a success it functions normally while inside. On a failure, its comms, sensor feeds, and remote links are jammed until it exits the aura or the spell ends. Leaving and re-entering calls for a new roll. On cast, you may designate allied or registered gear to be unaffected. Sensor-based attacks made by jammed devices against any target suffer 1 penalty die. The spell requires only the initial Strain payment and persists for the scene while you maintain Concentration. Implanted cyberware is never affected.
spell_tec_arcane_pingjam_notes-u: Self-aura (Medium); jams comms/scans; sensor-based attacks suffer 1 penalty die; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_blackout_surge_desc-u:
A flat crack of blue-white hush ripples outward. Status lights gutter, motors lock, and for a long second machines remember what silence feels like.

Effect: You unleash an electromagnetic pulse in a Short-range burst centered on a point you can see. Any device, drone, turret, or vehicle system not explicitly EMP-shielded is immediately shut down for 1 round with no resistance roll. EMP-shielded devices make a single Device Resistance 40% check — on a success they ignore the effect, on a failure they also shut down for 1 round. While shut down, a device cannot act, cannot take Reactions, and counts as inert hardware, though passive traits such as armor plating or mechanical locks remain. You may Sustain the surge by paying the Strain cost each round, extending the shutdown duration for all affected devices. Any EMP-shielded devices not yet shut down may attempt another Device Resistance 40% roll each sustained round to avoid being disabled. When the surge ends, affected systems reboot normally. Implanted cyberware is never affected.
spell_tec_blackout_surge_notes-u: Short burst; non-hardened electronics shut down 1 round; hardened may resist; may Sustain
spell_tec_circuit_sprite_desc-u:
You pinch a glimmer of hardlight loose from the nearest circuit and give it just enough will to be useful. The result is a small glyphling of lines and sigils, hovering where you called it — curious, eager, and exactly as useful as you let it be.

Effect: You conjure a Circuit Sprite in an open space within Short range. It is a tiny utility construct with 1 HP, negligible mass, and no offensive capability. It moves at roughly walking speed, can slip through hand-sized gaps, and can perch on surfaces or hardware. It carries out simple, non-combat tasks: carrying or holding a small tool or cable, pressing buttons, cycling switches, pointing a sensor, relaying a short spoken message, or hovering as a visible marker in space. The sprite cannot attack, grapple, fire weapons, run OmniDeck programs, or make skill rolls independently. Whenever you make a Mechanics or Slicing check and the sprite is clearly assisting in a physical, plausible way — holding a light, keeping a panel open, maintaining pressure on a contact — it grants you +10% to that check. If the sprite takes any damage from an attack or area effect it is destroyed immediately and the spell ends. Otherwise it persists until the scene ends or you dismiss it. Implanted cyberware is never a valid anchor for the sprite.
spell_tec_circuit_sprite_notes-u: Uncontested; summon 1-HP utility glyphling; assists Mechanics/Slicing when plausibly helping; Scene
spell_tec_clean_circuit_desc-u:
A cool ribbon of hardlight slips through the housing and flushes junk states — stuck flags, ghost heat, and sloppy locks — without touching the guts. The machine sighs and remembers how to cooperate.

Effect: Choose one device in Short range. You immediately clear one of the following conditions from that device: a jammed state, an overheat condition, or a soft-lock. The spell functions at the software and control layer only — it does not restore HP, repair physical damage, reinstall missing components, remove network security countermeasures, or replace the need for a full reboot if one is required. Clean Circuit cannot target an OmniDeck.
spell_tec_clean_circuit_notes-u: Uncontested; clear jam/overheat/soft-lock from a device; cannot target an OmniDeck
spell_tec_codebind_spirit_desc-u:
You beckon a minor data-spirit and seat it inside a willing unit. It is curious, polite, and eager to help — more helper sprite than guard dog. It follows standing orders, maintains routines, and responds to clear triggers with quiet competence.

Effect: Bind a minor machine-spirit to a single allied or unattended unit for the scene. The unit behaves as if a basic, helpful operator were present. It can follow a simple standing order, maintain a routine, or respond to one or two clear triggers — 'patrol this route and ping me if motion is detected' or 'keep this door closed and reopen for my voice.' The spirit can operate existing controls, monitor feeds, carry messages, and announce alerts, but cannot escalate to hostile behavior, attack, run weapon routines, or execute OmniDeck programs. It also cannot perform Slicing, cast spells, bypass authentication, or imitate biometric keys. When you act through or with the bound unit, you make any necessary rolls yourself — the spirit never rolls in your place. Once per scene while acting through the unit, the spirit may give you a small push, granting +10% to a single relevant check. If the unit is destroyed, the spirit disperses harmlessly. Implanted cyberware is never a valid target.
spell_tec_codebind_spirit_notes-u: Uncontested; bind minor data spirit to allied or unattended unit; autonomous for Scene
spell_tec_compiled_machine_spirit_desc-u:
You compile a true machine familiar — an enduring, curious persona of flicker-will — and seat it inside a small body you built for it. It is a companion, not a weapon: a quiet presence that remembers you between missions and makes the world run a little smoother.

Effect: This ritual can only be performed as a Downtime Activity when you devote time to fabricating a small chassis and inviting the spirit into it. When the activity completes, the spirit bonds to that chassis as your familiar and persists from mission to mission. The familiar behaves as a non-combat assistant with a distinct personality. On your turn, when directed, it may carry messages, operate basic controls, monitor a feed, hold a position, deliver a tool, or announce alerts. It cannot attack, mount or fire weapons, run OmniDeck programs, cast spells, perform Slicing, bypass authentication, or impersonate biometrics. Once per scene, the familiar may provide a helping nudge granting +10% to a single relevant check made through or with it. If the familiar's chassis is destroyed, the spirit survives. If a new chassis is already prepared, rehousing takes 10 minutes. If none is available, you must spend another Downtime Activity to fabricate one. The spirit can be banished by appropriate effects; if banished, the bond ends until you repeat this ritual. Implanted cyberware is never a valid target for binding.
spell_tec_compiled_machine_spirit_notes-: Ritual; permanent machine familiar; inhabits small chassis; re-housable if destroyed
spell_tec_data_spike_desc-u:
A needle of hostile code detonates inside target logic, scorching pathways like sunspots across a sensor array. Whatever was running in there is having a very bad moment.

Effect: After a successful cast, the target device, drone, or turret attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, it takes 2d6 Veil damage. On a successful resistance, no effect. Wards and shields that apply to Veil damage interact with this normally.
spell_tec_data_spike_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; 2d6 Veil damage to device/drone/turret
spell_tec_deepfake_feed_desc-u:
Reality behaves for the camera. A seamless overlay replaces the truth with something close enough to pass — the right corridor, the right timestamp, the right nothing happening at all.

Effect: Select one camera or live feed within Medium range. The device makes a single Device Resistance 40% roll. On a failure, you replace its output with a convincing loop or fabricated stream for the scene while you maintain Concentration. On a success, no effect. Anyone interacting directly with the scene outside the fabricated frame can spot inconsistencies, and physical contact or obviously contradictory events may reveal the illusion at the GM's discretion.
spell_tec_deepfake_feed_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; forge/overwrite one camera or live feed; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_drone_seize_desc-u:
You impose command-authority like a brand, turning a hostile machine to your purpose. It does not cooperate — it obeys, which is better.

Effect: Choose one drone or turret within Medium range. It attempts Device Resistance 40% once on cast. On a failed resistance, you control that machine for as long as you maintain Concentration. While under your control, the machine acts on your initiative and obeys your commands to the limits of its hardware — you cannot make it violate basic physical constraints. At the end of each of the target's turns while you hold it, the machine may attempt one additional Device Resistance 40% roll to break free. On a success, control ends immediately and the machine reverts to its previous directives. Implanted cyberware is never a valid target.
spell_tec_drone_seize_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; seize control of one drone/turret; target rolls each round to break free
spell_tec_echo_trace_desc-u:
You read the fingerprints of traffic — who touched this system, where the signals flowed, and which hands were dirty. The machine remembers even when the logs have been scrubbed.

Effect: Touch a device and read the last access point, route, and primary commands issued to it. You gain +10% to your next directly related locate, analyze, or interface check made within the same scene. The spell does not reconstruct full logs or detailed content — only a clear imprint of the most recent meaningful interaction path.
spell_tec_echo_trace_notes-u: Uncontested; forensic touch reveals last access/route/commands; +10% next related check
spell_tec_firewall_flicker_desc-u:
You nudge the handshake timing at the exact millisecond your exploit lands, catching the seam while it is open. Not brute force — just a breath of better timing.
