Effect: When you make your Access Roll to enter a network — the initial Slicing check that determines your starting Escalation — you may cast this spell as a Reaction before rolling. That Access Roll gains 1 bonus die, improving your chances of a cleaner entry and a lower starting Escalation. You cannot use this spell after seeing any dice result. Firewall Flicker does not stack with other effects that add a bonus die to the same roll — if multiple apply, use only the strongest single source.
spell_tec_firewall_flicker_notes-u: Reaction; before your Access Roll, gain 1 bonus die on the roll to enter a network; 1d6 Strain
spell_tec_firewall_unravel_desc-u:
You pull a precise stitch in the lattice the instant your exploit lands, and the warding slackens just long enough to matter. One breath of room. Make it count.

Effect: Cast this spell as a Reaction immediately before making your Access Roll. On a successful cast, when you establish connection your starting Escalation is reduced by 2 — applied after the Access Roll result sets the initial value. This is in addition to any bonus from the Access Roll itself, and can bring starting Escalation below zero, giving the Technomancer an even larger initial buffer than a Ghost Protocol entry alone would provide. Firewall Unravel may only be used once per slicing attempt. It has no effect if the Access Roll results in a Lockout.
spell_tec_firewall_unravel_notes-u: Reaction; before your Access Roll; reduce starting Escalation by 2 after connection established; once per slice
spell_tec_glyph_rewrite_desc-u:
Two bits touch that should not, and a dignified machine does something undignified — just long enough to matter. Unlike Magelock Circuit which locks a device out entirely, Glyph Rewrite picks its specific misbehavior, choosing the exact moment and manner of the glitch.

Effect: Choose one device in Short range. After a successful cast, the device attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, you impose a one-round glitch and choose one of the following specific effects: lock inputs so no new commands are accepted; cause a turret or drone to skip its next attack and lose Reactions for the round; make a camera or sensor blink so your designated allies are treated as Hidden until the round ends; toggle a door or mag-seal once; or force a control node's actionable output to arrive one full round late. A given device can be affected by Glyph Rewrite only once per round. On a successful resistance, no effect. Implanted cyberware is never a valid target.
spell_tec_glyph_rewrite_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; device glitches 1 round; choose specific glitch effect
spell_tec_hard_reset_desc-u:
A forced reboot is hammered in with the grace of a sledge, followed by the silence of a clean log. The machine comes back on having forgotten what it was angry about.

Effect: Choose one device, drone, turret, or similar system in Short range. It attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, the target becomes completely inert for 1 round — unable to act or take Reactions. At the end of that round it reboots, clearing recent locks, alerts, and tracking flags created in the last scene, as though it had undergone a controlled hard reset. On a successful resistance, no effect. Hard Reset does not repair damage or restore HP.
spell_tec_hard_reset_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; target inert 1 round; wipes recent locks/alerts on restart
spell_tec_hardlight_interface_desc-u:
Where a console should be, a console appears — keys of light and dials of promise. The hardware underneath is still broken. The ability to use it is not.

Effect: Conjure a functional interface for damaged or missing controls on one device in Short range. For the scene, you and your allies can operate that device as if its physical controls were intact. Any required authentication still applies — the spell does not bypass passwords, biometrics, or security tokens. It merely restores the means to input them.
spell_tec_hardlight_interface_notes-u: Uncontested; conjure temporary UI for damaged/missing controls; Scene
spell_tec_hardlight_panel_desc-u:
A slab of luminous matter extrudes from the air, rigid and faintly singing. It is not subtle. It is very much a wall.

Effect: You create a 1×2 meter hardlight barrier within Short range that provides Cover appropriate to its size and placement. The panel has HP equal to your Magic stat and can only take Physical damage. When reduced to 0 HP it shatters into fading shards of light and the spell ends. If you lose Concentration before that point, the panel dissolves harmlessly without shattering.
spell_tec_hardlight_panel_notes-u: Uncontested; 1×2m hardlight barrier providing Cover; Scene (Conc) or until broken
spell_tec_hardlight_tool_desc-u:
You print the tool you need from stubborn light and a guilty conscience. It looks wrong but it works exactly right.

Effect: Conjure a single tool or spare part appropriate to a repair or modification task you are attempting. For the duration of the scene, Mechanics checks that use this conjured implement ignore penalties for missing or improvised equipment. The tool is not a weapon and cannot be meaningfully used as one. When the scene ends, the construct dissolves.
spell_tec_hardlight_tool_notes-u: Uncontested; conjure tool/spare part; Mechanics repairs avoid missing-equipment penalties; Scene
spell_tec_interference_cloak_desc-u:
You scramble your electronic signature — the identifier that smart targeting systems use to distinguish a valid target from background noise. For one round you are noise. The turret can still swing toward where it thinks you are. It just can't be certain enough to fire clean.

Effect: For 1 round, sensor-guided weapons, smart targeting systems, and automated fire-control treat you as Hidden. They may still attack your general position but do so without targeting assistance — they cannot benefit from Smartlink bonuses, sensor locks, or similar targeting aids for attacks against you this round. Manual unassisted observation by living creatures is unaffected — this scrambles machine targeting, not human eyes. Stacks naturally with physical cover and Prism Screen. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_tec_interference_cloak_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Reflexive; 1 round; sensor-guided weapons treat you as Hidden
spell_tec_magelock_circuit_desc-u:
Invisible latch-threads cinch across an interface, freezing all inputs like a keypad gone to sleep mid-press. Unlike the specific glitch of Glyph Rewrite, Magelock locks the device out entirely — nothing new gets in until the lock breaks.

Effect: Choose one device or terminal in Short range. It attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, its controls lock for 1 round — no new inputs are accepted, mode changes cannot be made, and queued actions stall. At the end of the round, you may Sustain the lock without paying additional Strain, extending it by another round. Each new round the device makes a fresh Device Resistance 40% roll to break free. On a success, the lock ends. Ending your Concentration also ends the effect. Implanted cyberware is never affected.
spell_tec_magelock_circuit_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; lock device controls 1 round; may Sustain
spell_tec_network_map_desc-u:
You push a diagnostic thread through the local Veil-lattice and read what the network is hiding before it can use it against you. Where a standard Deep Scan costs a full round inside the network, this costs you Strain instead — buying the same intelligence without burning the clock.

Effect: While connected to a network during the Execution Loop, you may cast this spell as a Free Action at the start of your turn before declaring your Action for that round. It performs a Deep Scan equivalent: you immediately learn what traps, hidden protections, and anomalous assets are present in the network that were not revealed by the Baseline Scan. This replaces the need to spend your Action on a standard Deep Scan, allowing you to act on the intelligence in the same round you gathered it. Network Map cannot be used outside of an active slicing connection and cannot be used on a turn when you have already taken a standard Deep Scan action.
spell_tec_network_map_notes-u: Free Action; while connected, perform a Deep Scan without spending your Action; reveals hidden traps and anomalous assets
spell_tec_null_thread_desc-u:
You snap a single strand out of the lattice and the hostile effect unravels, leaving a clean seam and a headache where power used to be.

Effect: Trigger this spell when a spell or Slicing effect targets an allied device, drone, vehicle, or OmniDeck within Short range. Make a Spellcasting roll and compare its success tier to the triggering roll. If your tier is equal or higher, the effect is canceled for that target and the original attacker suffers 1d6 Strain. If you fail or roll a lower tier, no effect. Null-Thread cannot counter effects that target living beings or implanted cyberware directly.
spell_tec_null_thread_notes-u: Reaction; Contested vs triggering roll; cancel spell or Slicing targeting allied device/drone/vehicle
spell_tec_overclock_desc-u:
You goose the voltage and whisper little lies to the safeties. For a while, the machine believes it is better than it is — and for a while, it's right.

Effect: Choose one allied device or drone within Short range. For as long as you maintain Concentration through the scene, it gains +10% to a single chosen function — gunnery, tracking, maneuvering, or similar — and ignores 1 penalty die on checks tied to that function. When Overclock ends, the target suffers a brief thermal setback: -10% to its next action, or the GM may impose a short cooldown period on purely thermal systems before they can act again. Overclock cannot be cast on implanted cyberware.
spell_tec_overclock_notes-u: Uncontested; buff one allied device/drone; +10% chosen function; ignore 1 penalty die; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_relay_handshake_desc-u:
Two devices kiss through a hardlight bridge, and for three breaths the air-gap is not there. Long enough to coordinate a sequence, issue a chain of commands, or confirm a signal that jamming was keeping apart.

Effect: Touch two devices and create a temporary hardlight bridge that bypasses local jamming and air-gaps. For 3 rounds they can exchange simple commands or telemetry as though directly linked. Bulk file transfers, firmware updates, and similar heavy operations are not possible — this is a control-layer link, not a data pipeline. If either device requires authentication, those checks still apply. If either device breaks contact with you before the duration ends, the bridge collapses immediately.
spell_tec_relay_handshake_notes-u: Uncontested; bridge two devices through air-gap/jamming; 3 rounds
spell_tec_reroute_node_desc-u:
You do not fight the system so much as convince it that 'over there' is 'here,' and 'now' is 'later.' The machine follows its protocols exactly. They just happen to be wrong.

Effect: Choose a single node or control system in Short range. It attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, establish a bias in its behavior for the scene while you maintain Concentration. Choose one of the following: change targeting so the node treats a designated decoy or safe object as its valid target; misdirect alarms so that alerts flag a false zone or harmless subsystem; or delay its actionable output so that effects such as opening doors, firing turrets, updating logs, or pulsing alarms arrive one full round later than they should. Once chosen, the behavior remains fixed for the duration. The node's controller can end your influence by physically power-cycling the hardware or severing its outbound link. On a successful resistance, no effect. Implanted cyberware is never affected.
spell_tec_reroute_node_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; change targeting, misdirect alarms, or delay output; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_restore_substrate_desc-u:
Cold light traces fracture-lines and coaxes dead pathways to knit — like solder remembering the right shape. The machine doesn't thank you. It just starts working again.

Effect: Choose a drone, construct, vehicle system, or unattended device in Short range. It recovers 2d6 HP up to its normal maximum. You may only use Restore Substrate once per scene per target. This effect is not biological healing and does not interact with Healing Saturation or similar rules. It cannot repair components that are entirely missing or irreparably destroyed.
spell_tec_restore_substrate_notes-u: Uncontested; restore 2d6 HP to drone/construct/vehicle system; once per Scene per target
spell_tec_safeguard_sigil_desc-u:
You press a thread of Veil-logic into the machine's substrate — not a lock, but a scar. Remote hands that try to take hold find the grip keeps slipping. The machine remembers what it belongs to.

Effect: Touch one drone, vehicle, bot, or physical archanotech device. For the scene, any Technomancy spell targeting that asset suffers 1 penalty die on the casting roll. Any Contested Slicing Check made to seize, disable, or hijack that specific asset — such as a Drone Seize attempt or an Endpoint intrusion — is made with 1 penalty die. These penalties apply to hostile attempts only; allied Technomancy and Slicing interact with the asset normally. Safeguard Sigil has no effect on full network intrusions through an OmniDeck and cannot be placed on a living creature or implanted cyberware. A given asset can only carry one Safeguard Sigil at a time — casting it again on the same target replaces the existing ward.
spell_tec_safeguard_sigil_notes-u: Touch; scene; drone/vehicle/bot gains 1 penalty die vs hostile Technomancy and Endpoint Slicing attempts
spell_tec_sensor_ghost_desc-u:
You seed the cone with believable lies — pings that look right, move right, and keep watchers looking in the wrong place. The sensors aren't broken. They're just confidently incorrect.

Effect: Sensors within the cone each make one Device Resistance 40% roll when first affected. On a failed resistance, that sensor treats you and your designated allies as Hidden for the scene while both the sensor and the targets remain inside the cone. On a success, it reads you normally for the scene. Moving a sensor or target out of the cone and back calls for a new single roll on re-entry. Exotic detectors such as thermal or archanometric arrays are still sensors for this spell. Sensor Ghost does not affect living observers directly — only what their instruments tell them.
spell_tec_sensor_ghost_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance per sensor; Short cone; you and allies Hidden to affected sensors; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_signal_ping_desc-u:
A clean, bright sweep goes out and comes back with answers in tow. Not analysis — just the shape of what's there and where it is.

Effect: Reveal active devices, nodes, and live links in Short range and their general position. Choose one detected source — you gain +20% to your next locate, analyze, or interface check against that source this scene. Signal Ping is a pre-entry detection tool, useful for identifying targets and establishing access vectors before committing to a slicing attempt.
spell_tec_signal_ping_notes-u: Uncontested; reveal active devices/nodes in Short; +20% next locate/analyze/interface this Scene
spell_tec_signal_veil_desc-u:
The world hums and forgets you. Cameras drift, mics dull, and recognizers get everyone's faces wrong in the same harmless way. The crew isn't hidden — the city has simply decided they aren't there.

Effect: On cast, designate your crew. While you maintain Concentration, standard cameras, motion sensors, and recognition systems inside the aura fail to detect or correctly identify the crew unless they succeed on a Device Resistance 40% roll at the moment they would detect you. Exotic detectors such as thermal arrays or archanometric scanners attempt detection with 1 penalty die and also require Device Resistance 40% to succeed. A given system rolls at most once per scene while continuously in range. If either the system or the crew leaves the aura and re-enters, it may roll again. Signal Veil does not prevent direct physical observation by unaided eyes.
spell_tec_signal_veil_notes-u: Contested vs Device Resistance; Medium aura; crew invisible to standard cams/motion/recognition; Scene (Conc)
spell_tec_smartshot_assist_desc-u:
Your hardlight reticle nudges the barrel a breath to the left, and the shot goes where the shooter wanted all along. A gift of half a second and a perfect angle.

Effect: Trigger this spell when an ally with an active Smartlink makes a ranged attack within Short range of you. That attack gains +10% to hit. This bonus stacks with the Smartlink's own modifier, up to a total of +20% combined. For that attack, the target's Cover is treated as one step lower. Smartshot Assist does not stack with other effects that reduce Cover by the same step for a single attack — use only the strongest applicable source.
spell_tec_smartshot_assist_notes-u: Reaction; Uncontested; ally with Smartlink gains +10% to hit; Cover one step lower for that attack
spell_tec_traceback_mark_desc-u:
A luminous thread anchors in a live link and points homeward like a compass that knows where the lie started.

Effect: Choose an active live link in Medium range. The connected endpoint attempts Device Resistance 40%. On a failed resistance, you brand the link's origin — you learn the physical room or zone in which the origin resides, gain +20% to intercept traffic from that source for the scene, and receive a single directional update if the origin moves before the scene ends. The mark ends early if the link is severed or fully powered down. On a successful resistance, no effect.
spell_tec_traceback_mark_notes-u: Uncontested; brand live link origin; learn physical location; +20% to intercept this Scene
spell_tec_veil_thread_pulse_desc-u:
A diagnostic ripple tastes for otherworldly fingerprints where code meets intention. Some machines carry more than their specs suggest. This finds it.

Effect: You scan machines and constructs in Short range for Veil influence — bindings, glamours anchored to hardware, latent triggers, and similar effects. Detection is automatic within the spell's scope; you know which devices are affected and can sense the general strength of the enchantment. To purge a low-tier effect anchored to a machine, make a Spellcasting roll opposed by the original casting roll if known. If the original roll is unknown, treat the device as rolling Device Resistance 40% on behalf of the effect. On a successful purge, the anchored Veil effect ends. On a failure, it remains. Veil Thread Pulse does not dispel effects on living beings or implanted cyberware.
spell_tec_veil_thread_pulse_notes-u: Uncontested; detect Veil influence in machines; may dispel low-tier bindings/latent effects
spell_uni_anchor_field_desc-u:
You establish a ward that pins local arcane geometry into stability. Within the field, teleportation falters, dimensional breaches resist, and Veil disruptions bend back upon themselves. The effect is visible to magical sight as glowing threads lashing the air to the ground.

Effect: While active, the area within Medium range of you is treated as Anchored. Teleportation, phase-shifting, or dimensional movement into or out of the area automatically fails unless the effect's caster achieves a higher success tier than your original Magic roll. Veil breaches flicker but cannot fully open within the field. You may end the spell early as a free action.
spell_uni_anchor_field_notes-u: Self-centered; Medium area; blocks teleportation and dimensional movement; 5 minutes
spell_uni_arcane_attunement_desc-u:
You draw the threads of the Veil tighter around your mind, stretching your capacity to hold more structured intent than your training strictly allows. It costs something — a persistent low hum behind the eyes, a slight thinning of your reserves — but for the scene you can hold one more formula in memory than you otherwise could.

Effect: For the scene, your Arcane Capacity increases by 1. This extra slot may be filled with any spell you have access to, but the spell must be memorized after casting Arcane Attunement — it does not retroactively open a slot you have already filled. The slot disappears at the end of the scene, and any spell memorized into it is lost. Only one Arcane Attunement may be active at a time.
spell_uni_arcane_attunement_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; +1 Arcane Capacity for the scene; must be cast before memorizing the extra spell
spell_uni_arcane_pulse_desc-u:
You lash out with a sudden ripple of raw magical force, striking your target with a concussive arcane burst. It knocks back loose debris and leaves a faint shimmer in the air — blunt, immediate, and school-agnostic.

Effect: On a successful Spellcasting roll, the target takes 1d10 Physical force damage and is pushed 1 range band directly away from you. If the push would drive them into a solid obstacle, they are Knocked Prone at the impact point. This spell deals Physical damage and interacts with Physical Soak normally.
spell_uni_arcane_pulse_notes-u: Targeted; Short; 1d10 Physical force; target pushed 1 band on hit
spell_uni_arcane_relay_desc-u:
You send a brief magical pulse carrying a verbal message or Veil signature to a known target. The spell rides the arcane lattice like a signal ping, leaving a psychic echo in its wake.

Effect: Transmit a one-sentence message or arcane mark to a known target within Long range. The target hears the message in their mind and recognizes you as the sender. If they are warded against mental intrusion or are unconscious, the spell fails harmlessly. May also be used to trigger a Veilmark or magical trap you have linked to your signature.
spell_uni_arcane_relay_notes-u: Uncontested; Long; transmit one-sentence message or Veil signature to known target
spell_uni_arcane_shield_desc-u:
You weave a field of translucent arcane energy around yourself, forming a flickering defense that absorbs incoming harm. It shimmers faintly when struck, disrupting force and magic alike.

Effect: Reduces all incoming damage by 4 from any source. This does not stack with armor — use whichever value is higher. The shield ends if you cast another sustained spell. It does not distinguish between damage types — Physical, Veil, and Strain damage are all reduced equally.
spell_uni_arcane_shield_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Sustained; reduces all incoming damage by 4
spell_uni_arcane_shroud_desc-u:
A translucent shimmer of raw Veil energy wraps around you like a spectral mantle. It distorts light and absorbs stray magical charge, responding subtly to your will and intent.

Effect: You gain +1 Veil Soak for the scene. This stacks with Light armor but not with other magical armor effects. Once per scene, when you would suffer the Chilled condition, you may choose to negate it entirely. This spell does not stack with Arcane Shield or similar sustained defensive effects.
spell_uni_arcane_shroud_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Scene; +1 Veil Soak; negate Chilled once per scene
spell_uni_arcane_signature_desc-u:
With a subtle act of will, you inscribe your magical essence into a surface, spell, or object. The mark is not visible to mundane sight but glows faintly to those with arcane awareness. This is your personal GPS tag — a mark you can feel through the Veil and follow back to its location.

Effect: The marked object becomes traceable by you through arcane means for the scene. You gain +10% to Arcana or Tracking when attempting to follow, recover, locate, or analyze the marked target. The mark is personal — only you benefit from it. Where Veilmark creates a public beacon others can use for ritual targeting, Arcane Signature is a private thread only you can follow. Cannot stack with Veilmark on the same object.
spell_uni_arcane_signature_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene; personal traceable mark; +10% to locate/track/analyze marked target
spell_uni_counterspell_desc-u:
As magic takes shape in another's hands, you intercept the spell mid-formation, severing its energy threads before they stabilize. Veil-twisting pressure erupts in a brief flash. This is the one Universal spell that tests your casting skill directly against another caster's — the countering character rolls their best Magic skill against the original caster's.

Effect: Cast as a Reaction to a spell being cast within Medium range and line of sight. Make a contested roll using your best Magic skill against the original caster's Magic skill. If you achieve a higher success tier, the targeted spell is cancelled and has no effect. The original caster still pays all resources for the spell — Strain is lost regardless. On a failure or tie, the spell proceeds normally. Cannot be used blind or through solid barriers unless the spell itself is visible or the caster is being actively tracked.
spell_uni_counterspell_notes-u: Reaction; Contested vs Magic; Medium; negate a spell being cast
spell_uni_detect_magic_desc-u:
You extend your senses into the Veil, detecting magical resonance, enchantments, and arcane disruptions in the area. Faint trails of power glow in your vision, showing where magic has been used, where it currently operates, and what kind of force shaped it.

Effect: Reveals spells, magical objects, and Veil anomalies within Short range. You become aware of their presence and approximate location. With a successful Arcana check you can identify the school of magic associated with an effect and its general strength. Grants a +1 bonus die to Arcana or Veil Lore checks for the remainder of the scene when analyzing anything the spell revealed. Does not identify specific spell types without the Arcana check, and does not perceive spiritual contamination or Veil scarring — for that, see Veil Sight.
spell_uni_detect_magic_notes-u: Uncontested; Short cone or pulse; reveals spells/magical objects/Veil anomalies; Scene
spell_uni_dispel_magic_desc-u:
You seize a strand of magic from the air and snap it, unraveling an ongoing spell with sheer force of will. The Veil shudders as two conflicting patterns clash — yours insisting the effect is over, the original working insisting it is not.

Effect: Target one active spell, ward, or magical effect within Medium range. Make a contested Magic roll against the original caster's Magic skill. If the caster is not present or the effect has no living source, the GM sets an appropriate threshold. On a success, the magic ends immediately. On a failure, the effect persists. Persistent or layered effects may require multiple successful Dispel attempts to fully remove at the GM's discretion. This spell ends effects — it does not suppress them. For suppression, see Quiet the Veil.
spell_uni_dispel_magic_notes-u: Contested vs original caster; Medium; end one active spell or magical effect
spell_uni_feedback_lash_desc-u:
You anchor a shimmering arcane filament to your target, drawing power from them and disrupting their focus. The tether crackles with Strain, responding to each pulse of magic the target attempts. For the caster with patience, this is a slow drain that compounds — each round the target fails to shake it is a round they slide closer to exhaustion.

Effect: The targeted creature must roll POW opposed by your Magic skill each round to resist the lash. On a failure, they suffer 1d4 Strain damage — bypassing all Soak — and a -10% penalty to their next Magic roll. On a success, they resist the lash that round but remain tethered. The tether does not deal Strain to you to maintain beyond the initial cast cost — it is a sustained channel, not a per-round expenditure. The effect ends if the target moves to Long range or farther, if you lose line of sight, or if you cast another sustained spell. Only one Feedback Lash may be maintained at a time.
spell_uni_feedback_lash_notes-u: Contested (POW); Sustained; Medium; 1d4 Strain/round to target; -10% next Magic roll on fail
spell_uni_flashbind_desc-u:
With a snap of your focus, you release a pulse of Veil-warped brilliance that overloads nearby senses. It's not just light — it's pressure, sound, confusion. Minds reel before bodies can react.

Effect: All creatures in Engaged range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, they become Disoriented — suffering -10% to their next action — and lose their Reaction until the end of their next turn. On a success, no effect. Targets with arcane sight or enhanced optics gain no particular advantage against this effect — it is not purely visual but a full sensory overload. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_uni_flashbind_notes-u: Contested (CON); Engaged (Reflexive); AoE; Disoriented and lose Reaction on fail
spell_uni_force_step_desc-u:
You blink forward in a ripple of arcane momentum, propelled by a sudden surge of Veil power. It is not teleportation — it is will-accelerated movement, the space between here and there compressed into an instant.

Effect: Instantly move up to 1 range band in any direction. You may pass through occupied space or over low obstacles. You do not provoke attacks of opportunity from this movement.
spell_uni_force_step_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; instant; move 1 range band; no AoO
spell_uni_imbue_object_desc-u:
You charge a mundane object with arcane residue, granting it temporary interaction with magical systems. It glows faintly with borrowed potential, and its presence triggers Veil-reactive elements as if it were genuinely enchanted.

Effect: A non-magical item becomes temporarily treated as magical for the purposes of detection, wards, sensors, and rituals. The object radiates a faint magical signature and may be used to fool scrying spells, proximity glyphs, or arcane authentication systems. Grants +10% to checks involving arcane misdirection or ritual substitutions. The enchantment fades at the end of the scene or once triggered by a significant magical response — whichever comes first.
spell_uni_imbue_object_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Scene; mundane object registers as magical; fools detection and wards
spell_uni_mage_light_desc-u:
You conjure a soft magical glow that floats beside you or clings to a chosen item. It hums gently with Veil energy and can be adjusted with a thought — brighter, dimmer, colored differently, repositioned. The simplest spell in any caster's repertoire. Still used by masters.

Effect: Illuminates a 10-meter radius in the color and brightness of your choice. The light moves with you or remains in place at your discretion. It can be reduced to a faint glimmer or expanded to fill a room. Harmless and cannot be used offensively in any meaningful way.
spell_uni_mage_light_notes-u: Uncontested; Self or Object (Short); Scene; adjustable magical light source
spell_uni_manifest_tool_desc-u:
You conjure a small, semi-autonomous magical construct — a spectral hand, a floating tool, a translucent manipulator — capable of performing simple tasks or activating devices at a short distance.

Effect: Conjure a floating arcane manipulator that can perform one-handed tasks within Short range: press buttons, open unlocked doors, retrieve light objects, trigger glyphs, hold a panel steady, or support a repair attempt. It can lift, push, or manipulate objects up to a few kilograms. It cannot attack, carry weapons, or use complex tools requiring fine motor skill. The spell ends early if the manipulator is attacked or blocked by an anti-magic field.
spell_uni_manifest_tool_notes-u: Uncontested; Short; Scene; floating arcane manipulator for simple one-handed tasks
spell_uni_memory_trace_desc-u:
You brush your will against a target's surface thoughts and emotional residue. It is not full mind-reading — just a dip into what is echoing closest to consciousness, or burned into recent memory like a handprint on warm wax.

Effect: The target rolls POW opposed by your Magic skill. On a failure, you glimpse one of the following — the GM's choice or determined randomly: a vivid emotional imprint from the last 10 minutes, a surface-level thought they have had in the last minute, or a strong visual memory from the current scene. On a success, no information is revealed, but the target feels a momentary chill that tells them something tried to enter. Creatures with no mind or consciousness are immune.
spell_uni_memory_trace_notes-u: Contested (POW); Engaged; instant; read surface thoughts or recent emotional imprint
spell_uni_mystic_disruption_desc-u:
You send a destabilizing pulse of raw arcane static into the target's body or circuitry, disrupting flow and focus. The air warps faintly where the pulse strikes — a visible ripple in the space between the physical world and the Veil.

Effect: On a successful Spellcasting roll, the target takes 1d10 Veil damage and suffers -10% to their next Magic roll. This penalty applies to the first Magic roll made before the end of the target's next turn, then ends. This spell deals Veil damage and works against any target a Veil attack would normally affect.
spell_uni_mystic_disruption_notes-u: Targeted; Medium; 1d10 Veil damage; -10% to target's next Magic roll
spell_uni_quiet_the_veil_desc-u:
You suppress ambient arcane resonance in the surrounding area, silencing glows, echoes, and lingering auras. The air hums with dense quiet — a hollow between realities. Magic still works here. It just cannot be seen or traced easily.

Effect: Creates a zone of dampened magical visibility extending to Short range around you for 1 minute. Magical perception and detection checks made from within or against the zone suffer -20%. Casters attempting to maintain or analyze spells inside the zone suffer -10% to Arcana unless using a focus. This spell does not prevent casting — it suppresses magical visibility, aura detection, and spellprint traceability. It does not move with you — it is anchored to the point where you cast it.
spell_uni_quiet_the_veil_notes-u: Uncontested; Short (centered); 1 minute; suppresses magical visibility and detection in area
spell_uni_resonant_echo_desc-u:
You attune your senses to the memory of the Veil, revealing traces of spells cast in the area. Arcane trails flicker through your vision — remnants of purpose, emotion, and arcane will impressed on the local fabric of reality.

Effect: Reveals when and where spells were cast within Short range during the current or previous scene. Detects activity within Short range around you and identifies the approximate school of magic involved if the traces are strong enough. Grants +1 bonus die to Insight or Arcana when analyzing magical residue detected by this spell. Cannot penetrate arcane wards or suppression fields.
spell_uni_resonant_echo_notes-u: Uncontested; Short pulse; reveals recent spell use in area; +1 bonus die to analyze magical residue; Scene
spell_uni_seal_of_binding_desc-u:
You inscribe a temporary sigil of arcane force upon a doorway, container, or passage. The glyph pulses with subtle pressure, resisting all but brute force or counter-spellwork. Creatures who touch it without permission feel an ominous chill.

Effect: Locks and seals a single door, hatch, container, or object. Non-magical attempts to open or bypass it require a Hard STR check or a Hard Mechanics check. Dispel Magic or a successful Arcana roll opposed by your Magic skill may break the seal early. Does not affect electronic locks but may suppress mage locks or overlap with physical warding. The seal persists for 10 minutes or until dispelled.
spell_uni_seal_of_binding_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; 10 minutes; arcane lock on door/container; requires Hard STR or Arcana to break
spell_uni_spellshock_surge_desc-u:
With a flash of intent, you release a disruptive wave of arcane feedback. The surge courses through nearby beings and constructs, scrambling thoughts and overloading circuits in a burst of Veil-born interference that leaves everyone in reach momentarily rattled.

Effect: All creatures in Engaged range roll CON opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, they take 2d8 Veil damage and become Chilled until the end of their next turn — their next action or reaction is slowed and reduced in effectiveness. On a success, no effect. This spell is Reflexive and does not suffer the in-melee casting penalty.
spell_uni_spellshock_surge_notes-u: Contested (CON); Engaged (Reflexive); AoE; 2d8 Veil + Chilled on fail
spell_uni_veil_flicker_desc-u:
You blur the boundaries of your presence, creating a shimmering distortion that tricks the senses. You don't become invisible — you become forgettable. Half-glimpsed at the edge of perception, present but unregistered, like a thought the mind dismisses before it fully forms.

Effect: Gain +20% to Stealth rolls while in dim light or shadow. Anyone attempting to directly target you with an attack or ability must succeed on an Insight check opposed by your Arcana or suffer a penalty die on the attempt. Ends early if you attack or cast a non-subtle spell. Detect Magic reveals your presence but not your exact location. This is not invisibility — you remain partially visible in direct light, and determined observers who are actively looking for you are not fooled.
spell_uni_veil_flicker_notes-u: Uncontested; Self; Sustained; +20% Stealth in dim light or shadow; partial concealment
spell_uni_veil_snare_desc-u:
You manipulate unstable threads of the Veil around your target, binding them with force that saps movement and will. Their limbs hesitate, their flow disrupted by invisible resistance — the world briefly refusing to cooperate with their intentions.

Effect: The target rolls DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, they become Impaired until the end of their next turn — they may only take either one Action or one Maneuver, not both. On a success, no effect.
spell_uni_veil_snare_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Short; 1 round; Impaired on fail
spell_uni_veilmark_desc-u:
You imprint a subtle sigil of Veil-infused glyphs onto a surface, item, or willing target. The mark is invisible to mundane perception but detectable through arcane means. Unlike Arcane Signature — which is a personal tracking mark only you can follow — Veilmark is a public anchor, detectable by anyone with arcane senses and usable as a ritual target point.

Effect: Creates a persistent magical beacon detectable by Detect Magic, Resonant Echo, scrying rituals, and similar effects. May serve as a sympathetic anchor for teleportation, ritual targeting, or future spellwork that requires a known location. Any caster who detects the mark may use it as an arcane reference point. Only one active Veilmark may be maintained by a caster at a time — placing a new one ends the previous. Cannot stack with Arcane Signature on the same object.
spell_uni_veilmark_notes-u: Uncontested; Touch; Until Dispelled; arcane beacon for ritual targeting and tracking
spell_war_bastion_of_glass_desc-u:
A skin of shard-light orbits you like patient knives, catching neon and knuckles alike. It doesn't stop hits — it makes them expensive. Anyone close enough to swing learns quickly that proximity has a price.

Effect: While active, when a creature in Engaged range hits you with a melee attack, it must succeed on a Dodge roll opposed by your casting roll or take 1d6 Veil damage from the deflected force. This triggers once per attacker per round. Ranged and AoE attacks are unaffected — the glass catches swings, not bullets.
spell_war_bastion_of_glass_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); melee attackers take 1d6 Veil on miss vs Dodge; once/attacker/round
spell_war_counterpulse_desc-u:
You spike a syncopated pulse into a forming spell, pushing its timing just far enough off to matter. The caster feels it like a stutter in their own rhythm — there and gone, but enough.

Effect: When a creature within Short begins a Targeted spell, you may cast this as a Reaction and oppose with a POW roll. On a success, impose 1 penalty die on that spell's casting roll. On a failure, no effect. Does not affect AoE spells or passive effects — only spells with a specific target.
spell_war_counterpulse_notes-u: Reaction; Contested (POW); Short; impose 1 penalty die on a Targeted spell being cast
spell_war_cursebreak_sigil_desc-u:
A surgical, unthreading glyph that finds the knot in an active working and snaps it cleanly. No flourish, no spectacle — just the sound of something structured becoming unstructured.

Effect: Choose one visible ongoing magical effect or anchored ward within Short. Contest against the effect's original casting roll, or the active sustainer's POW if it is being maintained. On a success, that single effect ends immediately. On a failure, no effect. Non-magical conditions and passive traits are unaffected.
spell_war_cursebreak_sigil_notes-u: Contested vs original roll; Short; end one visible ongoing effect or sigil
spell_war_interdict_sigil_desc-u:
A sudden hard angle that shaves the edge off an impact at the last possible heartbeat. It doesn't stop the hit — it edits it.

Effect: When a creature — you or an ally — within Short range is hit, reduce the final damage by 2. This reduction applies to Physical or Veil damage equally. Declare after the hit is confirmed but before damage is applied. Once per round regardless of how many Reactions you have available.
spell_war_interdict_sigil_notes-u: Reaction; Short; reduce one hit's damage by 2 (Physical or Veil); once/round
spell_war_mirror_prison_desc-u:
A reflective shell snaps shut around the target, showing their momentum back to them and holding it there. They can see out. They simply cannot move — as though the world has decided their position is final.

Effect: On a failed POW roll, the target is Bound for 1 round. While Bound they cannot move or take actions but remain fully aware and may speak. If resisted, no effect. The shell is visible to anyone present and detectable by Sanctum Trace and Detect Magic alike.
spell_war_mirror_prison_notes-u: Contested (POW); Short; target Bound 1 round; aware, can speak, no actions or movement
spell_war_mirror_veil_desc-u:
A hair-thin mirror flickers into being, catching a pointed spell and bending it toward a more deserving face. Personal, precise, and immediate — this is defense for the individual, not the team.

Effect: Trigger when a Targeted spell selects you as its target. Oppose the original casting roll. On a success, choose a new valid target within Short range — the spell resolves against that target instead, using the original casting roll. On a failure, the spell proceeds against you as intended. Does not affect AoE spells, ongoing effects, or passive enchantments.
spell_war_mirror_veil_notes-u: Reaction vs original roll; Targeted only; retarget spell within Short; no effect on AoE
spell_war_mute_halo_desc-u:
Air turns heavy and absorbent around you — footsteps sink, whispers stay local, plans survive proximity. The halo doesn't silence the world. It keeps your world from reaching theirs.

Effect: Within your Engaged range, non-magical sound is dampened. Hearing-based Perception checks made to detect creatures inside the halo suffer -20%. Speech, command words, and spellcasting function normally within the halo — communication between those inside is unaffected. Sounds originating outside are unchanged.
spell_war_mute_halo_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); damps sound in Engaged; Hearing Perception -20% vs those inside
spell_war_null_bubble_desc-u:
A low static field that makes sigils feel gummy and uncooperative for anyone working from inside it — including you. Casting here is possible. It's just harder than it should be.

Effect: All creatures casting spells from within your Engaged-range bubble — including you — suffer -10% to their Spellcasting rolls. Ongoing effects already in place continue normally. Spells cast from outside into the bubble are unaffected. The field moves with you.
spell_war_null_bubble_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); casting from within -10% Spellcasting; ongoing effects unaffected
spell_war_null_lattice_desc-u:
You sketch a geometric fence in the air and space reads the sign, refusing shortcuts. Corridors stay corridors. Doors stay doors. The lattice doesn't stop determination — it stops convenience.

Effect: Establish a boundary within Short — a doorway, corridor choke, or ring — up to Engaged in width. While sustained, teleportation, phase-shifting, and similar dimensional displacement cannot cross the boundary and automatically fail, wasting the action and any resources spent. Invisible creatures that physically cross the boundary briefly shimmer as reality reasserts itself, revealing their crossing though not necessarily their exact form. Mundane movement is otherwise unaffected — the lattice stops shortcuts, not steps.
spell_war_null_lattice_notes-u: Zone; Short; Scene (Conc); blocks teleport/phase/invisible passage at boundary
spell_war_prism_barricade_desc-u:
A sheet of hard light rises — a bright, angular verdict that turns lanes into dead ends. It doesn't argue. It simply exists where passage used to be.

Effect: Create a continuous barrier within Short that provides total cover and blocks movement entirely. The barrier has no HP track and cannot be chipped away by conventional means. Only effects that explicitly breach barriers — heavy ordnance, specific spells, or archanotech — may contest your casting roll to open a gap. Otherwise the wall holds for the scene. It does not retroactively cancel effects already resolved beyond it, and it does not move once placed.
spell_war_prism_barricade_notes-u: Wall; Short; Scene (Conc); total cover; blocks movement; no HP track
spell_war_prism_screen_desc-u:
Panes of refracted light drift around you, clipping sightlines and making clean shots second-guess themselves. You don't disappear — you become harder to be certain about.

Effect: Ranged attacks against you suffer 1 penalty die for the scene, and you gain +10% to Dodge rolls against ranged attacks. Neither effect applies to melee or AoE attacks.
spell_war_prism_screen_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); ranged attacks vs you suffer 1 penalty die; +10% Dodge vs Ranged
spell_war_prismatic_reversal_desc-u:
Your palm blooms a many-faced mirror and the spell recognizes its owner, turning heel. Where Mirror Veil redirects a threat sideways, Prismatic Reversal sends it home — or chooses a new destination with more reach and more authority.

Effect: Trigger when a Targeted spell is cast at you. Oppose the original casting roll. On a success, reflect the spell back at the original caster. If the caster is not a valid target for their own spell, choose a different valid target within Medium range instead. On a failure, no effect. Does not affect AoE spells or passive ongoing effects.
spell_war_prismatic_reversal_notes-u: Reaction vs original roll; Targeted only; reflect back at caster or retarget within Medium
spell_war_redirective_nexus_desc-u:
A pop-up crossroads for hostile magic — your hand decides which lane it takes. Where Mirror Veil and Prismatic Reversal protect you, this one reaches out to cover someone else entirely.

Effect: Trigger when a Targeted spell is cast at any creature within Short range of you. Oppose the original casting roll. On a success, choose a new valid target within Short range — the spell resolves there instead. On a failure, no effect. Does not affect AoE spells or passive effects. The redirected target must be a valid target for the original spell.
spell_war_redirective_nexus_notes-u: Reaction vs original roll; Targeted only; yank spell aimed at ally to new target within Short
spell_war_ricochet_seal_desc-u:
A ward that speaks to angles rather than force — when a ranged shot misses you, the seal reads the trajectory and sends something back along it. Not the bullet. Just the consequence.

Effect: While active, whenever a ranged attack misses you, the attacker takes 1d6 Veil damage as deflected force rebounds along the shot's path. This triggers automatically — no additional roll is required. It does not trigger on AoE effects or area denial, only direct ranged attacks with a specific trajectory. The damage applies once per missed attack regardless of how many shots miss in a burst.
spell_war_ricochet_seal_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); missed ranged attacks rebound for 1d6 Veil to attacker; Reflexive
spell_war_sanctum_halo_desc-u:
A soft, steady crown settles on the ground — hits land duller inside it, and blood remembers when to stop. The halo is anchored in place; those who step outside it leave its protection behind.

Effect: Place an Engaged-sized aura at a point within Short range. Allies inside gain +1 Physical Soak and +1 Veil Soak. At the end of each of their turns inside the halo, they may attempt to end the Burning or Bleeding condition with a successful CON roll — no action required. Benefits pause the moment they leave the halo and resume on re-entry.
spell_war_sanctum_halo_notes-u: Zone; Engaged-sized; allies inside +1 Physical Soak and +1 Veil Soak; CON to end Burning/Bleeding
spell_war_sanctum_trace_desc-u:
Faint schematics overlay your vision — hidden circuits glow where the city keeps its secrets. You read the room not as it appears, but as it is actually structured beneath the surface.

Effect: You detect wards, sigils, and active magical effects within Medium range as luminous lines visible only to you. For each detected effect you learn its anchor point and its broad function — protection, alarm, charm, displacement, suppression, and so on. This does not reveal passwords, specific triggers, or non-magical traps, and it does not identify the caster.
spell_war_sanctum_trace_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); detect wards/sigils/active magic within Medium; shows anchor and function
spell_war_seal_of_denial_desc-u:
One clean canceling stroke — an arithmetic error the spell cannot recover from. Unlike Counterpulse, which disrupts a spell in progress, Seal of Denial erases it entirely. The caster paid their price for nothing.

Effect: When any spell is being cast within Medium range, oppose the caster's roll. On a success, cancel the spell entirely — it has no effect and all resources spent on it are lost regardless. On a failure, no effect. Works on both Targeted and AoE spells, making it the broadest counter in the school and the correct upgrade from Counterpulse for those who want absolute cancellation rather than disruption.
spell_war_seal_of_denial_notes-u: Reaction vs caster's roll; cancel a spell as it is cast; Targeted or AoE
spell_war_seal_of_shuttering_desc-u:
A neat lock-rune presses into place — doors remember they're doors and stay that way. The first uninvited hand tells you exactly where it came from.

Effect: Ward a single door, container, access panel, or similar object. Unauthorized attempts to open or bypass it suffer -20% to relevant skill checks. The first unauthorized opening attempt — successful or not — silently pings you with the direction it came from. A pass-phrase you designate at the time of casting allows free passage without triggering the ping or the penalty. This spell does not add structural hit points to the warded object.
spell_war_seal_of_shuttering_notes-u: Touch; Scene (Conc); ward a door/object; unauthorized opening -20 and silent ping
spell_war_shatter_hex_matrix_desc-u:
You cast a net of counter-angles that snags multiple workings simultaneously and tears them along their weakest seams. Where Cursebreak Sigil is a scalpel on one thread, this is a hand closing around a fistful of them.

Effect: Choose up to three visible ongoing magical effects or anchored sigils within Short range. For each, contest against its original casting roll or the active sustainer's POW. On a success for each individual effect, it ends immediately. On a failure for a specific effect, that effect persists. Resolve each contest separately — success on one does not guarantee success on others.
spell_war_shatter_hex_matrix_notes-u: Short; Instant; up to three visible effects/sigils; contest each vs original roll
spell_war_shelter_sigil_desc-u:
A compact aura of steadying force moves with you, keeping pace as you advance, withdraw, or reposition. Unlike a placed ward, it goes where you go — the protection travels with the warder rather than waiting for the team to find it.

Effect: An Engaged-radius aura of arcane dampening moves with you for the scene. Any ally within Engaged range of you gains +1 Veil Soak. The benefit applies dynamically — allies who move into your Engaged range gain it immediately, and allies who leave it lose it immediately. This is the mobile counterpart to Sanctum Halo, trading the Physical Soak bonus and condition removal for freedom of movement.
spell_war_shelter_sigil_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); mobile aura; allies adjacent to you gain +1 Veil Soak
spell_war_suppression_dome_desc-u:
A hush-heavy hemisphere that makes every incantation feel like wading upstream. You are inside it too — the dome does not discriminate. The advantage is knowing it's there before everyone else does.

Effect: While sustained, all Spellcasting rolls made from within the dome suffer 1 penalty die — including your own. Ongoing effects already active continue normally. Spells cast from outside into the dome are unaffected. The dome moves with you and cannot be separated from its caster.
spell_war_suppression_dome_notes-u: Self-centered dome; Scene (Conc); spellcasting from within suffers 1 penalty die
spell_war_tether_ward_desc-u:
Invisible anchor-lines bite into the ground beneath you. Blasts push. Explosions shove. Gravity itself seems to agree. You don't move unless you decide to.

Effect: You are immune to all forced movement — pushes, pulls, knockdowns, and repositioning effects — for the scene. You can still move yourself normally and may voluntarily allow friendly repositioning effects to work on you. This does not prevent physical Grapples from being initiated, only from forcing your position once established.
spell_war_tether_ward_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); immune to forced movement; does not stop Grapples
spell_war_traveling_ward_desc-u:
Where Shelter Sigil offers arcane dampening on the move, Traveling Ward offers something heavier — a full protective envelope that follows you into the breach. This is the warder as moving fortress, their presence itself a defensive structure. It costs more, demands more, and offers more.

Effect: An Engaged-radius aura of layered warding moves with you for the scene. Any ally within Engaged range of you gains +1 Physical Soak and +1 Veil Soak. The benefit applies dynamically — allies entering your Engaged range gain it immediately, allies who leave lose it. You may only maintain one of Shelter Sigil, Traveling Ward, or Sanctum Halo at a time; activating a second ends the first.
spell_war_traveling_ward_notes-u: Self; Scene (Conc); mobile Engaged-radius aura; allies adjacent gain +1 Physical and +1 Veil Soak
spell_war_tripwire_glyph_desc-u:
A hair-fine line of light spans a threshold — it notices first and argues fast. Whatever trips it has one chance to clear the snare before the floor decides their plans for them.

Effect: Place an invisible tripwire at any point within Short range. The first creature to cross it must succeed on a DEX roll opposed by your casting roll or become Immobilized until the end of its next turn. On a success, no effect. The glyph dissipates after triggering regardless of outcome. You may only maintain one Tripwire Glyph at a time.
spell_war_tripwire_glyph_notes-u: Trap; Short; Scene (Conc); first crossing DEX vs your roll; Immobilized until end of next turn
spell_war_warding_circle_desc-u:
This is the warder's answer to preparation — a proper circle, inscribed with care, anchored in place, and built to last without demanding your attention. When you have time to prepare a space, you make that space a different kind of problem for anyone who enters uninvited.

Effect: Inscribe a warding circle up to Medium radius centered on a point within Short range. The circle requires 1 full minute of uninterrupted casting to complete and cannot be cast in combat. Once complete, it persists for the entire scene without requiring concentration, leaving your hands free for other work. Allies inside the circle gain +1 Physical Soak and +1 Veil Soak for as long as they remain within its boundary. Enemies who enter the circle must succeed on a CON roll opposed by your original casting roll or become Disoriented until the end of their next turn. A creature can only be affected by this entry check once per scene — subsequent entries do not recheck. The circle is visible as faint luminous lines to anyone looking and is immediately identifiable to Sanctum Trace and Detect Magic. It cannot be moved once inscribed.
spell_war_warding_circle_notes-u: Ritual; 1 minute cast; Medium radius; no concentration; scene duration; entry CON or Disoriented; allies +1 Physical and +1 Veil Soak inside
spell_war_wardstorm_cascade_desc-u:
You chain-detonate brittle wards in a collapsing ring, turning forward momentum into stagger and sparks. This is Warding's one concession to offense — and it makes the point clearly: aggression has a cost, and sometimes that cost is delivered all at once.

Effect: Choose a point within Medium range. Creatures in Engaged range of that point roll DEX opposed by your casting roll. On a failure, they take 2d6 Veil damage and become Disoriented until the end of their next turn. On a success, no effect.
spell_war_wardstorm_cascade_notes-u: Contested (DEX); Medium AoE; 2d6 Veil + Disoriented on fail
spell_war_watchful_glyph_desc-u:
A quiet eye drawn in the cracks that tells you — only you — when the line is crossed. It does not discriminate between friend and foe, and it does not pierce illusions. It simply knows when something moved through its space and tells you so.

Effect: Place a subtle alarm ward at any point within Short range. The first time any creature crosses the ward's threshold, you receive a silent, private alert indicating the direction of approach and an approximate headcount — one, a few, or many. The glyph fades immediately after triggering. It does not distinguish friend from foe, and creatures using magical concealment are detected by crossing regardless of their invisibility.
spell_war_watchful_glyph_notes-u: Trap; Short; Scene (Conc); silent alarm; first crossing pings direction and headcount
spirit_lore_desc-u: Covers knowledge of spirits, soul-anchored entities, planar echoes, and other non-corporeal beings. Deals with the practical understanding of how spirits interact with the world, the rites used to summon or banish them, their temperaments, and their metaphysical laws. Used by mediums, summoners, wardens, and anyone who navigates the boundaries between life, death, and the unseen.
station_tech_or_dockmaster_desc-u: Knows how to bend port rules, delay inspections, or get access to restricted infrastructure.
stealth_desc-u: The practiced ability to move unseen and unheard—slipping through a guarded hallway, vanishing into urban crowds, or lying motionless while danger stalks past. Reflects control of breath, rhythm, posture, and situational awareness. In Arcadia, staying invisible means evading both human eyes and machine sensors.
strain_applied_other-u: Applied to another — mark their sheet
strain_applied_self-u: Applied to self — mark your dose counter
street_medic_or_fence_desc-u: Someone who patches people up or moves goods quietly, no questions asked.
streetwise_desc-u: Measures your understanding of urban culture, criminal behavior, and the unspoken rules of the streets. The skill of knowing where to look, who to talk to, what not to say, and when to disappear. From dealing with black market contacts to reading gang tags or navigating underground enclaves, Streetwise helps you survive where law and order are suggestions.
survival_urban_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure and adapt to the hostile conditions of mega-city sprawls and ruins. Covers scavenging, rooftop travel, safehouses, gang turf and curfew navigation. Attempting urban survival techniques in other environments defaults to Hard or Extreme difficulty at GM discretion.
survival_veil_touched_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure contaminated or Veil-touched areas with bio, chem, and rad hazards and unstable physics. Covers decontamination and filter rotation, dosimeter and ward use, hot-zone route planning, anomaly mapping, setting safe anchors and null-tethers, reading echo-weather, identifying mutagenic flora and fauna, and triage for exposure.
survival_wilderness_desc-u: Measures your ability to endure and adapt in natural terrain—forests, mountains, plains. Covers natural hazards, identifying edible flora and fauna, weather craft, and shelter craft. Attempting wilderness survival in other environments defaults to Hard or Extreme difficulty at GM discretion.
swim_desc-u: Measures your ability to stay afloat, propel yourself through water, and survive in aquatic environments. Covers both casual movement through calm water and survival-grade swimming in combat, strong currents, or while encumbered. Also used when swimming in hazardous conditions—carrying gear, dragging allies, or escaping from submerged vehicles.
systems_toolkit_desc-u: Diagnostic tools, calibration equipment, spare components, and interface cables scavenged from Offworld infrastructure.
tag_assess_rules-u: Reveal, infer, or analyze information; gain insight without altering outcomes directly.
tag_assist_rules-u: Grant bonus dice or rerolls to another character.
tag_attack_rules-u: Makes or modifies an attack roll.
tag_aura_rules-u: Ongoing area effect centered on a creature or location.
tag_brace_rules-u: Resist forced movement, knockdown, disarm, or loss of control.
tag_burst_rules-u: Short-term spike in damage or output.
tag_concentration_rules-u: Ends if another Concentration effect begins.
tag_debuff_rules-u: Apply penalty dice or negative conditions (Impaired, Slowed, etc.).
tag_disable_rules-u: Remove or limit Actions, Maneuvers, Reactions, or system functions.
tag_enhance_rules-u: Improve rolls, stats, or effectiveness numerically.
tag_exploit_rules-u: Gains benefit from an existing debuff, failure, or revealed weakness.
tag_guard_rules-u: Reduce or negate damage to self or ally.
tag_interrupt_rules-u: Interfere with an action before it resolves.
tag_mitigation_rules-u: Flat reduction to damage or effects without full negation.
tag_possession_rules-u: Spirit or mind inhabits and directs a host.
tag_repair_rules-u: Restore HP or clear damage states (machines, constructs, frames).
tag_stance_rules-u: Temporary mode that alters how the character functions.
tag_summon_rules-u: Creates or calls an allied entity.
tag_transformation_rules-u: Temporarily alters form, capabilities, or body rules.
tailored_wardrobe_desc-u: High-quality clothing and accessories appropriate to elite environments, designed to project status and credibility.
talent_alteri_author_of_the_moment_rules: Type: Special Immediate (Narrative Control) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — During a sustained social interaction, declare one reasonable assumption about your role, presence, or relationship to the situation that has not yet been contradicted. For the remainder of the scene, neutral observers treat that assumption as true unless actively challenged or disproven. You gain +1 bonus die on the first roll made to act in accordance with that assumption.
talent_alteri_behavioral_study_rules-u: Type: Action (Observation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — After a brief interaction or focused observation of a specific individual, you may attempt to prepare a Mask based on them. Make an Insight roll. On a Success, you may finalize the Mask without requiring a full scene of observation. If the subject is alert, guarded, or trained to resist scrutiny, this roll is opposed by their Willpower. On a Failure, a full scene of observation is still required.
talent_alteri_biometric_plausibility_rul: Type: Passive (Impersonation Systems) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When impersonating a specific individual or role, you may use Performance (Impersonation) in place of Slicing or Electronics to spoof non-hardened biometric systems such as facial recognition, voiceprint locks, gait analysis, or behavioral authentication. You gain +1 bonus die on Performance (Impersonation) rolls made to bypass such systems. This does not apply to hardened or multi-factor biometric security.
talent_alteri_compressed_observation_rul: Type: Passive (Study Acceleration) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — A brief interaction or short period of focused observation is always sufficient to attempt preparation of a Mask. On a Success, the Mask may be finalized immediately. On a Hard Success, the Mask does not suffer unfamiliarity penalties during its first scene of use.
talent_alteri_disciplined_morphology_rul: Type: Passive (Physiology) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Through practice and internal control, you can extend your morphic limits beyond standard Alteri capability. When assuming a Mask, you may convincingly impersonate humanoid targets that are significantly larger or smaller than your natural form by altering tissue density, posture, and internal structure. This expands your plausible appearance range beyond your base size but does not change your Size stat, reach, damage, encumbrance, or physical capabilities.
talent_alteri_hardened_biometric_assimil: Type: Free Action (Persona Clone) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Flawlessly replicate a target’s full persona (appearance, mannerisms, tone); gain +1 bonus die to Disguise, Performance, or Deception checks tied to that impersonation.
talent_alteri_identity_saturation_rules-: Type: Passive (Cognitive Integration) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you first activate a prepared Mask in a scene, your instincts and habitual responses immediately align with that identity. Once per scene, you may treat one failed Performance (Impersonation), Deception, or Etiquette roll made while operating under that Mask as a Success instead of a Failure.
talent_alteri_immaculate_impersonation_r: Type: Passive (Identity Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you activate a prepared Mask of a specific individual, neutral and friendly observers and non-hostile systems accept you as the legitimate individual for the duration of the scene unless presented with direct, compelling contradiction or incontrovertible proof. You gain +1 bonus die on Performance (Impersonation), Deception, and Etiquette rolls made to sustain this identity. This does not override prior personal familiarity or systems designed to verify genetic identity.
talent_alteri_institutional_passing_rule: Type: Passive (Systemic Plausibility) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While operating under a prepared Mask, routine institutional interactions such as basic credential checks, informal questioning, and low-level access control treat you as legitimate unless the system or personnel are actively alerted, hostile, or operating under heightened security. This does not bypass formal verification, insider recognition, or systems designed to detect altered physiology.
talent_alteri_intent_mirroring_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Behavioral Echo) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a character directs a clear social action toward you, such as asserting authority, probing familiarity, or applying pressure, you may mirror their intent through matching posture, cadence, and demeanor. Gain +1 bonus die to your next Insight, Deception, Persuade, or Performance (Impersonation) roll made directly in response.
talent_alteri_layered_identity_rules-u: Type: Passive (Identity Capacity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You may maintain two prepared Masks simultaneously. Switching between prepared Masks during an active scene requires an Action and costs 1d4 Strain. Outside of an active scene, switching between prepared Masks does not cost Strain. Only one Mask may be socially active at a time.
talent_alteri_living_mask_archive_rules-: Type: Passive (Identity Capacity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will / 1/Session — You may maintain up to four prepared Masks simultaneously. Outside of active scenes, you may switch freely between prepared Masks without cost. Once per session, you may switch to any prepared Mask during a scene without spending an Action or Strain, provided the transition is plausibly supported by the environment or social context.
talent_alteri_perfect_adaptation_rules-u: Type: Passive (Behavioral Mastery) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you enter a scene under a prepared Mask, choose one social domain relevant to that identity, such as authority, familiarity, subservience, professionalism, or anonymity. For the rest of the scene, you are treated as naturally fluent in that domain. Once during the scene, you may treat one failed Performance (Impersonation), Deception, Persuade, or Etiquette roll tied to that domain as a Success instead of a Failure.
talent_alteri_plausible_existence_rules-: Type: Passive (Assumption) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While acting within the expected role, behavior, and social position of your current Mask, neutral observers and non-alert systems treat your presence as legitimate by default unless they possess a concrete reason to doubt you. This does not override active investigations, formal verification, direct contradictions, or known hostile intelligence.
talent_alteri_predictive_empathy_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Behavioral Forecast) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose a character you are directly interacting with and make an Insight roll. On a Success, you may determine that character’s most likely behavioral approach in the next exchange, such as pressing, withdrawing, testing authority, seeking reassurance, or attempting deception. On a Hard Success, you also gain +1 bonus die to the first roll you make that directly exploits this expectation.
talent_alteri_seamless_transition_rules-: Type: Special Immediate (Identity Shift) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You may switch to a prepared Mask without consuming an Action, provided the transition is supported by plausible movement, environmental cover, or social interruption. This does not prevent scrutiny if the transition itself would be implausible within the situation.
talent_alteri_second_skin_rules-u: Type: Passive (Identity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You may maintain one additional Mask beyond your default identity. Switching Masks requires an Action and costs 1d4 Strain when performed during an active scene or situation. Outside of an active scene, switching Masks does not cost Strain.
talent_alteri_social_attunement_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Choose a character you are directly interacting with and make an Insight roll. On a Success, you learn the general social expectations they have of you in the interaction, such as authority, familiarity, distance, or formality. On a Hard Success, you also learn one behavioral adjustment that would improve alignment with those expectations.
talent_alteri_social_blind_spot_rules-u: Type: Passive (Cognitive Absence) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — While operating under a prepared Mask in a contextually appropriate setting, casual observers do not form strong or lasting impressions of you. Minor inconsistencies or forgettable details do not escalate scrutiny unless reinforced by direct contradiction, repeated engagement, or external evidence. This does not affect targeted surveillance, dedicated tracking, or characters already actively focused on you.
talent_alteri_social_null_field_rules-u: Type: Passive (Cognitive Absence) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — For the duration of a scene, your presence does not anchor attention or memory unless you take decisive or disruptive action. Casual observers struggle to recall your appearance, voice, or specific involvement once focus moves elsewhere. This does not affect targeted surveillance, active tracking, or characters already focused on you.
talent_draevi_body_like_the_road_rules-u: Type: Passive (Physiological Adaptation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Gain +1 bonus die on CON rolls to resist poison, disease, spoiled food, and environmental toxins. If you fail such a roll, reduce any ongoing non-magical effect by one step in duration or severity. This does not negate magical poisons or supernatural afflictions.
talent_draevi_bound_to_the_old_paths_rul: Type: Special Immediate (Enduring Passage) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you lead a group through sustained movement, escape, or travel under pressure, affirm the route and press on. One failed Survival, Navigation, or movement-related roll made by you or an ally as part of that sequence is treated as a success, reflecting inherited route knowledge and practiced continuity rather than improvisation or speed.
talent_draevi_circle_of_the_standing_sto: Type: Action (Ancestral Perimeter) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — You establish a protective perimeter within Medium range centered on your current position by marking space through repetition, placement, and practiced movement tied to ancestral memory. While you remain within the perimeter, you and allies inside it gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to resist forced movement, disorientation, or environmental hazards. If you leave the perimeter, the effect immediately dissipates.
talent_draevi_claim_the_ground_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Anchoring Stance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you deliberately take position at the start of a scene or after a brief pause, designate your location as claimed ground. Until you leave it, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls to resist forced movement, knockdown, or environmental disruption. Allies within Short range gain +1 bonus die on the first roll they make to brace, stabilize, or reposition in that space.
talent_draevi_echoes_beneath_the_stone_r: Type: Free Action (Ancestral Guidance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause in a location shaped by long use, travel, loss, or shared hardship, roll Spirit Lore. On a success, you receive symbolic impressions tied to what the place remembers. Your next roll made this scene that follows that guidance gains +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_endure_the_long_walk_rules: Type: Special Immediate (Deferred Strain) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain Strain, fatigue, or an environmental condition due to exposure, deprivation, weather, or sustained exertion, you may delay that effect until the end of the current scene. The effect is not negated and applies in full once the scene ends.
talent_draevi_eyes_of_the_pathkeeper_rul: Type: Free Action (Situational Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause to observe a physical space rather than act immediately, roll Perception or Survival. On a success, choose one: identify the safest route through the area, determine the most stable position to hold, or recognize signs of prior passage, habitation, or danger. Your next roll directly tied to that choice gains +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_ground_remembers_me_rules-: Type: Free Action (Place Familiarity) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you remain in a location and act with deliberate presence, that space becomes familiar ground for the rest of the scene. While within it, you and allies within Short range gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to navigate, brace, hide, or reposition using the environment. The effect ends if the location is abandoned or radically altered.
talent_draevi_keeper_of_the_last_fire_ru: Type: Free Action (Sanctuary) • Cost: — • Usage: Resets after each Long Rest — When you establish a short or long rest in a location you have personally secured, sheltered, or deliberately chosen, you draw on shared custom and hard-earned routine to keep the group steady. You and one ally of your choice each recover 1d6 Strain at the end of that rest. In addition, during the rest, all First Aid and Medicine checks made to tend wounds or assist recovery for you or that ally gain +1 bonus die.
talent_draevi_lessons_carved_in_flesh_ru: Type: Passive (Adaptive Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you face an environmental hazard, travel condition, or survival challenge you have previously endured during play, you gain +1 bonus die on the first roll you make to overcome it. If the situation would normally impose a penalty die, this bonus die instead cancels one such penalty.
talent_draevi_marks_of_the_road_rules-u: Type: Passive (Lived Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: Permanent — Choose one skill: Survival, Track, or Spirit Lore. When recalling routes, camps, hazards, land-bound rites, or lessons learned through travel or hardship, you gain +1 bonus die on checks using the chosen skill when lived experience is relevant.
talent_draevi_read_the_ground_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Pattern Recognition) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When entering or crossing a physical environment with coherent terrain or crowd flow, roll Survival or Perception. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to move through, reposition within, or brace against that environment. This does not detect threats or intentions.
talent_draevi_shared_burden_shared_breat: Type: Special Immediate (Endurance Aid) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range fails a CON, Athletics, or Survival roll to endure fatigue, exposure, or environmental hardship, they may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. If the reroll succeeds, you regain 1 Strain as the shared effort steadies you rather than exhausting you.
talent_draevi_stand_when_others_stagger_: Type: Reaction (Endurance Check) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a CON or Athletics roll made to resist fatigue, environmental stress, knockdown, or forced repositioning, you may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This reroll cannot be further modified.
talent_draevi_stonewise_bearing_rules-u: Type: Passive (Environmental Literacy) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You treat difficult, unstable, or uneven physical terrain as manageable for the purpose of movement and positioning. You do not suffer penalty dice or additional movement costs from rubble, loose ground, broken infrastructure, or natural hazards when repositioning deliberately. This does not increase speed, negate forced movement, or prevent falls caused by external force.
talent_draevi_those_who_walked_before_ru: Type: Special Immediate (Shared Memory) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range hesitates, falters, or fails a roll tied to judgment, morale, or decisive action, they may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This cannot be used on attack rolls.
talent_draevi_unbroken_path_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Continuity Assertion) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When a coordinated movement, journey, or escape would fail due to confusion, obstruction, or loss of direction, one failed Navigation, Survival, or positioning-related roll made as part of that effort is treated as a success. This represents practiced continuity rather than improvisation.
talent_draevi_voice_of_those_who_remain_: Type: Free Action (Rally of Purpose) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you state a clear, shared objective during a tense scene, choose up to three allies within Short range who can hear you. The first roll each chosen ally makes that directly advances that stated objective gains +1 bonus die. This effect lasts until used or until the scene ends.
