talent_draevi_weight_of_presence_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Stabilizing Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When chaos, panic, or instability threatens to scatter a group in a physical space, allies within Short range who can see you gain +1 bonus die on rolls to resist fear, disorientation, or rash action until the start of your next turn.
talent_draevi_whispers_of_what_was_rules: Type: Action (Ancestral Vision) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you pause in a location shaped by past movement, conflict, loss, or shared use, you still yourself and listen for what the place remembers. You receive fragmented, symbolic visions of events that occurred there in the past, shown as impressions, images, or moments without narration or explanation. These visions never reveal the future and never answer questions directly. After receiving the vision, you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll made this scene to investigate, interpret, or contextualize the location or revealed events using Spirit Lore, History, Survival, or Occult Lore. This bonus applies only to understanding and interpretation, not to direct action or combat.
talent_feran_anglecraft_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Situational Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you enter a space where movement, elevation, or sightlines meaningfully shape risk—such as alleys, rooftops, scaffolded structures, transit platforms, ruins, or dense interiors—roll Perception or an appropriate Survival skill, depending on the environment. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on your next Dodge, positioning, or escape-related roll made in that space this scene. This represents reading angles, terrain flow, and commitment rather than detecting specific threats or identifying solutions.
talent_feran_chaos_has_a_line_rules-u: Type: Action (Predatory Focus) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When operating in a physically disordered scene, choose one visible creature, location, or exit that you intend to reach, pursue, or break through. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to reposition under pressure, attack, Dodge, or perform physical skill actions that directly advance that stated goal. If you abandon that goal or redirect to an unrelated objective, this effect immediately ends.
talent_feran_chaos_is_a_map_rules-u: Type: Passive (Interpretive Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When a physical scene becomes disordered through crowd panic, structural damage, overlapping movement, or competing threats, you may treat that disorder as navigable rather than obstructive. You may attempt rolls to choose routes, timing, or positioning using Perception, Survival, or Track even if no clear path or stable option exists. This does not grant bonuses, negate rolls, or ensure safety; it establishes that Feran can act meaningfully where others would stall due to confusion.
talent_feran_clean_footing_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Movement Recovery) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a roll made to climb, leap, balance, or traverse unstable or hazardous terrain, you may immediately reroll the check and take the second result. This reroll may not be further modified. If the reroll fails, consequences apply normally.
talent_feran_finish_it_rules-u: Type: Action (Apex Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one visible hostile creature and commit to ending the threat. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on attacks, Dodges, and physical skill rolls made directly against that creature or to pursue, corner, or prevent it from escaping. You cannot willingly disengage from that creature or redirect your focus to other enemies. If the creature escapes the scene, this effect ends.
talent_feran_ive_got_you_rules-u: Type: Action (Pack Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one ally you can perceive and commit to watching their back. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on rolls made to reposition under pressure, Dodge, attack, or perform physical skill actions that directly protect that ally, relieve pressure on them, or prevent threats from reaching them. If you abandon that ally or act toward an unrelated objective, this effect immediately ends.
talent_feran_lines_between_us_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Pack Synchrony) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range repositions, disengages, or breaks from cover under pressure, you may immediately adjust your own position to remain within Short range of them, provided the movement is physically plausible. This movement follows normal rules, may trigger reactions, and does not grant attacks. It represents instinctive pack movement rather than command or coordination.
talent_feran_low_light_focus_rules-u: Type: Passive (Sensory Discipline) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When operating in low-light, obscured, or shadowed conditions where visibility is impaired but not completely absent, you may negate one penalty die imposed on Perception, Survival, or positioning-related rolls caused by lighting alone. This does not grant vision in total darkness, does not negate penalties from smoke, visual interference, or concealment, and does not apply to tasks requiring fine visual detail. It reflects trained focus and motion-reading rather than enhanced sight.
talent_feran_measured_chase_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Pursuit Discipline) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When your Instinct to Pursue ancestry feature triggers and you choose to pursue, you gain +1 bonus die on your next Athletics, Coordination, or Track roll made this scene to maintain contact, cut distance, or intercept the fleeing target. In addition, the complication introduced by Instinct to Pursue must be immediate and concrete rather than open-ended, reflecting controlled pressure instead of reckless overextension. This does not negate escalation or consequences.
talent_feran_path_sense_rules-u: Type: Passive (Environmental Tracking) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When attempting to track, follow, or reconstruct the movement of a creature, vehicle, or group through a physical environment, you gain +1 bonus die on Track or appropriate Survival rolls so long as the path involved movement, disturbance, or repeated passage. This applies even in dense urban spaces, vertical zones, or partially obscured terrain. This does not reveal destinations or intent; it improves your ability to stay on the trail once movement has occurred.
talent_feran_run_it_down_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Predatory Override) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain one or more of the following conditions: Hobbled, Slowed, Impaired, Stunned, Suppressed, Entangled/Restrained, or Panicked, you may instead push through them. Until the end of the scene, you ignore the effects of those conditions and may reposition under pressure, attack, Dodge, and take physical actions normally. Damage and all other consequences still apply. When the scene ends, your Strain is immediately reduced to 0 and you gain the Exhausted condition.
talent_feran_scent_of_disruption_rules-u: Type: Passive (Multisensory Tracking) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When following a trail, reconstructing movement, or assessing recent activity, you may use Perception or Survival even when visibility is severely limited by darkness, smoke, rain, crowd density, or visual interference. In such conditions, penalties imposed specifically by obscured vision do not apply. This does not reveal concealed or stationary targets and does not function in environments without meaningful physical cues.
talent_feran_sense_the_shift_rules-u: Type: Action (Flow Prediction) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - You deliberately observe and listen for changes in spatial rhythm, pressure, and movement. Roll Perception, Investigation , or an appropriate Survival skill depending on the environment. On a success, choose one: an opening in movement or access, a change in crowd or patrol behavior, or an impending structural or environmental reaction. You gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to exploit, avoid, or respond to that predicted shift. This does not reveal causes, identities, or outcomes, and does not prevent consequences if you misjudge the moment.
talent_feran_shared_blind_instinct_rules: Type: Reaction (Pack Reflex) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When you and at least one ally within Short range are targeted by the same threat, hazard, or area effect that relies on positioning or movement, you may allow one affected ally to use your result for a Dodge or positioning-related roll instead of their own. This represents instinctive synchronization born of trust rather than protection, command, or planning. This does not negate the effect, grant immunity, or allow multiple allies to copy the result.
talent_feran_silent_hand_off_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Instinctive Support) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When an ally within Short range is about to make a Dodge roll against a threat that you can also perceive, you may grant that ally +1 bonus die on the roll. You must declare this before the Dodge roll is made. This represents Feran danger-sense—subtle shifts in posture, timing, or spacing that help an ally move at exactly the right moment. This does not grant additional movement, negate the attack, or replace the ally’s action.
talent_feran_somethings_off_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Pattern Disruption Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene - When you pause briefly to observe a physical space rather than act immediately, roll Perception or Investigation . On a success, you identify one place where the natural flow of movement, sound, or use has been disrupted, such as an unusual pause, altered route, forced climb, or hurried exit. Your next roll made this scene to investigate, follow, or exploit that disruption gains +1 bonus die . This does not identify who caused it or why.
talent_feran_the_second_route_rules-u: Type: Action (Spatial Reframing) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When a route, plan, or approach becomes blocked, compromised, or dangerous, you may declare an alternate physical route that plausibly exists within the environment, such as an upper passage, maintenance access, crowd gap, structural bypass, or indirect path. The GM must allow this route to exist if it is physically reasonable. Using it still requires movement, rolls, and carries risk; this talent asserts Feran authority over finding viable paths others overlook.
talent_feran_there_is_still_a_way_rules-: Type: Special (Spatial Continuity) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a physical environment would deny you all viable movement or positioning options due to collapse, blockage, crowd pressure, or extreme instability, you may assert a single physically plausible path or position that keeps you active in the scene. You immediately take it, resolving any required rolls normally. This does not negate risk or consequences; it ensures the scene cannot force you into inaction, stalling, or automatic retreat.
talent_feran_unclaimed_ground_rules-u: Type: Passive (Spatial Opportunity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will - When you enter a contested or chaotic space, areas of partial cover, elevation, or indirect approach that others treat as unsafe or inefficient are considered viable positioning options for you if they are physically plausible. The GM should treat such spaces as usable without requiring additional justification. This does not negate risk, negate rolls, or guarantee safety; it establishes Feran comfort in marginal ground.
talent_feran_where_the_floor_gives_way_r: Type: Reaction (Environmental Foresight) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session - When an environment is about to respond violently to stress or movement, such as collapse, crowd surge, structural failure, or sudden instability, you may act just before that reaction resolves. You may reposition yourself or one ally within Short range to safer footing or partial cover. This does not negate the event, prevent damage entirely, or guarantee safety; it allows Feran to move on the beat before failure.
talent_human_bad_idea_lets_go_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Overcommitment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you attempt a skill check under risky, rushed, or clearly suboptimal conditions, you may commit fully before rolling. If you do, ignore one penalty die imposed by circumstance on that roll. Once this commitment is declared, the roll must be resolved immediately and cannot be pushed, delayed, withdrawn, or reattempted later in the scene. If the roll fails, all consequences apply normally.
talent_human_borrowed_authority_rules-u: Type: Action (Assumed Legitimacy) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When dealing with a person, group, or system that expects expertise, rank, or credentials you do not actually possess, you may proceed as though you plausibly belong there long enough to attempt one relevant social or technical roll. This does not grant trust, immunity, or lasting access, and failure results in immediate suspicion or escalation.
talent_human_close_enough_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Messy Progress) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a non-combat skill check by 5% or less, you may refuse the stall. The action produces unstable, partial, or temporary progress appropriate to the attempt while still introducing risk, cost, or complication. This does not convert failure into a clean success, does not negate consequences, and cannot be used if the roll fails by more than 5%.
talent_human_hold_it_together_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Last Moment Control) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a structure, device, vehicle, or mechanical system you are directly interacting with would fail, collapse, shut down, or break during the current round, you may delay that failure until the end of your next round. Until then, it remains functional only for the purpose of completing actions already in progress or actions you take before that time. When the delay ends, the failure occurs immediately and fully. This does not negate damage, prevent collapse, restore function, or make the situation safe.
talent_human_if_this_works_we_win_rules-: Type: Action (All-In Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Choose one clearly defined objective that would decisively end or turn the scene if completed. Until the end of the scene, all of your actions must directly advance that objective. While doing so, you gain +1 bonus die on skill rolls made toward it. This bonus applies only once per round. If the objective is completed, the scene immediately resolves or shifts in your favor. If you fail or abandon the objective, consequences escalate sharply and immediately.
talent_human_ill_handle_it_rules-u: Type: Action (Unqualified Attempt) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you attempt a skill check using a skill in which you are untrained, you may roll using your EDU instead of the skill’s normal value. This roll is treated as one difficulty step harder than normal. This does not count as being trained for any purpose and cannot be used to meet prerequisites. If the roll fails, consequences apply normally and reflect poor technique, guesswork, or incomplete understanding.
talent_human_ill_take_the_hit_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Deliberate Sacrifice) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When an ally within Short range would suffer damage or a condition that would remove them from meaningful participation in the scene, you may take that damage or condition instead. All effects apply to you exactly as if you were the original target. This transfer cannot be reduced, redirected, delayed, or avoided. If the effect would incapacitate you, it does so.
talent_human_inside_the_blast_radius_rul: Type: Reaction (Survival Instinct) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you enter or remain within an active environmental hazard such as fire, structural collapse, suppression, system overload, Veil disturbance, or similar lethal zone, you may trigger this talent. Until the end of the scene, you ignore one penalty die imposed by environmental danger on skill rolls, attack rolls, or Dodge rolls made while operating within that hazard. You still suffer all normal damage, conditions, and consequences caused by the environment, and any additional penalty dice beyond the first apply normally.
talent_human_it_only_has_to_work_once-u: It Only Has to Work Once
talent_human_it_only_has_to_work_once_ru: Type: Action (Improvised Sufficiency) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you attempt a task using inadequate tools, unstable systems, or incomplete setup that you know is unreliable, you may force the attempt anyway. Resolve the roll using the appropriate skill at one difficulty step harder, but gain +1 bonus die. On a success, the result functions only long enough to achieve the immediate objective, then degrades, fails, or creates follow-up problems. On a failure, consequences apply normally and are exacerbated by the improvised method.
talent_human_ive_been_in_worse_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Refusal to Fall) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would suffer a Major Wound, become Exhausted, or gain a condition that would remove you from action, you may ignore that effect until the end of the scene. You may act normally, but you cannot recover Strain, remove conditions, or benefit from effects that reduce damage or mitigate harm. When the scene ends, all ignored effects apply immediately and in full.
talent_human_ive_run_worse_odds_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Calculated Defiance) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you are faced with a situation where the rules, conditions, or circumstances would force you to withdraw, fail automatically, or abandon an objective, you may choose to attempt a risky alternative instead. Make the first skill roll required to pursue that alternative with +1 bonus die. This does not negate danger, remove conditions, or soften consequences; it allows you to act decisively when the system says retreat or failure is the safer option.
talent_human_make_it_someones_problem_ru: Type: Action (Forced Escalation) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a situation is stalled, ignored, or deliberately avoided by those in power, you may take decisive action that makes continued inaction impossible. Choose a single authority, system, or group present in the scene and immediately attempt a relevant skill check to force their attention through disruption, exposure, or pressure. On a success, the situation escalates and must be addressed directly. On a failure, consequences apply normally and fall on you first. This does not grant cooperation or success; it forces response.
talent_human_make_it_work_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Salvage Improvisation) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a skill check, instead of pushing the roll, you may immediately abandon the original method and force progress through a clearly inferior or improvised approach that uses a different relevant skill appropriate to how you change tactics. This second roll is treated as one difficulty step harder, cannot be pushed, and does not replace the original failure. If it succeeds, the objective advances in a compromised or unstable way. If it fails, the situation escalates with additional consequences tied to the improvised approach.
talent_human_never_tell_me_the_odds_rule: Type: Special (Defiant Resolution) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you attempt a single skill-based action whose success would decisively alter the scene, you do not roll to determine success. The action automatically succeeds. Immediately after, roll the appropriate skill to determine the severity of consequences caused by forcing the outcome. On a Success, the consequences are minor and contained. On a Failure, the consequences are serious and disruptive. On a Fumble, the consequences are catastrophic and lasting. This roll cannot be pushed, assisted, or modified, and this talent cannot be used for attacks.
talent_human_right_tool_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Improvised Solution) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you would normally require specialized equipment, preparation, or setup to attempt a task, you may attempt it immediately using whatever is on hand. The roll resolves at one difficulty step harder, and any failure produces additional wear, loss, or exposure tied to the improvised method.
talent_human_someone_has_to_do_it_rules-: Type: Action (Assumed Burden) • Cost: 2d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you attempt a task that is necessary to resolve the scene and failure would impose consequences on multiple allies, civilians, or the wider situation, you may declare this talent before rolling. Resolve the attempt normally. If the roll fails, you alone suffer all narrative, social, positional, or situational consequences that would have affected others. Secondary effects that are unavoidable still occur. This talent does not guarantee success or safety; it allows the scene to continue by placing the cost entirely on you.
talent_human_still_standing_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Refusal) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you would be knocked Prone, disarmed, or momentarily disrupted short of Incapacitation, you remain upright and retain control of your position. This does not negate damage, prevent conditions, or stop follow-up effects.
talent_human_this_is_on_me_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Consequence Redirection) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a consequence, complication, or pressure would primarily affect an ally within Short range, you may take that consequence yourself instead. You cannot redirect damage this way, only narrative, positional, social, or situational fallout.
talent_human_too_late_to_stop_me_rules-u: Type: Action (Irreversible Commitment) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you declare a dangerous action that would reasonably be interrupted, denied, delayed, or forced into withdrawal due to risk, opposition, or instability, you commit past the point of safe reversal. The action must be resolved immediately as a single attempt using the normal rules and cannot be voluntarily aborted, redirected, or postponed. When resolving a skill check as part of this action, you may ignore one penalty die imposed by circumstances that exist at the moment of declaration. This talent applies only to skill checks made to resolve the committed action. It does not grant success, protection, or immunity. Damage, conditions, countermeasures, and consequences apply normally during and after resolution, and failure escalates consequences appropriate to the scale of the commitment.
talent_human_wrong_place_right_time_rule: Type: Reaction (Opportunistic Entry) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When a scene shifts suddenly due to violence, exposure, or system failure, you may place yourself into the immediate action at Short range from a relevant threat, objective, or ally, provided it is physically plausible. This does not grant surprise or free actions; it represents being there when things break.
talent_khadra_borrowed_hands_rules-u: Type: Action (Ancestral Guidance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you attempt a non-combat skill task for which you are untrained , you may pause and invite the guidance of an ancestor who once walked this path. For the duration of that single task, you may roll Spirit Lore in place of the required skill. This represents inherited memory and steadying presence, not mastery. The roll is resolved normally and does not gain bonus dice, ignore penalties, or grant special outcomes. This talent does not apply to Attack, Dodge, Fight Back, social authority checks, or opposed rolls where another character’s will is the primary obstacle. The ancestor does not act independently, does not provide information beyond what the roll allows, and withdraws immediately once the task attempt concludes.
talent_khadra_burden_accepted_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Enduring Follow-Through) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a non-combat physical skill roll you have already committed to resolving, you may complete the action anyway in a limited, unstable, or taxing form appropriate to the attempt. The GM introduces one additional consequence tied to strain, minor injury, equipment damage, exposure, or narrative fallout. This does not convert failure into a clean success and cannot be used on a fumble or during combat.
talent_khadra_carried_to_the_end_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Burdened Success) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a non-combat skill roll made to complete or advance an ongoing task you have already committed to, you may choose to succeed with complications instead. If you do, the action achieves its primary intent in a flawed, incomplete, or costly manner appropriate to the attempt, and the GM introduces one significant complication tied to the method or situation, such as reduced quality, lingering danger, delayed effect, structural weakness, exposure, or future penalty dice. This talent cannot be used on a fumble, cannot be used on tasks normally resolved in a single trivial roll, and does not negate consequences already incurred by the failure.
talent_khadra_consult_the_old_spirits_ru: Type: Action (Spiritual Consultation) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you have a moment of calm, reverence, or preparation, roll Spirit Lore. On a success, choose one and mark it: Forewarning or Blessing. At any later point this scene, when circumstances align with that choice, you may expend the mark to either gain +1 bonus die on a single roll you make, or impose 1 penalty die on a single opposing roll directly related to that moment. This does not reveal hidden information or guarantee correctness.
talent_khadra_cost_before_collapse_rules: Type: Passive (Focused Endurance) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you are Exhausted (Strain 0), you may declare a single concrete task or objective you intend to complete before resting or disengaging. While Exhausted and actively pursuing that declared goal without changing approach, you gain +1 bonus die on all non-combat skill rolls made to directly advance it. The first time per session that you successfully achieve the declared goal while Exhausted, you immediately recover 1d4 Strain. If this recovery raises your Strain above 0, the Exhausted condition ends immediately. This talent does not apply to Attack, Dodge, or Fight Back rolls, does not prevent further harm or conditions, and cannot delay unconsciousness, death, paralysis, Major Wounds, or effects that fully remove control. If you abandon or redefine the declared goal, this talent immediately ends with no benefit.
talent_khadra_deliberate_force_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Measured Strength) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before making a Strength-based non-combat skill check, you may declare deliberate force. If you do, you gain +1 bonus die on the roll, but any failure results in an immediate cost such as strain loss, equipment stress, structural damage, or unwanted attention appropriate to the exertion. This cannot be used for attacks or combat maneuvers.
talent_khadra_endure_the_long_night_rule: Type: Reaction (Grim Endurance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When prolonged hardship or accumulated harm would force you out of the scene—such as extreme environmental conditions, deprivation, Veil exposure, accumulated Strain, or gaining a Major Wound or a condition that would remove you from meaningful participation—you remain active until the end of the scene. You may act normally, but this does not negate damage, remove conditions, reduce Major Wound penalties, or prevent further harm. When the scene ends, you immediately drop to 0 Strain, gain the Exhausted condition, and all endured consequences fully assert themselves. The GM may introduce lasting injury, recovery requirements, or narrative fallout appropriate to what you survived.
talent_khadra_endure_to_completion_rules: Type: Reaction (Committed Effort) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you fail a non-combat skill roll while attempting to complete a task, you may declare your commitment to see it through. If you do, you may immediately push the failed roll to complete the same task, gaining +1 bonus die on the pushed attempt. This pushed roll does not require the normal Strain cost for pushing, but all other push consequences apply normally.
talent_khadra_oath_set_before_the_storm_: Type: Free Action (Declared Oath) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — At the start of a scene, or during a clear pause before pressure or conflict begins, you may declare a specific course of action, duty, or boundary you intend to uphold. The first roll you make this scene that directly tests that oath gains +1 bonus die. If you voluntarily abandon or redefine the oath before that test occurs, the benefit is lost. No other penalty applies.
talent_khadra_prepared_not_rushed_rules-: Type: Free Action (Deliberate Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you choose to delay action in order to observe, assess, or decide your approach rather than acting immediately, mark Prepared. The next skill roll or talent activation you make this scene that benefits from forethought, positioning, or deliberate choice gains +1 bonus die. This benefit is lost if the delay avoids engagement entirely or if circumstances change such that the preparation is no longer relevant.
talent_khadra_still_standing_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Refusal) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain a condition that restricts your actions or reactions, such as Impaired, Shaken, or Disoriented , you may ignore that condition until the end of your next turn. Damage and all other effects still apply normally. This does not prevent conditions that remove control entirely, such as unconsciousness or paralysis.
talent_khadra_stone_set_focus_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Unbroken Attention) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When environmental pressure, shock, noise, crowd movement, or chaos would impose a penalty die on a non-combat skill roll you are making, you may ignore one such penalty die. This does not negate penalties caused by injury, conditions, or explicit hostile interference, and it cannot be used on Attack, Dodge, or Fight Back rolls.
talent_khadra_the_ancestors_are_watching: Type: Action (Ancestral Invocation) • Cost: 1d8 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You call on the weight of your lineage, allowing an ancestral presence to guide you for the remainder of the scene. Choose one ancestral path when activated; this choice cannot be changed until the scene ends. Stone-Ward Ancestor: Once per round, when you suffer damage, reduce that damage by half (rounded down) after all modifiers. This does not negate conditions or secondary effects. Shield-bearer Ancestor: Once per round, when you make a Dodge or Fight Back Reaction, gain +1 bonus die on that roll. Until the start of your next turn, you suffer 1 penalty die on all Attack rolls. Oath-breaker Ancestor: Once per round, whenyou make an Attack roll, gain +1 bonus die . Until the start of your next turn, you suffer 1 penalty die on all Dodge and Fight Back Reactions. This talent does not grant additional actions or reactions, and its bonus and penalty dice do not stack with themselves. You may not activate this talent while Exhausted. When the scene ends, the ancestral presence withdraws completely.
talent_khadra_the_line_is_here_rules-u: Type: Action (Immovable Declaration) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You openly declare a boundary, position, or person you will not yield. Until the end of the scene, the first roll each round you make to hold position, defend a declared space or creature, block passage, resist displacement, or oppose an attempt to bypass your stance gains +1 bonus die . This bonus may apply to Dodge, Fight Back, Athletics, Unarmed, Melee, or another physically appropriate skill , depending on how the line is being tested. You still suffer all damage, conditions, and consequences normally; this talent does not grant immunity or prevent escalation. Once declared, you cannot voluntarily abandon the line without ending this talent early.
talent_khadra_the_long_memory_of_stone_r: Type: Action (Spiritual Impression) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — When you take a quiet moment to touch or steady yourself against a place, structure, or object, you may invite the lingering echoes of strong emotion tied to it. Roll Spirit Lore. On a success, you experience a brief, symbolic impression of a single emotionally charged event that occurred there in the past—such as violence, grief, devotion, love, terror, or resolve. The impression is fragmentary and sensory rather than explanatory: a flash of motion, a feeling in the chest, a sound, a final choice. It provides no names, causes, or outcomes, and does not confirm truth or intent. The echo reflects what was felt, not what was understood, and fades immediately once perceived.
talent_khadra_the_sign_fulfilled_rules-u: Type: Action (Recognized Omen) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You declare that a specific moment, opening, or pattern you perceive must be acted upon, despite uncertainty. Choose one concrete course of action you will pursue this scene. Until the end of the scene, the first Action each round you take that directly advances that chosen course gains +1 bonus die. Once declared, you may not abandon, delay, or redirect that course of action for the rest of the scene. After resolving each such Action, you suffer 1 penalty die on your next Dodge or Fight Back Reaction before the start of your next turn. This talent does not confirm the correctness of your interpretation, reveal information, or prevent consequences. If the choice is wrong, the outcome is yours to bear.
talent_khadra_the_weight_of_my_words_rul: Type: Action (Ancestral Witness) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — You openly commit to a single concrete goal, duty, or line you will not abandon this scene, calling your ancestors to witness the commitment. Until the end of the scene, you gain +1 bonus die on skill rolls and Attack rolls made directly in furtherance of that commitment , but only when the action involves personal risk, hardship, or exposure to consequence. This bonus does not apply to indirect actions, preparation, delegation, manipulation at a distance, or actions taken from safety. While this talent is active, you may not voluntarily abandon the commitment or shift responsibility to another character; if you do, the effect immediately ends. This talent does not guarantee success, negate failure, or prevent consequences; it represents endurance under witnessed obligation, not authority or destiny.
talent_khadra_witness_to_honor_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Moral Confrontation) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you directly confront an NPC about a lie, broken promise, evasion, or act of cowardice , stating your accusation openly and without threat, you may invoke this talent. Your next social skill roll this scene made to press that confrontation gains +1 bonus die . This roll must be made immediately or as the next exchange in the conversation. This talent does not compel truth, prevent deception, or impose penalties on the target; it represents the steadiness and moral weight of your stance rather than supernatural pressure.
talent_khadra_words_carved_in_stone_rule: Type: Passive (Deliberate Speech) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you state a clear intent, refusal, or commitment in a social interaction, others treat your position as deliberate rather than impulsive. This may justify resistance to being rushed, dismissed, or coerced, and establishes that abandoning your stated position would carry social or reputational cost. This does not compel agreement, grant authority, or prevent escalation.
talent_khadra_work_without_turning_aside: Type: Passive (Sustained Effort) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you make a second non-combat skill roll in the same scene to advance the same task or objective as a prior roll, without changing approach or abandoning the attempt, that second roll gains +1 bonus die. This benefit does not apply if the second roll uses a different method, addresses a different objective, or follows a deliberate change in plan.
talent_kitsu_composure_under_watch_rules: Type: Reaction (Controlled Presence) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When sudden scrutiny, escalation, or pressure would impose penalty dice on your next single action due to surprise, haste, or public exposure, you may ignore one such penalty die. This does not negate danger, consequences, or follow-up effects.
talent_kitsu_cultural_pattern_sense_rule: Type: Passive (Shrewd Appraisal) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you enter a scene involving negotiation, bargaining, ritual exchange, or social maneuvering, you may determine whether the dominant behavior directed at you is genuine or performative/deceptive. Once per scene, you gain +1 bonus die on a single Insight roll made to interpret motives, sincerity, or leverage within that scene.
talent_kitsu_foxfire_step_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Displacement) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When targeted by an attack, you may declare this talent before the attack roll is resolved. Roll your Dodge as normal. If the Dodge succeeds, the attack misses and you immediately shift one Range Band in any direction at no Maneuver cost. If it fails, the attack resolves normally and no movement occurs. This does not grant additional Dodge rolls.
talent_kitsu_ghost_frequency_rules-u: Type: Passive (Sensory Obscurement) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Surveillance systems, arcane detection wards, and passive electronic sensing that are not actively alerted treat you as low-priority or unresolvable. The first scan or detection attempt against you each scene by a non-alert system suffers one penalty die. This does not apply to systems that have already registered you, direct observation, or actively hostile pursuit.
talent_kitsu_layered_address_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Subtext Delivery) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When making a Persuade, Deception, or Charm roll, you may deliver your words on two levels simultaneously — a surface message audible to all present and a secondary meaning perceptible only to those you designate. Creatures you did not designate cannot detect the secondary layer unless they achieve a greater degree of success on an Insight roll than your original result. This does not compel belief in either message.
talent_kitsu_measured_form_rules-u: Type: Passive (Disciplined Technique) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you make your first Attack, Dodge, or Fight Back roll in a scene, you may ignore one penalty die imposed by environmental instability, poor footing, crowd pressure, or unfamiliar surroundings. This does not apply to penalties caused by injury, conditions, concealment, or supernatural effects.
talent_kitsu_polyphonic_command_rules-u: Type: Action (Resonant Assertion) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Make a Charm or Persuade roll. Choose one creature that can hear and understand you — until the end of the next round or until they successfully contradict you, they must achieve a greater degree of success on a POW check than your result to attack you, refuse your stated demand, or leave the scene. This does not override compulsion or commands from a higher authority. Creatures that succeed are unaffected and cannot be targeted by this talent again this session.
talent_kitsu_pressure_mapping_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Exploit the Crack) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When an NPC fails a roll against you or one of your allies, you may immediately make one Insight, Deception, or Persuade roll against them before they act again. This roll gains +1 bonus die.
talent_kitsu_read_the_room_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Leverage Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you enter or observe a social scene before taking an action in it, roll Insight. On a success, the GM must truthfully answer one: who currently holds leverage, whether the other party's behavior toward you is sincere or performed, or whether the interaction is trending toward escalation or de-escalation. On a Hard Success or greater, you learn two. This does not reveal plans or hidden intentions.
talent_kitsu_resonant_dissonance_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Vocal Disruption) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you make a social roll, the target must make a POW check. On a failure, their next roll made in direct opposition to your stated position suffers one penalty die. This cannot be used against creatures that cannot hear or understand you.
talent_kitsu_rite_of_readiness_rules-u: Type: Action (Preparatory Focus) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — Before a scene where tension, negotiation, or opposition is anticipated, you may perform a brief ritual, calibration, or centering rite. Choose one non-combat skill. The first roll you make with that skill during the scene gains +1 bonus die only if the action reflects patience, restraint, or timing rather than haste or force. Acting impulsively forfeits this benefit.
talent_kitsu_signal_null_rules-u: Type: Action (Full Spectrum Suppression) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — For the remainder of the scene, you cannot be detected by passive electronic surveillance, arcane tracking, or automated sensory systems unless you take an overtly aggressive or disruptive action. Active, directed searches still function normally. When the scene ends or the effect breaks, suppressed systems resume immediately.
talent_kitsu_strike_on_the_beat_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Timing Exploitation) • Cost: 2 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you are targeted by an attack, grapple, or spell, you may act before it resolves. Make one attack or skill-based response. If the roll succeeds, your action resolves first. If it fails, both actions resolve simultaneously. This action cannot trigger additional reactions or talents.
talent_kitsu_the_false_floor_rules-u: Type: Action (Misdirection) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — Declare a credible false objective, position, or intent openly during a social scene. The first NPC who acts to counter or accommodate that false intent suffers one penalty die on that action and on their next roll made against you this scene.
talent_kitsu_the_patient_hand_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Prepared Pivot) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — If you have not made a social or deceptive roll during the current scene, you may declare this talent the moment you choose to act. Your first Persuade, Charm, or Deception roll gains +1 bonus die, and the target loses any bonus dice they accumulated this scene before your action.
talent_kitsu_the_pivot_point_rules-u: Type: Special Immediate (Scene Inversion) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a social scene has turned against you or your allies, choose one advantage an NPC currently holds — authority, information, or leverage established earlier in the scene — and declare it nullified. That NPC loses any bonus dice tied to that advantage for the remainder of the scene, and your next roll against them gains +1 bonus die.
talent_kitsu_the_shape_of_the_hunt_rules: Type: Passive (Strategic Patience) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — At the start of any scene, silently designate one creature, system, or objective as your mark. Until the mark is resolved or the session ends, you gain +1 bonus die on Insight, Deception, and Stealth rolls made directly in pursuit of it. Once per scene, if you succeed on a roll against the mark, you may ask the GM one observable question about it and receive a truthful answer.
talent_kitsu_vanishing_act_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Total Withdrawal) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would be captured, cornered, or removed from the scene, you immediately break contact and withdraw to the nearest plausible position of concealment within Medium range. All rolls to locate, track, or identify you this round suffer one penalty die. Cannot be used while Restrained or Entangled, or if no plausible withdrawal position exists within Medium range. Does not negate damage already dealt or conditions already applied.
talent_kitsu_veiled_commitment_rules-u: Type: Action (Marked Misdirection) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you deliberately present a clear intention to a creature during a social exchange, you may mark that creature for misdirection. The first Insight or social skill roll that creature makes against you during the scene suffers one penalty die. This mark ends immediately after that roll is resolved and does not apply to non-social perception or hostile actions.
talent_kitsu_veiled_read_rules-u: Type: Action (Arcane-Digital Scan) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You attune to the layered frequencies of the scene — concealed devices, active spellcraft, surveilled space. Roll Investigation. On a success, you determine whether the scene contains something deliberately concealed, actively observing, or recently altered — physical, digital, or arcane. On a Hard Success or greater, you also determine the general nature of what is hidden. This does not reveal identity, intent, or exact location.
talent_lyranni_arcane_composure_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Arcane Reserve) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene • Trigger: A creature you can perceive attempts to notice a spell being cast, prepared, or shaped by you, or you attempt to notice another creature casting or manipulating arcane energy. Perception and Arcana checks made to notice you casting or preparing a spell suffer one penalty die, and you gain +1 bonus die on Perception or Arcana checks made to notice another creature casting or manipulating arcane energy. This does not conceal overt spell effects once manifested and does not block direct magical detection or intrusion.
talent_lyranni_bearing_of_power_rules-u: Type: Passive (Veiled Authority) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — In social, political, or institutional contexts where arcane literacy, Veil familiarity, or magical lineage carries weight, you may gain +1 bonus die on a single social roll made to assert presence, claim relevance, or be taken seriously in matters involving the Veil or magical power. This does not grant obedience, trust, or immunity, and often places expectation or scrutiny on you in return. Your connection to the Veil is rarely neutral in such spaces.
talent_lyranni_between_the_currents_rule: Type: Action (Spirit Adjacency) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When spirits, echoes, or Veil entities are present in a scene, you may act as an intermediary without formal summoning or binding. Make a Veil Lore roll. On a success, you may pose one simple inquiry to the presence or receive one symbolic impression relevant to the scene. The response is indirect, incomplete, or metaphorical. This does not compel honesty, loyalty, or clarity. This exchange occurs once and does not allow follow-up questions.
talent_lyranni_born_adjacent_rules-u: Type: Passive (Veil Tolerance) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you are exposed to a Veil-based effect, hazard, anomaly, or environmental influence that requires a roll to resist, stabilize, endure, or remain functional, you gain +1 bonus die on the first such roll you make each scene. This applies only to effects originating from the Veil or arcane instability, not to mundane hazards or non-arcane conditions. This represents tolerance through familiarity, not protection, and does not stack with other ancestry-based resistances.
talent_lyranni_caste_memory_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Social Recall) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When interacting with a hierarchical organization, arcane institution, noble structure, or power-stratified group, you may declare this reaction to recall or recognize unspoken rank, obligation, or precedent. Gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to exploit, respect, or maneuver within that hierarchy. This does not grant authority; it grants understanding.
talent_lyranni_heir_to_the_veiled_houses: Type: Special (Inherited Authority) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you formally assert your lineage, House ties, or cultural standing in a social, political, arcane, or spirit-mediated interaction, choose one audience present: mortals, spirits, or institutional actors. For the remainder of the scene, that audience must treat you as a legitimate stakeholder whose words carry consequence. The first attempt by that audience to dismiss, override, or disregard you without engagement suffers one penalty die, and you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene to negotiate, assert precedent, invoke obligation, or demand acknowledgment. This does not compel obedience or trust and often invites scrutiny, challenge, or future consequence.
talent_lyranni_held_intent_rules-u: Type: Passive (Concealed Purpose) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Your true objectives are difficult to read. Opposed Insight, Perception, or magical sensing rolls made to determine your intent, emotional state, or immediate plans suffer one penalty die unless the observer has direct evidence or prolonged interaction. This does not prevent suspicion; it prevents certainty.
talent_lyranni_i_will_not_be_carried_rul: Type: Reaction (Defiant Continuance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session • Trigger: A spell, Veil effect, spiritual influence, or supernatural event would incapacitate you, forcibly remove you from the scene, or otherwise end your meaningful participation. — The triggering effect resolves, but you remain conscious, present, and able to act until the scene ends. You take all damage normally and still accrue conditions as they occur, but none of those conditions may remove you from play until the scene concludes. You cannot recover Strain, reduce damage, or remove conditions during this time. When the scene ends, all delayed effects, conditions, and consequences apply immediately and in full. This talent does not protect allies, negate harm, or reduce cost; it ensures you are not erased from the moment.
talent_lyranni_let_it_pass_through_me_ru: Type: Reaction (Veil Mediation) • Cost: 2d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session • Trigger: A spell, Veil effect, spiritual influence, or supernatural action would directly affect multiple creatures, escalate beyond a single target, or impose widespread supernatural consequences. — You interpose yourself and force the effect to resolve through you. The effect still occurs, but all transferable portions of it—damage, conditions, spiritual influence, and narrative consequences—are redirected to you. When resolving those effects, choose one: halve the total damage dice rolled against you (round down); or reduce the severity or duration of one condition applied to you to its minimum value; or remove one secondary rider effect attached to the original effect. Any excess power displaced by this mediation bleeds into the surrounding environment or the Veil, creating an immediate and visible disturbance, scar, omen, or spiritual backlash. You suffer all remaining effects immediately and in full; this talent does not negate harm or prevent incapacitation. After the resolution, you may define how responsibility, blame, or obligation is perceived in the aftermath, subject to normal narrative adjudication.
talent_lyranni_quiet_read_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — After observing a social interaction, negotiation, or confrontation for a brief moment, roll Insight opposed by the highest POW present. On a success, the GM must answer one of the following truthfully: who currently holds real leverage, who is concealing intent, or whether the situation is more likely to escalate or stall. This does not reveal plans, secrets, or future actions; it reveals pressure points.
talent_lyranni_spirit_marked_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Veil Claim) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a spirit, Veil entity, or arcane remnant directly interacts with you through influence, communication, possession attempt, or attack, you may assert recognition. Until the end of the scene, that entity suffers one penalty die on rolls made to deceive, surprise, or overwhelm you. This does not grant control, immunity, or protection.
talent_lyranni_the_veil_knows_my_name_ru: Type: Special (Recognized Presence) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you enter a scene containing a spirit, Veil entity, corrupted zone, or active arcane phenomenon, you may declare this talent. Until the end of the scene, Veil-aligned forces recognize you as an acknowledged actor rather than an intrusion. They do not automatically escalate, reject, or target you solely for being present. The first hostile action taken directly against you by a Veil-aligned entity this scene suffers one penalty die. This does not prevent hostility, manipulation, collateral damage, or indirect harm. At the end of the scene, the GM establishes how the Veil records this recognition—favor, obligation, scrutiny, or resentment—which persists beyond the scene.
talent_lyranni_the_weight_before_the_ste: Type: Special (Veil Pressure Awareness) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a spirit, Veil entity, summoning effect, breach, or supernatural intrusion would manifest, enter the scene, or exert influence during the current or next round within Medium range, the GM must alert you to the disturbance before it fully resolves, even if the source is concealed or indirect. Until the end of the scene, you perceive the entity’s tether to the world—whether it is summoned, bound, anchored, escaped, or freely present—and learn one structural truth about that tether chosen by the GM (such as what sustains it, what would weaken or sever it, what it is constrained from doing, or what kind of bargain or condition it responds to). In addition, choose one: gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene to banish, disrupt, bargain with, ward against, or safely withdraw from the entity; or grant that bonus to one ally acting on your insight before the end of the next round. This talent does not reveal intent, future actions, true names, or guaranteed solutions.
talent_lyranni_true_veilsight_rules-u: Type: Action (Perception) • Cost: 2 Strain (1 round) or 1d6 Strain (entire scene) • Usage: At will — You perceive Veil phenomena directly, including spirits, Veil entities, lingering arcane effects, corrupted zones, rifts, echoes, and magical residue. While True Veilsight is active, the first Arcana, Veil Lore, or Spirit Lore roll you make during its duration gains +1 bonus die. You may distinguish natural Veil fluctuation from deliberate arcane or spiritual influence, but you do not perceive spell formulas, prepared spells, or active casting mechanics. While active, you ignore penalties from low-light or shadowed conditions. This does not grant vision in total sensory voids and does not bypass non-Veil concealment.
talent_lyranni_unspoken_leverage_rules-u: Type: Passive (Social Pressure) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — In social scenes involving hierarchy, negotiation, or concealed power dynamics, neutral or hostile parties cannot treat you as harmless or irrelevant. The first roll made to dismiss, ignore, or bypass you without engagement suffers one penalty die. This does not compel respect or cooperation; it ensures your presence carries weight even when silent.
talent_lyranni_veil_hardened_mind_rules-: Type: Reaction (Arcane Resistance) • Cost: 1d4 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you would gain Disoriented, Shaken, Panicked, Suppressed, or Stunned due to a spell, Veil effect, spiritual influence, or arcane pressure, reduce the severity or duration to the minimum possible. If the condition would last multiple rounds, it instead ends at the close of the current round. This does not negate damage or remove conditions entirely and does not apply to purely mundane sources.
talent_lyranni_veil_literate_rules-u: Type: Passive (Veil Literacy) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — The first time each scene that you make a Veil Lore roll to interpret, interact with, stabilize, or safely approach a Veil-based effect, phenomenon, entity, or environment, you gain +1 bonus die on that roll. Whether the roll succeeds or fails, you experience minor Veil feedback such as sensory distortion, emotional bleed-through, or physical discomfort that is noticeable but imposes no mechanical penalty. This feedback does not stack or escalate.
talent_lyranni_veil_touch_rules-u: Type: Action (Veil Contact) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — You deliberately make brief physical or near-physical contact with a creature, object, location, or effect that is actively influenced by the Veil. Roll Veil Lore. On a success, you gain clear, immediate insight into the nature of that influence, such as whether it is stable or unstable, bound or uncontrolled, deliberate or accidental. In addition, choose one: either you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene made to interact with, analyze, disrupt, or resist that Veil influence, or you grant that same bonus to one ally who acts on your insight before the end of the current round. On a failure, you still sense the Veil’s presence, but suffer 1d4 Strain from arcane feedback.
talent_lyranni_veiled_composure_rules-u: Type: Passive (Controlled Presence) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you are subject to emotional pressure, intimidation, magical influence affecting mood or perception, or social provocation, you may ignore one penalty die imposed on social or Insight-based rolls caused by that pressure. This does not grant immunity to mind control, fear effects, or compulsion; it reflects disciplined restraint rather than resistance.
talent_lyranni_veilsight_rules-u: Type: Action (Sense) • Cost: 1 Strain (1 round) or 1d4 Strain (entire scene) • Usage: At-will — You open your perception to the Veil. While active, you can perceive spirits, Veil distortions, lingering magical effects, and arcane residues present in the environment. While Veilsight is active, you gain +1 bonus die on Veil Lore checks made to identify, analyze, or contextualize Veil phenomena. Veilsight reveals presence and nature only; it does not reveal intent, future outcomes, or grant control, and it does not replace investigation, divination, or spellcasting.
talent_veyra_acoustic_shadow_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Suppressed Presence) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When a detection attempt would locate you by sound, movement, or proximity, the triggering roll suffers one penalty die. This does not affect visual detection or magical tracking by non-acoustic means.
talent_veyra_compression_instinct_rules-: Type: Passive (Structural Familiarity) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Tight spaces — collapsed corridors, maintenance shafts, low-clearance ducts — count as normal terrain for you. You do not suffer movement penalties or additional Maneuver costs when traversing them.
talent_veyra_dark_geometry_rules-u: Type: Passive (Acoustic Navigation) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — You do not suffer penalty dice on Stealth, Athletics, or Coordination rolls from darkness or obscured visibility. If you are Deafened or in an environment that actively distorts sound, this feature is suppressed until resolved.
talent_veyra_dead_air_rules-u: Type: Action (Acoustic Suppression) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — For the remainder of the scene, all your Stealth rolls to avoid sound-based detection gain +1 bonus die. Allies within Short range also gain +1 bonus die on Stealth rolls for the remainder of the scene. This effect ends immediately if you take a loud or disruptive action.
talent_veyra_everywhere_at_once_rules-u: Type: Passive (Extended Acoustic Presence) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Your Ultrasonic Awareness permanently expands to Medium range. In environments with connected physical infrastructure, it extends to Long range. You cannot be Surprised by anything within your acoustic field unless the source suppresses sound. Once per scene, you may warn one ally within Medium range before they are Surprised, allowing them to act normally in the first round.
talent_veyra_fault_line_rules-u: Type: Action (Controlled Failure) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — After physically interacting with a damaged or failing device assessed by Read the Fault, choose one: allow it to fail at the end of your next turn, or make a quick adjustment that keeps it functional for the rest of the scene. Either effect ends if the device is destroyed or the scene ends.
talent_veyra_ghost_in_the_infrastructure: Type: Special (Scene Ownership) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you have moved through or observed a location’s infrastructure before the scene begins, declare this talent. Once per round as a Free Action, you may trigger or suppress one environmental feature within Medium range without a roll. Each use must be distinct. This ends if you leave the location or its infrastructure is destroyed.
talent_veyra_ghost_work_rules-u: Type: Passive (Invisible Repair) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you repair, modify, or sabotage a device without being observed, you leave no trace. Detecting the tampering requires a Hard Success or greater on a Mechanics or Electronics roll. On a regular success, they know only that something is wrong — not what or by whom. This does not apply if you were observed working.
talent_veyra_gone_before_the_echo_rules-: Type: Reaction (Acoustic Disappearance) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you are targeted by a scan, search, or detection attempt, you may declare this talent. For the remainder of the scene, all detection attempts relying on sound, movement, or proximity automatically fail against you unless you take a loud or disruptive action. Visual detection and direct magical tracking still function normally.
talent_veyra_perfect_fit_rules-u: Type: Reaction (Tactical Refuge) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Scene — When you would be cornered or cut off from movement, you immediately move to a viable overlooked position within the environment — a gap in the structure, a vertical shaft, a floor access point. This costs one Maneuver but requires no roll. Attackers must spend an Action to locate you before they can target you again.
talent_veyra_pressure_map_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Spatial Read) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you enter a physical space, or after its layout changes — collapse, an opening, movement of obstacles — roll Perception. On a success, you gain +1 bonus die on your next roll this scene to navigate, reposition, find cover, or detect hidden movement within that space. This functions in total darkness and does not require line of sight.
talent_veyra_read_the_fault_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Mechanical Assessment) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — When you first interact with or closely observe a damaged, malfunctioning, or improvised device, you immediately learn whether it is functional, partially functional, or beyond field repair, and which component is responsible for its current state. No roll is required.
talent_veyra_repurposed_rules-u: Type: Action (Field Reconstruction) • Cost: 1 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a device, drone, or mechanical system is destroyed or disabled, you extract one single-use function from its remains without tools or parts. Roll Mechanics. On a success, the device performs that function once then expires. On a failure, it cannot be salvaged further this scene.
talent_veyra_resonance_network_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Expanded Awareness) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Scene — You extend your acoustic perception through connected physical infrastructure — pipes, ducts, conduit, or framing. Roll Perception. On a success, you learn the general shape, occupancy, and movement activity of one connected space within Medium range that you cannot see. On a Hard Success or greater, you also identify the number of distinct moving presences within it. Does not function in spaces without shared physical infrastructure.
talent_veyra_still_as_the_shaft_rules-u: Type: Passive (Movement Stealth) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — Moving at standard pace does not impose penalty dice on Stealth checks. This does not apply to sprinting or combat movement.
talent_veyra_structural_exploit_rules-u: Type: Free Action (Environmental Leverage) • Cost: — • Usage: 1/Session — Identify one environmental feature in the current location — a load-bearing point, pressure valve, ventilation junction, or power conduit — and declare how it could be used to create an advantage. Your next roll to exploit, trigger, or sabotage that feature gains +1 bonus die. Using the feature may still require a skill check.
talent_veyra_the_invisible_repair_rules-: Type: Action (Systemic Rewrite) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When you modify, sabotage, or repair a device, that change is permanent. Detecting the modification requires a Hard Success or greater on a Mechanics or Electronics roll and a physical inspection of the system. Choose one: the modification activates on a trigger condition you define at installation, or it activates at a time you specify later this session.
talent_veyra_the_last_working_part_rules: Type: Action (Impossible Salvage) • Cost: 1d6 Strain • Usage: 1/Session — When a device or system has failed completely, you restore it to minimum viable function for the remainder of the scene without tools or parts. Roll Mechanics. On a success, it works — imperfectly, but enough. On a failure, you extract one final function from it as per Repurposed. Requires something physical to work with.
talent_veyra_the_narrow_path_rules-u: Type: Passive (Positional Advantage) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When you are inside a tight space others cannot easily enter, attacks made against you from outside suffer one penalty die. You may attack, use skills, and observe from this position without penalty. This does not apply if the tight space is breached or wide enough for your attacker to enter.
talent_veyra_the_system_knows_me_rules-u: Type: Passive (Infrastructure Fluency) • Cost: — • Usage: At-will — When making Mechanics or Electronics rolls in environments built around functional infrastructure, you never suffer penalty dice for unfamiliarity with the specific system. This does not apply to hardened or secured systems.
temporary_employer_desc-u: Someone who offers short-term work and asks few questions, as long as you deliver.
throw_desc-u: Covers the ability to accurately throw physical objects—knives, grenades, makeshift weapons, or any item used as a projectile. Used both for combat (lobbing explosives into cover) and utility (throwing a grappling hook, tossing gear across a gap). Especially effective with arcane-infused grenades, alchemical flasks, or high-velocity throwing knives. As a Combat Skill, Throw cannot be Pushed.
tool_harness_desc-u: A worn but functional set of general tools suitable for industrial repair, maintenance, or fabrication.
track_desc-u: Allows a character to follow the trail of a person, creature, vehicle, or drone across natural or artificial terrain. Includes recognizing footprints, tire marks, blood droplets, subtle environmental damage, and even psychic or magical traces when used alongside arcane tools. Environmental factors, urban density, time since passage, and weather may increase difficulty.
tradecraft_creative_desc-u: Covers skilled work in a creative trade such as tattooing, DreamFeed production, fashion design, or street art. Used to create, repair, or analyze work tied to the chosen domain. A successful roll produces saleable or functional items; higher success tiers reflect pieces of beauty, symbolic power, or technical mastery.
tradecraft_labor_desc-u: Covers skilled physical labor trades—construction, stevedoring, salvage work, mining, or other demanding manual professions. Used to perform or assess work, manage heavy equipment, or leverage physical trade knowledge in practical field situations.
tradecraft_media_desc-u: Covers work in information, journalism, broadcasting, and media production. Used to gather information, produce credible reports, navigate media networks, or leverage press credentials and publishing connections in investigative or social situations.
transit_locker_desc-u: Personal effects, ration packs, and travel documentation from multiple ports, some expired, some still useful.
transit_network_contact_desc-u: Knows schedules, unofficial routes, and how to move quietly through controlled spaces.
travel_pack_desc-u: Lightweight pack with preserved food, water filtration, layered clothing, and emergency shelter supplies.
unarmed_desc-u: Covers striking, grappling, and defensive maneuvers using only the body—punches, kicks, elbow strikes, tackles, and holds. Used to engage enemies in melee without a weapon and to resist grapples or disarm attempts. Becomes especially potent when enhanced by cyberware, martial training, or magical augmentation. As a Combat Skill, Unarmed cannot be Pushed.
union_or_work_crew_token_desc-u: Proof of past membership in a labor crew, shift collective, or industrial network that still looks out for its own.
vacuum_go_bag_desc-u: Emergency seals, environmental patches, compact oxygen reserve, and survival gear rated for hostile conditions.
vehicle_armored_personnel_carrier_effect: DR 12, HP 120. Virtually immune to small arms. Slow. 2 hardpoints, 10 passengers.
vehicle_asuka_cloudrider_5t_effect-u: DR 5, VS 2, HP 78. Standard speed. 4 crew. Corporate skylane presence with Veil hardening.
vehicle_asuka_gravleech_330_effect-u: DR 4, HP 25. Standard speed. The mid-range street bike favored by couriers and fringe operators.
vehicle_asuka_gravleech_440t_effect-u: DR 7, VS 3, HP 34. Fast speed. Upgraded frame with hardened arc-suspension and sealed cockpit.
vehicle_asuka_thunderdrift_t90_effect-u: DR 9, VS 4, HP 135. Veil-rated for atmospheric disruption — flies through Veilstorms and aurora surges.
vehicle_blackreach_ghosthook_v22_effect-: DR 10, HP 150, Defense 60 (H). Rapid. No heat bloom, no identifiable arcstream. The extraction vehicle of syndicate disappearances.
vehicle_blackreach_hellcarriage_v10_effe: DR 10, VS 2, HP 110, Defense 55 (H). Rapid, +1 Handling. 1 hardpoint. No registered address.
vehicle_brickhaul_skycage_m5_effect-u: DR 7, VS 2, HP 115. Standard speed. 6 passengers, 1 hardpoint. Rugged, widely distributed personnel VTOL.
vehicle_brickhaul_tempest_gr7_effect-u: DR 6, VS 1, HP 80. Standard speed. 4 crew. The workhorse of mid-city freight corridors.
vehicle_cargo_van_effect-u: DR 2, HP 40. Standard speed, −1 Handling. Up to 7 passengers. Invisible in urban freight traffic.
vehicle_compact_sedan_effect-u: DR 1, HP 30. Standard speed. Anonymous, ubiquitous. Best surveillance vehicle in the city.
vehicle_executive_transport_effect-u: DR 7, HP 80, Defense 45 (H). −1 Handling. Armored against anti-materiel rounds at distance.
vehicle_heliodyne_sablehorn_m3_effect-u: DR 5, VS 1, HP 28. Standard speed. Permit Required. Resonance-dampened coils and sealed nav array.
vehicle_industrial_sentinel_effect-u: DR 6, HP 70. Slow, −1 Handling. 1 hardpoint. Construction-grade biped. Adapted for use beyond its original purpose.
vehicle_kintari_drifthauler_6c_effect-u: DR 4, HP 65. Slow speed. The fringe hauler. It was never meant to be elegant — just dependable.
vehicle_kintari_streetjack_205x_effect-u: DR 1, HP 14. Fast, +1 Handling. The baseline for urban runners. You'll hear it before you see it.
vehicle_kintari_streetjack_209x_effect-u: DR 3, HP 22. Fast, +1 Handling. Permit Required. Upgraded frame and sealed coil housing.
vehicle_kintari_strider_44g_effect-u: DR 7, VS 2, HP 125. Standard speed. Modular rear hold. 4 mod slots. The backbone of urban logistics.
vehicle_lifeline_harklight_12r_effect-u: DR 8, VS 3, HP 130, Defense 40 (H). Medevac VTOL with arc-hardened frame and Veil-sealed patient bays.
vehicle_patrol_skimmer_effect-u: DR 5, HP 50. Standard speed. 1 hardpoint. Used by port authority, corp waterway security, and smuggling syndicates alike.
vehicle_peregrine_lancer_9x_effect-u: DR 6, VS 2, HP 36, Defense 45 (H). Rapid, +1 Handling. Arc-hardened interceptor. The exemption is the point.
vehicle_peregrine_luxsilence_99_effect-u: DR 5, VS 4, HP 85, Defense 50 (H). Fast. No drive signature. Biometric locks. Defense 50 (H).
vehicle_peregrine_nimbus_executive_arcli: DR 7, VS 6, HP 130, Defense 60 (H). Highest VS of any platform. Psionically dampened. 0 hardpoints — does not need them.
vehicle_phantom_viper_mkiv_effect-u: DR 4, VS 2, HP 30, Defense 40 (H). Rapid, +1 Handling. Veil-threaded cloaking. The Viper is exempt from most municipal traffic scans.
vehicle_phantom_wraith_gt_effect-u: DR 5, VS 4, HP 40, Defense 55 (H). Rapid, +1 Handling. Active camo plating. No coil fans. No arc signature.
vehicle_sablecorp_rampart_mkii_effect-u: DR 8, VS 3, HP 95, Defense 45 (H). Fast. 1 hardpoint. Corporate security command runner.
vehicle_sablecorp_watchwing_vt9_effect-u: DR 9, VS 4, HP 140, Defense 55 (H). 5 mod slots. AI-assisted threat tracking. When you hear the hum and nothing shows on sensors, it's already above you.
vehicle_security_sentinel_effect-u: DR 10, HP 100, Defense 50 (H). Slow, Standard Handling. Immune to small arms. 1 hardpoint.
vehicle_security_suv_effect-u: DR 5, HP 60. Fast speed. 1 hardpoint. Standard mid-tier corporate security vehicle.
vehicle_speedboat_effect-u: DR 1, HP 25. Fast, +1 Handling. Most maneuverable water platform for outer district channels.
vehicle_talon_skiff7_light_vtol_effect-u: DR 2, HP 45. Fast, +1 Handling. 0 hardpoints. Most common light VTOL in the city.
vehicle_vaultum_cloudback_heavy_transpor: DR 4, HP 80. Slow, −1 Handling. Up to 12 passengers. Heavy-lift logistics VTOL.
vehicle_veilcore_driftsurge_s2_effect-u: DR 2, HP 18. Standard speed. Quiet turbine ring, minimalist coil bank. Floats clean.
vehicle_veilcore_voxline_lx_effect-u: DR 3, VS 1, HP 70. Standard speed. 3 crew. Quiet drive signature. The go-to for ops requiring silence.
vehicle_volkov_strikewing_gunship_effect: DR 8, HP 90, Defense 55 (H). Fast. 2 hardpoints. Close-air-support platform.
veil_lore_desc-u: The study of the Veil—Arcadia's fragile boundary between known reality and the hidden substrata of consciousness, memory, and alternate perception. Encompasses knowledge of DreamFeeds, psychic resonance, reality slippage, memory interference, Echo States, and phenomena surrounding the Cataclysm. Rare and often found among DreamDiver technicians, ArcNet specialists, and Mindshock survivors.
vmod_additional_hardpoint_effect-u: Installs one additional weapon mount. Max total Hardpoints cannot exceed twice listed Mod Slots.
vmod_advanced_trauma_medbay_effect-u: +20% to First Aid and Medtech rolls in vehicle. Stabilize up to 2 critical patients simultaneously. Counts as Med Facility for downtime recovery. VTOL only.
vmod_arcane_drive_effect-u: Replaces standard fuel with P-Cell array. 500 Cr per cell. Vehicle produces no engine noise beyond a low hum at Engaged range.
vmod_arcfield_cloaking_shroud_effect-u: Complete visual concealment while stationary. In motion: bonus die on Evasion and detection-resistance. Cannot combine with ArcShield. Degrades at Critical.
vmod_arcjammer_beacon_effect-u: Once per scene: 30-meter ECM field for 2 rounds. Enemy tracking, lock, and guided weapon rolls suffer one penalty die.
vmod_arcnet_ghost_plating_effect-u: +15 to vehicle Defense vs. all Slicing and arcane hacking or possession attempts.
vmod_arcshield_pulse_modulator_effect-u: Once per scene: absorbs up to 20 damage from a single incoming attack. Depleted until downtime recharge. Produces visible arc flare.
vmod_auto_turret_conversion_effect-u: Converts one Hardpoint to remote-operated turret. Gunner need not expose themselves. +10% Gunnery when AI-targeted.
vmod_blast_shielding_effect-u: Crew take half damage from external explosions and Blast effects. Also halves crash damage crew take.
vmod_drone_jack_mount_effect-u: Deploys one scout drone once per scene. +10% Perception while active. Live AR-feed relay. Returns after 10 minutes or on command.
vmod_emergency_ejection_system_effect-u: When vehicle reaches 0 HP, pilot/driver may eject as Free Action before Crash Damage. Takes 1d6 fall damage instead.
vmod_engine_tune_effect-u: +1 Speed category (toggleable). While active: one penalty die on Drive/Pilot rolls due to heat and resonance. Cannot exceed Rapid. Once only.
vmod_handling_upgrade_effect-u: +1 Handling step. Includes grav-stabilizer and lateral inertia management upgrades. Once only.
vmod_hardened_chassis_conversion_effect-: Vehicle gains Hardened status. Cannot be targeted by spells or talents that don't affect Hardened targets. Requires 3+ Mod Slots.
vmod_internal_auto_pilot_node_effect-u: Autonomous piloting at fixed 35% skill. Can return to set location, maintain evasive maneuvers, or extract unconscious rider. Cannot attack or react to threats.
vmod_modular_drone_vault_effect-u: Stores and deploys up to 3 drones per scene. Launch 2 simultaneously as one action. Auto-recall if undamaged. VTOL only.
vmod_radar_absorb_coating_effect-u: Aerial detection checks suffer one penalty die. Ineffective vs. visual spotting or thermal scanning.
vmod_rapid_deployment_system_effect-u: Winch-mounted rappel and extraction. Up to 4 characters descend or ascend in one round. VTOL only.
vmod_reinforced_plating_effect-u: +3 DR. Speed drops one category. Cannot be installed on Arcbikes.
vmod_reinforced_suspension_effect-u: Removes one penalty die from Drive/Pilot rolls caused by rough terrain, debris, unstable ground, or atmospheric disturbance.
vmod_run_flat_coils_effect-u: When Heavily Damaged, no immediate Speed reduction for rest of current scene. Aerial: reduces crash severity by one result band on forced landing.
vmod_sigil_array_effect-u: Pre-inscribed wards activate on startup. Choose one fixed effect: Concealment (penalty die on visual detection while moving) or Alert (ping when living creature enters Short range while stationary).
vmod_signal_dampener_effect-u: −20% to Electronics and Slicing rolls to detect, track, or lock onto this vehicle remotely. Does not prevent visual observation.
vmod_smart_linked_hardpoint_effect-u: +10% to Gunnery checks with one Hardpoint weapon synced to pilot's HUD. Requires Smartlink-capable interface.
vmod_smoke_chaff_dispenser_effect-u: Once per scene: ranged attacks and Drone Operation checks targeting this vehicle suffer one penalty die until next turn. 3 charges.
vmod_stealth_drive_effect-u: +10% to Stealth while vehicle is in motion. Ground and water vehicles only — does not function on aerial vehicles.
vmod_stormchaser_arc_wings_effect-u: Bonus die on Pilot (Aircraft) or Stability checks in storm conditions, Veil disturbances, or gravity flux zones. Aerial vehicles only.
vmod_tactical_sensor_array_effect-u: +10% to Perception and Gunnery rolls from within the vehicle. When paired with Auto-Turret Conversion, enables AI-assisted targeting.
vmod_thermal_suppressor_effect-u: Thermal and IR scanning requires Hard Electronics check rather than standard Perception. Effective vs. drone overwatch and Veil-sight detection.
vmod_veil_flux_dampening_grid_effect-u: All crew: +20% CON vs. Veil Corruption while inside. Reduces Arcane Environmental Effects by one tier while active. Lasts one scene before cooldown.
vmod_veil_shielded_hull_effect-u: Vehicle gains VS 3, or increases existing VS by 3. Does not protect crew from Veil damage dealt directly to them.
vmod_ward_anchor_point_effect-u: Awakened character inside may sustain a Warding spell through the hull without line of sight. Vehicle counts as anchor. Normal Strain costs apply.
watcher_or_handler_desc-u: A figure tasked with monitoring, guiding, or retrieving members who stray too far.
work_through_it_desc-u: Once per session, when you fail a Mechanics, Electronics, or Tradecraft roll while repairing, building, or maintaining equipment, you may still complete the task at reduced effectiveness or increased wear, at the GM’s discretion. The job gets done—but something is stressed, degraded, or temporary. This feature reflects grit and experience, not perfection.
worn_map_set_desc-u: Physical or digital maps marked with personal annotations, dead routes, safe paths, and places you never return to.
